gmw_00002814.name: EGS-zs8-1
gmw_00002814.overview: EGS-zs8-1 is a bright, high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of approximately 7.73 [arXiv:1502.05399, arXiv:2301.07072]. It features strong Lyman-alpha and carbon III emission lines [arXiv:1502.05399, arXiv:1606.01304] and resides within a common ionized bubble alongside several neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2112.07675, arXiv:2301.03599].

sources.summary: The paper confirms EGS-zs8-1 as a luminous z=7.730 Lyman-break galaxy with detected Ly emission, making it an unusually bright spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift source.

quotes.quote: The spectra of our target source EGS-zs8-1 (see Table tab:specsummary for summary of properties) revealed a significant emission line at the expected slit position in both masks independently (right panels Fig fig:maskLayout ). The full 4 hr stacked 2D and 1D spectra are shown in Figure fig:spectrum , showing a line with a clear asymmetric profile, as expected for a Ly line at high redshift ( z 3 ). Furthermore, it lies at the expected wavelength based on our photometric redshift estimate z_ phot = 7.7 0.3 . We therefore interpret this line as Ly (other possibilities are discussed in section sec:caveats ). We fit the line using a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) approach based on the emcee python library emcee13 . Our model is based on a truncated Gaussian profile to account for the IGM absorption and includes the appropriate instrumental resolution. The model also includes the uncertainty on the background continuum level. The MCMC output provides full posterior PDFs and uncertainties for the redshift, line flux, significance, and line width. The line corresponds to a redshift of z_ Ly = 7.7302 0.0006 , with a total luminosity of L_ Ly =1.2 0.2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , and a total detection significance of 6.1 .

As shown in Fig. fig:fig4 , with z_ spec = 7.730 and an absolute magnitude M_ UV = -22.06 0.05 the source EGS-zs8-1 is currently the most distant and brightest spectroscopically confirmed galaxy apart from a gamma ray burst at z=8.2 ; Tanvir09,Salvaterra09 . EGS-zs8-1 also populates the brightest bin of the recent Bouwens14 z 8 UV luminosity function (LF), which makes it an unusually rare object. A spectroscopic confirmation of its high redshift is thus particularly valuable for proving the existence of bright H = 25.0 mag galaxies at z 8 and for validating the bright end LF constraints.

sources.summary: The paper treats z8 5 as the brightest, possibly central z=7.7 LAE cluster member whose lower redshift may boost Ly transmission for galaxies behind its ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: With the discovery of the clustered LAEs at z 7.7 , we calculate 3-dimensional (3D) separations of individual z 7.7 LAEs from the brightest (and potentially central) galaxy (z8 5). The left panel of Figure fig:laecluster shows the distribution of our z 7.7 LAEs (green) in addition to the three z 7.7 LAEs that were previously discovered white; Oesch2015a, Roberts-Borsani2016a, Tilvi2020a . The estimated 3D physical distances from z8 5 are listed in parentheses, ranging from 0.7pMpc at the nearest to 2.5pMpc at the farthest. The crowd of ionizing sources could create the extended ionized structure beyond a 1pMpc scale of individual ionized bubbles and eventually enhance Ly transmission in the IGM.

It is remarkable that the brightest member (z8 5) of the clustered LAEs has the lowest redshift in the cluster indicating that it is likely located in front of all the other LAEs toward the observer (see the right panel of Figure fig:cartoon ). That is, we may be witnessing a bright galaxy boosting the Ly visibility preferentially on its rear side. The left panel of Figure fig:cartoon illustrates how Ly transmissivity can be boosted in the rear side of a bright galaxy. The bright galaxy may be located at the center of the local ionized bubble.

sources.summary: The paper treats EGS-zs8-1 as an exceptionally bright z=7.73 galaxy whose lack of observed neighboring overdensity can be explained by luminosity-halo scatter.

quotes.quote: The recent spectroscopic confirmation of galaxies EGS-zs8-1 2015ApJ...804L..30O and EGSY8p7 2015ApJ...810L..12Z at z = 7.73 and z= 8.68 present an ideal testbed to investigate the relation between brightness/mass and clustering properties. In fact, these two sources have exceptionally bright magnitudes ( M_ UV 22 , to be compared with the characteristic magnitude M^ * _ UV 20.2 at z 8 2015ApJ...803...34B ). Consequently, these galaxies are objects with number densities of the order 10^ 6 Mpc ^ 3 2015ApJ...803...34B , which are rare in cosmological simulations of volumes comparable to (or a few times larger than) that of the CANDELS survey 2016MNRAS.461L..51W .
Curiously, neither EGS-zs8-1 or EGSY8p7 show evidence of being located in significantly clustered regions based on current photometric data in the EGS fields.

The motivation for this study was to understand whether the lack of neighbors around EGS-zs8-1 and EGSY8p7 at similar redshift was creating tension with modeling of galaxy formation and evolution. Our results provide a natural explanation for the absence of a clustering signature in the data at the current depth.

sources.summary: The paper includes EGS-zs8-1 as a spectroscopically identified high-redshift galaxy, derives expected NB1257/F125W colors from published Ly fluxes and EWs, and finds it beyond the survey depth.

quotes.quote: By comparison with other studies, we included the expected location of three very high- z galaxies from the literature in our color-magnitude diagram (Fig. color_magnitude , triangles). The F125W and NB1257 magnitudes were computed using the published Ly fluxes and EWs. In particular, we derived the expected colors of the spectroscopically identified galaxies MACS1149-JD at z = 9.1096 2018Natur.557..392H,2018ApJ...854...39H,2020ARA&A..58..617O,2021MNRAS.505.3336L , EGSY8p7 at z = 8.683 2015ApJ...810L..12Z and EGS-zs8-1 at z = 7.7302 2015ApJ...804L..30O . We conclude that all these galaxies, even the two luminous galaxies in the EGS, are beyond the limiting magnitude of this work and therefore would be undetectable in our NB1257 image.

The estimated Ly luminosities at z = 9.34 of galaxies MACS1149-JD and EGSY8p7 were 4.7 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 and 1.9 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. EGS-zs8-1 presents the same Ly flux as EGSY8p7 , hence the estimated Ly luminosity would be the same for both galaxies. As we already mentioned in Sect. finalsample , these galaxies are beyond the depth reached by our work and, in fact, only the surveys conducted by and were deep enough to be able to realistically detect LAEs with the same luminosity as the luminous galaxies of the EGS.

sources.summary: The paper describes EGS-zs8-1 as a luminous z=7.73 Ly emitter with strong radiation fields, nearby confirmed LAEs, and evidence for a common ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: These are EGS-zs8-1 at z_ spec = 7.7302 0.0006 ( Oesch2015 ; see also Stark2017 ; Tilvi2020 ), EGS-zs8-2 at z_ spec = 7.4770 0.0008 Borsani2016,Stark2017 , and EGSY-z8p7 at z_ spec = 8.683^ +0.001 _ 0.004 ( Zitrin2015 ; see also Mainali18 ). All of these sources are expected to exhibit strong radiation fields due to high-equivalent width [ OIII ] +H emission lines identified through their Spitzer/IRAC photometry . Indeed, highly excited UV lines have been confirmed in the MOSFIRE spectra of EGS-zs8-1 ( C iii ; Stark2017 ) and EGSY-z8p7 ( N v ; Mainali18 ).

One of these, EGS-zs8-1, has subsequently been observed through narrow-band observations, from which two fainter LAEs have been identified in its immediate proximity Tilvi2020 . These latter sources have also been confirmed through Ly spectroscopy and they were named z8 SM at z=7.767 and z8 4 at z=7.748 . They likely sit in a common, ionized bubble with EGS-zs8-1, since they are only separated by 0.7 to 0.8 physical Mpc (pMpc) from each other and their estimated ionized bubble radii reach 0.5-1.0 pMpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes EGS-zs8-1 as a bright red z>7 galaxy with extreme optical-line flux excess and a clean CIII] doublet detection with flux near half of Ly.

quotes.quote: EGS-zs8-1 EGS-zs8-1 is a bright (H _ 160 =25.0) galaxy with a red IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 0.53 0.09 ). As described in Roberts-borsani2015 , the 4.5 m flux excess suggests very large rest-frame equivalent width ( 911 122 ) [ OIII ] +H emission.

In Figure fig:egs-zs81 b, the narrow spectral window between 1.650 and 1.675 m is shown, revealing a clean detection of both components of the [ CIII ] , CIII ] doublet. We measure line fluxes of 4.5 0.5 10^ 18 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and 3.6 0.5 10^ 18 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for the 1907 and 1909 \ components, respectively. The total flux in the resolved CIII ] doublet is close to 50 that of Ly , nearly 10 greater than is seen in the most extreme CIII ] emitting galaxies at lower redshift (e.g., Erb2010,Christensen2012,Stark2014 ).

sources.summary: The paper models EGS-zs8-1 with spheroidal/elliptical SED templates, finding a Lyman/Balmer-break photometric redshift near z~8.7 and properties similar to the other mature sample galaxies.

quotes.quote: The galaxies EGS2 and EGS1 Best-fits with error bars of the two other sample galaxies are presented on Fig  EGS1E and EGS2 on Fig  EGS2E . Most other outputs as metallicity, masses after calibration are on Table  Results . For EGS1, the best-fitted SED shape gives the photometric redshift z_ LyB =8.72 ^ +1 _ 1.32 , within a few percent errors, consistent with the previously photometric redshift z_ phot = 7.42. After tests with multiple libraries of the two (spheroidal, spiral ) geometries and slab, the best-fitted SED shape of the spheroidal geometry associated with the scenario SFH of elliptical galaxies (E) is once more preferred. Geometry and SFH of spirals are not compatible with data while SB scenario is discussed in the last section.

sources.summary: The paper identifies EGS-zs8-1 as the first and brightest z=7.7 Ly-association galaxy in EGS, with confirmed Ly and C III] detections enabling velocity-offset measurement.

quotes.quote: One of the most intense C III ] emitters at z 7 is EGS-zs8-1 (EW =22 ; Stark2017 ), the brightest galaxy in the z=7.7 Ly association in the EGS field.

Deeper spectroscopy is required to provide a more stringent constraint on whether this faint galaxy may have enhanced Ly emission. z=7.7 Association of Ly Emitters EGS-zs8-1 was the first galaxy identified in the z=7.7 association of Ly emitters in the EGS. The source was spectroscopically confirmed at z_ Ly =7.730 by Oesch2015 following photometric selection as a bright ( H_ 160 =25.0 ) galaxy with an IRAC excess in Roberts-Borsani2016 . Detection of the [ C III ] , C III ] doublet Stark2017 enabled confirmation of the Ly velocity offset ( 340 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper cites EGS-zs8-1 as a bright high-redshift galaxy in a 1.02 pMpc common ionized bubble, supported by HST overdensity evidence, with C III] giving its systemic redshift.

quotes.quote: Based on a relation between luminosity and ionized bubble size derived from theoretical models, Tilvi+20 estimate that EGS-zs8-1 sits in a common bubble with at least 3 neighbouring galaxies, with a size of 1.02 pMpc. More recently, Leonova+21 present analysis of Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging that supports the conclusion of a bubble surrounding EGS-zs8-1 given it resides in an overdensity.
The systemic redshift is obtained from the additional emission lines present in each galaxy's spectrum (EGSY-8p69: N V, Mainali+18 ; EGS-zs8-1: C III ] , Stark+17 ; COSY: [ C II ] , Pentericci+16 ).

gmw_00002815.name: G1
gmw_00002815.overview: The galaxy G1 hosts a compact, unresolved radio source at its center [arXiv:2402.13207]. This radio emission exhibits a steep spectrum and a high brightness temperature, indicating the presence of an active galactic nucleus rather than star-forming activity [arXiv:2402.13207].

sources.summary: The paper detects a compact 1.7 GHz radio feature in G1, identifies it with the optical galaxy, and finds brightness temperature requiring nuclear activity rather than star formation.

quotes.quote: Radio emission in G1 We detected a single feature with a S/N of 6.3 in the 1.7 GHz image of G1. The right ascension and declination of the radio feature are _ G1 = 03^ h 01^ m 51 5127 0 0003 and _ G1 = +35 50' 28 294 0 004 , respectively.
Our VLBI map shows an unresolved source without any prominent jet structure. The radio emission can be well described by a single circular Gaussian brightness distribution model.

With the mas-scale resolution of the EVN, we detected compact radio-emitting features in two galaxies, G1 and G3. Both have brightness temperatures exceeding the value expected from star-forming galaxies, which must be due to nuclear activity. The brighter radio source of the two, located in G3, was detected at both 1.7 and 4.9 GHz and shows a flat radio spectrum. The weaker one in G1 has a steeper radio spectrum and remains undetected at the higher frequency of 4.9 GHz.

gmw_00002816.name: G3
gmw_00002816.overview: Galaxy G3 is the second brightest member of a galaxy nonet [arXiv:1607.05080]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1607.05080] and exhibits nuclear activity [arXiv:2402.13207] associated with a compact, flat-spectrum radio core [arXiv:1607.05080, arXiv:2402.13207].

sources.summary: The paper describes galaxy G3 as a nonet member with a compact radio core, a very massive black hole, and an alternative restarted-AGN explanation for 4C 35.06.

quotes.quote: A 5 GHz VLBA observation at 4.87 2.23 mas ^ 2 resolution detected a compact radio core of 2.6 mJy peak flux (3.5 mJy integrated) associated with the galaxy G3, which is the second brightest member of the nonet b28 .

In an alternative scenario, have suggested that the rapid movement of galaxy G3 and its episodic AGN radio activity is the reason for the observed peculiar radio morphology of 4C 35.06. In their interpretation, the large scale jet morphology is due to the earlier phase of activity of G3, which had switched off its radio emission and then restarted while it was moving to its current position, resulting in the offset inner double-lobed morphology and steep-spectrum larger jet structure to the north, similar to dying radio galaxies b98 . Thus, at present, we are observing an aged FRI-like large scale structure with an embedded restarted radio source.

These results show that galaxies G1, G3, G5, G7, and G9 all host supermassive black holes of mass ( M_ BH few 10^ 8 M_ ). For the other three galaxies G2, G4 and G6, the estimate of M_ BH has large errors. Interestingly, the most massive black hole of mass M_ BH 10^ 9 M_ resides in the galaxy G3, which showed a radio loud AGN core in previous VLBA observations b28 .

sources.summary: The paper detects G3 at 1.7 and 4.9 GHz, gives VLBI coordinates, and interprets its high-brightness, flat-spectrum radio source as nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: Radio emission in G3 We detected radio emission in G3 with S/Ns of 27 and 45 at 1.7 GHz and 4.9 GHz, respectively. The 4.9 GHz right ascension and declination coordinates of the detected feature are _ G3 = 03^ h 01^ m 51 8129 0 0001 and _ G3 = +35 50' 19 588 0 002 , respectively.

With the mas-scale resolution of the EVN, we detected compact radio-emitting features in two galaxies, G1 and G3. Both have brightness temperatures exceeding the value expected from star-forming galaxies, which must be due to nuclear activity. The brighter radio source of the two, located in G3, was detected at both 1.7 and 4.9 GHz and shows a flat radio spectrum.

gmw_00002817.overview: 0300+470 is a core-dominated BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1006.4777] that lacks a reliable redshift [arXiv:1308.2713]. The source features a one-sided kiloparsec-scale halo [arXiv:1006.4777], a jet with an inward-moving component [arXiv:1308.2713], and a transverse rotation-measure gradient across its core region [arXiv:1709.09062].

sources.summary: The paper describes B0300 + 470/4C +47.08 as a compact asymmetric BL Lac source with a kiloparsec-scale one-sided halo and monthly radio variability.

quotes.quote: B0300 + 470 4C +47.08 is a BL Lac object, which shows a compact asymmetric morphology in VLBI observations lister09a . It displays a one-sided halo at kilo-parsec scale. This source is variable on a monthly timescale at centimeter wavelengths aller85 in total flux and linear polarization, and the source is core-dominated nan99 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0300+470 as a BL Lac lacking a reliable redshift, gives an upper limit from absent intergalactic absorption, and reports a newly significant inward-moving jet component.

quotes.quote: 0300+470: No reliable redshift exists for this BL Lac object. derive a statistical upper limit of z < 1.63 based on the absence of intergalactic absorption features. With the addition of a new epoch in 2008, a component (ID = 2) first identified in Paper VI in this BL Lac jet now has a statistically significant inward motion.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBA RM maps of 0300+470 showing a core-region rotation-measure gradient, clearer with a circular beam and significant at 4.9 sigma.

quotes.quote: 0300+470. The RM maps for this source are shown in Fig. fig:rmmaps-BG173-1 (top row). The RM map made using the elliptical convolving beam (left panel) shows an RM gradient across the core region, whose significance is about 3 . The RM map made using an equal-area circular beam (middle panel) likewise shows a clear, monotonic transverse RM gradient across the core region, with a significance of 4.9 (right panel).

gmw_00002818.overview: The active galactic nucleus 0355+508 is a flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1003.3542] located at a redshift of z = 1.517 [arXiv:1609.09575, arXiv:1003.3542]. It hosts a relativistic jet [arXiv:1609.09575] that exhibits superluminal motion [arXiv:1609.09575, arXiv:0803.3388], extreme wobbling [arXiv:0803.3388], and a transverse rotation measure gradient [arXiv:1503.03411].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 5BZQJ 0359+5057 as a new gamma-ray detection, explaining its prior catalog absence through variability and Pass 7 threshold limits.

quotes.quote: 5BZQJ 0359+5057. Computing a TS map in the 0.5-12 GeV energy range (Fig. bzq0359 ), we identified a bright point-like source clearly emerging from a low and flat TS background. The light curve plotted in Fig. bzq0359 , shows that the source 5BZQJ 0359+5057 (4C +50.11) was undetectable by Fermi-LAT, most of the time with the exception of the period between June 2013 and April 2016 when it underwent a phase of strong \ activity. This is consistent with its nondetection up to the 3FGL catalog (since 3FGL only integrate observations from Aug. 2008 up to Aug. 2012).

As result we found TS ^ Pass \ 7 _ 4.0 \ yrs =15.2, with model parameters N _0 = 8.1 2.2 10 ^ 13 ph/cm ^2 /s/MeV and =2.55 0.19, using E _0 =1000 MeV as pivot energy. Hence, this source was out of the 3FGL catalog simply because it did not meet the TS > 25.0 criteria. We also noticed that the parameters estimated at the time of 3FGL catalog were already in good agreement with those we present on Table detectiontab , showing that \ signatures at 10 to 25 TS level contain rich information, as discussed in . As an exercise, we used a setup similar to 3FGL (integrating over 2008-2012) but now with PASS 8 data. This results in a firm detection with TS > 25 and the ray SED shown as red points in Fig. bzq0359 (bottom).

sources.summary: The paper describes c50 as a high-redshift FSRQ/NRAO 150 with strong radio/millimeter AGN emission, a superluminal jet, harder gamma-ray activity, and hour-scale flares.

quotes.quote: We investigate the ray and X-ray properties of the Flat Spectrum Radio Quasar (FSRQ) c50 at redshift z= 1.517 . The Fermi LAT data indicate that this source was in an active state since 2013 July. During this active period, the source's emission appeared harder in rays, with the flux having increased by more than a factor of three. We analyze two distinct flares seen in the active state and find that the variability is as short as several hours.

The FSRQ c50 (also known as NRAO 150) is one of the strongest radio and millimeter AGN sources in the northern sky 1966ApJS...13...65P,2008ASPC..386..249A, 2010ApJS..189....1A . The VLBI monitoring observations showed that the inner jet (inner 0.5 mas from the core) exhibits superluminal motions with _ app (6.3 1.1) and a large, >100^ projected misalignment of the jet within the inner 0.5 mas to 1 mas from the core 2007A&A...476L..17A, 2014A&A...566A..26M . These properties imply that a relativistic jet points toward the Earth with a very small viewing angle 2007A&A...476L..17A .

sources.summary: The paper describes NRAO 150 as a strong high-redshift radio source with a curved, possibly rotating jet whose millimeter VLBI structure changes orientation near the core.

quotes.quote: NRAO 150: The last important aspect, which we like to address in this paper, is the possibility to study with mm-VLBI the nature of jet bending and trace strongly curved jets down to their physical origin. In almost all sources so far imaged by mm-VLBI, this jet curvature increases with decreasing core separation. An exciting example for a curved and possibly `rotating' jet nozzle is seen in the strong radio source NRAO 150 ( z 1.52 , ). It shows a `sinusoidally' appearing long-term light curve (Michigan and Mets ahovi flux monitoring programs), and a remarkable flux density increase from 2 Jy to 12 Jy at 37 GHz during the last 15 yrs ( , see also Fig. paswing , left).

In Figure nrao150maps we show three VLBI maps obtained at 8, 43, and 86 GHz in spring 2002. At cm-wavelengths, a one-sided core jet structure with a jet extending to at least = 20 mas and oriented along PA (30-40)^ is seen. At higher angular resolutions, the mm-VLBI images reveal a very different jet orientation of PA 140^ , showing very pronounced jet bending of PA 110^ within only 0.5 mas (4.3 pc) core separation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NRAO 150 as an extreme quasar jet-wobbling case, with millimeter VLBI showing non-ballistic superluminal motion and magnetic fields possibly shaping the jet dynamics.

quotes.quote: Recently, the discovery of an extreme case of jet wobbling in the quasar NRAO 150 has been reported through the first ultra-high-resolution VLBI set of images obtained from this source at 86 GHz (3.5 mm) and 43 GHz (7 mm) see Fig NRAO150 Agu07 . To compute the first quantitative estimates of the basic physical properties of the jet in NRAO 150, we have analyzed the ultra-high-resolution images from a new sub-milliarcsecond-scale data set (covering from 1997 to 2007) at 86 GHz and 43 GHz with the GMVA, the Global Millimetre VLBI Array (GMVA, see www.mpifr bonn.mpg.de/div/vlbi/globalmm/) and the VLBA. The data shows an extreme projected counter-clock-wise jet wobbling of up to 11^ / yr within the inner 61 pc of the jet, which is associated with a highly non-ballistic superluminal motion of the jet within this region.

NRAO 150 seems to be an extreme case, which reflects the remarkable non-ballistic nature of its jet features. In view of this intriguing result, we argue that the magnetic field might play an important role in the dynamics of the jet in NRAO 150, which is supported by the large values of the magnetic field strength obtained from our first estimates B 0.7 G. Agu07 . It is still unclear whether the reported change of the direction of ejection in NRAO 150 is related to a regular (strictly periodic or not) behavior or to a single event.

sources.summary: The paper measures the first redshift for NRAO 150, classifies it as a powerful flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar, and links its SED to accretion-disk and jet activity.

quotes.quote: NRAO 150 is one of the brightest radio and mm AGN sources on the northern sky. It has been revealed as an interesting source where to study extreme relativistic jet phenomena. However, its cosmological distance has not been reported so far, because of its optical faintness produced by strong Galactic extinction.

The near--IR spectroscopic observations reveal strong H and H emission lines from which the cosmological redshift of NRAO 150 ( z=1.517 0.002 ) has been determined for the first time. We classify the source as a flat--spectrum radio--loud quasar, for which we estimate a large super--massive black--hole mass 5 10^ 9 M_ .

Comparison of these source properties, and its broad--band spectral--energy distribution, with those of Fermi blazars allow us to predict that NRAO 150 is among the most powerful blazars, and hence a high luminosity --although not detected yet-- -ray emitter.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes 0355+508 VLBA RM maps and finds a core-region transverse RM gradient that remains visible with a circular beam at about 3 sigma.

quotes.quote: A visual inspection of the published RM images indicated the possible presence of transverse RM gradients across the jets of 0355+508 and 0748+126, as well as 1749+096, whose transverse RM gradient was previously reported by Gabuzda et al. (2008).

0355+508. Our map in Fig. fig:rmmaps8 a shows a monotonic transverse RM gradient across the core region, with a significance of 4.2 . The orientation of the beam in our image is roughly along the jet, making it possible that the significance of the gradient has been artificially increased by this effective averaging along the jet. The RM gradient remains visible when the RM map is made using a circular beam, with a significance of about 3 .

sources.summary: The paper says 0355+508/NRAO 150 has a wide range of inner jet position angles and is the most rapidly varying monotonic jet, consistent with earlier VLBA work.

quotes.quote: Some jets (e.g., NRAO 150 = 0355+508) show a very wide range of inner jet position angle and Gaussian component position angles, likely because the viewing angle to the inner jet lies within the opening angle of the (presumably conical) outflow.

The most rapidly varying jet, at 9.8 1 y^ 1 , is 0355+508 (NRAO 150). Our measurement is consistent with the swings of up to 11 y^ 1 seen previously in this jet by 2007AA...476L..17A in VLBA images at 43 and 86 GHz.

gmw_00002819.overview: The active galaxy 0814+425 is a BL Lacertae candidate [arXiv:1005.2177, arXiv:1308.2713] that exhibits quasi-periodicity in the radio and optical bands [arXiv:1005.2177] alongside infrared variability [arXiv:1005.2177]. Its radio jet appears bent [arXiv:1005.2177, arXiv:astro-ph/0111279], and its innermost jet component showed inward motion between 1995 and 2006 [arXiv:1308.2713].

sources.summary: The paper uses S4 0814+42 as an example light curve, finds compatible multiwavelength periods, and reports a radio-gamma lag consistent with earlier results.

quotes.quote: The results of Table tab:periodicity_results also reveal compatible periods with those reported in P20 and P22 in 5 of them: PG 1246+586, S4 1144+40, S4 0814+42, PKS 0301 243, and TXS 1902+556.

These authors report time lags for the radio- ray correlation of 160-240 days for TXS 0059+581, S4 0814+42, TXS 0518+211, and TXS 1902+556, with local significance < 3 . We find compatible results for S4 0814+42, with a time lag of 207.2 28.1 days.

We find compatible periods w..t. those from P22 for PKS 0426-380 (V band), PKS 0447-439, PKS 0250-225, S4 1144+40, S4 0814+42, TXS 0518+211, PKS 2052-47 (V and R bands) and 87GB 164812.2+524023. The blazars S4 0814+42, TXS 0518+211 also have compatible periods with Table tab:periodicity_results .

sources.summary: The paper describes 0814+425 as a post-merger BL Lac candidate with radio and optical quasi-periodicity, infrared variability, transitory colors, and a bent VLBA jet.

quotes.quote: * 0814+425 (OJ 425) (BL Lac, z=0.245 ): Almost periodical variability in the radio and optical. Variable in the infrared. This system is positioned in the transitory region of the color-color diagram. Its VLBA jet appears bent.

sources.summary: The paper says 0814+425 lacks a secure redshift, gives host-magnitude and absorption constraints, and reports an innermost jet component with significant inward motion between 1995 and 2006.

quotes.quote: 0814+425: No reliable spectroscopic redshift exists for this BL Lac object. found z > 0.75 based on a lower limit to the host galaxy magnitude, and derive a statistical upper limit of z < 2.47 based on the absence of intergalactic absorption features. The innermost component of this jet showed significant inward motion between 1995 and 2006. We did not previously consider this component to be robust in Paper VI due to confusion arising from an emerging new component in 2007 (now identified as ID = 6).

sources.summary: The paper describes 0814+425 as a mildly distorted source whose VLBI and MERLIN images show a southeast jet bending eastward, without enough structure to indicate a helical jet.

quotes.quote: 0814+425 : it is one of the less distorted objects, with a PA=28 . The VLBI image (Fig. 0814e ), shows a hint of a jet extended about 10 mas towards the SE direction, confirming previous observations (Polatidis et al., 1995). The MERLIN image (Fig. 0814m ) shows that the jet continues its path for about 100 mas in a direction about 10 bent towards the East. This structure, despite its initial bending, is not a sufficient indication of the presence of a helical jet.

gmw_00002820.overview: The active galaxy 0954+556 is classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1510.06763, arXiv:1807.02085] or blazar [arXiv:1510.06763, arXiv:2009.05082] with high optical polarization [arXiv:2009.05082]. It features a radio jet extending west-northwest [arXiv:1101.5822] and exhibits strong, variable gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1004.3767, arXiv:1308.2713].

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C +55.17 as a gamma-ray source whose morphology, variability, luminosity, and SED leave its classification open between FSRQ/blazar and CSS/MSO scenarios.

quotes.quote: 4C + 55.17 ( z = 0.896) first appeared as a ray source during the EGRET era (as 3EG J0952+5501, Hartmann et al. 1999, and EGR J0957+5513, Casandjian Grenier 2008). The tentative association, due to poor EGRET localization, was confirmed by Fermi LAT after the first three months of observations (Abdo et al. 2009b). The VLBI morphology at 5 GHz reveals two distinct emission regions, which may resemble compact hot spots and lobes. In this scenario the kpc-scale emission observed with the VLA might be interpreted as a remnant of previous jet activity.

The source showed no evidence of blazar flaring activity at any wavelength, nor any significant evidence of long-term variability from radio to rays. Moreover, the low brightness temperature and the hard ray spectrum observed by Fermi LAT is unlikely for a flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ). However, the presence of broad optical emission lines in its spectrum and high optical/UV core luminosity, together with its high ray luminosity (L _ 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 ) have in turn led to the common classification of 4C + 55.17 as a FSRQ. The spectral energy distribution (SED) of the source is modelled by both the blazar and CSS scenario (McConville et al. 2011), leaving the debate on the nature of this object still open.

sources.summary: The paper uses published Mg II and black-hole measurements for J0957.6+5523, models its broadband FSRQ SED, and discusses its VHE detectability and MAGIC comparison.

quotes.quote: 2012ApJ...748...49S reported M_ BH and Mg ii line luminosity for J0957.6+5523 and J1224.7+2124, which we use to calculate L_ disk .

We generate steady-state, i.e. time-averaged, broadband SEDs of all 8 FSRQs following the details outlined in Section sec3 and reproduce them using both leptonic and lepto-hadronic emission scenarios. The leptonic model SEDs are presented in Figure fig_leptonic_sed and the associated SED parameters are given in Table tab:leptonic_sed_param . The results of the lepto-hadronic SED modeling are shown in Figure fig_hadronic_sed and we provide the physical parameters derived from the modeling in Table tab:hadronic_sed_param . Using the results of the SED modeling, we compute the degree of X-ray and ray polarization predicted by the leptonic and hadronic models.

2011ApJ...738..148M predicted J0957.6+5523 as a plausible candidate for VHE detection due to its hard 0.1 300 GeV spectrum and the lack of significant ray flux variability, however, a dedicated observing campaign of 35 hours with MAGIC and 45 hours from VERITAS only resulted in flux upper limits 2014MNRAS.440..530A,2013arXiv1303.1103F . A comparison with the MAGIC sensitivity curve in the ray spectrum of J0957.6+5523 (Figure fig_gamma_sed ) suggests that the source would be difficult to detect even in 50 hours of integration.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0954+556 as an extreme high-polarization RA blazar whose MgII and C IV broad-line profiles show unusually strong redward asymmetry.

quotes.quote: There are three very redward asymmetric (RA) examples of a MgII BEL, 3C 279 (z = 0.536), 0954+556 (z = 0.899, 4C +55.17) and S5 1803+784 (z = 0.684). They are very high polarization quasars (HPQs) that have been detected with 10 optical polarization imp90,lis16 . The MgII profiles are shown in Figure 1 in order to demonstrate the extreme circumstance under consideration. This is not a subtle effect nor a consequence of FeII multiplets. The preponderance of the line shape is redshifted emission from, ostensibly, receding gas even though these objects have powerful jets relativistic of plasma beamed toward Earth.

The quasars 3C 279 and 0954+556 have the largest redward asymmetry, A (defined in Equation (1)), of the C IV BEL in the sample of HST spectra from the Evans and Koratkar Atlas of Hubble Space Telescope Faint Object Spectrograph Spectra of Active Galactic Nuclei and Quasars eva04,pun10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C+55.17 as a bright Fermi-LAT FSRQ and VHE candidate, combining earlier and new MAGIC observations after non-detections.

quotes.quote: 4C+55.17 ( z=0.902 , 2018A&A...613A..51P ) is a bright Fermi LAT FSRQ, which made the source a promising VHE emission candidate, due to high brightness and lack of strong variability (a low variability index is reported in all data releases of the 4FGL catalogs). MAGIC monitored this source in the VHE band (during the low state, flux below average ) from November 2010 to January 2011 for 28 hours of good-quality data. No significant VHE ray signal above 100 GeV was detected. Integral and differential ULs on the ray flux were derived 2014MNRAS.440..530A .

Between 2008 and 2020, the source state was stable, as can be seen in   fig:plot_mwl_4C55 ). In this paper, old and new observations were merged to investigate this source in more detail at the VHE range.

sources.summary: The paper treats 4C +55.17 as a distant hard-spectrum gamma-ray quasar targeted by MAGIC; no VHE signal was detected, and upper limits agree with low-flux EBL attenuation models.

quotes.quote: The bright gamma-ray quasar 4C +55.17 is a distant source ( z = 0.896 ) with a hard spectrum at GeV energies as observed by the Large Area Telescope (LAT) on board the Fermi satellite. This source is identified as a good source candidate for very-high-energy (VHE; > 30 GeV) gamma rays. In general VHE gamma rays from distant sources provide an unique opportunity to study the extragalactic background light (EBL) and underlying astrophysics. The flux intensity of this source in the VHE range is investigated. Then, constraints on the EBL are derived from the attenuation of gamma-ray photons coming from the distant blazar.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C +55.17 as a z=0.8955 gamma-ray source with a soft 1-300 GeV spectrum and decade-scale variability between EGRET and Fermi measurements.

quotes.quote: 4C +55.17 at z=0.8955 also has a relatively soft spectrum in the 1-300 GeV band. The \ measurements are compared to the broad band data in Fig. fig:4C55 . The source was previously detected in the 0.1-1 GeV band by EGRET. Comparison of the historical EGRET measurement with the Fermi measurement of the source flux shows that the source is variable at 10 yr time scale.

sources.summary: The paper describes two VLA radio features, a MERLIN/VLA jet toward the west-northwest, and X-ray detection of the five-arcsecond feature likely associated with the jet.

quotes.quote: 0954 + 556 (4C +55.17) This X-ray image was first published by and is included in our analysis for completeness. The VLA image shows two distinct features, one at 5 \ from the core along a PA of -60 , and the other at a PA of 45 , about 2.5 \ from the core. A MERLIN map at 5 GHz shows a jet at a PA of about -60 \ extending 150 mas to the WNW 1995ApJS...99..297X and a 5 GHz map with the VLA shows a 5 \ long jet at the same PA as well as the knot 2.5 \ from the core at a position angle of 45 . The 5 \ feature is detected in the X-ray image and is the more likely to be associated with a jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0954+556/4C +55.17 as an unusual quasar that is largely resolved by 15 GHz VLBA, has strong variable gamma-ray emission, and lacks robust jet components.

quotes.quote: 0954+556 (4C +55.17): This unusual quasar is largely resolved by the VLBA at 15 GHz, yet has strong and variable ray emission. have suggested that it may be a young radio source. We were not able to identify any robust components in this jet.

gmw_00002821.overview: The object 1458+718 is a compact steep-spectrum quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.905 [arXiv:2005.11535, arXiv:astro-ph/0612573]. It features a triple radio morphology with a central core, a one-sided jet, and multiple components showing apparent inward motion [arXiv:astro-ph/0612573, arXiv:1308.2713].

sources.summary: The paper summarizes 3C 309.1 as a steep-spectrum radio source with extended rotated structure, possible cooling-flow environment, VLBA core/extended emission, variability, and relativistic motion.

quotes.quote: 3C 309.1 located at z=0.904 (S5 1458+71, Burbidge Burbidge 1969). The overall extent of 3C 390.1 is 2 and it has a steep spectrum of =0.69 between 1 and 10 GHz (Wilkinson 1972). Its extended structures have a steep spectrum with =0.94 0.08 between 100 MHz and 15 GHz and exhibit a large rotation in position angle (Kus et al. 1981). Forbes et al. (1990) reported that 3C 309.1 is surrounded by very massive cooling flows, however, this is not consistent with the small rotation measures (Aaron et al. 1997). The image at 15 GHz observed with the VLBA shows a compact core and a extended component 20 mas to the south with some extended diffuse emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 309.1 as a luminous compact steep-spectrum quasar at z=0.905 with an 80 kpc radio extent, triple radio morphology, and a one-sided jet ending in a hot spot.

quotes.quote: 3C 309.1 is one of the most luminous object in the class ( L_ 178 MHz =8 10^ 28   W Hz ^ 1 ) and its radio structure has been studied with the VLA, MERLIN and the VLBI . The overall extent of the source is 2 2 which translates to 80 kpc We assume a cosmology with H_0=75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 _M=0.3 , _ =0.7 for the redshift z=0.905 . This quasar appears as a triple source in the VLA and MERLIN images. There is a dominant central compact region, a curved, one-sided, 1 arcsecond-long jet emerging to the east with the hot-spot at the end and a low surface brightness component with extended north-south structure.

sources.summary: The paper confirms inward motion for two components in 1458+718 and adds a third inward-moving component in the same complex region, with apparent speeds from 1.4 to 4.6.

quotes.quote: 1458+718: We confirm the apparent inward motion reported in Paper VI of two components in a complex emission region located 25 mas south of the core in this compact steep spectrum quasar. With our new data we have found one additional component in this complex (ID = 2) that is also moving inward, in a non-radial direction. The inward speeds of the three components range from 1.4 to 4.6 .

gmw_00002822.overview: The active galaxy 2021+614 is classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:1603.00947, arXiv:2312.13418] and a GHz-Peaked Spectrum radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0007068]. It is a Compton-thick candidate [arXiv:1603.00947, arXiv:2002.12870] featuring high intrinsic X-ray absorption [arXiv:2312.13418, arXiv:2002.12870] and an unusually high mid-infrared luminosity [arXiv:2002.12870].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2021 + 614 as a CSO with an unusually hard X-ray photon index, possible Compton-thick absorption, and unresolved ambiguity with non-thermal lobe emission.

quotes.quote: 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518 The absorbed power-law model applied to two CSOs, 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518, resulted in an unusually hard photon index of = 0.8 0.3 and = 1.0 0.2 respectively. Typically, such a low value indicates a strong nuclear obscuration able to cover completely the source of the primary X-ray radiation. The observed spectrum is then dominated by a Compton reflection hump (and an accompanying neutral iron line) from a distant material, presumably a molecular torus, resulting in an artificially low photon index. AGN with these properties are called `Compton thick', and the amount of the hydrogen absorbing column required to distort the X-ray spectrum in this manner is N_H 10^ 24 e.g., comastri2004 .

By analogy with OQ 208 and 1607 + 26, it is likely that 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518 are Compton thick CSO candidates. Our result on the intrinsic absorption in these sources would then be significantly underestimated because in our fit we do not account for the column density that would be needed to absorb the primary continuum. Indeed, when we fit the data of 2021 + 614 with the sum of an intrinsically absorbed power law and unabsorbed reflection ( pexrav ) component we obtain Cstat/d.o.f. = 209/442 , N_H^z 9.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 . The resulting photon index of the underlying power law would be, however, very soft, = 3.3 0.3 , and the unabsorbed intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosity would increase by an order of magnitude making 2021 + 614 the second CSO in our sample (in addition to 0710 + 439) to exceed L_X 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 (comparable to the Eddington luminosity of a 10 ^6 M _ black hole).

sources.summary: The paper treats 2021+614 as a compact, X-ray-obscured CSO/AGN whose high intrinsic X-ray continuum requires low ambient density, high UV luminosity, and tight gamma-ray constraints.

quotes.quote: With this picture in mind, we model the broad-band spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of three objects: J1511+0518, OQ+208 (also known as 1404+286 or Mrk 668), and 2021+614, to quantitatively explore the validity of the above expectation. These objects were selected from a sample of compact symmetric objects CSOs, a subset of AGNs known for their compact, young radio structures, see e.g., ODea98,ODea21,Kiehlmann23a,Kiehlmann23b,Readhead23 considered in based on their distinctive X-ray spectral features derived based on extensive observations by , XMM- Newton , and NuSTAR see further denoted as S19 and S23 respectively Sobolewska19b,Sobolewska23 . Key features are a high intrinsic X-ray absorption (equivalent hydrogen column densities, N_ H > 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ), as well as a narrow fluorescent Fe K line and the broad Compton reflection component indicating a reflection from a toroidal obscurer.

2021+614 2021+614 is characterized by an enhanced level of the intrinsic X-ray continuum emission when compared to J1511+0518 and OQ+208. The 0.3-40 keV luminosity of the source, 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , could not be obtained within the framework of the applied scenario for high values of the ambient density, regardless of the equipartition ratio U_e/U_B , without invoking an additional, phenomenological X-ray component. On the other hand, the ISM density at the level approaching n_ low and an equipartition parameter 1 U_e/U_B 10 , allowed us to match the intrinsic X-ray power-law continuum with the lobes' IC emission without violating the LAT upper limits. Interestingly, for J2021+614, the ray upper limits were more restrictive than the X-ray constraints. An increase in L_ UV or a decrease in n_0 violated the ray limits before the X-ray limits.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2021+614 as a GPS radio galaxy and compact symmetric object, measuring separating radio components, subluminal hotspot motion, and an inferred kinematic age of about 440 years.

quotes.quote: We have analysed VSOP (VLBI Space Observatory Programme) data at 5 GHz and ground-based VLBI (Very Long Baseline Interferometry) data at 15 GHz for the GHz-Peaked Spectrum (GPS) radio galaxy 2021+614. Its morphology is consistent with it being a compact symmetric source extending over 30 h^ 1 pc. From a comparison with earlier observations we have detected an increase in the separation and a decrease in the size of the two most prominent components. We determine the projected speed with which these two components recede from each other to be 0.12 0.02 h^ 1 . Given the projected separation of the two components of 16.1 h^ 1 pc, the infered kinematic age is 440 80 years, measured in the source reference frame For all calculations involving cosmological models we use H_0=100 h km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 , q_0 = 0.5 . .

The subluminal character of the separation speed argues in favour of a CSO classification for 2021+614. And so do the low, total linear polarization of the source and the absence of compact components south-west of component D, the nucleus in the core-jet scenario. An undetectable counter-jet would require strong relativistic beaming effects, which would imply high apparent expansion speeds, on the order of speed of light or higher.

sources.summary: 2021+614 is identified as a Compton-thick compact radio galaxy with quasar-like WISE colors and unusually high MIR luminosity relative to its intrinsic X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Only in a few cases, subjected to deeper exposures (1404+286, 1511+0518 and 2021+614), was there a detection of the neutral fluorescence iron line and/or hydrogen column densities in excess of 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , indicating the disk coronal emission is seen through a Compton-thick circumnuclear tori Guainazzi04,Siemiginowska16,Sobolewska19a,Sobolewska19b .

Interestingly enough, all the objects in the sample confirmed as Compton-thick, based on the X-ray spectroscopy 1404+286, 1511+0518 and 2021+614; see respectively Siemiginowska16,Sobolewska19a,Sobolewska19b , are characterized by the WISE colors as being consistent with a quasar identification, as in fact expected.

On the related note, we comment that in the case of z<0.4 young radio galaxies, the only ones with L_ 12 m exceeding 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , are the three Compton-thick objects 1404+286, 1511+0518 and 2021+614, and the peculiar 1345+125, which can also be classified as Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxies see Rodriguez09 . Those four sources appear in fact over-luminous in MIR for their given intrinsic (absorption-corrected) X-ray luminosities when compared to the general local AGN population, as well as other local GPS/CSOs (see in this context the right panel in Figure fig:redshift ).

sources.summary: The paper finds the two outermost components of 2021+614 moving inward at slow speeds, with one non-radial motion vector and an uncertain core location.

quotes.quote: 2021+614: We find the two outermost components (ID = 1 and 2) to have inward motions, albeit with very slow speeds (7.2 and 15.2 , respectively). The fitted motion vector for component 1 is non-radial. The core component location in this jet remains uncertain (see Paper VI).

gmw_00002823.name: NGC 3223
gmw_00002823.overview: NGC 3223 is an Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1502.04716] characterized by a regular, two-armed spiral morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0010358]. Analysis of its arm structure indicates that the galaxy possesses a submaximal disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0010358].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3223's regular two-armed spiral morphology with density-wave arguments, using the arm structure to infer disk surface density and conclude that the disk is submaximal.

quotes.quote: NGC 3223 is a good example to illustrate the constraint on the disk mass obtained this way from the morphology of the galaxy in some more detail. In Fig. 7 a NIR image is reproduced and in Fig. 8 the theoretical model of the spiral arms of the galaxy is shown, where the same value of Oort's constant A/ _0 as in the optical part of NGC 3223 has been adopted.

Since NGC 3223 has two well defined spiral arms, this constrains the critical wave length _ crit according to (3) and (4). This, in turn, determines the surface density of the disk. Together with the radial scale length of the disk measured by Grosb l and Patsis (1998) this allows finally to calculate the disk rotation curve (cf. (1)) shown in Fig. 9. Obviously the disk of NGC 3223 is submaximal, and the same is also found for the two other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3223 as an Sb spiral, measuring stellar and gas kinematics to constrain disk heating, stellar mass-to-light ratio, and dark matter distribution.

quotes.quote: We present the stellar and gaseous kinematics of an Sb galaxy, NGC 3223, with the aim of determining the vertical and radial stellar velocity dispersion as a function of radius, which can help to constrain disk heating theories. Together with the observed NIR photometry, the vertical velocity dispersion is also used to determine the stellar mass-to-light (M/L) ratio, typically one of the largest uncertainties when deriving the dark matter distribution from the observed rotation curve. We find a vertical-to-radial velocity dispersion ratio of _ z / _ R =1.21 0.14 , significantly higher than expectations from known correlations, and a weakly-constrained Ks-band stellar M/L ratio in the range 0.5--1.7, at the high end of (but consistent with) the predictions of stellar population synthesis models. Such a weak constraint on the stellar M/L ratio, however, does not allow us to securely determine the dark matter density distribution. To achieve this, either a statistical approach or additional data (e.g. integral-field unit) are needed.

gmw_00002824.name: MCG+04-48-002
gmw_00002824.overview: MCG+04-48-002 is a galaxy that hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1109.0921]. Although optical spectroscopy shows weak active galactic nucleus signatures, its presence is indicated by radio, hard X-ray, and mid-infrared emissions alongside star formation [arXiv:1109.0921].

sources.summary: The paper argues that MCG+04-48-002 hosts an obscured AGN inferred from radio, hard X-ray, and mid-infrared properties despite weak optical AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: MCG+04-48-002 This galaxy is classified as \ from optical spectroscopy, however its radio, hard X-ray emission and mid-IR spectrum suggest the presence of an obscured AGN Masetti2006, Pereira2010c . Suzaku observations of this galaxy were analyzed by Winter2009 . We detect an emission line at 6.47 ^ +0.05 _ 0.06 that is compatible with neutral . The high hydrogen column density ( N_ H = 63.2 ^ +10.0 _ 5.5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) towards the AGN and the powerful star-formation might explain why no AGN signatures are found in its optical spectrum.

gmw_00002825.name: SDSS J0132 1027
gmw_00002825.overview: SDSS J0132 1027 is a velocity-offset active galactic nucleus that displays several colinear knots of emission [arXiv:1709.09177]. These knots may represent a collimated outflow driven by a radio jet, with F160W observations potentially tracing shock emissions in the interstellar medium [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper interprets SDSS J0132 1027 as a velocity-offset AGN with colinear emission knots, possible radio-jet-driven outflow, and shock-tracing F160W emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0132 1027. This galaxy displays several colinear knots of emission (Figure  fig:hstresults ), which are often seen in radio jets driving collimated AGN outflows (e.g., MI04.1, RO10.1,TO12.1 ). Indeed, SDSS J0132 1027 is detected in the FIRST radio survey BE95.1 with a 20 cm flux density of 1.6 mJy, and higher resolution radio observations would reveal whether it hosts a radio jet. We also note that the southwestern most knot is also detected in the F160W observations. While it is possible that this the stellar bulge of a minor merger, it seems an unlikely coincidence that the minor merger would be colinear with the other knots of emission. Instead, the F160W observations may be tracing 1.6436 m emission, which is a common indicator of shocks (e.g., AL97.1 ) and could be produced as the jet drives into the interstellar medium.

gmw_00002826.name: SDSS J1055+1520
gmw_00002826.overview: SDSS J1055+1520 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits an asymmetric morphology, suggesting it may be a merger remnant [arXiv:1709.09177]. The galaxy hosts an energetic outflow modeled as a bicone, which is not driven by a radio jet [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J1055+1520 as an AGN outflow with enough energy for feedback, possible extended X-rays, no FIRST radio detection, and asymmetric morphology.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1055+1520. The outflow in this galaxy has been modeled as a bicone using the Keck/OSIRIS observations of Pa , and the ratio of the outflow energy to the AGN bolometric luminosity was found to be E _ out /L_ bol = 0.06 0.015 MU16.1 . This exceeds the energy threshold for an outflow to drive a powerful two-stage feedback process that removes cold molecular gas from the inner parts of a galaxy and suppresses star formation, as found by theoretical studies ( E _ out /L_ bol > 0.005 ; HO10.2 ). Further, the bicone is oriented with a position angle 138^ 6^ east of north, which is consistent with the spatial orientation of the X-rays (Figure  fig:chandraresults ). This hints that there may be spatially extended X-ray emission associated with the spatially extended ionized gas, and deeper X-ray observations would be required to confirm this. SDSS J1055+1520 is not detected in FIRST, so its outflow is not radio jet driven.
The HST image also shows that the galaxy itself is asymmetric, which suggests that it may be a merger-remnant galaxy (Figure  fig:chandraresults ).

gmw_00002827.name: SDSS J1346+5228
gmw_00002827.overview: SDSS J1346+5228 is an active galactic nucleus host that features a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of approximately 1.5 to 2.0 million solar masses [arXiv:1801.06332, arXiv:2211.06486]. The galaxy hosts an energetic bicone outflow, possibly powered by a radio jet, that is capable of suppressing star formation [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J1346+5228 as an AGN-outflow host with a Keck/OSIRIS bicone energetic enough to suppress star formation and a possible FIRST radio jet.

quotes.quote: In each galaxy (SDSS J1055+1520, SDSS J1117+6140, and SDSS J1346+5228), the peak of the line emission (Pa , Pa , and in each galaxy, respectively) was spatially offset from the galaxy center by 0 1 (0.2 kpc), 0 2 (0.5 kpc), and 0 3 (0.2 kpc), respectively. Based on the kinematics of the gas in the OSIRIS observations, found that SDSS J1055+1520 and SDSS J1346+5228 host AGN outflows while SDSS J1117+6140 has gas inflow along a bar. They concluded that the spatially-offset peaks in line emission are the result of the outflows or inflows driving shocks into off-nuclear gas.

SDSS J1346+5228. This galaxy's outflow was modeled as a bicone with the Keck/OSIRIS observations, and it is energetic enough ( E _ out /L_ bol =0.01 0.002 ; MU16.1 ) to suppress star formation in the galaxy (as was also the case for SDSS J1055+1520, above). SDSS J1346+5228 is also detected in FIRST with a 20 cm flux density of 1.1 mJy, indicating that there may be a radio jet powering the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper finds J1346+5228's faint high-velocity megamasers are consistent with a small disk and estimates its SMBH mass at 1.5-2.0e7 solar masses.

quotes.quote: J1346+5228 In the VLBI map for J1346+5228 (Figure mapa ), the high-velocity masers are only marginally detected (5 for the redshifted and 4 for the blueshifted maser). The distribution of masers is consistent with a disk of diameter 0.5 mas or 0.3 pc . Applying the ``edge-on'' disk assumption, we defined the line going through the red and blue points as the disk plane, with position angle PA 79.2^ . We also approximated the location of the dynamic center as the unweighted average position of the six systemic masers.
This is within the range of masses enclosed by the high-velocity masers, adding confidence to our estimate that the SMBH in J1346+5228 is between 1.5 10^7M_ and 2.0 10^7M_ .

sources.summary: For J1346+5228, the paper assumes an edge-on maser disk with two masers and uses a relativistic model to estimate its mass-to-distance ratio and black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: For the maser system of J1346+5228, we assume a completely edge-on disc with a P.A. of 79.2 . In this system, we only have two masers, one redshifted feature and a blueshifted one (see Fig. fig:posteriors2 ). We perform an estimation for M/D and v_ rec while considering the position of the BH to be fixed and aligned with the redshifted and blueshifted masers on the vertical axis, and aligned with the geometric center of the systemic masers on the horizontal axis. As one can see from Table tab:prev , in MCPX it is stated that there is no best-fitted value for the BH mass, but its upper and lower bounds are reported. In contrast, we estimate the M/D=1.43^ +0.15 _ 0.13 10^ 5 M_ /Mpc ratio by employing our general relativistic model. This result allows us to calculate the BH mass by considering the distance to the galaxy D=120.8 \ Mpc (see Table tab:prev ), that leads to M=(1.73 0.37) 10^ 7 M_ .

gmw_00002828.name: SDSS J2323+1405
gmw_00002828.overview: SDSS J2323+1405 is a galaxy that hosts a central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.09177]. It features symmetric emission line gas extending north and south of its center, which is produced by shocks and is characteristic of an active galactic nucleus outflow [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J2323+1405 as a central AGN with shocked, spatially offset emission and symmetric gas morphology consistent with an AGN outflow.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2323+1405. This galaxy has symmetric emission line gas extending north and south of the galaxy center, out of the plane of the galaxy (Figure  fig:hstresults ). This morphology is typical of AGN outflows (e.g., MU96.1,SC03.3 ), and we conclude that SDSS J2323+1405 most likely hosts an AGN outflow.

All seven velocity-offset AGNs have central AGNs, yet each galaxy's peak in emission is spatially offset from the stellar centroid. The spatial offsets range from 0.05 to 0.4 kpc, and they are all >3 in significance. The spatially offset emission is produced by shocks, and the velocity offsets of the emission lines observed in the SDSS spectra originate from the spatially offset, shocked emission.

gmw_00002829.name: SDSS J0839+4707
gmw_00002829.overview: SDSS J0839+4707 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that features a stellar bar [arXiv:1709.09177]. The galaxy has a close companion, though there is currently no morphological evidence of an interaction between them [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J0839+4707 has a stellar bar, an emission peak offset along the bar, and a close companion without current interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0839+4707. This galaxy has a stellar bar that is visible in Figure  fig:chandraresults , and the peak of emission is spatially offset along the bar (Figure  fig:hstresults ). SDSS J0839+4707 is also the only galaxy in our sample that has a close companion.

Using the ratio of the stellar bulge luminosities as a proxy for the merger mass ratio, the merger ratio is 3.59:1 (SDSS J0839+4707 is the more massive galaxy). There is no morphological evidence that SDSS J0839+4707 and its companion are interacting, though a future interaction may trigger new accretion onto the central AGN.

gmw_00002830.name: SDSS J083902.50+470813.9
gmw_00002830.overview: SDSS J083902.50+470813.9 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.053454 [arXiv:1709.09177]. It is a close companion to SDSS J0839+4707, situated 18.8 kpc to the northwest of the primary galaxy [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J083902.50+470813.9 as the close companion to SDSS J0839+4707, gives its projected separation and redshift, and classifies it as star-forming.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy, SDSS J083902.50+470813.9, is located 18.8 kpc ( 18 4 ) to the northwest and has a redshift of z=0.053454 0.000045 (Figure  fig:0839companions ). This corresponds to a velocity difference of 311.9 21.4 km s ^ 1 redshifted away from the primary galaxy. Emission line diagnostics of the companion's SDSS spectrum show that it is a star-forming galaxy BR04.1 .

gmw_00002831.name: SDSS J1117+6140
gmw_00002831.overview: SDSS J1117+6140 is a galaxy characterized by a stellar bar and a disturbed rotating disk on large scales [arXiv:1709.09177]. On smaller scales, it hosts a counterrotating nuclear disk, with an emission peak located where this nuclear disk and the bar intersect [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1117+6140 as having disturbed large-scale rotation, a counterrotating nuclear disk, a stellar bar, and an emission peak at their intersection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1117+6140. The OSIRIS observations of this galaxy reveal two kinematic components: a disturbed rotating disk on large scales and a counterrotating nuclear disk on the small scales of the central kpc MU16.1 . The galaxy's stellar bar is apparent in Figure  fig:chandraresults , and the peak of emission is spatially offset along the bar (Figure  fig:hstresults ). Based on the model of the counterrotating disk MU16.1 , the emission peak is located where the nuclear disk and the bar intersect.

gmw_00002832.name: SDSS J1654+1946
gmw_00002832.overview: SDSS J1654+1946 is a highly inclined, almost edge-on galaxy [arXiv:1709.09177]. Hubble Space Telescope observations show no obvious signatures of an outflow, a bar, or a merger, though a knot of emission northwest of the center may be a nuclear star cluster [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper labels SDSS J1654+1946 ambiguous: HST lacks clear outflow, bar, or merger signatures, but an inclined nuclear bar could drive the off-nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1654+1946. The HST observations of this galaxy show no obvious signatures of an outflow, a bar, or a merger. There is a knot of emission northwest of the galaxy center (Figure  fig:hstresults ), which could be a nuclear star cluster (e.g., GE14.1 ). Since SDSS J1654+1946 is highly inclined (almost edge-on), we hypothesize that there could be a small nuclear bar that is too inclined to clearly see in the HST data. Gas inflowing along this bar could be the cause of the off-nuclear peak in emission, though without evidence of this bar we classify this system as ambiguous.

gmw_00002833.name: SDSS J1115+5423
gmw_00002833.overview: SDSS J1115+5423 is an interacting galaxy system [arXiv:2006.01846] containing a confirmed offset active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.09177]. This active nucleus is located 0.8 kiloparsecs from the host galaxy center [arXiv:1709.09177] and exhibits emission lines consistent with pure photoionization [arXiv:1709.09177].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1115+5423 as a comparison offset AGN in a merger, with offset emission lines, an off-nuclear hard X-ray source, and photoionization-dominated ratios.

quotes.quote: Now, for comparison, we consider a velocity-offset AGN that has been confirmed as an offset AGN: SDSS J111519.98+542316.65. SDSS J1115+5423 is z=0.07 galaxy that is in the velocity-offset AGN catalog CO14.1 from which we selected the seven targets in this paper, and it is the only galaxy in that catalog that has been shown to be an offset AGN so far. The emission lines in SDSS J1115+5423 are offset 68.5 11.9 km s ^ 1 from systemic. By analyzing archival Chandra observations of this galaxy, found that it has a hard X-ray source with L_ 2-10 keV =4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 that is located 0.8 0.1 kpc ( 0 64 0 05 ) from the host galaxy center. This offset AGN is located within the 3^ SDSS fiber and presumably is the source of the velocity-offset emission lines in the SDSS spectrum, which could be confirmed with a spatially resolved spectrum of the system.

Interestingly, SDSS J1115+5423's / vs. / line flux ratios, as well as its / vs. / line flux ratios, are consistent with models of pure photoionization, in contrast to the seven velocity-offset AGNs studied here (Figure  fig:shocks ).

sources.summary: SDSS J1115 + 5423 is treated as a dual-AGN candidate with a weak secondary classification, low H equivalent width, and X-ray follow-up that generally supports only one AGN.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert-Seyfert pre-selection from SDSS of the two nuclei is confirmed by the BPT diagram shown in Fig. fig:BPT (upper left panel), although the second nucleus of SDSS J1115 + 5423 and SDSS J1646+2411 is in the weak AGN regime following the WHAN diagram. Hence, all seven systems have been assumed as dual AGN in interacting galaxy systems based on these optical diagnostics. However, the MODS spatially resolved spectroscopy reveals a significantly different picture, as shown in the right panels of Fig. fig:BPT . Except for SDSS J1115+5423, all the fainter secondary nuclei, which were initially classified clearly as Seyfert-like AGN, are changing their apparent emission-line classification.
This may also explain the low equivalent width of H for the secondary nucleus in SDSS J1115 + 5423 despite the clear Seyfert-like emission-line ratios.
X-ray follow-up observations usually identified only one AGN in such SDSS-selected interacting systems, even for the large-separation source SDSS J1115 + 5423.

gmw_00002834.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 28476 is located near two brighter H-band sources [arXiv:1502.02562] and has a photometric redshift of approximately 6.26 [arXiv:1603.08522]. It is identified as a candidate active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1502.02562] with a possible, though less robust, X-ray detection [arXiv:1603.08522].

sources.summary: The paper presents 28476 as a very high-redshift AGN candidate, faint in H band with uncertain morphology/redshift, and uses it to demonstrate H/X-ray positional agreement.

quotes.quote: The second AGN candidate on the right, 28476, is among the highest redshift sources in our sample. It is much fainter in the H band lacking any morphological information.
Thus five sources have been used for the LF estimate in the highest redshift bin, among them 20765, 28476 and 33160 have the most uncertain redshift estimate as shown in Figure A.1.

We have selected the object 28476 already shown in Figure 1 which appears located near two brighter H band sources. In Figure B.1 we also show the position accuracy of a bright source on the left bottom corner which results comparable to that of 28476.

sources.summary: The paper lists object 28476 as a z~6.26 source that falls in the DCBH-color selection region, but treats it as a less secure X-ray detection with a problematic optical counterpart association.

quotes.quote: For 3 of these sources the photometric redshift is z 6 : object 33160 at z 6.06 , object 29323 at z 9.73 and object 28476 at z 6.26 .

Instead, object 28476 is a possible X-ray detection: for this source there are 15 detected counts in the soft-band ( 0.5-2 keV ), compared to 5 background counts within a radius of 3 pixel, corresponding to a probability of spurious detection of 10^ 4 . Anyway, the association between this X-ray source and its optical counterpart is more problematic.
Conservatively, we have not included in Fig. fig:colors_highz the position of the z 6 object 28476 , which also falls in our selection region, but whose X-ray detection is less robust.

gmw_00002835.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 29323 is identified as a candidate active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1502.02562, arXiv:1603.08522] with fitted hydrogen column absorption [arXiv:1502.02562]. It has a highly uncertain photometric redshift estimated at z > 6 [arXiv:1502.02562, arXiv:1603.08522], and it is considered a robust direct collapse black hole candidate [arXiv:1603.08522].

sources.summary: The paper selects object 29323 as a robust high-redshift DCBH candidate, notes its uncertain photometric redshift, and reports that simulations reproduce its H-band magnitude while its inferred X-ray luminosity depends strongly on redshift.

quotes.quote: For 3 of these sources the photometric redshift is z 6 : object 33160 at z 6.06 , object 29323 at z 9.73 and object 28476 at z 6.26 . H band images of the first two sources, with X-ray contours from the Chandra 7 Ms field, are shown in Fig. fig:pdf . As we will show in Section sec:results , these two sources are our DCBH candidates. The photometric redshift of 29323 is more uncertain, but its probability distribution function suggests that z 6 with high confidence (see Fig. fig:pdf ). Objects 33160 and 29323 , both at z 6 , are to be considered robust detections, being also included in the catalog, with identification numbers 85 and 156, respectively.

Source 29323 at z 9.73 has a X-ray luminosity slightly above our predicted range (corresponding to a maximum black hole mass of 5 10^6 ), while we successfully reproduce its H band magnitude (see Fig. fig:seds ). If this object is instead at redshift z 6 , its X-ray luminosity would be downgraded to Log _ 10 (L_x) 43.5 , well inside our predicted range.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 29323 as a high-redshift AGN candidate with fitted absorption, excludes it from the luminosity-function analysis because of its peculiar SED/PDF, and notes uncertain z>6 photometry.

quotes.quote: We derived hydrogen column densities of N_H 23.5 for 273, 4356, 16822, 29323, 33160 and N_H 22.5 for 33073, 20765.
Indeed we have already excluded from the LF analysis the object 29323 because of its too peculiar SED and PDF(z).

The most uncertain redshifts being for the objects 20765, 28476, 29323, 33160 although the larger PDFs distributions appear mostly distributed at z>4 . It is to note that the latter three objects at z>6 are among the most uncertain due to combination of power-law shape of their SEDs and faintness of the sources.

gmw_00002836.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 33160 is located at a photometric redshift of approximately 6 [arXiv:1603.08522, arXiv:1502.02562]. It is identified as an active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1502.02562] and a potential direct collapse black hole host [arXiv:1603.08522].

sources.summary: The paper selects source 33160 as one of two robust z>6 GOODS-S DCBH candidates, with simulations reproducing its H-band magnitude and X-ray luminosity and implying a black hole mass above 10^6.

quotes.quote: For 3 of these sources the photometric redshift is z 6 : object 33160 at z 6.06 , object 29323 at z 9.73 and object 28476 at z 6.26 . H band images of the first two sources, with X-ray contours from the Chandra 7 Ms field, are shown in Fig. fig:pdf . As we will show in Section sec:results , these two sources are our DCBH candidates.

For the couple of z 6 X-ray detected sources in the CANDELS/GOODS-S field we estimate a black hole mass in excess of 10^ 6 . For the source 33160 we are able to reproduce simultaneously with simulations both its H band magnitude, H=25.9 , and its 2-10 keV X-ray luminosity emerging from the host halo, Log _ 10 (L_x) = 43.65 . Since we assume the photometric redshift reported in , also its X-ray flux is automatically reproduced.

sources.summary: The paper treats 33160 as a z about 6 AGN candidate with fitted absorption and uncertain photometric redshift, testing how removing it affects the high-redshift luminosity function.

quotes.quote: We derived hydrogen column densities of N_H 23.5 for 273, 4356, 16822, 29323, 33160 and N_H 22.5 for 33073, 20765.
The second object, 33160 (X85), appears at z 6 although with a larger uncertainty and we have already checked in the previous paragraph the effects of its removal from the sample.

The most uncertain redshifts being for the objects 20765, 28476, 29323, 33160 although the larger PDFs distributions appear mostly distributed at z>4 . It is to note that the latter three objects at z>6 are among the most uncertain due to combination of power-law shape of their SEDs and faintness of the sources.

gmw_00002837.name: S2 0109+22
gmw_00002837.overview: S2 0109+22 is an intermediate synchrotron peak BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1807.02095, arXiv:2202.07353] with a featureless optical spectrum [arXiv:1602.08703]. It exhibits variable very high energy gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1807.02095] and displays intra-night optical variability [arXiv:2202.07353].

sources.summary: The paper presents GTC spectroscopy of S2 0109+22, finding a featureless power-law BL Lac spectrum and setting z>0.35 under the assumption that the nucleus is hosted by a massive elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on the BL Lac S2 0109+22, recently detected at very high energies. We obtained a high signal-to-noise optical spectrum at Gran Telescopio Canarias for the source over the spectral range 4000 - 10000 , to search for spectral features of the host galaxy and/or of the nuclear source and it was compared with previous observations in order to confirm and/or dispute its redshift determination. We find the S2 0109+22 optical spectrum is well represented by a power law continuum without any absorption or emission feature of Equivalent Width > 0.1 . Based on the new optical spectrum we are able to set a redshift lower limit z> 0.35, assuming the source is hosted by a massive elliptical galaxy typical for this class of sources.

The source S2 0109+22 (GC 0109+22, TXS 0109+224, RGB J0112+227) is a compact radio object davis1971, pauliny1972 with a 5GHz flux of 0.78 Jy. The optical identification of the radio source was done with a stellar object of magnitude 15.5 through the optical Palomar Sky Survey Owen1977 . The first spectroscopic observation of S2 0109+22, performed by , did not find absorption or emission lines, suggesting a BL Lac nature for the object. Subsequent higher quality spectroscopic data S2 0109+22 spectra are available in digital form in the ZBLLAC database (http://archive.oapdaf.it/zbllac/) falomo1994, sbarufatti2006, sbarufatti2009, landoni2013 confirmed the featureless spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper reports MAGIC detection of S2 0109+22 during a gamma-ray flare, classifies it as an ISP BL Lac, gives historical radio/optical identification and coordinates, and estimates z = 0.36.

quotes.quote: The MAGIC telescopes observed S2 0109+22 in 2015 July during its flaring activity in high energy gamma rays observed by Fermi LAT. We analyse the MAGIC data to characterise the very high energy (VHE) gamma-ray emission of S2 0109+22, which belongs to the subclass of intermediate synchrotron peak (ISP) BL Lac objects. We study the multi-frequency emission in order to investigate the source classification.

MAGIC observations resulted in the detection of the source at a significance level of 5.3 . The VHE gamma-ray emission of S2 0109+22 is variable on a daily time scale. VHE gamma-ray luminosity of the source is lower than the average of TeV BL Lacs.

S2 0109+22 (also known as GC 0109+224), at coordinates (J2000) RA = 01h12m05.8s and DEC=+22d44m39s , was first detected as a compact radio source in the 5 GHz Survey of the NRAO 43 m dish of Green Bank, West Virginia 1971AJ.....76..980D,1972AJ.....77..265P . In 1976, it was optically identified as a stellar object of magnitude 15.5 on the Palomar Sky Survey plates, 1977AJ.....82..776O also measured a strong millimetre emission (1.53 Jy at 90 GHz) The eleven meter telescope (National Radio Astronomy Observatory) observed the source in 1976. and defined it as a BL Lac object.

sources.summary: The paper describes this ISP BL Lac as newly studied for intra-night optical variability, with one detected INOV episode of about two percent amplitude.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0112.1+2245: The source is an ISP BL Lac. It was observed on three epochs over a period of about an year. Of the three epochs, the source was found to show INOV on 20 December 2017, with an amplitude of variability of about 2 . This source has not been studied for INOV before.

gmw_00002838.name: NGC 1232
gmw_00002838.overview: NGC 1232 is a face-on spiral galaxy of Hubble type SABc [arXiv:2102.08961] with a morphology similar to the Milky Way [arXiv:1204.5599]. It features a short central bar, a tight two-armed spiral pattern that splits, and a more open outer structure [arXiv:1204.5599].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Chandra observations of NGC 1232, adopts a 14.5 Mpc distance, and rejects a claimed diffuse hot-gas cloud or recent dwarf-galaxy collision.

quotes.quote: We studied the apparent galaxy pair NGC 1232/NGC 1232A with Chandra , looking for evidence of interactions and collisions. We report that there is no cloud of diffuse emission in NGC 1232, contrary to previous claims in the literature.

The larger galaxy in the pair is the face-on spiral NGC 1232, of Hubble type SABc, with a Hubble distance = (21.3 1.5) Mpc (based on its recession speed of 1450 km s ^ 1 with respect to the cosmic background), and a redshift-independent distance 14.5 Mpc.

The original purpose of our work was to investigate the claimed cloud of hot gas in the disk of NGC 1232. We found that such cloud does not exist. Thus, there is no X-ray evidence of a recent dwarf galaxy interaction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1232 as Milky-Way-like in morphology, with a short bar, a tight symmetric two-armed spiral, arm splitting, and a more open outer pattern.

quotes.quote: NGC 1232: It has been argued that this galaxy has a morphology similar to that of our Milky Way becker70 . It has a bar out to a radius of 16 \ with a_2 = 20 . A tight, symmetric, two-armed spiral with ( a_2 , i ) = (0.2, 14.7 ) emerges from the end of the bar. Its symmetric part terminates at =36 , after which the northern arm splits as the southern arm continues. A more open pattern can be traced to 120 .

gmw_00002839.name: NGC 1232A
gmw_00002839.overview: NGC 1232A is a late-type barred spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 93 Mpc [arXiv:2102.08961]. Although it appears to be a companion to NGC 1232, it is actually an isolated field galaxy that hosts three luminous ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:2102.08961].

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 1232A is a distant barred spiral with discrepant redshift, not a nearby dwarf satellite, and highlights its unusual three luminous ULX population.

quotes.quote: Instead, we find that the small ``companion'' galaxy NGC 1232A contains three ultraluminous X-ray sources with peak 0.3--10 keV luminosities above 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 (assuming a cosmological distance of 93 Mpc for this galaxy). For its mass, morphology, metal abundance and bright ULX population, NGC 1232A is analogous to the more nearby late-type spiral NGC 1313.

Our second objective was to study the apparent companion, NGC 1232A.
