quotes.quote: The location of M59cO is highlighted; the Mieske et al. (2013) values suggest it has a large dynamical mass.

This includes M59cO with its dynamical mass based on our velocity dispersion of 25.7 2.2 km s ^ 1 . The dynamical mass of M59cO calculated by Mieske et al. (2013) is much higher as they used the literature value of 48 5 km s ^ 1 which N14 note was probably incorrect as it is close to the spectral resolution of the instrument used to obtain it (Chilingarian Mamon 2008). This object illustrates how the quoted uncertainties in the literature are usually measurement uncertainties only and do not include systematic errors.

gmw_00002873.name: UCD 320
gmw_00002873.overview: UCD 320 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2302.08082]. Spatially resolved observations of the galaxy indicate that its dynamical mass-to-light ratio was overestimated in previous studies, revealing that it does not possess an elevated mass [arXiv:2302.08082].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UCD 320 as an observed UCD whose previous dynamical M/L ratios were overestimated, with Voggel et al. finding no elevated mass from spatially resolved data.

quotes.quote: Voggel_2018 studied the black hole content of UCD 320 and UCD 330 and found that their dynamical M/L ratios had been overestimated in previous studies. For UCD 320, Taylor2010 measured = 2.5 , while Mieske2013 's analysis found = 1.6 . In contrast, Voggel_2018 found = 0.9 , a mass measurement that finds UCD 320 does not have an elevated mass. Their lower is derived from VLT/SINFONI IFU observations that spatially resolve the UCD, giving a smaller derived effective radius for the UCD than found by Taylor2010 and higher stellar population M/L estimates.

gmw_00002874.name: UCD 330
gmw_00002874.overview: UCD 330 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2302.08082]. Its dynamical mass-to-light ratio was overestimated in early studies, but more recent velocity dispersion measurements have yielded a lower ratio [arXiv:2302.08082].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UCD 330 as an observed UCD whose prior dynamical M/L estimates were high; Voggel et al. find a lower ratio from better velocity-dispersion data.

quotes.quote: Voggel_2018 studied the black hole content of UCD 320 and UCD 330 and found that their dynamical M/L ratios had been overestimated in previous studies.

Similarly, for UCD 330, Taylor2010 measured = 2.3 , while Mieske2013 's analysis found = 1.7 . In contrast Voggel_2018 found = 0.9 . This lower result stems primarily from better data giving lower velocity dispersion measurements and may also be due to the use of a two-component mass model.

gmw_00002875.name: ESO185--13
gmw_00002875.overview: ESO185--13 is a blue compact dwarf [arXiv:2304.02450] and starburst galaxy [arXiv:1208.5461] that displays morphological perturbations, including a tidal tail and two counter-rotating dynamical components, resulting from a recent merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179, arXiv:2304.02450]. The galaxy also features a central bar-like structure [arXiv:2304.02450] and a low-surface-brightness component [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: ESO 185-IG13 is described as a merger-perturbed blue compact galaxy with a tidal tail, compact star clusters, two dynamical components, and merger-like morphology.

quotes.quote: ESO 185-IG13 ESO 185-IG13 presents a clear example of classical morphological perturbations produced in galaxy mergers and/or collisions (Barnes and Hernquist ; Schweizer ). The broad band images of this galaxy (see fig. 10) clearly reveal the presence of a tidal tail 30 long in the north- east direction (about 10 kpc at the distance of the galaxy main body). On CCD images, there are two small faint galaxies with fairly low surface brightness approximately 1.5 arcminutes south-west of ESO 185-IG13. None of these were detected in the Fabry-Perot data, hence their distances are unknown. An HST image of ESO 185-IG13 (obtained from the archive) reveals the presence of numerous compact star clusters with luminosities up to M_V 15 ( Ostlin ), a typical signature of colliding system (see Whitmore et al. 1993, 1995).

The analysis of the velocity field of ESO 185-IG13 revealed the presence of two dynamically distinct components (Paper I). The secondary component is counter-rotating with respect to the main component (which spins faster and dominates the H emission). The two components have marginally different position angles.

sources.summary: The paper revisits ESO185-IG13 as a blue compact dwarf with high star formation intensity, bar-like structure, tidal merger features, and a revised lower young-cluster formation fraction.

quotes.quote: The three blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxies ESO185-IG13, ESO338-IG04, and Haro11 have played a central role on the observational side because they have some of the highest known _ SFR and published values of . We present new estimates of for these BCDs in three age intervals (1-10 Myr, 10-100 Myr, 100-400 Myr), based on age-dating which includes H photometry to better discriminate between clusters younger and older than 10 Myr.

Galaxy Properties Color images of ESO185, ESO338, and Haro11 taken in the BVI filters with the HST are shown in Figure fig:sample . ESO185 has a bright, bar-like structure crossing the center, and tidal features indicating the system experienced a recent merging event.

The fraction of stars formed in 1-10 Myr clusters, or _ 1-10 Myr , was previously estimated to be 50 for the BCD galaxies ESO338 and Haro11, and 26 for ESO185 Adamo10, Adamo11a . In this work, we found significantly lower values of 15 4 (ESO185), 39 10 (ESO338), and 18 5 (Haro11), based on deeper HST observations which include narrow-band H imaging taken as part of the Clusters, Clumps, Dust and Gas (CCDG) survey.

sources.summary: ESO 185--IG13 is included as a blue compact merging galaxy whose clumps have younger ages and whose tail properties resemble morphologically similar Haro 11.

quotes.quote: For this paper, we used multi-wavelength observations of 7 merging systems in the survey: Arp 220, ESO 185--IG13, Haro 11 (ESO 350--IG038), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690. Arp 220 is an Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690 (Arp 299) are LIRGS, and ESO 185--IG13 and Haro 11 are blue compact galaxies. Their distances range from 38 to 90 Mpc.
The other galaxies, ESO 185-IG13, Haro 11, NGC 1614, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690 have clumps spanning a similar range of younger ages so they are combined.
Both of these quantities are similar in ESO 185 and Haro 11, which are morphologically similar also.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO185--13 as a strongly starbursting collision product with compact clusters, disturbed morphology, and a newly detected low-surface-brightness component.

quotes.quote: ESO185--13 This strongly starbursting galaxy SFR=5.4 M_ / yr 2001A&A...374..800O is likely the product of collision as evidenced by the detection of numerous bright compact star clusters 2000ASPC..211...63O and by the galaxy morphology. Beyond _B 26.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 the elliptical surface brightness profile reveals a prominent low surface brightness (LSB) component, which was previously undetected. In Appendix eso185plume we show how much detailed structure of this component we actually detect. Clearly visible in all optical filters is the flux overdensity to the North--East of the prominent tail.

sources.summary: ESO 185-IG13 is treated as a luminous blue compact host whose starburst began within 30-40 Myr; its optically bright young clusters include partially embedded systems with near-infrared excess.

quotes.quote: The age distributions of the YSCs suggest that the starburst episode in Haro 11, ESO 185-IG13, and Mrk 930 started not more than 30-40 Myr ago.

In all the three host galaxies, the analysis is limited to the optically brightest objects, i.e., systems that are only partially embedded by their natal cocoons (since deeply embedded clusters are probably too faint to be detected).

A significant fraction of young star clusters in Haro 11 2010MNRAS.407..870A , ESO 185-IG13 2011MNRAS.414.1793A , and Mrk 930 2011MNRAS.tmp..739A shows a clear signature of a flux excess at near-IR wavebands.

gmw_00002876.name: ESO249--31
gmw_00002876.overview: ESO249--31 is an ongoing merger of dwarf galaxies [arXiv:2102.05244, arXiv:2309.07919] with a strongly distorted morphology [arXiv:2210.01556] featuring two nuclei and two tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0006084]. The system exhibits widespread star-forming bursts [arXiv:2102.05244], scattered star-forming regions [arXiv:1208.5461], and a young star-cluster complex [arXiv:2210.01556].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1487 with MUSE, finding merger-driven abundance mixing, star-forming bursts, gas kinematic disturbance, and evidence for dwarf-galaxy formation from smaller clumps.

quotes.quote: We present optical VLT/MUSE integral field spectroscopy data of the merging galaxy NGC 1487. We use fitting techniques to study the ionized gas emission of this merger and its main morphological and kinematical properties. We measured flat and sometimes inverted oxygen abundance gradients in the subsystems composing NGC 1487, explained by metal mixing processes common in merging galaxies. We also measured widespread star-forming bursts, indicating that photoionisation by stars is the primary ionization source of the galaxy.

The kinematic map revealed a rotating pattern in the gas in the northern tail of the system, suggesting that the galaxy may be in the process of rebuilding a disc. The gas located in the central region has larger velocity dispersion ( 50 km s ^ 1 ) than the remaining regions, indicating kinematic heating, possibly owing to the ongoing interaction. Based on all our measurements and findings, and specially on the mass estimates, metallicity gradients and velocity fields of the system, we propose that NGC 1487 is the result of an ongoing merger event involving smallish dwarf galaxies within a group, in a pre-merger phase, resulting in a relic with mass and physical parameters similar to a dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1487 as an ongoing merger with a three-pointing MUSE mosaic, complex anti-correlated regions, and the largest scatter in the resolved metallicity relations.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains a total of nine galaxies: ESO 59-01, IC 1959, NGC 1487, NGC 1796, NGC 853, UGC 3755, UGC 5889, NGC 4592 and UGC 8041. The first seven were observed in 2020 and 2021 as part of the MUSE programme 0108.B-0904 (PI: Fahrion) making use of adaptive optics-supported wide-field mode. The last two were retrieved from the ESO Science Archive. The nine galaxies display different morphologies and NGC 1487 deserves a special note given that it is an ongoing merger (see e.g. buzzo_et_al_2021 for a more detailed description of this galaxy).

For the lowest mass galaxies (NGC 853 and UGC 3755), the points present significant scatter, but also a different trend with regions that show a Z_ g _ * anti-correlation, which leads to overall negative correlation coefficients. NGC 1487 introduces the largest scatter of all the galaxies in the sample. Given that this galaxy is undergoing a merger, we propose that the characteristics of this galaxy might complicate the analysis in this case.

sources.summary: ESO249--31 is classified as an iI,M BCG with merger signs, extinction, scattered star-forming regions, a negative NIR metallicity gradient, and both old and younger populations.

quotes.quote: ESO249--31 This peculiar spiral with extended arms shows clear signs of an ongoing merger event 2003A&A...405...31B . We thus classify it as iI,M BCG. The profiles should be affected by a significant amount of extinction H_ /H_ 4.1 2003A&A...405...31B .

The star--forming regions are spread out at various radii resulting in many small scale fluctuations in the optical color profiles but no clear large--scale gradients. In hybrid and purely NIR colors, both the radial color profiles and the integrated colors in color--color diagrams show a negative radial metallicity gradient. The presence of an old stellar population >2 Gyr with low or intermediate metallicity ( Z<0.008 ) is indicated, as well as a younger population with metallicity closer to solar ( 0.008 Z 0.02 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1487 as an ongoing merger with distorted morphology; its putative NSC is a young, low-metallicity star-cluster complex likely formed in the merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 1487 is currently undergoing a merger, leading to high levels of star formation and a strongly distorted morphology (e.g. Buzzo2021 ). The star cluster classified as its NSC is visually removed from the centre of the old stellar populations in either of the merger remnants. This star cluster is located in one of the regions that show the highest levels of star formation, but there is no clear peak in the SFR density map at its location. Additionally, there seems to be a significant velocity offset between the gaseous and stellar component in the NSC spectra of v = 13.6 3.3 km s ^ 1 .

An analysis of the other star clusters next to the central one reveals similarly young ages of a few hundred Myr and low metallicities, possibly indicating that the complex of star clusters formed during the merger. Instead of being formed through one of the discussed NSC formation channels, we propose that this star cluster complex in NGC 1487 has formed recently, likely in the ongoing merger.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 1487 as an early-stage merger with two tails and two nuclei, placed morphologically between the Mice and Antennae systems and shown in new radio contours.

quotes.quote: NGC 1487 This system is in an early stage merger revealing two clear tails and two nuclei but sufficiently advanced to be one galactic body (e.g. Bergvall Johansson 1995). It is slightly more evolved than NGC 4676 (``The Mice'') but less so than NGC 4038/9 (``The Antennae''). The radio contours at 4.79 GHz overlayed on the optical image of NGC 1487 are shown in Figure fig_n1487 .

gmw_00002877.name: ESO400--G043
gmw_00002877.overview: ESO400--G043 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy with regular elliptical outer isophotes and two off-center star-forming regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179, arXiv:1208.5461]. It is dynamically perturbed, likely due to a merger or an interaction with a companion galaxy located 70 kpc away [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179, arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 400-G43 as dynamically perturbed, with an extended H I cloud, shell-like structure, rapid central rotation decline, and likely interaction or minor merger.

quotes.quote: ESO 400-G43 This galaxy has a regular outer morphology, embedded in a large H i cloud that is extended towards a companion galaxy with a projected distance of 70 kpc (Bergvall and J ors ater 1988, see next subsection). At fainter isophotal levels peculiar morphological features start to appear. Approximately 15 north of the centre there is a bright H emitting blob, apparently not rotating with the galaxy at the pattern speed expected at its location. The rotation curve shows a rapid increase followed by a dramatic decline, that drops faster than the keplerian prediction (see Fig. massmodlast4 and Paper I).

The observed H line widths (see Table 4) could be sufficient for gravitational support if the linewidth traces virial motions. However, this can not explain the observed shape of the rotation curve. Clearly, this galaxy is badly perturbed dynamically. The only likely explanation is interaction with the companion ESO 400-G43B or that a merger with a smaller galaxy has occurred.

sources.summary: ESO400--G043 has two off-center star-forming regions, regular outer isophotes, a comparable-mass dI companion, evidence for older stars, and radial color gradients.

quotes.quote: ESO400--G043 There are two prominent star--forming regions in this galaxy, both of which are off-center. We classify it as iE BCG due to its regular elliptical outer isophotes. It has a dI companion at a projected distance of 70 kpc 1988Natur.331..589B with a comparable mass.

1984Msngr..38....2B,1986A&AS...64..469B and 2002A&A...390..891B demonstrate the presence of an old stellar population. We cannot completely isolate this population in any of the investigated regions, though the measured V-I 0.5 at large radii would require stars 1 Gyr of age. Additionally, the strong negative H-K gradient we observe indicates a radial decrease in metallicity and/or an increase in age.

gmw_00002878.name: ESO421--02
gmw_00002878.overview: ESO421--02 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy classified as an iE type, featuring an extended multinuclear starburst embedded within an elliptical host [arXiv:1208.5461]. The system is undergoing a merger between two similar-looking dwarf galaxies, with the starburst region located along their contact zone [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: ESO421--02 is described as an iE BCG with an extended multinuclear starburst in an elliptical host, flat color profiles, and evidence for an ongoing merger between two dwarfs.

quotes.quote: ESO421--02 Together with ESO462--IG 020, this galaxy was first studied by 1986A&AS...64..469B . It shows a spatially extended starburst with multiple nuclei, embedded in a common elliptical host. We therefore classify it as iE BCG. Neither of our population synthesis models fits the observed colors for the burst or the two outer regions. This is probably due to contribution from nebular emission which is not in--situ, which the models do not account for. The color profiles are predominantly flat.

However, the RGB image suggests that this is a merger since two very similar--looking dwarf galaxies are seen in physical contact with each other, with the starburst located between them along the region of contact. This is further supported by unpublished kinematic data, which give a clear indication that this is indeed an on--going merger between two dwarfs, one to the East, the other to the West.

gmw_00002879.name: ESO462--IG 020
gmw_00002879.overview: ESO462--IG 020 is classified as a distorted contact-pair galaxy merger [arXiv:1208.5461]. Its optical and near-infrared color profiles show sharp discontinuity jumps that indicate the presence of multiple spatially mixed stellar populations with different star formation histories [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: ESO462--IG 020 is treated as a distorted contact-pair merger whose color-profile jumps imply several spatially mixed stellar populations and poorly modeled current star formation.

quotes.quote: ESO462--IG 020 This galaxy has been classified as a distorted contact pair in the surface photometry catalog of ESO--Uppsala galaxies. We see indications of the ongoing merger in the optical and optical--NIR isophotal color profiles, which show sharp color changes visible as discontinuity jumps at 1, 2, and 4 kpc. These jumps in color imply the presence of more than two distinct stellar populations with different SFH which have mixed spatially on large but not on small scales. We therefore classify this galaxy as iI,M BCG.

The color--color diagrams indicate that perhaps more than two populations are present and influencing the integrated colors. Nebular emission is implied, but the current SF is poorly modeled with an instantaneous burst.

gmw_00002880.name: IIZw40
gmw_00002880.overview: II Zw 40 is a low-metallicity blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1107.5902, arXiv:1710.03149] undergoing an intense, young starburst [arXiv:2012.10054, arXiv:2111.14875]. This irregular system is the result of an ongoing merger between two smaller dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1208.5461, arXiv:1302.3464], which has produced extended tidal tails [arXiv:0803.2404, arXiv:2111.14875].

sources.summary: The paper studies II Zw 40 as a nearby dwarf starburst dominated by a giant HII region, with intense star formation and irregular HI tidal-tail morphology suggestive of a merger.

quotes.quote: We present integral field spectroscopy in the near-infrared of the nearby starburst galaxy II Zw 40. Our new observations provide an unprecedented detailed view of the interstellar medium and star formation of this galaxy. The radiation emitted by the galaxy is dominated by a giant HII region, which extends over an area of more than 400 pc in size.

The morphology of II Zw 40 is highly irregular with two tails extending over a few tens of arcsecs which could be interpreted as parts of two different galaxies in the process of merging, and is often considered responsible for triggering the intense central starburst (Baldwin et al. 1982). HI imaging shows the presence of a flat extended HI structure that could be a highly-inclined disk, or a pair of tidal-tails of neutral hydrogen emerging from the galaxy and extending over more than 6 arcmin. Van Zee et al. (1998) find a strong radial velocity gradient along these tails with a clear velocity reversal in the SE tip reminiscent of the tidal tails observed in the prototypical merger NGC 7252 where the velocity reversal is caused by material falling back toward the dynamical center (Hibbard Mihos, 1995). Notice, however, that as stressed by van Zee et al. (1998), the colliding galaxies in NGC 7252 are much more massive than II Zw 40, for which no merger models have yet been calculated. Therefore, while the HI observations are highly suggestive of a merger of two roughly similar disk galaxies, the evidence is not yet conclusive, and we must be very cautious when interpreting kinematical data of the central regions of II Zw 40.

sources.summary: The paper observes II Zw 40's central star-forming complex with the SMA, finding extended 880 micron emission dominated by free-free radiation, with compact optically thick centimeter emission in a low-metallicity blue compact dwarf.

quotes.quote: The central star-forming region in a blue compact dwarf galaxy, II Zw 40, was observed in the 340 GHz ( 880 ) band at 5 arcsec (250 pc) resolution with the Submillimetre Array (SMA). A source associated with the central star-forming complex was detected with a flux of 13.6 2.0 mJy. The structure is more extended than the beam in the east--west direction. The SMA 880 flux is analyzed by using theoretical models of radio spectral energy distribution along with centimetre interferometric measurements in the literature. We find (i) that the SMA 880 flux is dominated ( 75 per cent) by free--free emission from the central compact star-forming region, and (ii) that the contribution from dust emission to the SMA 880 flux is at most 4 2.5 mJy.

II Zw 40 is a well studied BCD with a low oxygen abundance, 12+ ( O/H )=8.13 thuan05 . This galaxy hosts a high star formation activity associated with the SSCs in the centre. At centimetre wavelengths, the central star-forming region in II Zw 40 is compact and optically thick for free--free absorption, and is categorized as a `supernebula' or `ultradense \ region' turner98,kobulnicky99 .

sources.summary: II Zw 40 is described as a blue compact dwarf with a young starburst and DGS membership; its hard ionization conditions make density-corrected N/O diagnostics preferable.

quotes.quote: II Zw 40 is also a Blue Compact Dwarf galaxy with a young starburst. The stellar mass is estimated to be 9 10^ 5 \ M_ , and SFR 0.8 \ M_ /yr 2014A&A...568A..62D . The age of the starburst is estimated to be 3 \ Myr 2018ApJ...865...55L . II Zw 40 is also among the DGS sample.
The [ N iii ] line from II Zw 40 is also very close to a deep absorption feature.

Comparing the N3O3 in Column (2), the most simplistic estimation of N/O using only two spectral lines, to the value in Column (5) which is the most precise and reliable diagnostic utilizing 5 lines, the value changes by < 0.15 dex for all targets except Haro 3 and II Zw 40. This small change is because the effect of density correction which would lift N/O ratio result, is offset by the model calibration for the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] in the -0.5 to 1.5 range which would decrease the N/O estimate. For the exceptions Haro 3 and II Zw 40, they have moderately high electron densities as well as hard ionization conditions, resulting in noticeable differences between N3O3 and the model calibration, but N3O3_ n_e parameter instead shows great agreement with the model calibrated results.

sources.summary: The paper describes II Zw 40 as a compact dwarf galaxy at 9.2 Mpc with a bright core, diffuse tails, and a starburst possibly caused by collision between small systems.

quotes.quote: II Zw 40 General description \ is at a distance of 9.2 Mpc vacca92 . This distance accounts for Galactic rotation but no Virgocentric flow, and assumes H_0=75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . At this distance, 1 \ corresponds to 44.6 pc. This compact galaxy consists of a bright core only 3 5 \ in size with two faint and diffuse tails extending about 30 \ to the south-east sargent70 .

carried out an emission-line optical study of the morphology and kinematics of . They considered that the two tails are reminiscent of interacting galaxies and suggested a possible collision between two small systems as the source of the present starburst.

\ is one of the prototype of the class of dwarf galaxies known as ``detached extra-galactic \ regions'' sargent70 . These systems are characterised by emission-line spectra e.g. walsh93 similar to those of individual giant \ regions superimposed on a continuum which appears to be mainly due to O stars. The presence of about 200 WR stars is inferred from the flux of the \ 4684 \ line cf. vacca92 .

sources.summary: The paper studies II Zw 40 as a prototypical blue compact dwarf starburst, finding clumpy molecular gas, high CO-to-H2 conversion factors, and merger-driven shocks shaping its clouds.

quotes.quote: Using the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array, we have made the first high spatial and spectral resolution observations of the molecular gas and dust in the prototypical blue compact dwarf galaxy . The \ and \ emission is clumpy and distributed throughout the central star-forming region. Only one of eight molecular clouds has associated star formation. The continuum spectral energy distribution is dominated by free-free and synchrotron; at 870 , only 50 of the emission is from dust.

The molecular clouds within \ resemble those in other strongly interacting systems like the Antennae: overall they have high size-linewidth coefficients and molecular gas surface densities. These properties appear to be due to the high molecular gas surface densities produced in this merging system rather than to increased external pressure. Overall, these results paint a picture of \ as a complex, rapidly evolving system whose molecular gas properties are dominated by the large-scale gas shocks from its on-going merger.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on II Zw 40 as a nearby, low-metallicity blue compact dwarf where ALMA maps reveal compact star formation, weak CO, and localized dust enrichment near massive clusters.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: A single field with an 18 \ field-of-view centered on 05:55:42.620, +03.23.32.0 was observed

quotes.quote: The dominance of the starburst makes II Zw 40 an excellent target for studying the effects of massive clusters on their environments. We present here some of the first ALMA submillimeter and 870 m continuum and CO observations of an extragalactic star-forming region with massive young star clusters. We have mapped CO(3-2), CO(1-0), and continuum in . From these images we can study how well correlated CO and submillimeter dust continuum emission are on the scales of star-forming regions.

We present ALMA continuum maps at and 870 m and integrated intensity images of the CO(3--2) and CO(1--0) lines in the blue compact dwarf galaxy , at a resolution of 0.4 \ ( 20 pc). Based on a continuum flux of 7.6 0.2 mJy, we obtain a Lyman continuum rate of N_ Lyc ^ corr =1.2 0.2 10^ 53 s^ 1 with 72 of the uv photons ionizing the gas. From and 870 m continuum maps, we construct a dust-only continuum map.

sources.summary: The paper compares II Zw 40 to the simulated dwarf starburst, describing its merger-driven burst, metallicity, gas and dust ratios, HI content, and stronger long-infrared emission.

quotes.quote: Comparison to observed dwarf starbursts For the observational perspective, we compare in Fig. fig:SED_dwarfs our starburst SED to archival photometry of two dwarf galaxies, NGC 1569 and II Zw 40, obtained from the The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . These dwarf galaxies have very similar gas and dust masses to our system and, most importantly, are actively forming stars. The dust properties of these galaxies have been discussed in 2003A&A...407..159G, 2005A&A...434..867G and the star formation history of NGC 1569 in 2005AJ....129.2203A and of II Zw 40 in 1996ApJ...466..150V . II Zw 40 has been undergoing a starburst for the past few Myr with a SFR of 1.2 - 1.4 M _ yr ^ 1 caused by a merger Kepley_2014, 2018ApJ...865...55L , indicated by tidal tails seen in optical and IR.

II Zw 40 has a metallicity of 1/6 - 1/5 Z _ , best-fit dust-to-metals and gas-to-dust ratios of 530 - 1460 and 1/5 - 1/2 , approximately 5 to 10 times more HI than our system and very little molecular hydrogen 2001AJ....121..740M, 2016ApJ...828...50K .

The shape of the dust emission in both galaxies coincides with our resulting SED. II Zw 40, which has a higher gas mass and star formation rate, shows higher rates of IR emission at long IR-wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper treats II Zw 40 as a BCD whose submillimetre excess requires shielded cold dust, with models explaining its data point using significant extinction and a high UV radiation field.

quotes.quote: In particular, II Zw 40, which is included also in the current sample, shows a clear excess of the submm flux. In Fig. \ fig:clr_Av0_submm , we also plot the data point taken from galliano05 for II Zw 40 (see also hunt05 ). Indeed, we observe a clear deviation from the model prediction, which can be interpreted as a significant contribution from a very cold component.

Fig. \ fig:clr_varAv_submm also demonstrates that the data point of II Zw 40 is explained with A_V 5 with 300 . Thus we conclude the existence of shielded cold dust in II Zw 40 (and in other galaxies whose data points deviate rightwards in Fig. \ fig:clr_Av0_submm ). galliano05 also show that the UV ISRF of II Zw 40 is higher than that of the Milky Way by two orders of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper classifies II Zw 40 as a blue compact dwarf galaxy, noting its metal-poor, gas-rich starburst character, previous radio-continuum measurements, and purely thermal high-frequency emission.

quotes.quote: II Zw 40 and II Zw 70 are classical blue compact dwarf galaxies (BCGDs). BCDGs are also called H ii galaxies because they are dominated by giant H ii regions occupying much of their total volumes. Their emission-line spectra indicate that they are metal-poor, while they are gas-rich and are experiencing intense bursts of star formation.

II Zw 40 was observed in the radio continuum by klein84a , klein91 , and by deeg93 . II Zw 70 is a small and distant BCDG so that existing radio continuum measurements by klein84a, skillman88, deeg93 only provided integrated flux densities. The high-frequency radiation of these two galaxies is purely thermal.

sources.summary: The paper describes IIZw40 as a low-metallicity dwarf with strong mid-infrared lines, weak UIB/PAH emission, young massive stars, and dust dominated by very small grains.

quotes.quote: In Figure cvfs we show ISOCAM spectra covering 5 to 17 m for 3 galaxies from our [ CII ] survey, IIZw40, NGC 1140 and NGC 1569 along with that of the notoriously metal-poor SBS0335-052 . While the UIBs are not obvious in the spectra of IIZw40 and SBS0335-052, and are only very weakly present NGC 1569, they can be distinguished in the spectrum of NGC 1140. When compared to spectra characteristic of PDRs and HII regions, ie, M17 , IIZw40 is remarkably similar to that of an HII region.

We can fit the MIR region of the IIZw40 spectrum with a blackbody of 193 K and and an absorption equivalent to A _ v 4. For NGC 1569, NGC 1140 and IIZw40, we construct composite stellar SEDs that require a 75 to 95 mass fraction of an 'older' population ranging in age from about 10 Myr to 30 Myr along with 5 to 30 of a very young population, < 5 Myr. This model gives a PAH/VSG mass ratio for NGC 1569 and IIZw 40 of 2 to 3x10 ^ 4 and 10 times this for NGC 1140.

sources.summary: The paper discusses II Zw 40 as a star-forming galaxy with controversial sub-mm/mm observations, high foreground extinction, and a characteristic free-free equality wavelength near 960 microns.

quotes.quote: We note that sub-mm and mm observations for II Zw 40 are controversial (Fig. fig6 b1). Submillimetre array (SMA) observations at 880 m H11 and MRT observations at 1.25 mm A04 are in very nice agreement with our fit, while 850 m SCUBA and MAMBO observations by G05 and H05 are not. It is possible that differences in observed fluxes are due to the fact that II Zw 40 is located at low Galactic latitude with a high Milky Way extinction.

Regarding Haro 11 and II Zw 40, _ ff are 700 m and 960 m, respectively (Figs. fig6 a1 and fig6 b1). Therefore, a sub-mm excess in Haro 11 due to the free-free emission is expected at shorter wavelengths, and it is seen by Herschel at 500 m, while it is not in II Zw 40.

sources.summary: The paper treats II Zw 40 as an HII galaxy whose infrared-line data fall in the low-velocity, low-density regime where photoionization dominates over shocks.

quotes.quote: The points corresponding to the two galaxies NGC 5253 and II Zw 40, studied by Schaerer Stasinska (1999), are located at the top/right zone. Assuming that all the objects are photoionized by a similar radiation spectrum, our single-cloud results show that the distribution on the diagram can be reproduced if the shock velocity and the pre-shock density increase from the top to the bottom part of the diagonal. When the intensity of ionizing radiation is kept constant, the higher the shock velocity the stronger is the shock effect on the physical conditions in the cloud, relative to photoionization. Since NGC 5253 and II Zw 40 data points are located in the low velocity --- low density zone, photoionization is expected to be the dominant mechanism for these two objects, while shocks are required for explaining the data for the other objects, in agreement with Lutz et al. (1998).
In HII galaxies, like NGC 5253 and II Zw 40, the star forming region is young enough to contain a high fraction of massive O and WR stars which produce a large amount of ionizing photons.

sources.summary: The paper models II Zw 40 as a young starburst/H II system where high shock velocities may trace massive-star winds and line ratios require multiple H II, starburst, and AGN-like cloud components.

quotes.quote: Higher \ in young starbursts, e.g. for II Zw 40, can be related to strong stellar winds produced by young massive stars (e.g. Wolf-Rayet stars).

In a previous analysis by Viegas, Contini, Contini (1999), the modelling of II Zw 40 and NGC 5253 led to gas with low \ and 0, in agreement with their young age, on the basis of high [ OIV ] /( [ NeII ] +0.44 [ NeIII ] ) versus [ NeIII ] / [ NeII ] line ratios. However, in the analysis of [ SIV ] / [ SIII ] , we find that the same two galaxies are explained by HII region clouds with relatively high \ (500 ), by clouds in starbursts with an age of 2.5 Myr and velocities of 200-300 , and by clouds in AGNs with velocities of 300 .

More particularly, the galaxies of the Verma et al. (2003) sample show that II Zw 40, NGC 5253, NGC 4038/4039, NGC 3690A and NGC 3690 B/C present a multiple nature of HII regions, AGN, and starburst galaxies.

sources.summary: IIZw40 is described as a very blue, strongly starbursting iI,M BCG formed by a dwarf-galaxy merger, with a tidal tail, young central colors, and nebular contamination.

quotes.quote: IIZw40 This iI,M BCG galaxy is very blue and strongly starbursting 1996ApJ...466..150V . Its optical and NIR spectra have been extensively studied e.g. 1982MNRAS.198..535B,1983ApJ...268..667T , as well as its morphology and companions e.g. 1991A&A...241..358C , gas content e.g. 1992A&A...265...19S and metallicity e.g. 1983ApJ...273...81K,1991A&AS...91..285T,1994MNRAS.270...35M . IIZw40 is the result of a merger of two dwarf galaxies, judging by its optical morphology e.g. 1970ApJ...162L.155S,1997MNRAS.286..183T and its extreme tidal tail extending South--East approximately 8.5' in diameter 1998AJ....116.1186V which does not seem to have an optical counterpart. The central colors are consistent with a very young stellar population of a few Myr, while at large radii all colors are strongly contaminated by nebular emission.

sources.summary: The review describes II Zw 40 IFU observations showing that its brightest star-forming knot dominates the integrated line width and traces underlying turbulent motions.

quotes.quote: We chose to target the well known HII galaxy: II Zw 40 with Gemini GMOS-IFU and SINFONI . The analysis of a set of kinematics diagnostic diagrams, such as the intensity versus velocity dispersion (I- ), intensity versus radial velocity (I-V) and V- , for global and individual analysis in sub-regions of the nebula separates the main line broadening mechanisms responsible for producing a smooth supersonic integrated line profile (fig fig:zw40 ). As can be seen, the brightest knot dominates the light weighted integrated line width, deconvolved from the local effects of stellar evolution represented by regions of low intensity and higher . This means that when we measure the line widths of any aperture including the kinematic core, we measure the velocity dispersion of the underlying turbulent motions that we interpret as being due to motions in the gravitational potential.

sources.summary: The paper treats II Zw 40 as a high-excitation galaxy with no clear large-scale outflow signature, higher ionization parameter, radiation-dominated gas, and a young star-forming event.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, the high-excitation \ galaxies (II Zw 40 and NGC 5253) do not show any clear signature of large scale outflows of gas.
An ionization parameter of 0.01 was adopted for nearly all the models except for II Zw 40 for which U = 0.02 . For this galaxy the effect of radiation dominates the shock effect (see Sect. obs ).
In the former galaxies (II Zw 40 and NGC 5253), the star-forming event is still young enough to contain a high proportion of massive O and Wolf-Rayet stars.

sources.summary: The paper studies II Zw 40 as a nearby dwarf starburst formed by a merger, finding a compact infrared/radio core with kinematically distinct embedded clusters.

quotes.quote: 3 Department of Physics and Astronomy, UCLA, Los Angeles, CA 90095-1547 4 Department of Astronomy, University of Texas at Austin, Austin Tx 78712 5 Southwest Research Institute, San Antonio Tx 78228-0510 The nearby dwarf galaxy II Zw 40 hosts an intense starburst.
II Zw 40 appears to have formed in a collision or merger between two smaller dwarf galaxies.

TEXES spectroscopy of the [ SIV ] line from II Zw 40 has shown that the [ SIV ] emission is spatially very concentrated; all the [ SIV ] seen by Spitzer for the whole galaxy is emitted from an area only 1.4 or 70 pc across.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGCA 116 as an interacting, H I-rich galaxy pair with a well-developed tidal tail and bridge and a smooth velocity gradient along the tidal structures.

quotes.quote: UGCA 116 It is an interacting pair with well-developed tidal tail and bridge as evident from the channel images and the total image. The velocity field has smooth gradient along the tidal structures. This galaxy pair is rich and is most likely in a merger stage, as also reported previously by van1998AJ....116.1186V .

gmw_00002881.name: MK600
gmw_00002881.overview: MK600 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy classified as an iE blue compact galaxy [arXiv:1803.00537, arXiv:1208.5461]. It exhibits a distorted cometary or tadpole-like stellar morphology [arXiv:1803.00537], with evidence supporting a mode of sustained star formation rather than a single starburst episode [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 600 as a star-forming dwarf with distorted cometary morphology, concentrated young star-forming regions, and chemical homogeneity on both large and small scales.

quotes.quote: Using VIMOS-IFU observations, we study the interstellar medium (ISM) of two star-forming dwarf galaxies, UM 461 and Mrk 600. Our aim was to search for the existence of metallicity inhomogeneities that might arise from infall of nearly pristine gas feeding ongoing localized star-formation.
Mrk 600 in contrast, appears to be chemically homogeneous on both large and small scales.

Mrk 600 (lower panel in Figure figure_image_campo_Halpha ) was classified as an iE BCD according to the classification scheme. However, the elongated shape and the presence of several fainter regions beyond the main body of the galaxy indicate a tadpole or cometary-like stellar morphology. The ongoing star-forming activity in this object see Cairos2001a is mainly concentrated in the two principal GH ii Rs.

sources.summary: MK600 is described as an iE BCG with a suspicious faint double-disk component, deep B-band data affected by nearby stars, and evidence favoring sustained star formation.

quotes.quote: MK600 At first glance this iE BCG has a double exponential disk profile, with a faint population which dominates the surface brightness profile beyond _B 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 . Previous studies of this galaxy do not mention such a structure 2006ApJS..162...49H . Our B band profile is the deepest to date, however, MK600 is situated between three very bright stars and the second exponential disk component could be due to contamination from these stars.

1990A&A...233..348A find strong evidence against a single starburst episode in MK600, and favor instead a mode of sustained star formation over 100 Myr. We find some supporting evidence for this since the color--color diagrams strongly indicate that the current star formation is poorly modeled with an instantaneous burst.

gmw_00002882.name: MK900
gmw_00002882.overview: MK900 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2001.02573, arXiv:1403.6294] with no known companion galaxies [arXiv:1403.6294]. Its central starburst contains young stellar clusters [arXiv:2001.02573] and a dust lane [arXiv:2001.02573], which are situated on top of an older, redder underlying stellar host [arXiv:2001.02573, arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 900 with integral-field spectroscopy and imaging, mapping its morphology, ionized gas, H II regions, older host, young clusters, and dust/extinction structure.

quotes.quote: We investigate the morphology, structure, and stellar content of BCG Mrk 900, as well as the excitation, ionization conditions, and kinematics of its H ii regions and surrounding ionized gas. We obtained integral field observations of Mrk 900 working with the Visible Multi-Object Spectrograph at the Very Large Telescope. The observations were taken in the wavelength range 4150-7400 covering a field of view of 27 27 on the sky with a spatial sampling of 0 67. From the integral field data we built continuum, emission, and diagnostic line ratio maps and produced velocity and velocity dispersion maps.

We also generated the integrated spectrum of the major H ii regions and the nuclear area to determine reliable physical parameters and oxygen abundances. Integral field spectroscopy was complemented with deep broad-band photometry taken at the 2.5 m NOT telescope; the broad-band data, tracing the galaxy up to radius 4 kpc, allowed us to investigate the properties of the low surface brightness underlying stellar host. We disentangle two different stellar components in Mrk 900: a young population, which resolves into individual stellar clusters with ages 5.5-6.6 Myr and extends about 1 kpc along the galaxy minor axis, is placed on top of a rather red and regular shaped underlying stellar host, several Gyr old. We find evidence of a substantial amount of dust and an inhomogeneous extinction pattern, with a dust lane crossing the central starburst.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mk 900 as a BCD without known companions, where central star formation is offset, kinematically distinct, younger, and more metal rich than the galaxy body.

quotes.quote: According to the HyperLeda grouping algorithm, MK900 (NGC7077) has no known companions. Mk900 is the other BCD from our sample with central star formation. Looking into detail at the ( B-I ) image (Fig. fig:BmI ) we see that the peak of the luminosity of the major axis profile (Fig. fig:Mrk900_prop ) is actually a region of recent star formation, which is a bit shifted with respect to the centre of the outer isophotes.

This star-formation region has lower velocity dispersion and rotates at about 5 , it has also younger ages (less than 1 Gyr) and higher metallicity ( 0.7 dex) than the rest of the galaxy, which has a mean age of around 1.7 Gyr and metallicty around 1 dex. The J band image is much smoother than in the case of Mk 324, but we can see peaks of luminosity in this region. The central disk rotation is also visible in the minor axis profile (Fig. fig:Mrk900_prop ), as well as the disturbance in the velocity and sigma at 4 arcsec, where another knot of star formation is observed.

sources.summary: MK900 is described as an nE BCG with symmetric minor-axis star-forming regions, a homogeneous old metal-poor host, little extinction, and centrally confined nebular contamination.

quotes.quote: MK900 The star forming regions in this nE BCG seem to follow the semi--minor axis of the underlying galaxy and are found symmetrically on both sides of the geometric center. All color--color diagrams ubiquitously indicate the presence of a homogeneous old ( 4 Gyr) metal--poor ( Z 0.004 ) host. In this case the instantaneus burst assumption fits the observed colors very well. U-B shows no extinction even in the SF regions, which is to be expected as this is another faint IRAS source 1995ApJS...98..369B . Contamination from nebular emission is confined to the star formation region, with its effect on the colors being negligible beyond the central 10 arcsec.

gmw_00002883.name: MK996
gmw_00002883.overview: MK996 is a local, metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1002.0991, arXiv:2307.00710] featuring a dense nuclear region [arXiv:1002.0991], a dust lane [arXiv:1208.5461], and Wolf-Rayet stars [arXiv:2307.04234]. It exhibits recent interactions indicated by warped neutral hydrogen contours [arXiv:2008.04528], with shocks from supernovae and stellar winds heating its interstellar medium [arXiv:1009.5382].

sources.summary: Mrk 996 is described as a BCD with a dense compact nuclear region, large warm molecular gas mass, high-ionization emission, and likely shock-driven hard radiation.

quotes.quote: This is the third in a series of papers; the first two papers have focused on two particular BCDs, Haro 3, the highest metallicity object in our sample hunt06 , and Mrk 996, a BCD with an unusually dense and compact nuclear \ region thuan08 .

Converting to masses, the two most extreme cases are Mrk 996 with a total warm (98 K) \ mass in the IRS beam of 1.5 10^8 \ and \ (at 120 K) of 1.3 10^8 . Compared to the SINGS galaxies, these are rather high warm \ masses. There are only three SINGS galaxies with warm ( 100 K) \ masses which exceed 10^8 , and only one (NGC 7552) with a warm \ mass greater than that of Mrk 996.

The line intensity of \ in Mrk 996 can be reproduced with such a population of WNE-w stars. However, thuan08 do not favor such an hypothesis because WNE-w stars are very rare. Rather, those authors favor fast radiative shocks thuanizotov05 as the origin of the high-ionization radiation. Shocks are a more realistic expectation in dense compact regions with intense star formation because of SN explosions and massive stellar winds.

sources.summary: The paper uses VIMOS data for J0127-0619, handles a Milky-Way-contaminated UV line, and identifies it as the only CLASSY galaxy with clear N III] multiplet emission.

quotes.quote: For the remaining galaxies of the sample (J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, and J0144+0453), we used integral field spectroscopy data, when available, or long-slit spectroscopy, instead of SDSS. Specifically, we used VLT/VIMOS integral field unit (IFU) from james09 for J0127-0619, MMT Blue Channel Spectrograph spectra from senchyna19a for J0144+0453, Keck/KCWI IFU spectra from rickardsvaught21 for J0934+5514, and VLT/MUSE IFU spectra for the remaining three galaxies. MUSE spectra are also available for the galaxies J0021+0052 (PI: Göran Östlin), J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (PI: Dawn Erb). We used these data to retrieve emission line-ratios involving faint auroral lines, if undetected ( S/N<3 ) in SDSS spectra. Finally, for the galaxies J0808+3948, J0944-0038, J1148+2546, J1323-0132 and J1545+0808, Multi-Object Double Spectographs (MODS) data from the LBT telescope, presented in arellano-cordova22 , are also available ( Paper V arellano-cordova22 hereafter). Information on each of the optical datasets is provided in the following paragraphs. Concerning the IFU data available for J0021+0052, J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, we extracted a spectrum from a 2.5" aperture centred at the same coordinates of COS observations to match the COS aperture (see also berg22 and Paper V arellano-cordova22 ). Specifically, the integrated VIMOS spectrum of J0127-0619 is obtained combining the high-resolution blue and orange grisms, covering the wavelength range 4150-7400 \ with a spectral resolution R 1150-2150 (see james09 for more details).

In J0127-0619 and J1225+6109, where one of the two lines of the \ doublet is contaminated by a MW absorption line, we estimated the flux from the other line, using a line ratio measured from \ of 0.4 (valid at n_e=100 cm ^ 3 and T_e=10^4 K).

Interestingly, only one galaxy of the CLASSY sample, J0127-0619 (i.e., Mrk 996) shows this multiplet in clear emission with S/N 9 . WR features (mainly late type WN stars) in this galaxy were discovered for the first time by thuan96 , while their distribution as well as the ISM abundances and kinematics were investigated by james09 . This could indicate a non-ISM origin of this emission (see also mingozzi22 ).

sources.summary: The review discusses Mrk 996 as a low-metallicity dwarf where IFU/PCA analysis links nitrogen enhancement, broad emission components, outflows, rotation, and oxygen abundance near its nucleus.

quotes.quote: Ionization is mainly due to the UV ionizing flux from massive star, and Wolf-Rayet stars may be responsible for the observed local nitrogen enhancement, particularly in the dense nucleus of Mrk 996 (see talk by Bethan James). The origin of the observed broad component in the emission lines in Mrk 996, as in other low metalicity dwarfs is still under debate (see ), and we have used adaptive optics assisted near-IR IFU spectroscopy with Gemini-NIFS to address this issue (in preparation).
In we applied the technique of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) tomography to extract spatial and spectral information from data cubes of Mrk 996 in a statistical manner.

The PCA tomography analysis that corroborates, in a completely independent manner, the kinematic picture outlined for Mrk 996: an outflow from the inner region, associated with winds from WR stars, superposed on an underlying rotation pattern, affecting the motions of low-density clouds. This recently developed statistical method for handling data cubes has also resulted in the independent detection of the narrow component of the [ O III ] 4363 line, not seen previously in integrated spectra. This detection allows a reliable and direct measurement of the chemical abundances in the region outside the nucleus. We obtain an oxygen abundance 12+log(O/H)=7.90 0.30 ( 0.2 ) for the low-density narrow-line region around the nucleus of Mrk 996.

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra data to test AGN accretion in MRK 996, finding X-ray limits too low for [OIV] ionization and favoring shocks from supernovae and young-star winds.

quotes.quote: The possibility is explored that accretion on an intermediate mass black hole contributes to the ionisation of the interstellar medium of the Compact Blue Dwarf galaxy MRK 996. Chandra observations set tight upper limits (99.7 per cent confidence level) in both the X-ray luminosity of the posited AGN, L_X( 2 - 10 keV) < 3 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 , and the black hole mass, 10^ 4 ^ 1 M_ , where is the Eddington ratio. The X-ray luminosity upper limit is insufficient to explain the high ionisation line [ OIV ] 25.89 m , which is observed in the mid-infrared spectrum of the MRK 996 and is proposed as evidence for AGN activity. This indicates that shocks associated with supernovae explosions and winds of young stars must be responsible for this line. It is also found that the properties of the diffuse X-ray emission of MRK 996 are consistent with this scenario, thereby providing direct evidence for shocks that heat the galaxy's interstellar medium and contribute to its ionisation.

sources.summary: The paper finds H I emission associated with MRK 996 plus isolated nearby regions, interpreting extensions, lopsidedness, and warped contours as signs of recent interaction.

quotes.quote: MRK 996 The emission is seen associated with the galaxy and also from the separate regions in east-south and north of the galaxy. The emission from the galaxy shows extension towards the north. These isolated emission regions are very likely some clouds or faint dwarf galaxies interacting with MRK 996. The global profile shows lopsidedness. The intermediate resolution column density map shows mild warping of the contours, which is also an indication of recent interaction.

sources.summary: Mrk 996 is analyzed as a nearby blue compact dwarf with strong UV nitrogen lines, high electron density, WR stars, enhanced central N/O, and WR-supported enrichment.

quotes.quote: To extend our comparison, we have also examined the low-redshift galaxy Mrk 996, which is a well-known Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy with peculiar properties, such as a high electron density, broad emission line components in , \ and other lines, the presence of Wolf-Rayet stars of WN and WC type, and a high N/O abundance see e.g. Thuan1996Hubble-Space-Te,Pustilnik2004HS-08374717---a,James2009A-VLT-VIMOS-stu,Telles2014A-Gemini/GMOS-s . This galaxy also shows N iii ] and N iv ] emission lines in the UV Mingozzi2022CLASSY-IV:-Expl,Senchyna2023GN-z11-in-conte . From integral-field observations have found a normal N abundance ( ( N/O ) 1.43 ) across the galaxy and a N-enhancement by a factor 20 ( ( N/O ) 0.13 ) in the broad line component, emitted in the central region.

Furthermore, in this galaxy the WR-scenario is directly supported by the presence of WR stars both of WN and WC types Telles2014A-Gemini/GMOS-s . Similarly, N and C enrichment found in the Lynx arc could also be explained by the WR scenario, and earlier studies have argued for the presence of WR stars, from emission line modelling of this peculiar object see e.g. Villar-Martin2004Nebular-and-ste . Is there any direct evidence for WR stars in the N-emitters discussed here? In short, WR stars have been reported only in the low- z galaxy Mrk 996, as mentioned earlier.

sources.summary: MK996 is described as an nE Wolf-Rayet BCG with compact off-center star formation, an old globular system, metal-poor old stars, nebular contamination, and a dust lane.

quotes.quote: MK996 This nE BCG is also a Wolf-Rayet galaxy with a compact off--center star formation region. It is old, as evidenced by 1996ApJ...463..120T who find an old globular system ( 10 Gyr) distributed around the galaxy. All of our data are consistent with an old ( >3 Gyr) metal--poor ( Z 0.004 ) stellar population with moderate contamination of nebular emission predominantly visible in U-B , B-V , and H-K diagrams, which is negligible outside the compact star formation region. The presence of a dust lane has previously been confirmed by both 1996ApJ...463..120T and 1999A&AS..138..213D , and is also visible in our RGB image.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk996 as a local metal-poor dwarf star-forming galaxy with high N/O, low C/N, WR-star evidence, and abundance ratios matching JWST N-rich galaxies.

quotes.quote: We find that Mrk996, a local metal-poor dwarf star-forming galaxy with a high value of ( N/O) 0 Senchyna2023arXiv , has a low C/N value similar to the JWST N-rich galaxies (bottom left panel of Figure fig:nco ). The bottom right panel of Figure fig:nco also shows that Mrk996 coincides with the JWST N-rich galaxies on the N/O vs. C/O plane. Mrk996 is known to host WR stars evidenced by the presence of the WR bumps e.g., Telles2014 . Similar to Mrk996, the JWST N-rich galaxies may also host WR stars.

gmw_00002884.name: Tol 0341--407
gmw_00002884.overview: Tol 0341--407 is a blue compact galaxy and merger of two distinct blue star-forming galaxies [arXiv:1208.5461, arXiv:astro-ph/0106179]. This irregular system contains two nuclei belonging to separate dynamical systems, which may be dwarf galaxies coming together [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179].

sources.summary: Tol 0341--407 is classified as an iI,M merger of two distinct blue star-forming galaxies with irregular morphology, separate profiles, and evidence for three stellar populations.

quotes.quote: Tol 0341--407 The blue star--forming regions in the East and West are two separate galaxies with distinctly different photometric and kinematic properties 1999A&AS..137..419O , hence we classify this merger as iI,M . Due to the very irregular morphology of the galaxy the isophotal and elliptical centers of integration are very different and the two surface brightness profiles do not trace each other at any radius. The elliptical color profiles start at the center of mass (using the center of light as proxy), which happens to be close to the geometric center due to the axisymmetric placement of the two starburst regions. Judging by B-V or U-B vs V-I diagrams, the central region is completely dominated by a population younger than 1 Gyr, while beyond _B 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 we detect another population, 5 Gyr. Thus the color--color diagrams are consistent with the existence of three separate stellar populations in this galaxy, as already suggested by 2004A&A...423..133W .

sources.summary: Tololo 0341-407 is presented as the sample's faintest blue compact galaxy, with two nuclei belonging to distinct systems that may be dwarf galaxies coming together.

quotes.quote: Tololo 0341-407 This galaxy is the faintest BCG in the sample. It has two apparent nucleii embedded in an irregular envelope (Fig. conttol ). The velocity field shows the two nuclei to belong to distinct dynamical systems. In Paper I we successfully decomposed this galaxy into two dynamical components coinciding with the optical components on the broad band image. This may be two dwarf galaxies currently coming together.

gmw_00002885.name: UM133
gmw_00002885.overview: UM133 is a cometary blue compact dwarf galaxy with no evidence of interaction [arXiv:1208.5461]. It features a rotating neutral hydrogen disk aligned with its optical structure [arXiv:1501.06709], containing at least two stellar populations of different ages [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: UM133 is described as an iI,C BCG without interaction evidence, with off-center starburst effects, moderate nebular contamination, and at least one older plus one younger population.

quotes.quote: UM133 This is an iI,C BCG, with no evidence of interaction 1997MNRAS.288...78T . The elliptical color profiles are influenced by the off--center location of the strongest starburst knot. 2000A&A...361...33N suggest that cometary BCGs like UM133 and UM417 could be an evolutionary link between BCGs and other dwarf galaxies because their structural parameters ( _0 and h_R ) are intermediate between those inferred for iE/nE BCGs and the dIrrs/dEs. Color--color diagrams indicate moderate contamination from nebular emission. Due to the morphology of the galaxy we cannot give separate metallicity and age limits for the different populations, but we do detect at least two, one 2 Gyr, and the other younger.

sources.summary: UM 133 has a single-peaked Northeast-Southwest H I disk aligned with its optical structure, a smooth central velocity gradient, and distorted outer gas.

quotes.quote: UM 133 From Ekta Chengalur (2010; their Fig. 1 and 2), with the GMRT. The lower resolution (42 x 36 ) map of the H 1 gas displays a single-peaked Northeast-Southwest disk (PA _ HI 105 ^ ), with a small extension to the South and a larger extension to the East. The H 1 and optical coincide in terms of position angle and geometric (disk) center/ H 1 peak. There is a less than 10 distance between the H 1 peak and the bright star formation knot to the Southwest, leading the cometary structure. In the central regions, the H 1 gas velocity gradient (PA _ VG 105 ^ ) is smooth and parallel to both the optical (PA _ opt 105 ^ ) and H 1 morphology, which is consistent with a rotating disc. However, distortions are seen in the outskirts, particularly towards the South and East, in the direction of the H 1 extensions.

gmw_00002886.name: UM160
gmw_00002886.overview: UM160 is a gas-rich Wolf-Rayet galaxy featuring blue star-forming regions that trace distorted spiral arms around a red center [arXiv:1208.5461]. The galaxy has a low-mass dwarf companion located one arcminute to the north [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: UM160 is described as a Wolf-Rayet, gas-rich galaxy with blue star-forming regions in distorted spiral arms, a low-mass dwarf companion, mixed populations, and nebular contamination.

quotes.quote: UM160 Numerous very blue star forming regions are spread out around the red center of this Wolf-Rayet 1999A&AS..136...35S galaxy. The starbursting knots are tracing what looks like distorted spiral arms. This is a gas--rich ( 9 10^9M_ ) galaxy with a low--mass ( 3 10^8M_ ) dwarf companion 1 arcmin to the North 1990ApJ...364...23S . The color profiles are consistent with a mixed stellar population contaminated by nebular emission but with no detectable dust effects. Due to the inside--out distribution of the burst regions the color--color diagrams show increasing nebular emission contamination at larger radii.

gmw_00002887.name: UM238
gmw_00002887.overview: UM238 is a starburst galaxy that exhibits a cometary appearance due to a bright starburst knot on its western side [arXiv:1208.5461]. The galaxy hosts at least six super-star-cluster complexes and contains three distinct stellar populations ranging from young to old [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: UM238 has multiple super-star-cluster complexes around the host, prior evidence for young, intermediate, and old populations, and color diagrams indicating a population near 1 Gyr.

quotes.quote: UM238 Previous observations have compared this galaxy to UM 133 because its West starburst knot was the only one visible, giving the galaxy a cometary appearance 1997MNRAS.286..183T . Our contour and RGB plots indicate at least six such regions, which must be super star cluster complexes. The brightest one is to the West, as already observed by the previous authors, one is to the North, and the remaining four to the South--East and East of the geometric center. Both 2004A&A...423..133W and 2006A&A...455..825C find three distinct populations in this galaxy, young ( 6.8 Myr), intermediate ( 10 Myr) and old ( 1 Gyr), with the old one clearly dominating the total mass of the galaxy. Due to the morphology of the galaxy we detect a population 1 Gyr in our color--color diagrams but are unable to impose any limits on the age of the star forming regions.

gmw_00002888.name: UM417
gmw_00002888.overview: UM417 is an isolated blue compact dwarf galaxy with no detected companions within a 1 Mpc projected distance [arXiv:1208.5461]. Its starburst region contains a very young low- or intermediate-metallicity burst with negligible dust and a large contribution from nebular emission [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: UM417 is described as an isolated iI,C BCG whose color diagrams show nebular emission, a very young low- or intermediate-metallicity burst, and little dust.

quotes.quote: UM417 This is an isolated iI,C BCG in the sense that there are no detected companions within 1 Mpc projected distance 1991A&A...241..358C . 1989ApJS...70..479S classify it as a SS object. The color--color diagrams show a large contribution from nebular emission, though the effect is negligible outside of the starburst region. These diagrams also suggest that the burst is of intermediate ( Z 0.008 ) or low ( Z 0.004 ) metallicity, and no older than a few Myr, which makes it the youngest burst in the sample. We see no clear indication of dust in the same diagrams, which is consistent with the very low extinction values found in this galaxy by 1991A&AS...91..285T , who measure H_ /H_ =3.71 .

gmw_00002889.name: UM448
gmw_00002889.overview: UM448 is a starburst galaxy with star formation confined to a spiral-like structure [arXiv:1208.5461]. It displays a broad northwest tidal arm [arXiv:1208.5461] and kinematics that show evidence of merging activity [arXiv:2306.10433], including a complex emission line profile possibly associated with an outflow [arXiv:2306.10433].

sources.summary: UM448 has star formation confined to a spiral-like structure, regular faint isophotes, a broad northwest tidal arm, and old metal-poor stars in the outer elliptical region.

quotes.quote: UM448 The star formation in this Wolf-Rayet galaxy is occurring only in the spiral--like structure visible in the RGB plot since those are the only regions with detectable H_ emission 1997MNRAS.286..183T . 1989ApJS...70..479S classify it as a H II H galaxy due to its spectral properties. 2003A&A...410..481N classify it as iI based on NIR images. In our contour plot it is evident that beyond _B 25.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 the isophotes become regular and elliptical, albeit somewhat boxy. This would make an iE BCG classification more suitable.

However, further examination of this faint structure shows that we can trace it out to an additional 35 arcsec beyond the _B 25.5 isophote. Figure um448plume in Appendix um448 demonstrates this, clearly showing a broad tidal arm to the North--West. It is visible in all optical filters.

sources.summary: The paper models UM448 with a three-component [CII] profile, citing previous Hα evidence for complex emission and a possible outflow associated with merger activity.

quotes.quote: We found that, for three galaxies (i.e., Haro3, Mrk1089, and UM448), a three-Gaussian fit (with two narrow and one broad components) provided better residuals than obtained from the two-Gaussian fit, improving the modeling of their global [ CII ] profiles. Interestingly, the morphology and kinematics of these sources show evidence for merging activity (e.g., Dopita02,Johnson00,Johnson04,Cairos07,Galametz09,James13 ). For instance, made use of the Fibre Large Array Multi Element Spectrograph (FLAMES; Pasquini02 ) at the Very Large Telescope (VLT) to detect H emission over UM448. They found a complex emission line profile, composed of two narrow components and a third broad component possibly associated with an outflow. Similarly, we modeled the [ CII ] spectrum of UM448 adding a third narrow Gaussian component to the fit.

gmw_00002890.name: UM619
gmw_00002890.overview: UM619 is classified as a DANS galaxy that has no detected companion galaxies [arXiv:1208.5461]. Its stellar content is dominated by an old population of approximately 8 Gyr, with a moderate-to-negligible contribution from nebular emission [arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: UM619 is described as a DANS galaxy with no detected companions, moderate-to-negligible nebular emission, an H-K excess in bright regions, and an old 8 Gyr population.

quotes.quote: UM619 This is a DANS galaxy 1989ApJS...70..479S with no detected companions 2001A&A...371..806N . It has an mass of 6.9 10^ 8 M_ 2001AJ....122.2341I . The contribution from nebular emission is moderate to negligible in the measured colors, judging by how well they are modeled by a pure stellar population. There is, however, a 0.2 mag excess in all H-K diagrams coming from areas brighter than _B 24 mag arcsec ^ 2 , likely due to nebular emission. The color--color diagrams consistently reveal an old population of 8 Gyr.

gmw_00002891.name: IGR J18249-3243
gmw_00002891.overview: IGR J18249-3243 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.355 [arXiv:2203.01450]. It is optically classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy and exhibits a complex radio morphology [arXiv:2203.01450].

sources.summary: The paper treats IGR J18249-3243 as a radio-loud AGN/radio galaxy associated with PKS 1821-327, reporting z=0.355, complex morphology, and GeV emission that may be produced mostly in its lobes.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The X-ray core is located at RA(J2000)= 18:24:56.11, Dec(J2000)= --32:42:58.9 with a positional uncertainty of 3.7 arcsec and thus is around 15-20 arsec away from the radio source position (depending on the radio catalogue used).

quotes.quote: We present here a newly discovered radio galaxy, namely IGR J18249-3243, which belongs to the complete sample of AGN detected by INTEGRAL /IBIS in its first years of survey (Malizia et al. 2009). It is associated to PKS 1821-327 which has been classified as AGN of unknown type in the 4th Fermi catalogue (4FGL, 2020ApJS..247...33A ). In this work, we discuss its properties, and how the GeV gamma ray emission might be mostly produced in the lobes.

IGR J18249-3243 was first detected by INTEGRAL /IBIS and reported as a new AGN by Bassani et al. (2006) who tentatively associated it to the poorly studied radio source PKS 1821-327. The X-ray counterpart measured by Swift /XRT, needed to confirm this radio association and to provide an optical classification throughout follow up observations, was reported by . The X-ray core is located at RA(J2000)= 18:24:56.11, Dec(J2000)= --32:42:58.9 with a positional uncertainty of 3.7 arcsec and thus is around 15-20 arsec away from the radio source position (depending on the radio catalogue used). In due course, observed the optical counterpart of IGR J18249-3243 with the 1.5 m at the Cerro Tololo Interamerican Observatory (CTIO) in Chile, providing for the first time the source redshift (z=0.355), optical spectrum and AGN class (Seyfert 1, showing broad emission lines). At the same time explored the broad band characteristics of the system (from radio to X-rays) finding that the source is radio loud, has a complex radio morphology and a steep radio spectrum, while confirming at the same time the AGN nature of the system.

gmw_00002892.name: NGC 3620
gmw_00002892.overview: NGC 3620 is a nearby peculiar barred spiral galaxy located approximately 20 Mpc away [arXiv:0904.4816]. Its nuclear region hosts a water kilomaser and experiences intense star formation, with no clear evidence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0904.4816].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3620 as a nearby peculiar SB galaxy hosting a nuclear-region water kilomaser and radio continuum dominated by intense star formation rather than clear AGN activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3620 is a peculiar southern SB galaxy (Elfhag et al., ). Located at a distance of 20 Mpc ( 1'' 100 pc ; H_ 0 = 75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ), it shows a significant velocity gradient across the nucleus in optical lines ( H_ , [ N II ] , and [ S II ] ), which is likely caused by a rotating disk of gas (Schwartz, ). There is good agreement between the two optical velocity components, 2'' .5 east and west of the nucleus ( 1649 9 and 1930 33 km s^ 1 , respectively; Schwartz, ), and the values of the two major peaks in the CO profile (Elfhag et al., ).

In February 2005, we detected an maser in NGC 3620 that has been monitored until April 2008. The maser emission is composed of two main spectral features (see Fig. ngc3620 ), denoted ``a'' ( V_ LSR 1823 km s^ 1 ) and ``b'' ( V_ LSR 1833 km s^ 1 ). Both features are redshifted with respect to the systemic LSR velocity of the galaxy ( 1691 km s^ 1 ).

These values indicate that the radio emission is most likely dominated by optically thin synchrotron emission typical for a collection of sources related to star formation activity like radio supernovae (RSNs) and supernova remnants (SNRs). Hence, the nuclear region of NGC 3620 is linked to intense star formation, and, apparently, there is no evidence for an AGN (whose spectral index may be flatter) in the center of the galaxy.

gmw_00002893.name: GRB 080325 host
gmw_00002893.overview: The GRB 080325 host is a faint, extended galaxy located at an estimated redshift of approximately 1.9 [arXiv:1003.3717]. It is characterized as a massive, dusty system with a stellar mass of (1.2–1.8) × 10^11 solar masses and a star formation rate of 10–80 solar masses per year [arXiv:1003.3717].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint extended source as the GRB 080325 host, measures red near-infrared and optical photometry, and models it as a dusty, massive galaxy at z roughly 1.9.

quotes.quote: We also conclude that the faint extended source just south of the afterglow is the host galaxy of GRB 080325. The aperture ( 2 .0 ) magnitudes of the host galaxy are J = 23.0 0.18 mag and K_ s = 21.7 0.06 mag. These magnitudes substantially represent total magnitudes because of convergence of growth curves at the aperture size of 2 .0 in J and K_ s bands.

The SFH of the best-fit stellar population synthesis model is the (= 1 Gyr) declining model ( ^ 2 =5.4 ), and the best-fit result and output parameters of the host galaxy are summarized in Figure figure5 . However = 10 Gyr and constant SFR models can also reasonably reproduce the observed SED ( ^ 2 =7.8 ). These models give that the host is at z = 1.8 - 2.2 with the best estimate of 1.9 (see Figure figure6 ), and the dust extinction is large ( A_ V = 0.8 - 1.6 mag, E(B-V)=0.2 - 0.4 mag). The M_ *, Salp (stellar mass for Salpeter IMF), SFR, and age of the host derived from SED fitting are (1.2 - 1.8) 10^ 11 M_ , 10 - 80 M_ yr ^ 1 , and 3.0 - 3.5 Gyr, although uncertainties of the SFR and age are relatively large.

gmw_00002894.name: PKS 0723--008
gmw_00002894.overview: PKS 0723--008 is a flat-spectrum blazar [arXiv:1607.04041] and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1505.00935, arXiv:1607.04041] that exhibits active jet behavior [arXiv:1607.04041]. The galaxy has shown a fivefold increase in radio flux density over a decade [arXiv:1607.04041] and is a candidate counterpart for a high-energy neutrino event [arXiv:1505.00935, arXiv:1607.04041].

sources.summary: The paper proposes PKS 0723--008 as the best track-type high-energy neutrino counterpart candidate, citing its flat Planck-frequency spectrum, rising radio flux, coordinates near ID5, and possible jet activity.

quotes.quote: By cross-correlating both the Parkes Catalogue and the Second Planck Catalogue of Compact Sources with the arrival direction of the track-type neutrinos detected by the IceCube Neutrino Observatory, we find the flat-spectrum blazar PKS 0723--008 as a good candidate for the high-energy neutrino event 5 (ID5). Apart from its coordinates matching those of ID5, PKS 0723--008 exhibits further interesting radio properties. Its spectrum is flat up to high Planck frequencies, and it produced a fivefold-increased radio flux density through the last decade.

The sky-coordinates of the flat-spectrum blazar PKS 0723--008 are RA _ J2000 =+111. 4610 and DEC _ J2000 =-0. 9157 , being near the position of HE event ID5. ID5 was observed on 2010 November 12 with energy 71.4 TeV and centred about RA _ J2000 =+110. 6 , Dec _ J2000 =-0. 4 , with median angular error of 1. 2 . We summarize the corresponding PCCS2 detections in Table table:planck_fluxes , and show the spectrum in the left-hand panel of Fig. figure:planck_spectra . We also show a more detailed spectrum in the right-hand panel of Fig. figure:planck_spectra , taken from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). PKS 0723--008 has flat spectrum towards the high Planck frequencies, revealing the most internal component of the jet to be active.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0723-008 as a ray-bright BL Lac object near IceCube event 5, measures its LAT flux and spectrum, and argues the neutrino coincidence is probably chance.

quotes.quote: Located ( 1.04 0.04 ) ^ from event 5 is PKS 0723-008. PKS 0723-008 is a BL Lac object, present in both the 1 year and 2 year Fermi LAT AGN catalogues (1LAC and 2LAC; 1lac , acker3 ). In our 70-month binned analysis, PKS 0723-008 is detected with a TS of 176.8, a spectral index =2.07 0.12 and a flux of (9.35 1.20) 10^ 10 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Both PKS 0723-008's 70-month flux and spectral index are consistent with the values published in the 2LAC catalogue.

This large probability suggests that the spatial coincidence between event 5 and PKS 0723-008 is a chance event. Nonetheless, to investigate the temporal characteristics of PKS 0723-008's 1-300 GeV ray flux around the time the 5 neutrino candidate was detected, the data were binned into 50 day periods, with an unbinned gtlike routine applied to each bin separately.

The resultant lightcurve is shown in Figure lc . While the lightcurve does reveal periods of enhanced ray flux from PKS 0723-008 when compared to the 70-month average, there is no obvious flare event associated with the detection of the candidate neutrino event.

gmw_00002895.name: 4C+00.81
gmw_00002895.overview: 4C+00.81 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=2.25 [arXiv:1607.04041]. The source is classified as a blazar and has a detected Planck counterpart with a flat spectrum across Planck frequencies [arXiv:1607.04041].

sources.summary: The paper lists 4C+00.81, explicitly PKS B2224+006, as a z=2.25 quasar candidate near ID44 with a Planck counterpart and flat spectrum but much lower 15 GHz flux than PKS 0723-008.

quotes.quote: However, in the Parkes catalogue we found four candidates: PKS 0723--008 ( z=0.128 ; ID5), PKS B1206--202 ( z=0.404 ; ID8), PKS B2300--254 ( z unknown; ID18), PKS B2224+006=4C+00.81 ( z=2.25 ; ID44).

Here we found the PCCS2-counterpart of the blazar PKS 0723-008 (ID5) and of the quasar 4C+00.81 (ID44) only. These sources are flat across to the Planck frequencies. The spectral index of PKS 0723--008 is =-0.29 0.02 between 30 and 217GHz, and =-0.18 0.04 between 30 and 857GHz, with the convention S( ) ^ . The spectral index of 4C+00.81 is =-0.30 0.07 between 30 and 217GHz, and =0.16 0.07 between 30 and 545GHz (we did not find the counterpart of the source at 857GHz).

This is almost 13 times larger than the 15GHz flux density of the 4C+00.81, which is 328 mJy by the Owens Valley Radio Observatory Richards2011 , making PKS 0723-008 the best HE neutrino counterpart candidate.

gmw_00002896.name: ESO 302-G014
gmw_00002896.overview: ESO 302-G014 is a nearby, gas-rich dwarf galaxy characterized by a lopsided, low-column-density outer disc [arXiv:2009.09766]. It features an extending tail of clouds and a southern isolated cloud, which suggest a minor interaction that may have triggered recent star formation [arXiv:2009.09766].
