sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 302-G014 as a nearby gas-rich dwarf with a lopsided low-column-density disc, an H I tail and cloud, and signs of recent interaction-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: We used the available data sets, along with ancillary data at other wavelengths, to study the morphology of the MHONGOOSE sample galaxy, ESO 302-G014, which is a nearby gas-rich dwarf galaxy. We find that ESO 302-G014 has a lopsided, asymmetric outer disc with a low column density. In addition, we find a tail or filament of clouds extending away from the galaxy, as well as an isolated cloud some 20 kpc to the south of the galaxy. We suggest that these features indicate a minor interaction with a low-mass galaxy. Optical imaging shows a possible dwarf galaxy near the tail, but based on the current data, we cannot confirm any association with ESO 302-G014.

Nonetheless, an interaction scenario with some kind of low-mass companion is still supported by the presence of a significant amount of molecular gas, which is almost equal to the stellar mass, and a number of prominent stellar clusters, which suggest recently triggered star formation.

gmw_00002897.name: ESO 302-G009
gmw_00002897.overview: ESO 302-G009 is a major galaxy located at a distance of approximately 13.4 Mpc [arXiv:2009.09766]. It is the nearest major neighbor to the galaxy ESO 302-G014, though tidal interactions between the two are unlikely to explain the small-scale features observed around its neighbor [arXiv:2009.09766].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 302-G009 as the nearest major galaxy to ESO 302-G014, but says its distance and lack of intervening features make it unlikely to cause the observed small-scale H I structure.

quotes.quote: Currently, the nearest major galaxy to ESO 302-G014 is ESO 302-G009 (HIPASS J0347--38), at a projected distance of 180 kpc. It is more massive and slightly more distant ( meurer_et_al_2006 give a distance of 13.4 Mpc for ESO 302-G009). The deep GBT observations presented in sorgho_et_al_2019 and sardone_et_al_2020 show no evidence of features between the two galaxies and it is unlikely that tidal interactions between these two galaxies can cause the more small-scale features that we observe around ESO 302-G014.

gmw_00002898.name: NGC 3003
gmw_00002898.overview: NGC 3003 is a tidally perturbed, low-mass-density galaxy undergoing an interaction with its neighbor NGC 3021 [arXiv:2212.14824, arXiv:2205.10263]. It features asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:1712.03780], a highly asymmetric velocity field [arXiv:1103.4928], and signatures of a recent gas-rich minor merger in its southwest corner [arXiv:2205.10263].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 3003 as a disturbed outlier whose uncertain inclination affects scale-height reliability and whose ratios may indicate a synchrotron-loss-dominated convective halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 3003 reveals the largest difference between the two bands. However, its inclination is not well determined and was very difficult to estimate even with our scale height fits (as described in Sect. sec:sample ). It is described as a disturbed galaxy with asymmetric spiral arms karthick+2014 with indications for a starburst superwind hoopes+1999 . Hence, the inclination may be smaller than 84 and we cannot fully trust our scale height values for this galaxy.

Remarkably, the ratios for all galaxies range between 1 and 2, except for the outliers NGC 3003 and NGC 3079, mentioned already at the beginning of this section. Including the errors, only NGC 3003 could be a galaxy with a synchrotron energy loss-dominated halo with convective propagation. Nevertheless, from the frequency analysis the clearest candidates for having an escape-dominated halo with convective propagation are NGC 4157, NGC 4217, and NGC 4302; the others are in the range of a synchrotron energy loss-dominated or escape-dominated halo, both with diffusion, except NGC 3003 and NGC 3079.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3003 as an outlier with unusually large H I and radio scale heights, extended eDIG, tidal interaction, and possible background-source bias in radio measurements.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy, NGC 3003, shows an unusually large H 1 scale height, together with a very extended eDIG. The extended and distorted multi-phase CGM could be explained by the tidal interaction with the neighboring galaxy NGC 3021 Hoopes99, Karthick14 .

The most significant outlier, NGC 3003, as mentioned above, is a galaxy undergoing interaction with a neighboring galaxy, which may result in unusually large radio features, at least on low resolution images. Furthermore, there exists a background radio source projected close to the disk of this galaxy, which may also bias the radio scale height measurements Wiegert15 . This galaxy is also a significant outlier in many statistical analyses of radio scale heights Krause18 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 5251 as Type 5, with about 50 km/s offsets between receding and approaching sides and a highly asymmetric velocity field.

quotes.quote: UGC 5251 is classified as Type 5. In both the inner and outer parts, receding and approaching sides are offset by about 50 \ with a change of sides at a radius of 1 R_ 25 . The velocity field is highly asymmetric.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3003 as a tidally perturbed, low-mass-density outlier with large H I scale height and a recent gas-rich minor-merger signature in its southwest corner.

quotes.quote: After these corrections NGC 5084 whose distribute in a large ring, and the tidally perturbed galaxy NGC 3003 which has the lowest mass density among the sample, still have large h , while the remainder of the galaxies have scale heights approaching those in the literature 2019A&A...622A..64B .

The most significant outliers include two tidal interacting systems (NGC 3003 and NGC 5775), and the peculiar galaxy N5084. From Figure 5 in Z21, NGC 3003 presents signature of recent gas-rich minor merger in the south-west corner.

gmw_00002899.name: NGC 3992
gmw_00002899.overview: NGC 3992 is a strongly barred spiral galaxy located in the Ursa Major cluster [arXiv:1501.06424, arXiv:1605.01638]. Its bar region is devoid of neutral, ionized, and molecular gas [arXiv:1501.06424, arXiv:1605.01638], while the outer disk displays regular neutral hydrogen kinematics and well-defined spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0204335, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393].

sources.summary: The paper adopts an Ursa Major cluster distance for NGC 3992, uses BRI photometry for bulge-disc decomposition, cites detailed rotation measurements, and notes its gas-poor bar region.

quotes.quote: NGC 3992 NGC 3992 is one of the most prominent members of the Ursa Major cluster of galaxies. Tully Pierce (2000) determined a distance of 18.6 Mpc to this cluster, which will be adopted as the distance for NGC 3992. There is photometry in the BRI bands by Tully et al. (1996) of which the I profile is used to calculate the disc rotation and the R-band for the total light. The signature of the bulge is clearly present in the observed radial light profile and consequently the bulge to disc decomposition is straightforward. The rotation curve has been measured in detail by Bottema Verheijen (2002), who also give a rotation curve decomposition.
This galaxy has a bar and this bar region is devoid of gas so that no rotation is available for the inner regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents HI observations of NGC 3992, describing its regular gas distribution, central bar-related gas hole, regular kinematics, and rotation-curve decomposition.

quotes.quote: Detailed neutral hydrogen observations have been obtained of the large barred spiral galaxy NGC 3992 and its three small companion galaxies, UGC 6923, UGC 6940, and UGC 6969. For the main galaxy, the distribution is regular with a low level radial extension outside the stellar disc. However, at exactly the region of the bar, there is a pronounced central hole in the gas distribution.

Likely gas has been transported inwards by the bar and because of the emptyness of the hole no large accretion events can have happened in recent galactic times. The gas kinematics is very regular and it is demonstrated that the influence of the bar potential on the velocity field is negligible. A precise and extended rotation curve has been derived showing some distinct features which can be explained by the non-exponential radial light distribution of NGC 3992.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 3992 as potentially affected by missing interferometer flux, reports high integrated polarization, and notes most detected emission originates outside the bar region.

quotes.quote: A comparison of 6 cm flux densities derived by beck2002 with published integrated flux densities showed that NGC 1313 and NGC 3992 in Table barred-tab may be affected by missing short spacings, because published flux densities listed in the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED) are substantially higher than the 6-cm flux density in beck2002 .

Of the two barred galaxies with suspected missing flux in the interferometer image, NGC 1313 showed no significant integrated polarization, while NGC 3992 has a high degree of polarization of 14.9 4.8 \ . Most of the emission of NGC 3992 detected in the interferometer image originates outside the bar region beck2002 .

Some galaxies (M 31, M 81, NGC 4565 in Table nearby-tab , NGC 4192, NGC 4302, NGC 4535 in Table virgo-tab , NGC 1300, NGC 1493, NGC 3953, NGC 3992 in Table barred-tab ) have integrated fractional polarization _0 > 7 . Five of these galaxies (M 31, NGC 4565, NGC 4192, NGC 1493, and NGC 3992) are more than 10 polarized after integration over solid angle.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3992 as a strongly barred Sbc galaxy with little central gas, a bar-length HI hole, detected dwarf companions, and regular outer neutral-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3992 NGC 3992 is a strongly barred Sbc galaxy with prominent spiral features visible in the stellar disc. The archival VLA HI observations reveal a complete absence of detectable neutral gas within the central 80 arcsec -- about the length of the bar (Fig.  fig:n3992sum ). Even the narrowband H image shows little ionized gas emission around the bar. Likewise, find a remarkable lack of molecular gas in the central region. Beyond the bar, the HI disc has approximately the same radial extent as the high surface brightness stellar disc observed at 3.6 m.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3992 as its later-type barred galaxy test case, deriving a small corotation-to-bar-radius ratio and warning dust may affect the result.

quotes.quote: The fourth galaxy, NGC 3992, an SBbc type is of somewhat later type and was included to empirically assess how well the TW method can recover the pattern speed in such a system. A fourth galaxy, NGC 3992, was observed from La Palma on the night of January 30 2000 using the blue arm of the ISIS spectrograph on the William Herschel Telescope (WHT).

NGC 3992 The gas rich system NGC 3992 turns out to be the most problematic galaxy in the sample. The emission line gas in this system shows signs of large non-circular velocities presumably due to the bar. An alternative explanation for the the small R value in NGC 3992 is that it is due to dust absorption.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3992 as the most massive M81-group spiral, with regular emission distribution, a prominent bar, defined spiral arms, a faint radial extension, and a central gas hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 3992: This galaxy is the most massive spiral of the M81 group. Its \ distribution is regular. It has a prominent bar and very well defined spiral arms. It has a faint radial \ extension outside its stellar disc. There is a pronounced central \ hole in the gas distribution at exactly the radial extent of the bar ( 2002A&A...388..793B 2002A&A...388..793B ), also visible in the emission line.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3992 as a barred spiral with a central gas hole, a faint outer gas extension, and possible stripping from interactions with its companions.

quotes.quote: Immediately obvious is the central hole in the gas distribution of NGC 3992 at exactly the position of the bar. Furthermore, NGC 3992 has a faint gas extension outside its stellar disc. This is contrary to the companions where the distribution ends abruptly at the optical edge of the galaxy. A possible explanation for this is stripping of the gas from the companions when these have passed by, or interacted with NGC 3992.

sources.summary: NGC 3992 is described as having strong spiral-arm polarized radio emission with aligned magnetic fields, weak nuclear emission only at shorter wavelengths, no bar emission, and significant Faraday rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3992 (Fig. 11) features strong polarized radio emission from spiral arms with aligned magnetic fields. Weak emission from the nucleus has only been detected at 6 cm and 3 cm (the sensitivity was too low at 22 cm and 18 cm). No emission from the bar has been detected. Faraday rotation is significant ( +20 ) between 22 cm and 6 cm.

gmw_00002900.name: J1313+696
gmw_00002900.overview: J1313+696 is a Fanaroff-Riley II type giant radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.106 [arXiv:1005.3357]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations reveal a compact, steep-spectrum core that may indicate renewed jet activity [arXiv:1005.3357].

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI observations of J1313+696/4C +69.15, identifying it as an FR II giant radio galaxy whose compact steep-spectrum core may indicate renewed jet activity.

quotes.quote: Liu Xiang Liu 1 Jun Liu 1,2 liux@uao.ac.cn 1 National Astronomical Observatories/Urumqi observatory, CAS, 40-5 South Beijing Rd, Urumqi 830011, PR China 2 Graduate University of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing 100049, PR China We report the result of VLBI observation of the giant radio galaxy J1313+696 (4C +69.15) at 2.3/8.4 GHz, only the core component of the giant radio galaxy was detected in the VLBI observation at the dual frequencies.
J1313+696 (B1312+698, DA340, 4C +69.15, z=0.106), is an FRII type giant radio galaxy.

The VLBI images of J1313+696 show a point source, not as expected to show a core-jet, but reveal a steep spectrum core which may imply a renewed jet in the core. Considering the upper limit flux density at 605 MHz the core of the J1313+696 has a GHz-peaked spectrum (GPS), indicating the core is very compact and absorbed at lower frequency.

gmw_00002901.name: KiDS 0920
gmw_00002901.overview: KiDS 0920 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.3216 [arXiv:2207.12073]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus or LINER-like galaxy, and it hosts CO(1-0) molecular gas that likely originates from an elongated disk-like structure [arXiv:2207.12073].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KiDS 0920/G198598 as a z=0.3216 BCG, classifies it as AGN/LINER-like, and detects CO(1-0) molecular gas likely from an elongated disk-like structure.

quotes.quote: The three intermediate- z BCGs of this work are KiDS 0920 (i.e., G198598 at z=0.3216 ), KiDS 1220 (G172934 at z=0.3886 ), and KiDS 1444 (G363753 at z=0.4417 ).

These imply that KiDS 0920 falls in the AGN region of the [ O III ] 5007/ H versus [ N II ] 6584/ H plane Kewley2006 as well as in the LINER region, close to that of Seyferts, of the [ O III ] 5007/ H versus [ O I ] 6300/ H plane e.g., Sarzi2010,Singh2013 .
We detect KiDS 0920 in CO(1 0), and it is likely that the detected molecular gas comes from the elongated disk-like structure seen in Fig. fig:BCG_images .

gmw_00002902.name: KiDS 1444
gmw_00002902.overview: KiDS 1444 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.4417 [arXiv:2207.12073]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology with a faint southern tail and has a close companion galaxy that may be interacting with it [arXiv:2207.12073].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KiDS 1444/G363753 as a z=0.4417 BCG with disturbed morphology, a faint tail and close companion, and CO emission attributed to the BCG during possible interaction.

quotes.quote: The three intermediate- z BCGs of this work are KiDS 0920 (i.e., G198598 at z=0.3216 ), KiDS 1220 (G172934 at z=0.3886 ), and KiDS 1444 (G363753 at z=0.4417 ).

Similarly, KiDS 1444 shows disturbed morphology, with evidence of a faint elongated tail to the south. Both KiDS 1220 and KiDS 1444 have nearby companions at a projected separation of 3 arcsec (i.e., 17 kpc at the redshifts of the sources).

With a similar argument, we believe that the CO emission observed for KiDS 1444 comes from the BCG only, with null or negligible contamination from the nearby companion within the beam. However, it is possible that the KiDS 1220 and KiDS 1444 BCGs are interacting with their companions, which may favor the replenishment of the BCGs' gas reservoirs, thus ultimately contributing to sustain the high level of star formation activity observed in the targeted BCGs.

gmw_00002903.name: RX1532
gmw_00002903.overview: RX1532 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.36 in a strong cool-core cluster [arXiv:1612.08297, arXiv:2207.12073]. The galaxy exhibits a high star formation rate [arXiv:1612.08297, arXiv:2207.12073], molecular gas [arXiv:1612.08297], radial ultraviolet filaments, and curved dust lanes wrapping its central knots [arXiv:1504.00598].

sources.summary: The paper targets the RXJ1532.9+3021 BCG, finding UV knots, high star formation, molecular gas, broad emission, and cooling-rate limits below its star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: To make simultaneous measurements of O 6 and C 4 in a similar BCG with optical emission-line filaments and a relatively high rate of star formation, we chose the BCG in the cluster RXJ1532.9+3021 at a redshift of 0.362 , which shifted the wavelengths of both of these emission lines to a range that could be observed by COS. We obtained COS FUV and NUV spectrosocpy, along with high-resolution UV imaging and long-slit spectroscopy with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS). We present these spectroscopic observations targeting the central region of the BCG.

The BCG in RXJ1532.9+3021 (Shown in Figure figure:HST ) bears a strong resemblance to other BCGs in strong cool cores with high star formation rates such as that in the Phoenix Cluster 2012Natur.488..349M or extended, filamentary optical nebular emission such as in NGC1275 1970ApJ...159L.151L . It is located in the center of a luminous X-ray cluster with a highly peaked X-ray profile and relatively low central gas entropy. It has a significant amount of molecular gas. 2012ApJS..199...23H reports a detection with Spitzer FIR corresponding to a SFR similar to that of a vigorous starburst ( >100 solar masses per year), with an unobscured UV SFR that is nearly as high 2015ApJ...805..177D . A detailed analysis of 2-d CLASH imaging and ground-based spectroscopy from 2015ApJ...813..117F estimates a total star formation rate of 120^ +220 _ 40 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper says R1532 has radial UV filaments, southern extended filaments, clumps around the nucleus, dust lanes, and a close resemblance to NGC1275 among CLASH BCGs.

quotes.quote: R1532 also has radial filaments extending 20 kpc in all directions, but the longest filaments extend to the south, and multiple clumps surround the nucleus and extend southward. Among the CLASH BCGs, this one probably exhibits the most similarity to NGC1275. The brightest of its knots has been targeted for HST COS photometry and STIS long-slit observations later this year (PI: Donahue.) This BCG also exhibits curved, comma-shaped dust lanes wrapping the central knots; dust lanes appear alongside filaments and knots.

sources.summary: The paper uses RX1532 as a z=0.361 CLASH BCG comparison object, noting CO detections, a CN non-detection, and highly excited gas in a highly star-forming cool-core BCG.

quotes.quote: These are the intermediate-redshift ( z 0.4 ) BCGs RX1532 and M1932 from CLASH Postman2012 . RX1532 was detected both in CO(1 0) and CO(2 1) with our recent CLASH IRAM 30m campaign Castignani2020a .
In our recent study we performed a similar search for the CN(N=1 0) doublet in the BCG RX1532 at z 0.4 , which also led to a non-detection Castignani2020a .

These findings suggest that highly excited gas is preferentially found in highly star-forming and cool-core BCGs such as RX1532 and M1932, as well as in star-forming galaxies of the comparison samples (e.g., ASPECS).

gmw_00002904.name: M1932
gmw_00002904.overview: M1932 is a highly star-forming brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1504.00598, arXiv:2207.12073] hosting a luminous radio and X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1504.00598]. It contains a massive molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:1905.01377, arXiv:2210.14922] with extended CO emission, north-south filaments, and a curved dust lane [arXiv:1504.00598, arXiv:1905.01377].

sources.summary: The paper describes the m1931 BCG as an extreme star-forming CLASH BCG with central clumps, north-south filaments, extended UV structure, a dust lane, and a luminous radio/X-ray AGN.

quotes.quote: The most extreme SF BCGs in CLASH are in clusters m1931 and r1532. m1931 has multiple clumps, with the largest and most luminous clumps in the center. There are several clear filaments, aligned preferentially north/south. The knot detail is also visible in continuum images, indicating that the stellar density is also enhanced in those regions, not just the emission lines. The UV structures extend over 20 kpc NW of the BCG, and somewhat to the SE, for an overall elongated appearance.

There is a curved, clearly resolved dust lane cupping the southeast quadrant of the clumpy nuclear region, while the brightest three non-nuclear clumps spray to the NE, N, and NW. This BCG exhibits GALEX emission at even shorter wavelengths (Table table:GALEX_NVSS ).
The only low- K_0 cluster lacking an NVSS counterpart (m1931) has a luminous X-ray and radio AGN in its core 2011MNRAS.411.1641E, 2014ApJ...794..136D .

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of the MACS 1931 BCG, finding a massive molecular gas reservoir, extended CO emission, dust continuum, star formation, and recent AGN feedback signatures.

quotes.quote: We present new ALMA observations of the molecular gas and far-infrared continuum around the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the cool-core cluster MACS 1931.8-2635. Our observations reveal 1.9 0.3 10^ 10 M _ of molecular gas, on par with the largest known reservoirs of cold gas in a cluster core. We detect CO(1-0), CO(3-2), and CO(4-3) emission from both diffuse and compact molecular gas components that extend from the BCG center out to 30 kpc to the northwest, tracing the UV knots and H filaments observed by HST . Due to the lack of morphological symmetry, we hypothesize that the 300 km s ^ 1 velocity of the CO in the tail is not due to concurrent uplift by AGN jets, rather we may be observing the aftermath of a recent AGN outburst. The CO spectral line energy distribution suggests that molecular gas excitation is influenced by processes related to both star formation and recent AGN feedback.

Continuum emission in Bands 6 and 7 arises from dust and is spatially coincident with young stars and nebular emission observed in the UV and optical. We constrain the temperature of several dust clumps to be 10 K, which is too cold to be directly interacting with the surrounding 4.8 keV intracluster medium (ICM). The cold dust population extends beyond the observed CO emission and must either be protected from interacting with the ICM or be surrounded by local volumes of ICM that are several keV colder than observed by Chandra .

sources.summary: MACS 1931.8-2634 has a very massive, compact molecular gas distribution near its radio core and no detected absorption despite disturbed kinematics.

quotes.quote: MACS 1931.8-2634 has a molecular mass of 7.8 0.7 10^ 10 M _ centred a few hundred parsecs from its radio core. There is no clear velocity structure. The gas has a roughly circular distribution with a diameter of approximately 5 kpc. The molecular gas of MACS 1931.8-2635 has previously been studied by Fogarty19 , though the CO(1-0) observation we present has not previously been published.

In the three galaxies in our sample which contain emission but no absorption (Fig. fig:maps_of_emitters ), one has a smooth velocity structure (RXC J1350.3+0940), while two are irregular (MACS 1931.8-2634 and RXC J1603.6+1553). Although there is no detectable absorption in these systems, we have provided upper limits in Table tab:gas_densities .

sources.summary: The paper uses M1932 as a z=0.353 CLASH BCG comparison object, noting ALMA CO detections and highly excited gas in a highly star-forming cool-core BCG.

quotes.quote: These are the intermediate-redshift ( z 0.4 ) BCGs RX1532 and M1932 from CLASH Postman2012 . RX1532 was detected both in CO(1 0) and CO(2 1) with our recent CLASH IRAM 30m campaign Castignani2020a . M1932 was instead detected in CO(1 0), CO(3 2), and CO(4 3) with ALMA by Fogarty2019 .
These are RX1532 z=0.361 , Castignani2020a and M1932 z=0.353 , Fogarty2019 , in addition to the KiDS BCGs of this work.

These findings suggest that highly excited gas is preferentially found in highly star-forming and cool-core BCGs such as RX1532 and M1932, as well as in star-forming galaxies of the comparison samples (e.g., ASPECS).

gmw_00002905.name: CGCG 049-033
gmw_00002905.overview: CGCG 049-033 is an elliptical galaxy located in the Abell 2040 cluster [arXiv:2103.06573]. It hosts a highly asymmetric Fanaroff-Riley type II active galactic nucleus with a massive central black hole and a weak neutral hydrogen absorption feature [arXiv:2103.06573].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 049-033 as an Abell 2040 elliptical with a powerful FR II AGN and weak H I absorption confirmed by FAST observations.

quotes.quote: CGCG 049-033 The z=0.04464 elliptical galaxy CGCG 049-033 in Abell 2040 cluster possesses a highly asymmetric Fanaroff-Riley type II AGN, comprising of one of the largest radio jet yet discovered, a > 10^9 M_ Sun central black hole, and intense polarised synchrotron radiation extending to a distance of 440 kpc away from the galactic centre Bagchi2007 . The HI absorption feature with a maximum line depth S_ HI,peak 4.3 mJy, an FWHM 123.9 km s ^ 1 , and d v 7.23 km s ^ 1 reported by Song et al. (2021, in preparation) is consistent with the general trend that early-type galaxies are in lack of abundant gaseous contents. The FAST telescope conducted targeted observations twice towards CGCG 049-033, on October 31st and December 28th, 2018, respectively, each was comprised of 1200 s of on source exposure time. Although weak, the expected absorption feature did appear at the right frequency channels in both set of data, thus confirming the correctness of our detection.

As it can bee seen in Fig. figCGCG , the HI absorption line from CGCG 049-033 is the weakest among all five sources detected by Arecibo and confirmed by FAST, with a maximum depth of 5.851 mJy only, as measured by FAST. However, the integrated optical depth, 3.069 km s ^ 1 , is less than half of that of the ALFALFA results. Even if the lower continuum flux provided by VLA FIRST, as adopted by Song et al. (2021, in preparation), is applied to FAST data, the resulting d is still smaller than ALFALFA.

gmw_00002906.name: J1534+2513
gmw_00002906.overview: J1534+2513 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.03396 [arXiv:2103.06573]. Observations have resolved a double-horned neutral hydrogen absorption feature, which is blueshifted relative to the optical redshift and suggests the presence of line-of-sight gas outflows [arXiv:2103.06573].

sources.summary: The paper reports that J1534+2513 has FAST-resolved double-horned H I absorption, a blueshifted radio line, and probable line-of-sight H I outflows.

quotes.quote: J1534+2513 The existence of HI absorption feature in radio source J1534+2513 at a redshift of z=0.03396 was first proposed by , and later confirmed by and Song et al. (2021, in preparation), with a line depth of S_ HI,peak 23.0 mJy, FWHM 26.4 km s ^ 1 , and d v 18.31 km s ^ 1 . This absorber has also been detected by the WSRT survey, with a wider line width (FWHM 40.88 km s ^ 1 ), and an integrated optical depth of 10.92 km s ^ 1 , against a 43.39 mJy background Maccagni2017 . As can be seen in Fig. figJ1534 , the most prominent feature clearly shown in the FAST spectrum should be the double-horned structure over a relatively narrow 27.53 km s ^ 1 frequency range, which is only barely seen with the 5.3 km s ^ 1 resolution of the ALFALFA data (Song et al. 2021, in preparation), and the 16 km s ^ 1 resolution WSRT survey ( , see Fig. figJ1534_WSRT ), thus demonstrating the necessity of performing observations with finer spectral resolution.

And the centroid of this absorption line is again significantly deviates from the optical redshift, although in this case, the radio line is blueshifted by 82.15 km s ^ 1 compared with the optical z , although the extent of discrepancy is smaller than that of UGC 00613. Considering the error in cz measurement for J1534+2513 is as small as <2.7 km s ^ 1 (which is the smallest among all five sources discussed in this work), such a line velocity difference must be originated from the absorbing HI content itself. Again, this may indicate the existence of HI outflows along the line-of-sight, and inteferometer observations should be of help to unveil the nature of the absorbing gas.

gmw_00002907.name: PGC 070403
gmw_00002907.overview: PGC 070403 is a galaxy with a detected neutral hydrogen absorption feature at a redshift of approximately 0.0251 [arXiv:2103.06573]. This absorption line has a relatively weak line depth and is redshifted from the galaxy's measured optical velocity [arXiv:2103.06573].

sources.summary: The paper treats PGC 070403 as a weak FAST drift-scan H I absorber with a tentative ALFALFA detection, uncertain optical velocity offset, and shallower FAST line depth.

quotes.quote: PGC 070403 It was who put forward the first indication on the existence of z= 0.0251 absorption feature PGC 070403, and later the same structure has been tentatively identified by and Song et al. (2021, in preparation) from the .40 data, with an estimation of line depth as S_ HI,peak 12.9 mJy, FWHM 88.9 km s ^ 1 , and d v 11.11 km s ^ 1 . As noted by Song et al. (2021, in preparation), similar to the case of UGC 00613, this absorption line is also redshifted from the optical cz by as many as 191.5 km s ^ 1 . However, it is also noticed that the error of optical cz measurement is as large as 118.4 km s ^ 1 , which means that such a large discrepancy may be due to observational uncertainties. Among all of the 5 HI absorbing sources observed by the FAST telescope and mentioned in this paper, PGC 070403 is the one with the second-weakest line depth, and the only sample observed with the drift scan mode by the FAST telescope.

As can be seen in Fig. figPGC_original , the absorption ``dip'' only appear during the 12 s period around the transit time of PGC 070403 in both polarisations, thus making the FAST detection reliable. Considering the spectral baseline fluctuations appearing during the entire drift scan session, only 12 s observations around target transit time are utilised in our data reduction process. The final spectrum is an average of the 12 s transit data, and the background continuum is subtracted with similar smoothing and fitting method as the tracking data for sources described above.

gmw_00002908.name: M0909 3133
gmw_00002908.overview: M0909 3133 is a narrow-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.884 [arXiv:2107.09705]. It is a radio source featuring extended radio emission that spans a projected linear size of approximately 330 kpc [arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: The paper identifies M0909 3133 as a z=0.884 narrow-line AGN and the survey's largest radio source, with projected radio emission extending about 330 kpc.

quotes.quote: The farthest narrow line AGN in our sample is M1513 2524 ( z_ em =3.132 ), and the one with largest projected linear size of radio emission (size 330 kpc) is M0909 3133 ( z_ em =0.884 ).
In general, the NLAGN in our sample exhibit extended radio emission with lobes and in some cases cores -- the largest being M0909-3133 ( z_ em = 0.884) with a projected linear size of 330 kpc.
M0909 3133 ( = 0.884): The radio emission associated with this NLAGN has an extent of 330 kpc.

gmw_00002909.name: M1312-2026
gmw_00002909.overview: M1312-2026 is a reddened quasar with a systemic redshift of z=0.977 [arXiv:2107.09705]. It is a radio-loud source that was previously considered a candidate high-redshift quasar at z=5.068 [arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: The paper first considers M1312-2026 as a possible z=5.068 radio-loud quasar, then reports X-shooter lines identifying it instead as a reddened z=0.977 quasar.

quotes.quote: In the SALT spectrum of M1312-2026 (Fig. fig:saltz5 ), we identify only a single narrow emission line over the wavelength range covered. If this emission line is , then the corresponding redshift will be z = 5.068 . At 4.86 GHz, the radio source has a flux density of 233 mJy and a size of <2^ (Table z5flux ), and is unambiguously identified with a WISE and PS1 object with colors that are consistent with it being a high- z radio loud quasar.

In order to confirm the redshift of M1312-2026, we reobserved the object with X-shooter of the Very Large Telescope on 2022 February 01. In the X-shooter spectrum, we do not detect expected Mg ii , C iii ] and C iv lines corresponding to the presumed \ at z=5.068. Instead, the detection of [ O iii ] , H and H emission lines confirm the above identified line to be [ O ii ] and the systemic redshift of the quasar to be z=0.977 .

gmw_00002910.name: M0552 3459
gmw_00002910.overview: M0552 3459 is a narrow-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.776 [arXiv:2107.09705]. At 3 GHz, the galaxy exhibits resolved radio emission that is split into two lobes separated by 71 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: The paper identifies M0552 3459 as a z=1.776 narrow-line AGN with C iii], C ii], and Ne iv detections and double-lobed 3 GHz radio emission.

quotes.quote: M0552 3459 ( = 1.776): This NLAGN has clear detections of , C iii ] , C ii ] and Ne iv (see Fig. fig:emission ). The FWHM measured based on the C iii ] emission line is 835 . At 3 GHz, the associated radio emission is resolved into two lobes separated by 8.3 (71 kpc).

gmw_00002911.name: M0516+0732
gmw_00002911.overview: M0516+0732 is a compact narrow-line active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=2.594 [arXiv:2107.09705]. It exhibits strong optical continuum emission and has emission line ratios similar to those observed in high-redshift radio galaxies [arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: The paper describes M0516+0732 as a z=2.594 compact narrow-line AGN with strong optical continuum, affected emission, and line ratios like high-redshift radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: M0516+0732 ( = 2.594): The radio emission associated with this NLAGN is compact with a deconvolved size of <0.5 , i.e., <4 kpc. It also shows strong continuum emission in the optical spectrum. The \ emission profile may be affected by the associated absorption. We measure the \ to \ emission line ratio, f _ /f _ = 2.40 0.10. This is similar to the ratios observed in high- z radio galaxies.

gmw_00002912.name: M1315 2745
gmw_00002912.overview: M1315 2745 is a narrow-line active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=1.911 [arXiv:2107.09705]. It features double-lobed radio emission spanning 280 kiloparsecs, a very weak optical continuum, and a clear detection in WISE imaging [arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: The paper describes M1315 2745 as a z=1.911 narrow-line AGN with 280 kpc double-lobed radio emission, weak optical continuum, WISE detection, and high-redshift radio-galaxy-like ratios.

quotes.quote: M1315 2745 ( = 1.911): The radio emission associated with this NLAGN exhibits a double-lobed morphology with an extent of 32.8 (280 kpc). We detect emission lines of , , C iii ] , C ii ] and Ne iv . The optical continuum emission is very weak. While we do not clearly detect the AGN in PS1 images, it is clearly seen in the WISE image. The deconvolved velocity width of the \ emission line is 375 .

gmw_00002913.name: M1455 0957
gmw_00002913.overview: M1455 0957 is a high-redshift narrow-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.995 [arXiv:2107.09705]. The galaxy is associated with double-lobed radio emission that extends across 69 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: The paper presents M1455 0957 as a z=1.995 narrow-line AGN with 69 kpc double-lobed radio emission and optical/IR and line-ratio properties like M1315 2745.

quotes.quote: M1455 0957 ( = 1.995): The double-lobed radio emission associated with this NLAGN has an extent of 8.1 (i.e., 69 kpc). The optical and IR characteristics are also very similar to M1315 2745. The , , C iii ] , C ii ] and Ne iv emission lines are clearly detected. The deconvolved velocity width of the \ emission line is 430 . We estimate f_ /f _ = 0.69 0.14, which is again typical of the ratios observed in high- z radio galaxies.

gmw_00002914.name: candidate in RXJ 1347
gmw_00002914.overview: The candidate in RXJ 1347 is a high-redshift primordial galaxy candidate with a best-fit redshift near z=8 [arXiv:1312.5320, arXiv:1411.5691]. It exhibits a visible Lyman break [arXiv:1312.5320] and appears slightly elongated, which suggests it is subject to high magnification from gravitational lensing [arXiv:1312.5320, arXiv:1411.5691].

sources.summary: The RXJ 1347 candidate is a high-redshift population III galaxy candidate with a best fit near z=8, a visible Lyman break, and possible lensing distortion.

quotes.quote: Concentrating on the candidate in RXJ 1347 the fit as a function of redshift is displayed in Figure fig:rxj1347cv . There is a quite broad range with good fit for z between 6.2 and 8.8 with a sharp peak at z=8.0 . The comparison models generally have much worse fits even though there are a peak around z=6.4 where at least a Gissel model fits rather well.

The Figure fig:rxj1347thumbnails shows 2.0'' 2.0'' thumbnail images of the candidate in RXJ 1347. The eight filters in CLASH covering the longest wavelengths are used, ranging from 6,500 (F775W) to 17,000 (F160W). The Lyman-break is clearly visible between F850LP and F105W since the object is observed in F105W but not in F850LP. In the filters F110W (which contains the most significant detection with S/N=8.3) to F160W the galaxy appears slightly distorted, seemingly stretched from upper right to lower left. This could imply gravitational lensing with high magnification.

sources.summary: Object 1347 is a lensed Pop III candidate behind RXJ1347, with attractive colors, F110W detection, high magnification, and Pop III fits favored near z=8.

quotes.quote: 1347 The object we have discovered behind RXJ1347 possesses several interesting properties. In Figure  fig:colorcolordiagram , we see that it has a very attractive combination of colors to correspond to a Pop III galaxy. The galaxy has one very clear ( S/N 8.8 ) detection in F110W, while the F125W and F160W falls short of 5 detections by a narrow margin. Even though vague, the galaxy also appears elongated from upper right to lower left in some of the thumbnails in Figure  fig:RF1 . This plausibly confirms the high magnification (as discussed in Section  sec:gravitationallensing ).

Since this means that all magnitudes would be 30 without magnification, we could not have detected the object without the flux boost due to gravitational lensing. In Figure  fig:RF2 we show the best fit models from each grid compared to the observations. As can be seen, the Pop III model fits the data better, especially for the peculiarly low flux in F140W (compared to the surrounding filters).

gmw_00002915.name: BOSS1244-QG1
gmw_00002915.overview: BOSS1244-QG1 is a massive quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.244 [arXiv:2303.09726]. It has a half-light radius of 6.76 kpc and is likely merging with a nearby quiescent galaxy to form a massive brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2303.09726].

sources.summary: BOSS1244-QG1 is a massive quiescent protocluster galaxy at z=2.244 with a 6.76 kpc half-light radius, likely merging with QG2 to form a brightest cluster galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of a pair of massive quiescent galaxies likely in the process of merging at the center of the spectroscopically confirmed, extremely massive protocluster BOSS1244 at z=2.24 0.02 . These galaxies, BOSS1244-QG1 and BOSS1244-QG2, were detected with Hubble Space Telescope (HST) grism slitless spectroscopic observations. These two quiescent galaxies are among the brightest member galaxies with z=2.223-2.255 in BOSS1244 and reside at redshifts z=2.244 and z=2.242 , with a half-light radius of 6.76 0.50 and 2.72 0.16 kpc, respectively.

BOSS1244-QG1 and BOSS1244-QG2 are separated by a projected distance of about 70 physical kpc, implying that the two galaxies likely merge to form a massive brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) with size and mass similar to the most massive BCGs in the local Universe.

gmw_00002916.name: BOSS1244-QG2
gmw_00002916.overview: BOSS1244-QG2 is a massive quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.242 [arXiv:2303.09726]. It has a half-light radius of 2.72 kpc and is likely merging with a nearby quiescent galaxy to form a massive brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2303.09726].

sources.summary: BOSS1244-QG2 is a massive quiescent protocluster galaxy at z=2.242 with a 2.72 kpc half-light radius, likely merging with QG1 to form a brightest cluster galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of a pair of massive quiescent galaxies likely in the process of merging at the center of the spectroscopically confirmed, extremely massive protocluster BOSS1244 at z=2.24 0.02 . These galaxies, BOSS1244-QG1 and BOSS1244-QG2, were detected with Hubble Space Telescope (HST) grism slitless spectroscopic observations. These two quiescent galaxies are among the brightest member galaxies with z=2.223-2.255 in BOSS1244 and reside at redshifts z=2.244 and z=2.242 , with a half-light radius of 6.76 0.50 and 2.72 0.16 kpc, respectively.

BOSS1244-QG1 and BOSS1244-QG2 are separated by a projected distance of about 70 physical kpc, implying that the two galaxies likely merge to form a massive brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) with size and mass similar to the most massive BCGs in the local Universe.

gmw_00002917.name: J0111+3906
gmw_00002917.overview: J0111+3906 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.668 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410663] that is classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2303.11361]. It exhibits kiloparsec-scale extended radio emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0410663] and faint large-scale structures that indicate multiple epochs of recurrent activity [arXiv:2303.11361, arXiv:astro-ph/0410663].

sources.summary: The paper uses B0108+388 as its worked GPS-galaxy SED example, modeling its synchrotron radio emission, thermal host/nucleus components, X-rays, and predicted gamma rays.

quotes.quote: In Fig. \ fig_seds , we show, as an example, the modelling of the intrinsic Throughout this paper, we use the cosmological paramters: _ =0.7 , _ M = 0.3 , with H_ 0 = 72 km Mpc ^ 1 broad-band SED of B0108+388, a source with LS=41 pc. The SED data were derived from the literature, and properly de-absorbed. The modelling of the complete SED sample will be presented elsewhere. The radio data of IERS B0108+388 were modelled as synchrotron radiation produced by a lobes' electron population N_e( ) derived from the evolution of an injected hot-spots' population Q( ) ^ s , with s=s_1=1.8 for < _ int , and s=s_2=3.2 for > _ int . FFA effects enable us to best fit the spectral behaviour at frequencies below the 6 GHz turnover.
For this source, the X-ray emission is dominated by the comptonization of the IR radiation. Our model well reproduces the observed X-ray spectrum, and predicts significant ray emission.

sources.summary: J0111+3906 is classified as a CSO 2.0 with a pinpointed flat-spectrum center, outer steep-spectrum hot spots, previous large-scale activity, and a quiescent optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: J0111+3906, CSO 2.0 [ 3 ] : 1996ApJ...463...95T ^ , using multi-frequency, multi-epoch observations, pinpointed the flat-spectrum center of activity - marked by the red cross in plt:linsizedist 3 , and provided compelling evidence of steep-spectrum hot spots situated at the outer edges of the lobes straddling the nucleus. This was the first CSO to be shown to have some faint large-scale structure, and thus to have had previous activity, which established the existence of multiple epochs of activity in some CSOs 1990AA...232...19B .

The optical spectrum for J0111+3906 looks like a typical quiescent galaxy. No strong [ O III ] emission or other emission lines are detected, and stellar absorption features consistent with old stars are visible.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0111+3906 as a galaxy with kiloparsec-scale extended radio emission, interpreting that emission as evidence for recurrent radio-source activity.

quotes.quote: J0111+3906 : well known galaxy at z=0.668 . We have detected extended emission on kpc scale (see Fig. fig:J0111+3906 ), already discovered and imaged by Baum et al. (1990), Taylor et al. (1996), Stanghellini (2003), at different resolutions. The extended emission is in contrast with a recent origin for the radio activity in this source but can be explained if the radio source is recurrent (Baum et al. 1990).

gmw_00002918.name: J0131+5545
gmw_00002918.overview: J0131+5545 is a young compact symmetric object classified as an active galactic nucleus with a compact, two-sided radio structure [arXiv:2006.16970]. The source exhibits lobe-dominated gamma-ray emission with no detected flux variation above the two-confidence level [arXiv:2401.03479], and its radio jets show evidence of episodic activity [arXiv:2006.16970].

sources.summary: The paper presents TXS 0128+554 as a lobe-dominated gamma-ray CSO with the strongest LAT detection, highest luminosity and flattest spectrum of the three, but no significant variability.

quotes.quote: The ray emitting compact symmetric objects (CSOs) PKS 1718--649, NGC 3894, and TXS 0128+554 are lobe-dominated in the radio emission. In order to investigate their ray radiation properties, we analyze the 14-yr Fermi/LAT observation data of the three CSOs. They all show the low luminosity ( 10^ 41 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) and no significant variability in the ray band. Their ray average spectra can be well fitted by a power-law function.

TXS 0128+554 . The analysis with the 14-yr Fermi/LAT observation data yields a significant ray detection with TS=146.5 for TXS 0128+554. It has the highest ray luminosity of (1.62 0.17) 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 and the flattest spectrum with _ =2.11 0.08 among the three CSOs. Same as PKS 1718--649 and NGC 3894, no significant flux variation of rays more than the 2 confidence level is observed for TXS 0128+554.

sources.summary: The paper presents TXS 0128+554 as a young gamma-ray AGN/CSO with unresolved Chandra emission, compact two-sided radio structure, an 82-year kinematic age, and evidence for restarted episodic jets.

quotes.quote: We have carried out a Chandra X-ray and multi-frequency radio VLBA study of the AGN TXS 0128+554, which is associated with the source 4FGL J0131.2+5547. The AGN is unresolved in a target 19.3 ks Chandra image, and its spectrum is well fit by a simple absorbed power law model, with no distinguishable spectral features. Its relatively soft X-ray spectrum compared to other CSOs may be indicative of a thermal emission component, for which we were able to obtain an upper temperature limit of kT = 0.08 keV. The compact radio morphology and measured advance speed of 0.32 0.07 indicate a kinematic age of only 82 y 17 y, placing TXS 0128+554 among the youngest members of the compact symmetric object (CSO) class.

The lack of compact, inverted spectrum hotspots and an emission gap between the bright inner jet and outer radio lobe structure indicate that the jets have undergone episodic activity, and were re-launched a decade ago.

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets J0131+5545 as a CSO 2.2 whose nucleus may not be C0, with lobe outward motion stopped and material moving across the jet axis.

quotes.quote: 2020ApJ...899..141L have identified the nucleus of J0131+5545 as component C0. We suggest that, as with the above three CSOs, the nucleus in J0131+5545 is not coincident with the bright component C0. The jet axis is almost east-west, and, using the velocities measured by 2020ApJ...899..141L , shown by the red arrows, we find that C1 is moving west with a speed of (0.166 0.007) relative to C0, while C5 is moving west with a speed of (0.188 0.013) relative to C0. Thus, the difference between C1 and C5 in westward motion relative to C0 is (0.022 0.015) , consistent with zero.

gmw_00002919.name: J0713+4349
gmw_00002919.overview: J0713+4349 is a compact symmetric radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9907120, arXiv:2303.11361] featuring a flat-spectrum core, a steep-spectrum jet, and terminal hot spots [arXiv:2303.11361]. Its X-ray spectrum reveals a mildly ionized iron emission line [arXiv:1603.00947], while its radio flux density is weakly polarized and non-variable [arXiv:astro-ph/9907120].

sources.summary: The paper finds that 0710 + 439 matches earlier XMM-Newton continuum results but shows a hard-band emission line, likely tied to ionized iron or jet/ISM emission.

quotes.quote: 0710 + 439 The photon index and the amount of intrinsic absorption in our Chandra spectrum of 0710 + 439 agree very well with the earlier XMM-Newton results of Vink et al. (2006). The source did not vary between the XMM-Newton and \ observations showing consistent 2--10 keV flux of about 4.1 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . However, we find clear residuals in the hard X-ray band suggestive of the presence of an emission line (Figure fig:0710 , top/left). To account for this feature we added a Gaussian component to the absorbed power-law spectral model (Model B in Table tab:0710 ; Figure fig:0710 , top/right). The line is unresolved ( = 10 eV; fixed) with the best fit line energy of E = 6.62 0.04 keV (rest frame) and the equivalent width EW = 154^ +65 _ 58 eV.

We conclude that with the present data we are not able to state if the soft X-rays and the He-like iron line in 0710 + 439 are due to scattered nuclear emission (Models B or F) or thermal emission of the ISM heated through interactions with the expanding jet (Models D and E).

The presence of a mildly ionized Fe line in the spectrum of 0710 + 439 indicates that there might be a systematic error in the redshift derivation of PMN J1603 4904 because of a range of ionization states of iron producing the emission lines in the CSO sources. Lastly, we note that 0710 + 439 has the highest number of counts (1,679) among the sources in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0710+439 as a compact symmetric radio source with high luminosity, weak polarization, multi-epoch VLBI hotspot motion, and an inferred age near 1100 years.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of multi-epoch global VLBI observations of the Compact Symmetric Objects: 2352+495 and 0710+439 at 5 GHz. Analysis of data spread over almost two decades shows strong evidence for an increase in separation of the outer components of both sources at a rate of 0.2 h^ 1 (for q_ =0.5 and H_ =100 h kms ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ). Dividing the overall sizes of the sources by their separation rates implies that these Compact Symmetric Objects have a kinematic age 10^ 4 years.

The radio source 0710+439 z=0.518 , lzr96 has a high radio luminosity L_ 5GHz =5 10^ 26 h^ 2 W Hz ^ 1 , rtx96 . The radio flux density is very weakly polarised and it is not time variable aah92 . ht 0710+439 has been observed at 5 GHz with a global VLBI array at 6 epochs spread over a period of 17 years. The first five epochs were analysed by oc98 . Here we add new data from multi-snapshot 14 station global observations made at 18th of Sep 1997. The highest dynamic range image (see Fig. Fig_0710 a) was obtained using DIFMAP spt94 and was used as a starting point in re-mapping the other epochs following the procedure describe by oc98 . This image shows clearly the overall triple structure of the source. The northern and the southern components show compact, bright subcomponents which are associated with hotspots surrounded by the faint extended lobe emission. The middle component is associated with the base of the northern jet, and the centre of activity lies at the southern end of this component trp96 . The image in Fig. Fig_0710 a shows clearly for the first time at this frequency a bridge of emission between northern hotspot and the middle component. In addition the emission previously detected at 1.6 GHz x94 between the middle and southern components is for the first time now also detected at 5GHz. The hotspot components are well separated on CLEAN images which allows us to use the AIPS task JMFIT to fit the position of hotspot at each epoch. The fitted linear regression line to this data gives an estimated separation rate of 13.614 0.988 as/yr (Fig. Fig_0710 b). From the obtained correlation coefficient of 0.989 we can reject the null hypothesis of no motion at a better than 0.1 confidence level for a fuller discussion on errors see oc98 . Dividing the distance between hotspots of 87.07 h^ 1 pc by their observed separation velocity of 0.243 0.018 h^ 1 we estimate that the source 0710+439 is 1100 100 years old.

sources.summary: J0713+4349 is a CSO 2.0 with a flat-spectrum core, steep jet and lobes, terminal hot spots, and a measured lobe-separation speed.

quotes.quote: J0713+4349, CSO 2.0 [ 10 ] : 1984IAUS..110..131R showed that this object has a flat spectrum core, a steep spectrum jet, and two steep spectrum lobes. 1996ApJ...463...95T pinpointed the center of activity and showed that there are hot spots at the extremities of the lobes.

The separation speeds of the three CSO 2.0s are easy to determine because they have well-defined hot spots at the leading edges of their outer lobes. These are (0.52 0.03) , for J0713+4349 2003PASA...20...69P ; (0.24 0.015) , for J1247+6723 2003PASA...20...69P ; and (0.19 0.03) , for J1511+0518 2012ApJS..198....5A .

gmw_00002920.name: J0741+2706
gmw_00002920.overview: J0741+2706 is classified as an indeterminate compact symmetric object [arXiv:2303.11361]. It features a flat-spectrum core and two steep-spectrum radio lobes that exhibit an edge-dimmed structure without clear hot spots [arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: J0741+2706 is an indeterminate CSO 2.1 with a flat-spectrum core, steep lobes, edge-dimmed structure, no clear hot spots, and split blind-test classifications.

quotes.quote: J0741+2706, CSO 2.1 [ 8 ] : 2016MNRAS.459..820T ^ observed this object at 5 GHz and 8 GHz, and identified the flat spectrum core and two steep spectrum lobes. The object is edge-dimmed - i.e., the regions of highest surface brightness outside of the core are closer to the core than to the outer edges of the lobes. There are no clear hot spots. The blind tests were split equally between a 2.1 and a 2.2. This shows that the classification is indeterminate, and hence this is a CSO 2.1 object.

gmw_00002921.name: J0832+1832
gmw_00002921.overview: J0832+1832 is classified as a compact symmetric object of type CSO 1 [arXiv:2303.11361]. It features a flat-spectrum core and two steep-spectrum radio lobes with an edge-dimmed morphology that lacks clear hot spots [arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: J0832+1832 is classified as a CSO 1 object with a flat-spectrum core, steep lobes, edge-dimmed morphology, and no clear hot spots.

quotes.quote: J0832+1832, CSO 1.0 [ 54 ] : Like J0741+2706, 2016MNRAS.459..820T ^ observed this object at 5 GHz and 8 GHz, and identified the flat spectrum core and two steep spectrum lobes. The object is edge-dimmed - i.e., the regions of highest surface brightness outside of the core are closer to the core than to the outer edges of the lobes. There are no clear hot spots. The edge-dimmed morphology makes this a CSO 1 object.

gmw_00002922.name: J0855+5751
gmw_00002922.overview: The background radio source J0855+5751 is classified as an intermediate compact symmetric object [arXiv:2303.11361]. Its radio emission is absorbed by SDSS J085519.05+575140.7 [arXiv:1607.05995], a foreground dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.026 [arXiv:1607.05995].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J085519.05+575140.7 as a z=0.026 dwarf absorber whose H I gas is mapped on parsec scales through 21-cm absorption toward J0855+5751.

quotes.quote: We present global VLBI observations of the 21-cm transition of atomic hydrogen seen in absorption against the radio source J0855+5751. The foreground absorber (SDSS J085519.05+575140.7) is a dwarf galaxy at z = 0.026 . As the background source is heavily resolved by VLBI, the data allow us to map the properties of the foreground H i gas with a spatial resolution of 2 pc. The absorbing gas corresponds to a single coherent structure with an extent > 35 pc, but we also detect significant and coherent variations, including a change in the H i optical depth by a factor of five across a distance of 6 pc.

The dwarf galaxy SDSS J085519.05+575140.7 at a redshift of z=0.026 displays strong 21-cm absorption with a peak optical depth of 24 per cent. The background source, J0855+5751, has a 1.4-GHz flux density of 636 mJy white97 and a projected distance from the foreground galaxy of 6.8 kpc, a typical impact parameter for H i absorption detected in the gas disk of a galaxy with a luminosity of 0.05 L^* zwaan05a .

sources.summary: J0855+5751 is classified as an intermediate CSO 2.1 because its two lobes show asymmetric hot-spot placement in re-reduced VIPS observations.

quotes.quote: J0855+5751, CSO 2.1 [ 39 ] : 2005ApJS..159...27T , in pilot VIPS 5 GHz observations, identified this object as a CSO based on the morphology of the two lobes and the hot spots in the lobes. 2016MNRAS.462.2819B ^ re-reduced the data and their map makes clear that the northern hot-spot is at the outer extremity of the lobe, whereas the southern hot-spot is not at the outer extremity of the lobe. This discrepancy in the morphologies of the two lobes leads to the classification as ``intermediate'', or CSO 2.1.

gmw_00002923.name: J0943+1702
gmw_00002923.overview: J0943+1702 is classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2303.11361]. It features a flat-spectrum core and two steep-spectrum radio lobes that host hot spots at their outer edges [arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: J0943+1702 is a revised CSO 2.0 classification with a flat-spectrum core, steep lobes, outer-edge hot spots, and unanimous post-test agreement.

quotes.quote: J0943+1702, CSO 2.0 [ 9 ] : 2016MNRAS.459..820T identified this as a CSO based on 5 GHz and 8 GHz observations, which showed a clear flat-spectrum core and two steep spectrum lobes. The lobes are barely resolved in the 2016MNRAS.459..820T maps, but the MOJAVE ^ 15 GHz observations show clear hot spots at the outer edges of the lobes, making this a clear CSO 2.0 object. This is one of only three CSOs whose classification was revised after the blind tests . The CSO 2.0 classification was agreed unanimously in a group discussion of how the MOJAVE image was embedded in the 5 GHz image and the positions of the 15 GHz hot spots at the outer edges of the 5 GHz lobes.

gmw_00002924.name: J1158+2450
gmw_00002924.overview: J1158+2450 is a flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1707.03121] that is also classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2303.11361]. High-frequency observations reveal a central core, extended northern emission, and a southern component that may harbor a secondary black hole [arXiv:1707.03121].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1155+251 as a flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar whose 24/43 GHz VLBA images reveal extended northern emission, a central core, bright S1 component, and a possible secondary black hole.

quotes.quote: PKS 1155+251 is classified as a flat-spectrum radio loud quasar Healey+08 but is not detected in the ray band. The radio spectrum appears to peak at 5 GHz based on the data available in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database. In our observation, the spectral index is 0.5 for the VLBA image flux density of 188.6 mJy at 24 GHz, and 142.4 mJy at 43 GHz.

We made high-frequency VLBA observations of quasar PKS 1155+251 at 24 and 43 GHz. The results reveal extended northern emission, a central flat-spectrum core C, and a westward-jetted southern component S1 with a relatively flat spectrum of 0.38 between 24 and 43 GHz. The component S1 is the brightest with a brightness temperature of 10^9 K, which is higher than that of the central core.

The CSO scenario cannot fully explain the radio properties of this source (e.g., the significant western emission at lower frequencies and the VLBA flux density variability at 15 GHz in S1). An alternative scenario is proposed that, in addition to the central core, the southern complex possibly harbors a secondary BH for the jetted structure of S1 in the high-frequency images, which could account for its significant flux density variations at 15 GHz and apparent shrinking of the source.

sources.summary: J1158+2450 is described as an unusual CSO whose higher-frequency maps reveal a north-south embedded CSO, yielding a unanimously revised CSO 2.2 classification.

quotes.quote: J1158+2450, CSO 2.1 [ 32 ] : A very unusual object, as revealed in the 5 GHz, 8 GHz and 15 GHz maps of 2008ApJ...684..153T ^ . The 5 GHz map shows an apparent east-west double source, but the 15 GHz map shows that the eastern component has a north-south CSO embedded in the slightly larger east-west structure. The 8--15 GHz spectral index map of 2008ApJ...684..153T clearly identifies the flat spectrum core and steep spectrum lobes in the north-south structure in the eastern component, making this a clear CSO 2.2. This is one of only two CSOs whose classification was revised after the blind tests had been carried out . The final CSO 2.2 classification was unanimously agreed in a group discussion of the 2008ApJ...684..153T 8 GHz and 15 GHz maps.

gmw_00002925.name: J1227+3635
gmw_00002925.overview: J1227+3635 is a high-luminosity quasar located at a redshift of 1.973 [arXiv:2303.11361]. It is classified as a compact symmetric object with a narrow jet, a strong hot spot, and relativistic beaming effects [arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: J1227+3635 is interpreted as a high-luminosity CSO 2.0 quasar whose narrow jet, strong hot spot, and de-boosted counter-side indicate relativistic beaming effects.

quotes.quote: J1227+3635, CSO 2.0 [ 16 ] : This object is observed in the 1.7 GHz and 5 GHz observations of 2013MNRAS.433..147D , as well as the 5 GHz and 8 GHz observations of 2016MNRAS.459..820T ^ to have a flat spectrum core straddled by two steep spectrum lobes, one of which is at the end of a long, narrow jet and is strongly edge brightened. The optical identification is a quasar at redshift 1.973, which suggests that the jet axis cannot be close to the plane of the sky. The most likely interpretation is that this is a CSO where some effects of relativistic beaming are being seen, which might also explain the fact that this is the highest luminosity CSO in this sample of 55. The side opposite the jet on this interpretation is de-boosted and the hot-spot radiation on that side is likely also de-boosted. The morphology of the very narrow jet and strong hot-spot on one side mark this as a CSO 2.0 object.

gmw_00002926.name: J1313+5458
gmw_00002926.overview: The radio galaxy J1313+5458 is classified as a compact symmetric object of type CSO 2.2 [arXiv:2303.11361]. It features two heavily resolved, steep-spectrum radio lobes that lack hot spots at their overall extremities [arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: J1313+5458 is classified as CSO 2.2 because its heavily resolved steep-spectrum lobes lack hot spots at the overall source extremities.

quotes.quote: J1313+5458, CSO 2.2 [ 20 ] : The 5 GHz map of 1994ApJS...95..345T ^ shows two heavily-resolved lobes. 5 GHz and 8 GHz observations of 2016MNRAS.459..820T shows these lobes to have steep spectra. The highest surface brightness region, possibly a hot-spot, in one lobe is not at the extremity of the source, and the other lobe has an amorphous structure with no hot-spot.

gmw_00002927.name: J1400+6210
gmw_00002927.overview: J1400+6210 is classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2303.11361]. Although its radio jets exhibit a lot of structure, they lack visible hot spots [arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: J1400+6210 is a CSO 2.2 whose center of activity was pinpointed, but whose jet structure lacks visible hot spots.

quotes.quote: J1400+6210, CSO 2.2 [ 15 ] : 1996ApJ...463...95T pinpointed the center of activity in this object, which made clear that it is a CSO. They also pointed out that, while there is a lot of structure in the jets, there are no visible hot spots. So this is a CSO 2.2 object.

gmw_00002928.name: J1205+2031
gmw_00002928.overview: J1205+2031 is a radio galaxy associated with NGC 4093 at a redshift of z = 0.024 [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2303.11361]. It is classified as a compact symmetric object with a flat-spectrum core, a two-sided northeast-southwest jet, and an intrinsically slow jet velocity [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2303.11361].

sources.summary: The paper associates J1205+2031 with NGC 4093 at z=0.024, finding an unresolved low-frequency source, a two-sided NE-SW jet, flat-spectrum core, and intrinsically slow jet.

quotes.quote: J1205+2031 The source J1205+2031 is associated with the galaxy NGC 4093 at redshift z = 0.024 2004ApJ...607..202M . The source is unresolved in 150-MHz LOFAR observation and 1.4-GHz NVSS observation 1998AJ....115.1693C,2020A&A...642A.107C . Our 5- and 8- GHz EVN images (last row of Figure fig:VLBI ) show a two-sided structure with jet extending in the northeast-southwest direction. The source has a flat spectrum with a spectral index of = 0.01 between 150 MHz and 1.4 GHz 2020A&A...642A.107C , a spectrum with = 0.44 between 1.4 GHz and 5 GHz 2019A&A...631A.176C , and a flat spectrum with ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.13. The central component C shows the most compact morphology, highest flux density and a flat spectrum ( ^ 4.87-8.37 _ VLBA = 0.49, ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.22) and is identified as the core.

Using the core as a reference, a linear regression fit gives the proper motion velocities for three components: 0.047 0.009 mas yr^ 1 for SW2, 0.028 0.021 mas yr^ 1 for SW1, and 0.130 0.114 mas yr^ 1 for NE. These angular velocities correspond to 0.07 , 0.04 , and 0.21 for SW2, SW1, and NE, respectively. The low radial velocity obtained from proper motion measurements suggests an intrinsic slow jet.

sources.summary: J1205+2031, also identified as NGC 4093, is the least luminous CSO 2.1 in the sample and has very low estimated radio-emitting energy.

quotes.quote: The least luminous CSO 2 in our sample is the CSO 2.1 object 43 in Fig. 1, J1205+2031 (NGC 4093). This CSO has been mapped with the European VLBI Network by 2021MNRAS.506.1609C . From their 5 GHz VLBI image we have estimated the angular sizes and flux densities of the two lobes, and, assuming a spectral index of =-0.7 for both lobes, we obtain a total energy of 1.5 10^ 4 M_ ^2 for this object.

gmw_00002929.name: ESO 602-- G 031
gmw_00002929.overview: ESO 602-- G 031 is an active galactic nucleus with a recessional velocity of approximately 10,200 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0510551]. It exhibits a redshifted absorption edge that is non-local, as well as narrow, unresolved emission from neutral iron [arXiv:astro-ph/0510551].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 602-- G 031 as the exception among its absorption-edge detections: its edge is redshifted, apparently non-local, and accompanied by narrow neutral iron emission.

quotes.quote: With the exception of ESO 602-- G 031, the measured edge energies were all consistent with absorption from O vii (Figure fig:edge ). The only distinguishing characteristic in this sub-sample was that the average luminosity ratio between the blackbody and power law components is higher than it is for the entire sample (Figure fig:samp ). It is not reasonable to claim that this is a significant result given the small size of the sample, but it may be worth examining in studies with more objects. In the case of ESO 602-- G 031, the edge was slightly redshifted with respect to O vii . McKernan (2005) demonstrated that in some objects, redshifted absorption edges could be associated with absorption by hot local gas since the velocities measured in the absorber correspond to the recessional velocities of the AGN. Assuming O vii absorption, the measured velocity of the ionised absorber in ESO 602-- G 031 is cz = 21095^ +6490 _ 6085 , whereas the recessional velocity of the AGN is about 10200 . This implies that the detected feature in ESO 602-- G 031 is not local.

In seven objects (ESO 602-- G 031, Mrk 728, UGC 12139, ESO 244-- G 017, UGC 3973 (first epoch), ESO 15-- IG 011 (second epoch), CGCG 039--167) the line was consistent with narrow, unresolved emission from neutral iron.

gmw_00002930.name: HCG 4a
gmw_00002930.overview: HCG 4a is a low-luminosity type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0510551] that is also classified as a composite Seyfert and star-forming galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9911380]. It exhibits a strong soft X-ray excess [arXiv:astro-ph/0510551] and displays strong X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911380].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IRAS00317-2142 as a composite Seyfert/star-forming galaxy, finding luminous variable X-rays consistent with AGN emission while considering a Compton-thick interpretation.

quotes.quote: I present observations of IRAS00317-2142, the most luminous ( L_x 10^ 43 0.1-2 keV) of the 'composite' class of galaxies. This enigmatic class of objects presents narrow-emission line optical spectra classifying these galaxies as star-forming on the basis of the diagnostic emission line ratios; yet, the presence of weak H_ broad wings also suggests the presence of a weak or obscured AGN. The spectrum can be represented with a power-law with a photon index of =1.76 0.08 . Strong variability is detected (by about a factor of three) between the and 1-2 keV flux. These characteristics clearly suggest an AGN origin for the X-ray emission.
Finally, IRAS00317-2142 could be a heavily obscured (Compton thick) AGN like eg NGC1068.

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 4a as a low-luminosity type 2 AGN with a particularly strong soft excess and signs of a narrow line from highly ionized iron.

quotes.quote: Indeed, weak and strong soft excesses seem to be distributed amongst type 1 and type 2 (we will include type 1.8 and 1.9 in this group for discussion purposes) objects, with the low luminosity type 2 AGN (HCG 4a, NGC 5005, NGC 7331) possessing particularly strong soft excesses.

HCG 4a also showed indications of a narrow, unresolved emission line, but at E = 6.93^ +0.07 _ 0.14 , which can be attributed to completely ionised iron. This in itself is potentially interesting given the rather low luminosity observed in HCG 4a (see Table tab:cont ).
With the exception of HCG 4a and the two LINERs, the unabsorbed Seyfert 2s in our sample have 2-10 luminosities on the order of 10^ 42 .

gmw_00002931.name: II Zw 177
gmw_00002931.overview: II Zw 177 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1601.00503, arXiv:astro-ph/0510551] that exhibits a soft X-ray excess [arXiv:1601.00503]. Its ultraviolet flux is weakly variable and does not correlate with its X-ray emission [arXiv:1601.00503].

sources.summary: The paper studies II Zw 177 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, modeling its soft X-ray excess, correlated X-ray components, and weak, X-ray-uncorrelated UV variability.

quotes.quote: We study X-ray and UV emission from the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy II Zw 177 using a 137 long and another 13 short observation performed in 2012 and 2001, respectively. Both observations show soft X-ray excess emission contributing 76.9 4.9 in 2012 and 58.8 10.2 in 2001 in the 0.3-2 band. We find that both blurred reflection from an ionized disc and Comptonized disc emission describe the observed soft excess well. Time-resolved spectroscopy on scales of 20 reveals strong correlation between the soft excess and the powerlaw components.

The UV flux is found to be weakly variable at a level of F_ var 1 . However, the UV flux is not correlated to the total X-ray flux nor to the X-ray fluxes in the 0.3 2 and 2 10 bands. The UV flux is also not correlated to the soft excess or powerlaw X-ray components.

sources.summary: The paper flags II Zw 177 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 with a very broad, redshifted feature whose origin may be near the black hole or due to partial covering.

quotes.quote: Four objects in the sample (UGC 3973 (second epoch), Mrk 590, MCG--02-14-009 (see also Porquet 2005), II Zw 177) were fitted better if a broad Gaussian profile was considered rather than an intrinsically narrow profile (see Table tab:adds ).

The profile in the NLS1, II Zw 177, appeared tantalising. The feature was very broad, with 1 , and the best-fit line energy was redshifted from 6.4 , though not formally inconsistent with neutral iron (see Figure fig:line ). We reiterate that given the available statistics, absorption models (such as partial covering), which would introduce curvature in the high-energy spectrum, cannot be formally excluded.
The feature in the NLS1, II Zw 177, was very broad and an origin close to the central black hole, or partial covering, should be considered.

gmw_00002932.name: MCG--02-14-009
gmw_00002932.overview: MCG--02-14-009 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511387] classified as a bare Seyfert due to its minimal absorption [arXiv:1010.2080]. Its X-ray spectrum is reflection-dominated [arXiv:astro-ph/0511387] and exhibits a broad iron-line feature that may originate from the outer regions of its accretion disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0510551, arXiv:astro-ph/0511387].

sources.summary: The paper reports that MCG--02-14-009 is better fitted with a broad Gaussian feature and that its iron-line width points to outer accretion-disk emission.

quotes.quote: Four objects in the sample (UGC 3973 (second epoch), Mrk 590, MCG--02-14-009 (see also Porquet 2005), II Zw 177) were fitted better if a broad Gaussian profile was considered rather than an intrinsically narrow profile (see Table tab:adds ).

We note that in three objects: MCG--02-14-009, UGC 3973, and Mrk 590 (e.g. Figure fig:mrkln ), the full-width at half-maximum of the Gaussian profiles indicated velocities on the order of 10000 . For all three of these AGN, the widths of the iron lines are only about a factor of 2-4 greater then the velocities measured in the H emission line from the broad line region. This suggests that, at least in these cases, the iron emission could be originating from the outer regions of the accretion disc, where Doppler effects dominate relativistic effects.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first above-2-keV XMM-Newton observation of MCG-02-14-009, finding a broad asymmetric iron-line feature and a reflection-dominated spectrum implying emission close to a rapidly rotating black hole.

quotes.quote: I report here the analysis of a short XMM-Newton /EPIC observation (PN net time exposure 5 ks) of the Seyfert 1 MCG-02-14-009 (z=0.028). The present X-ray observation is the first X-ray observation above 2 keV of this object.

The spectrum is reflection dominated and this would indicate that the primary source in MCG-02-14-009 is located very close to the BH, where gravitational light bending effect is important (about 3--4 R _ g ), and that the BH may rapidly rotate. The discovery of this broad and asymmetrical feature (probably relativistic iron line) is of great interest since with XMM-Newton and Chandra only few relativistic iron line have been confirmed or discovered: e.g., MCG-6-30-15 Wilms01 , MCG-5-23-16 Dewangan03 , Mrk 335 Gondoin02 , and Q0056-363 PR03 .

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG-02-14-009 as a bare Seyfert with little absorption impact and only weak improvement from a broad Gaussian component.

quotes.quote: We methodically model the broad-band Suzaku spectra of a small sample of six 'bare' Seyfert galaxies: Ark 120, Fairall 9, MCG-02-14-009, Mrk 335, NGC 7469 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654.
Therefore MCG-02-14-009 can be considered as 'bare' for the purposes of this paper.
Only in MCG-02-14-009 does the addition of a broad Gaussian not make a particularly significant improvement ( ^ 2 3 ).

gmw_00002933.name: NGC 5101
gmw_00002933.overview: NGC 5101 is a barred galaxy classified as a LINER based on its central emission lines [arXiv:1204.6212, arXiv:1503.06550]. It features a strong, ansae-type bar [arXiv:0808.1841, arXiv:2304.00515], a barlens region [arXiv:1405.6726], an inner pseudo-ring [arXiv:2304.00515], and a lopsided outer disk with stellar and neutral hydrogen rings [arXiv:1503.06550, arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 5101 with a later fixed-pattern-speed simulation, noting its stronger bar, both R1 and R2 rings, misaligned R2 ring, and more elliptical observed ring.

quotes.quote: From top to bottom Figure b2var.eps shows NGC 5701, and NGC 5101.

NGC 5101 is somewhat stronger with Q_T=0.19 , and displays both R1 and R2 rings. It is compared to a simulation 7.5 periods following bar growth. The R2 ring at this time is misaligned with the bar similar to the misalignment in the galaxy. The galaxy displays a more elliptical ring than that of our simulation.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 5101 with simulation gtr115, finding similar barlens regions and radial profiles whose thin-bar end is marked by a jump while barlens extent lacks a distinct feature.

quotes.quote: The right-hand panel of Fig. fig:NGC5101_gtr115_prof compares the radial projected surface density profiles along the direction of the major axis of the bar of gtr115 and NGC 5101. Their barlens regions look remarkably similar, although the former was not made specifically so as to model the latter. There is also a further point to note in this figure, which can also be seen in the radial profiles of Fig. fig:comp-morpho . The end of the thin bar component can be easily distinguished on these profiles because of a clear jump, or step, of the radial profile at this radius. On the contrary, the profile has no feature which could indicate the existence of a barlens or its extent.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 5101 has an ansae bar, inner pseudo-ring, outer stellar and HI rings, ordered HI kinematics, and enhanced HI/SFR at the oUHR.

quotes.quote: NGC 5101: The bottom right panels in Fig. fig:mosaic2 show the DSS optical image with the 1 contours overlaid on top and the 1 velocity map. This galaxy is observed to have a bright `ansae' type bar and inner pseudo-ring and outer stellar ring. A ring of 1 gas is observed to coincide with the outer stellar ring. The kinematic of the 1 gas is seen to be fairly ordered. Based on the resonance model analysis, we are only able to derive the location of the oUHR radius for this galaxy. From the optical image an outer ring is observed to be co-located at oUHR (pink ellipse) and a corresponding 1 ring is also observed.
