In terms of the surface density of the 1 gas and SFR, again an enhancement in both is observed at the location of the oUHR.

sources.summary: The pipeline paper uses NGC 5101 as a barred-galaxy decomposition example, showing that adding a bar changes bulge properties and noting its type II disk and lopsided outer disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 936 (Fig. ngc0936_eija ) and NGC 5101 (Fig. ngc5101_eija ) are good examples demonstrating the importance of preventing the bar from mixing with the bulge flux. Adding a bar component to a simple bulge/disk model drastically changes the obtained properties of the bulge (for NGC 936 B/T drops from 0.46 to 0.19; for NGC 5101 from 0.99 to 0.22). NGC 5101 has also a type II profile in the disk break/truncation classification associated to a broad outer ring (Laine et al. 2014).
Note that the outer disk of NGC 5101 is clearly lopsided (see the residual plot in Fig. ngc5101_eija ).

sources.summary: The paper makes NGC 5101 a diagnostic-classification exception, reclassifies it as a LINER, and notes all measured bar-position emission lies in the bulge.

quotes.quote: Studying the diagnostic diagrams for individual galaxies, we confirm the classification carried out by Veron-Cetty Veron (2006) with the exception of NGC 5101 and NGC 1433.
According to the emission line in the central region, we reclassify NGC 5101 as a LINER even if it was not classified in such a way in Veron-Cetty Veron (2006), but in agreement with Moiseev (2001).

For two galaxies (NGC 1169 and NGC 5101) all the measured nebular emission in the bar position is in the bulge zone where the scatter is larger than in the bar region and, therefore, errors are also larger.

gmw_00002934.name: NGC 6782
gmw_00002934.overview: NGC 6782 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0808.1841, arXiv:0806.0471] featuring a weak central bar [arXiv:0808.1841] and a star-forming nuclear ring that contains a nuclear bar [arXiv:1304.3529]. The galaxy also hosts a lemon-shaped, oval inner ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0309583] and an outer pseudoring structure [arXiv:0806.0471].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 6782 with stabilized R1R2 simulations, citing its inclination, weak bar, delayed R2 alignment, and reasonable match between its R1 structure and the model.

quotes.quote: Figure stbar.eps compares NGC 6782 and NGC 3504 with density distributions showing simulations that have stabilized and exhibit R1R2 morphology.

In Figure stbar.eps NGC 6782 and NGC 3504 are both compared to the eleventh frame of simulations with bars of strengths | _ tgrow |=0.085 and 0.145, respectively. Both simulations are shown 2 periods after bar growth. NGC 6782 is an (R')SB()0/a galaxy with an inclination of 56.0 ^ and Q_T=0.17 . Because of the weaker bar, the R2 ring in NGC 6782's comparison simulation remains misaligned with the bar for 6 bar periods following bar growth. The spiral-like structure evident during bar growth does not close to form R1 and R2 rings until 2 periods after bar growth, in the frame shown in Figure stbar.eps . There is a reasonable match between the R1 structure of NGC 6782 and that exhibited by our simulation.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 6782 has an inner ring and spiral mostly reproduced by R 1.3, but the model lacks the observed outer pseudoring.

quotes.quote: NGC 6782 : the inner ring and the large part of the spiral of this SBa ( T=1 ) galaxy are reproduced by a model giving R 1.3 (see Fig. modsel1 ). The main difference between the observed and modeled morphology is that the real galaxy has an R_1' outer pseudoring whereas the spiral in the model does not form the pseudoring. The Fourier phase method clearly overestimates the bar radius by setting it well outside the inner ring. This is possibly caused by the outer pseudoring that affects the phase angle of the Fourier decomposition.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6782 to illustrate nuclear bars inside star-forming rings, bar-end H II regions, and resonant interpretations of its inner and outer rings.

quotes.quote: The presence of a nuclear bar inside a star-forming nuclear ring (as, e.g., in NGC 6782) should produce gravity torques that could evolve the central regions (Shlosman et al.
H ii regions are seen to `bunch up' around the ends of the bar, as in NGC 6782.

Such a shape seems present in the inner ring of NGC 6782, and indeed Lin et al. (2008) interpreted the shape of this galaxy's inner ring in terms of an interaction between waves excited at the inner 4:1 resonance and waves excited at the inner Lindblad resonance, based on hydrodynamical simulations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6782 as an example of a barred spiral with a lemon-shaped oval inner ring whose isophotes peak on the bar major axis and lack minor-axis corners.

quotes.quote: We illustrate this latter type in Fig. rgal a with an image of NGC 6782. The isophotes peak on the major axis, indicated with arrows on the second outermost isophote, giving to the ring a cuspy or pointed oval morphology. There are no ``corners'' close to the minor axis of the bar, which would have given to the ring a diamond shape.

gmw_00002935.name: NGC 3504
gmw_00002935.overview: NGC 3504 is a double-barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0808.1841, arXiv:2104.07227] that forms a pair with NGC 3512 [arXiv:0709.1236]. It hosts a composite, starburst-dominated nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342] featuring a compact nuclear ring [arXiv:0709.1236, arXiv:2104.07227] and a high concentration of molecular gas [arXiv:0809.4177, arXiv:2106.13099].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3504 with NGC 2903, detecting emission mainly at the center and interpreting NGC 3504 as an older barred system with gas concentrated centrally.

quotes.quote: During May and August 2002, we observed two barred galaxies, namely NGC 2903 and NGC 3504, using the IRAM-30m telescope. NGC 2903 and NGC 3504 were observed in good weather conditions. The basic parameters of the NGC 2903 and NGC 3504 are given in the Table tab_n2903_n3504 . The main goal was to map the emission along the bar of each galaxy.

In NGC 3504, the emission is detected only at the center. The fraction of dense molecular gas mass in the center is about 15 . Comparison of the SFR with the predictions from numerical simulations suggest that NGC 2903 harbors a young type B bar with a strong inflow of gas toward the center whereas NGC 3504 has an older bar and has already passed the phase of inflow of gas toward the center.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3504 as a starburst nucleus galaxy paired with NGC 3512, with an intense nuclear starburst, circumnuclear clumps, compact HII ring, and high molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 3504 is the brightest galaxy in the optically selected catalogue of starburst galactic nuclei in . It forms a pair with NGC 3512. Various studies at different wavelengths confirmed that it harbours a very intense nuclear starburst 1989ApJ...346..126D,1990ApJ...364...77P . Infrared observations in the J and K bands reveal a ring with five discrete clumps of star formation with colours indicating ages of about 10 ^7 yr 1997AJ....114.1850E . The H emission from traces a compact ring structure with a radius of 2 (200 pc) around the nucleus where four separated HII regions can be identified.

From the H emission, these authors derive a global star formation rate for the circumnuclear region of 0.62 M _ yr ^ 1 , while from their CO observations, a molecular gas mass of 21 10 ^ 8 M _ inside a circle 2.7 Kpc in diameter is derived.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3504 as an R1R2 comparison galaxy with a stronger bar than NGC 6782, faster ring formation, R2 alignment, and weakening of the R1 ring.

quotes.quote: Figure stbar.eps compares NGC 6782 and NGC 3504 with density distributions showing simulations that have stabilized and exhibit R1R2 morphology.
In Figure stbar.eps NGC 6782 and NGC 3504 are both compared to the eleventh frame of simulations with bars of strengths | _ tgrow |=0.085 and 0.145, respectively.

NGC 3504 is classified as an (R)SAB(s)ab galaxy with an inclination of 53.4 ^ and Q_T=0.29 . NGC 3504 has a much stronger bar than NGC 6782. R1 and R2 rings are evident only 1 period after bar growth, and the R2 ring is aligned with the bar within 4 bar rotation periods following bar growth. As with NGC 4314 in Figure b2var.eps , the strong bar leads to a weakening of the R1 ring.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA CO data to show NGC 3504 is a gas-rich double-barred galaxy with an inner molecular gas bar, nuclear ring, inner spiral structures, and bar-driven radial motions.

quotes.quote: We present results obtained from ALMA CO (2--1) data of the double-barred galaxy NGC 3504. With three times higher angular resolution ( 0 8) than previous studies, our observations reveal an inner molecular gas bar, a nuclear ring, and four inner spiral arm-like structures in the central 1 kpc region. Furthermore, the CO emission is clearly aligned with the two dust lanes in the outer bar region, with differences in shape and intensity between them. The total molecular gas mass in the observed region ( 50 57 ) is estimated to be 3. 1 10^9 M _ , which is 17 per cent of the stellar mass. We used the Kinemetry package to fit the velocity field and found that circular motion strongly dominates at R= 0.3-0.8 kpc, but radial motion becomes important at R<0.3 kpc and R=1.0-2.5 kpc, which is expected due to the presence of the inner and outer bars.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3504 has a composite, starburst-dominated nucleus with weak Seyfert-like emission and no detected Liner component.

quotes.quote: NGC 3504 . This Sb galaxy (Sandage Bedke 1994) has a ``composite'' nucleus showing both non-thermal activity and recent star formation, the optical spectrum being dominated by the regions of stellar activity (Keel 1984). Ho et al. (1993a), who published line ratios for this object, suggested that it could be a ``transition'' between a \ region and a Liner, but Ho et al. (1997a) called it a \ region. Fitting our red spectrum with a single set of Gaussians does not give a satisfactory fit and indicates the presence of weak broad wings in the \ lines; two sets of Gaussians are needed, revealing the presence of a weak Seyfert-like nebulosity with \ = 1.87. The blue spectrum was also fitted by two sets of Gaussians satisfying, respectively, \ = 0.1 and \ = 10.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3504 as a barred galaxy with an outer ring whose stellar-particle model favors R 1.2, while gas and Fourier methods fit worse.

quotes.quote: NGC 3504 : this is a SBab ( T=2 ) galaxy with an outer ring. The ring and the extent of the bar is best reproduced with a simulation of stellar test particles giving R 1.2 (see Fig. modsel1 ). In the gas component the same pattern speed is favored, but the fit is worse because the outer ring does not form. The outer pseudoring in this galaxy causes the Fourier phase angle criterion to work incorrectly, giving too high value for bar length.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3504 among observed spiral galaxies and reports that its R3+R4 circumnuclear region has the highest oxygen abundance and H luminosity.

quotes.quote: The region with the highest oxygen abundance is R3+R4 in NGC 3504, 12+log(O/H) = 8.85, about 1.5 solar if the solar oxygen abundance is set at the value derived by AGS05 , 12+log(O/H) _ = 8.66 0.05.
For 12 CNSFRs in the galaxies NGC 2903, NGC 3351 and NGC 3504 chemical abundances of O, N and S were derived from the lower resolution spectra.
The region with the largest H luminosity is R3+R4 in NGC 3504, for which a value of 2.02 10 ^ 40 is measured.

sources.summary: NGC 3504 is treated as a barred galaxy with nuclear and bar H II regions, asymmetric high molecular gas, and one of the sample highest SFE values.

quotes.quote: According to the catalogue of quasars and active galactic nuclei by 2010A&A...518A..10V , NGC 4593 is classified as Seyfert 1.0, NGC 4548 is a Seyfert 3.0 or hosts a low-ionisation nuclear emission-line region (LINER), and NGC 6217 and NGC 3504 harbour nuclear H ii regions.

PLOTS_ALLTOGETHER_A ); in the case of NGC 3504 _ mol goes beyond 100 M_ pc ^ 2 and is rather asymmetric, which is likely explained by the efficiency of these strong bars in sweeping the disk gas and enhancing its central concentration e.g. and references therein 1993RPPh...56..173S .

NGC 3504 and NGC 4593 are the galaxies in the sample with the highest SFE values (even larger than 1 Gyr ^ 1 ); we note, however, that the measurement in the central pointings may be affected to some extent by AGN contamination (x symbols in Figs.

gmw_00002936.name: SDP.9
gmw_00002936.overview: SDP.9 is a strongly lensed high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1709.10427, arXiv:1801.01831] that forms stars at a rate of 366 solar masses per year [arXiv:1709.10427]. It contains a compact far-infrared morphology [arXiv:2207.06967] and hosts a dust-obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1311.5898, arXiv:2207.06967].

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs SDP.9 as a lensed high-redshift galaxy with a compact near-IR component, substantial stellar and gas masses, vigorous star formation, and obscured X-ray nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: In the current paper we present the data, model and analysis in the X-ray and mm bands for two strongly lensed galaxies, SDP.9 (HATLAS J090740.0-004200) and SDP.11 (HATLAS J091043.1-000322) that we selected in the Herschel-ATLAS catalogues as having an excess emission in the mid-IR regime at redshift 1.5 , suggesting the presence of a nuclear activity in the early stages of galaxy formation.

In the near-IR SDP.9 consists of a dominant emitting region of 1 kpc in size, and a smaller and fainter one separated by a few kpc responsible for the fainter structure observed in the 0.71 arcsec radius Einstein ring (Dye et al. 2014). By the SED fitting, Negrello et al. (2014) estimated that the background source has a mass in stars M 7.1 10^ 10 M_ . Lupu et al. (2012) estimated a comparable mass in molecular gas, M_ gas 3.4 10^ 10 M_ on the basis of CO emission. The gas fraction is f_ gas = 0.32 , and the galaxy forms stars at a rate of 366 \ M_ /yr .

Fitting SDP.9 spectrum with a powerlaw provides =1.1 1.0 , suggestive of obscuration. If we fix to 1.8, as typically observed in AGN and quasars (e.g., Piconcelli et al. 2005), we obtain a column density of 5.2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 at the source redshift (3 upper limit of 3.3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ). The observed 2--10 keV flux is 3.6 10^ 15 , and the derived, apparent rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity (corrected for the absorption but not for the magnification) is 3.7 10^ 43 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SDP.9 as a gravitational lensing system with a foreground elliptical lens, a revealed near-infrared ring, optical redshift information, and evidence for a dust-obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: SDP.9 has an optical spectroscopic redshift z_ spec =0.613 recently obtained with the Gemini-South telescope Buss13 . A summary of available photometric and spectroscopic information is given in Table tab:phot_param . ciccio According to Fig. fig:HST_images1 and Fig. fig:HST_images2 (left panels), the HST data alone strongly support the idea of a gravitational lensing event in three of the five targets, namely SDP.9, SDP.11 and SDP.17, through the detection of a diffuse ring-like structure around a central elliptical galaxy.

SDP.9 . At wavelengths 1 m the emission is dominated by the foreground galaxy. The flux density ratio between the source and the lens increases from 0.08 to 0.2 going from 1.1 m to 1.6 m, so that we expect the H and Ks VIKING photometry to carry significant contributions from both the lens and the background source.

There is a clear excess at mid-IR wavelengths that may be due to emission from a dusty torus around an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). In fact, the presence of a dust-obscured AGN in SDP.9 is suggested by the analysis of on the H _ 2 O(2 _ 02 −1 _ 11 )/CO(8-7) and I(H _ 2 O)/L _ FIR ratios.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HATLASJ090740.0 004200 as SDP.9, a well-studied H-ATLAS lensed galaxy whose SMA lens model agrees with multiwavelength studies but differs from B13 in axis ratio.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ090740.0 004200 : This is one of the first five lensed galaxies discovered in H ATLAS 2010Sci...330..800N , and is also known as SDP.9. High-resolution observations at different wavelengths are available for this system, from the near-IR with HST /WFC3 2014MNRAS.440.1999N , to sub-mm with NOEMA 2017arXiv170105901O or with ALMA 2017ApJ...843L..35W , to the X-ray band with Chandra 2017arXiv170910427M . The results of our lens modelling of the SMA data are consistent with those obtained by other groups at different wavelengths e.g. 2014MNRAS.440.2013D, 2017arXiv170910427M . However, B13 found a significantly lower lens axis-ratio compared to our estimate ( q_ L =0.50 versus q_ L =0.75 ).

sources.summary: The paper contrasts SDP.9 with the new sample, noting its high HCN/CO ratio, compact FIR morphology, Chandra-detected buried AGN, and AGN-like excited CO SLED.

quotes.quote: The two extant HCN(1--0) detections in DSFGs seem to conform to this trend, with HCN/CO ratios of 0.11 (J16359) and 0.3 (SDP.9), higher than most ULIRGs.

Namely, SDP.9 - the source with the highest HCN/CO ratio - has a very compact FIR morphology ( r_ 1/2 300 pc), with _ FIR almost 1 dex higher than any other source in our sample. Moreover, SDP.9 and SDP.11 are detected in the Chandra X-ray imaging, indicating a powerful buried AGN Massardi2018 ; SDP.9 also shows an unusually excited CO SLED, which is more consistent with AGN hosts than purely star-forming galaxies Oteo2017 .
It is therefore plausible that the large HCN/CO ratio in SDP.9 is driven by the buried AGN rather than by its high dense-gas fraction.

gmw_00002937.name: SDP.11
gmw_00002937.overview: SDP.11 is a lensed high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1709.10427] that appears as an elongated Einstein ring [arXiv:1801.01831] with a complex, knotty near-infrared structure [arXiv:1709.10427]. The system exhibits high star formation [arXiv:1709.10427] and a twin-peaked line profile that likely indicates merger activity [arXiv:1807.07080].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.11 as a lensed high-redshift galaxy with knotty near-IR structure, high star-formation and stellar mass, significant gas mass, and obscured X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: SDP.11 is reconstructed, in the near-IR, in a complex combination of several knots distributed within a region of a few kpc, which are responsible for the small-scale structure observed in the 0.84 arcsec radius Einstein ring (Dye et al. 2014). According to the SED fitting, compared to SDP.9, the source has a higher star formation rate, SFR =650 \ M_ /yr , and a higher mass in stars M 1.9 10^ 11 M_ . Lupu et al. (2012) CO observations indicate a comparable gas mass M_ gas 3.0 10^ 10 M_ and hence a lower gas fraction, f_ gas = 0.14 .

For SDP.11, we combined the photons of the two observations into a single spectrum. As for SDP.9, a powerlaw model provides an apparently flat spectrum, with =1.0 0.9 . If =1.8 was assumed, the column density would be 3.2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 (3 upper limit of 2.4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ). The observed 2--10 keV flux is 1.2 10^ 14 , and the apparent rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity (again corrected for the absorption but not for the magnification) is 1.2 10^ 44 . Once corrected for the magnification factors, the intrinsic X-ray luminosities are 5.9 10^ 42 \ and 1.5 10^ 43 \ for SDP.9 and SDP.11, respectively. We can estimate the contribution from star-formation processes to the 2--10 keV luminosities assuming the relation reported in Ranalli et al. (2003) and the SFRs obtained from SED fitting ( 2). The derived values are 1.9 10^ 42 \ (SDP.9) and 3.3 10^ 42 \ (SDP.11), i.e., the X-ray intrinsic luminosities are a factor 3--4.5 higher than those possibly associated with stellar processes.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HATLASJ091043.1 000321 as SDP.11, with HST imaging showing an elongated Einstein ring; its lens model includes external shear and broadly agrees with previous radius estimates.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ091043.1 000321 : This is SDP.11, another of the first lensed galaxies discovered in H ATLAS 2010Sci...330..800N . HST /WFC3 imaging data at 1.1 reveals an elongated Einstein ring 2014MNRAS.440.1999N , hinting to the effect of an external shear possibly associated with a nearby edge-on galaxy. In fact, introduced an external shear in their lens modelling of this system, which they constrained to have strength 0.23 . We also account for an external shear in our analysis.
Our results are consistent with those of Dye et al. They also agree with the Einstein radius estimated by B13, although our lens is significantly more elongated and has a higher rotation angle.

sources.summary: The paper follows up SDP.11 with APEX, finds line flux consistent with Herschel data, and resolves a two-component line profile consistent with CO and likely merger activity.

quotes.quote: APEX observations and data reduction To check the \ flux and to resolve the line profiles of galaxies detected in our \ observations, we obtained ground-based observations of SDP.11 and NA.v1.186 with the 12-m Atacama Pathfinder EXperiment (APEX) telescope on the Chajnantor Plateau in Chile, in good (pwv < 0.6 mm) weather conditions.

We also compare the \ spectra of SDP.11 and NA.v1.186 obtained with \ and those observed with APEX, which are shown in Fig. fig:apex . For SDP.11, the velocity-integrated \ flux obtained with APEX is 265 65 Jy , fully consistent with our \ detection, 269 30 Jy . This is also close to the \ flux measured with the second-generation z (Redshift) and Early Universe Spectrometer (ZEUS-2) on APEX Ferkinhoff2014 . We note that the \ line profile of SDP.11 is resolved into two velocity components, separated by 300 , which were not resolved by either the \ or ZEUS-2 observations. We also overlay the CO J=4 3 spectrum with the \ lines, and find that the twin-peaked profile of the APEX \ line is consistent with CO (also for HCN) detections in Oteo2017 , likely indicative of a merger.

sources.summary: The paper compares two z=2 measurements of H-ATLAS J091043.0-000322, notes discrepant Herschel and ALMA CII luminosities, and places the galaxy between measurements and simulations.

quotes.quote: At z=2 , we only have two measurements for one galaxy: H-ATLAS J091043.0-000322 . These two measurements come from Herschel observations following different data reduction methods (i.e. \ pipeline versions) in 2018MNRAS.481...59Z and 2018ApJ...867..140L . ALMA observations presented by 2018ApJ...867..140L show that the [ C II ] luminosity is around half of the Herschel measurement (right data point on the panel), but this difference cannot be fully explained. Therefore, we assume that H-ATLAS J091043.0-000322 lies somewhere between the two measurements presented in the z=2 panel, agreeing with the predictions from Recal-L0025N0752 .

For example, calibration pipelines or the use of different instruments may affect the comparison, as shown by 2018ApJ...867..140L in the case of H-ATLAS J091043.0-000322 , discussed in Sect. subsec:comobs .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDP.11 as the brightest H-ATLAS/SDP lens candidate, showing an Einstein ring with substructure and a foreground elliptical or lenticular lens model.

quotes.quote: SDP.11 . This is the 500 m brightest lens candidate selected in the H-ATLAS/SDP field (see Table tab:phot_param ) and even without the subtraction of the foreground galaxy it is clear that the background source is lensed into an Einstein ring. The ring is particularly elongated with a significant amount of substructure, which suggests the presence of several clumps of rest-frame UV/optical emission in the source plane (D13), consistent with what was found for the H-ATLAS lensed galaxy presented in . The foreground galaxy required two S e rsic profiles in each of the bands, where one profile is approximately an exponential disk ( n_ s 1 ) and the other profile has index n_ s = 2.8 at 1.1 m and n_ s = 4.5 and 1.6 m. Also in this case the light profile is indicative of an elliptical/lenticular galaxy.

gmw_00002938.name: UDF3
gmw_00002938.overview: The high-redshift galaxy UDF3 is identified as a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.10091], though its classification remains ambiguous [arXiv:1607.07710]. Its optical emission line ratios place it within the distribution of star-forming galaxies [arXiv:2009.10091], and its soft-band X-ray detection suggests the X-ray emission could originate solely from star formation [arXiv:1607.07710].

sources.summary: The paper estimates UDF3's Balmer-based SFR, finds it below the total UV-plus-far-IR SFR, and notes its optical line ratios look star-forming despite a radio-loud AGN label.

quotes.quote: We estimated dust-corrected H SFRs (SFR(H )) for UDF3 and UDF4, which have coverage of both H and H .

We take ``total" SFRs from dunlop2017 (SFR _ tot ) as the sum of UV (uncorrected for dust) and far-IR SFRs, and compare them with dust-corrected SFR(H ) for UDF3 and UDF4 (shown in Table tab:physical ). We find that dust-corrected SFR(H ) is a factor of 3.7 ^ +2.7 _ 2.0 and 8.2 ^ +13.0 _ 5.5 lower than SFR _ tot , respectively, for UDF3 and UDF4, although, because of the large error bars on both sets of measurements, the difference is not highly significant.

UDF3 is identified as a radio-loud AGN in dunlop2017 , yet its [ OIII ] 5007 /H and [ NII ] 6584 /H ratios (Table tab:mosfiremeas ) place it well within the distribution of z 2 MOSDEF star-forming galaxies in the [ OIII ] 5007 /H vs. [ NII ] 6584 /H ``BPT" diagram shapley2019 .

sources.summary: The paper flags UDF3 as an exception in star-formation surface-density alignment, reports only soft-band X-ray detection, and treats its AGN classification as ambiguous.

quotes.quote: The spatially-resolved \ for 9 of 11 galaxies peaks within 1.1 kpc of the stellar-mass distribution (with the exception of UDF3 and 7; Table 3), and all have \ in the range of 0.4 - 18 \ (median \ = 2.5 ), the higher end of which is comparable to local starbursts Kennicutt98 .
Two among the X-ray detected SFGs (UDF3 and 11) are only detected in the soft X-ray band ( 0.5-2 keV), suggesting that their X-ray emission could originate solely from star formation.

Omitting the only ambiguous case of UDF3 from the AGN sub-sample, the AGN fraction (5 of 11 galaxies) is still higher than typically reported e.g., Xue10 at these redshifts, but not unexpected since AGN activity is known to depend on both stellar mass and gas mass as evident by its clear dependence on star formation of its host even on a galaxy-wide scale e.g., Silverman09, Brusa09 .

gmw_00002939.name: UDF4
gmw_00002939.overview: UDF4 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2 [arXiv:2009.10091]. Its stellar mass is comparable to the most massive galaxies in the MOSDEF sample, and it has a dust-corrected hydrogen-alpha star formation rate that is lower than its total ultraviolet-plus-far-infrared star formation rate [arXiv:2009.10091].

sources.summary: The paper estimates UDF4's Balmer-based SFR, compares it with total UV-plus-far-IR SFR, and notes that UDF4 overlaps the most massive MOSDEF stack in stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We estimated dust-corrected H SFRs (SFR(H )) for UDF3 and UDF4, which have coverage of both H and H .

We take ``total" SFRs from dunlop2017 (SFR _ tot ) as the sum of UV (uncorrected for dust) and far-IR SFRs, and compare them with dust-corrected SFR(H ) for UDF3 and UDF4 (shown in Table tab:physical ). We find that dust-corrected SFR(H ) is a factor of 3.7 ^ +2.7 _ 2.0 and 8.2 ^ +13.0 _ 5.5 lower than SFR _ tot , respectively, for UDF3 and UDF4, although, because of the large error bars on both sets of measurements, the difference is not highly significant.

The ALMA HUDF points lie at the massive end of the MOSDEF sample, with only UDF4 overlapping the most massive MOSDEF stack in M _* , and scatter around the best-fit linear MZR relation. Given the observed redshift evolution in the MZR, we restrict the comparison to UDF3, UDF4, and UDF11, all at z 2 .

gmw_00002940.name: UDF6
gmw_00002940.overview: UDF6 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2 [arXiv:2009.10091]. Its molecular gas mass has been estimated using higher-order CO transitions, which serve as a proxy for its overall gas mass [arXiv:2009.10091].

sources.summary: The paper treats UDF6's H SFR as a lower limit because H is not covered, and uses higher-order CO transitions to estimate its molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: H is not covered for UDF6 and UDF11, so the uncorrected H SFR is listed as a lower limit. Gas-phase oxygen abundance based on the linear N2 indicator pettini2004 . d ALMA 1.2 mm and 3.0 mm fluxes from the ASPECS survey aravena2020 . e Dust mass in units of 10^8 M_ , based on either S_ 1.2 or S_ 3.0 , assuming an optically-thin modified blackbody function, and dust temperature, T_ dust =35 5 K. f Molecular gas mass in units of 10^ 10 M_ . M_ mol for UDF3 and UDF4 are taken from riechers2020 , based on CO(1-0) luminosities, while those for UDF6 and UDF11 come from aravena2020 , based on higher-order CO transitions. g Dust-to-gas ratio in units of 10^ 3 , defined as the ratio between either M_ dust,1.2 or M_ dust,3.0 and M_ mol .

We further assume that the gas mass is well-approximated by the molecular gas mass, as is standard for z 2 star-forming galaxies tacconi2018 , and take M_ mol from the literature. riechers2020 present M_ mol for UDF3 and UDF4 based on CO(1-0) measurements, while aravena2020 present M_ mol for UDF6 and UDF11 based on higher-order CO transitions, using the average CO excitation properties of the ASPECS sample boogaard2020 to convert to CO(1-0).

gmw_00002941.name: UDF11
gmw_00002941.overview: UDF11 is a high-redshift galaxy at z~2 that contains a radio-active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1607.07710, arXiv:2009.10091]. Its star-formation concentrations are located approximately 1 kiloparsec away from its stellar-mass barycenter [arXiv:1607.07710].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF11 as the only multi-component VLA source, soft-band X-ray detected yet radio enhanced like an AGN, with star formation near the AGN and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: All VLA sources except UDF11 and all ALMA sources are well described with a single component.

Two among the X-ray detected SFGs (UDF3 and 11) are only detected in the soft X-ray band ( 0.5-2 keV), suggesting that their X-ray emission could originate solely from star formation. However, UDF11 also has radio emission enhanced by more than twice the level associated with star formation (Figure 2 Left; this level of radio enhancement is 15 the scatter around the emission level implied by the far-IR/radio correlation, sec:FRC ) that suggests a presence of radio AGN.

For UDF7, 11, and 13 where the radio flux enhancement allows pinpointing the AGN location using the VLA images, we find that the AGN are within 0.5 kpc of the barycenter of star formation of their host galaxies as measured from the ALMA images (an upper limit of given by our ability to pinpoint AGN and star formation given their S/N). For UDF11 and 13, the star-formation concentrations are 1 kpc from the stellar-mass barycenters, whereas UDF7 is notable for these activities being 6 kpc away from the dominant stellar-mass concentration, a possible consequence of galaxy interactions.

sources.summary: The paper treats UDF11's H SFR as a lower limit, includes it in the z~2 metallicity comparison, and uses CO-based molecular gas estimates.

quotes.quote: H is not covered for UDF6 and UDF11, so the uncorrected H SFR is listed as a lower limit. Gas-phase oxygen abundance based on the linear N2 indicator pettini2004 . d ALMA 1.2 mm and 3.0 mm fluxes from the ASPECS survey aravena2020 . e Dust mass in units of 10^8 M_ , based on either S_ 1.2 or S_ 3.0 , assuming an optically-thin modified blackbody function, and dust temperature, T_ dust =35 5 K. f Molecular gas mass in units of 10^ 10 M_ . M_ mol for UDF3 and UDF4 are taken from riechers2020 , based on CO(1-0) luminosities, while those for UDF6 and UDF11 come from aravena2020 , based on higher-order CO transitions. g Dust-to-gas ratio in units of 10^ 3 , defined as the ratio between either M_ dust,1.2 or M_ dust,3.0 and M_ mol .

Given the observed redshift evolution in the MZR, we restrict the comparison to UDF3, UDF4, and UDF11, all at z 2 . According to the ``Fundamental Metallicity Relation" FMR; mannucci2010,andrews2013 discovered among z 0 galaxies and describing the dependence of metallicity on both M _* and SFR, galaxies with higher than-average SFR at fixed M _* , lie at lower than-average metallicity at fixed M _* . sanders2018 demonstrated that the FMR holds in this sense for z 2 MOSDEF galaxies. In dunlop2017 , UDF3, UDF4, and UDF11 are shown to have bolometric SFR _ tot that place them above the SFR vs. M _* main sequence on average, yet we observe no corresponding average offset below the MZR.

We further assume that the gas mass is well-approximated by the molecular gas mass, as is standard for z 2 star-forming galaxies tacconi2018 , and take M_ mol from the literature. riechers2020 present M_ mol for UDF3 and UDF4 based on CO(1-0) measurements, while aravena2020 present M_ mol for UDF6 and UDF11 based on higher-order CO transitions, using the average CO excitation properties of the ASPECS sample boogaard2020 to convert to CO(1-0).

gmw_00002942.name: NGC 5216
gmw_00002942.overview: NGC 5216 is an interacting elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1009.0219, arXiv:astro-ph/0701452] classified as a Type II shell galaxy [arXiv:2103.05006] with a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1009.0219]. It features outer shells and a tidal tail [arXiv:1009.0219] that facilitates ongoing mass transfer from its companion, NGC 5218 [arXiv:2103.05006].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5216 as the elliptical member of Arp 104, lying on the red sequence with an R^1/4 profile, faint shells, a tidal tail, and a LINER nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 5216 The structural classification of NGC 5216 as an elliptical galaxy is supported by its mean radial brightness profile which is well fit by an R^ 1/4 law from a radii of 3 to 30 arcsec, i.e. to about 6 kpc (Figure 4 where an R ^ 1/4 profile will be a line). Beyond this the presence of shells and the tidal tail produce deviations in the profile, even though the overall trend remains close to an R ^ 1/4 intensity scaling out to the last reliable data point located slightly beyond = 18 kpc.

Beyond the possibly dusty nucleus with its emission lines, the g color of NGC 5216 also is consistent with a typical elliptical galaxy and it then is not surprising to find that this system lies on the red sequence. Its LINER nuclear spectrum (Zhao et al. 2006) also is a relatively common feature in an elliptical galaxy.

However, for radii of R > 6 kpc, the brightness profile of NGC 5216 flattens, and our results are consistent with those of Roche (2006). In this region shells exist which can be seen in the WIYN 3.5-m images (Figure 5) along with the presence of the southern tidal tail that extends across the galaxy (Figure 3). The tidal tail is the only region with confirmed recent star formation as indicated by its blue colors. The outer shells in NGC 5216 unfortunately are too faint, _r 25 mag arcsec ^ 2 , for us to obtain reliable color measurements from our data, although such measurements would be useful in constraining when the shells formed in NGC 5216 (e.g., McGaugh Bothun 1990).

sources.summary: NGC 5216 is the early-type southern companion in Arp 104, with an E0 pec classification, large projected diameter, and tidal features whose colors differ from both galaxy centers.

quotes.quote: Arp 104, shown in Fig. a104hi_mom0.fig , consists of two interacting galaxies, see NED see http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ : the southern galaxy, NGC 5216, is an E0 pec ( 2 5 1 5 ) and the northern galaxy, NGC 5218, is an SB(s)b? pec ( 1 8 1 3 ). At an assumed distance of 41.1 Mpc (from a recessional velocity of 2880 , and using a Hubble constant of 70 Mpc ^ 1 ), the projected major axis diameter ( D_ 25 ) is 29.9 kpc ( 2 5 ) for NGC 5216 and 21.5 kpc ( 1 8 ) for NGC 5218. The projected separation of the two galaxies is 49.1 kpc. 1990AJ.....99..497S have conducted a multicolour photometric study of the optical tidal features of a sample of interacting galaxies, including Arp 104. They found the tidal features of Arp 104 are bluer than the central regions of both NGC 5218 and NGC 5216, but similar to the colours of the outer disk in NGC 5218.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5216 as a Type II shell galaxy undergoing mass transfer from NGC 5218, with shells possibly from that interaction or another merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 5216 is a Type II shell galaxy with at least four distinct shells visible in the Legacy Surveys image, Fig. fig:ls7 . There is an on-going mass transfer from a bluer companion galaxy -- a thin blue tail stretches across the whole body of NGC 5216 and extends about 3 north, connecting to the strongly disturbed NGC 5218. The shells of NGC 5216 can originate from a previous interaction with the companion galaxy NGC 5218 or another past merger event.

gmw_00002943.name: SPRC-260
gmw_00002943.overview: SPRC-260 is a classic polar-ring galaxy with a polar component inclined at an angle of 89 degrees relative to its central galaxy [arXiv:1211.1307]. The galaxy features a tightly wound, two-arm spiral structure that transforms into a lopsided system [arXiv:1211.1307].

sources.summary: The paper treats SPRC-260 as a classic polar-ring galaxy, comparing observed velocity fields with simulations and favoring a mildly triaxial halo while its polar component forms a lopsided structure.

quotes.quote: t Spectroscopic observations at the 6-m telescope of the SAO RAS were used to study two polar ring galaxies from the catalogue by moisav2011 : SPRC-7 and SPRC-260. SPRC-7 is an inclined system with the relative angle i = 70^ towards the central galaxy, meanwhile SPRC-260 is a classic polar case with i = 89^ . The observations were carried out with the multi-mode focal reducers SCORPIO AM05 and SCORPIO-2 AM12 in the scanning Fabry-Perot interferometer and long-slit spectroscopic modes.

There is a 2-arm tightly wound spiral structure formation in SPRC-260, which transforms to a lopsided system. For SPRC-7, multiple arms formed with a large pitch angle (fig. 4). The best fits (minima of ^2_ ring distributions) pointed out the oblate character of the DM halo potential distribution in the polar plane for both galaxies: s = 0.8 for SPRC-7 and s=0.95 for SPRC-260. For the halo density distribution q and s should be significantly smaller. We propose an oblate DM halo potential for SPRC-7 with the semiaxis relations: s=0.8 , q=1 and a triaxial halo for SPRC-260: s=0.95 , q=1.1 .

gmw_00002944.name: J0952+0114
gmw_00002944.overview: J0952+0114 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 3.02 [arXiv:1906.06347]. It is surrounded by a giant Lyman-alpha nebula, extended carbon IV and helium II emission, and a central clumpy outflow [arXiv:1906.06347].

sources.summary: The paper observes the z=3.02 quasar J0952+0114 with MUSE, finding a giant Ly nebula, extended C IV and He II emission, and evidence for a central clumpy outflow.

quotes.quote: Johnson Department of Astrophysical Science, 4 Ivy Lane, Princeton University, Princeton, NJ 08644, USA The Observatories of the Carnegie Institution for Science, 813 Santa Barbara Street, Pasadena, CA 91101, USA Themiya Nanayakkara Leiden Observatory, Leiden University, PO Box 9513, NL-2300 RA Leiden, the Netherlands The well-known quasar SDSS J095253.83+011421.9 (J0952+0114) at z = 3.02 has one of the most peculiar spectra discovered so far, showing the presence of narrow Ly and broad metal emission lines. Although recent studies have suggested that a Proximate Damped Ly system (PDLA) causes this peculiar spectrum, the origin of the gas associated with the PDLA is unknown. Here we report the results of MUSE observations that reveal a new giant ( 100 physical kpc) Lyman nebula. The detailed analysis of the Ly velocity, velocity dispersion, and surface brightness profiles suggests that the J0952+0114 Ly nebula shares similar properties of other QSO nebulae previously detected with MUSE, implying that the PDLA in J0952+0144 is covering only a small fraction of the QSO emission solid angle. We also detected bright and spectrally narrow C 4 1550 and He 2 1640 extended emission around J0952+0114 with velocity centroids similar to the peak of the extended and central narrow Ly emission.

The presence of a peculiarly bright, unresolved, and relatively broad He 2 1640 emission in the central region at exactly the same PDLA redshift hints at the possibility that the PDLA originates in a clumpy outflow with a bulk velocity of about 500 km s ^ 1 . The smaller velocity dispersion of the large scale Ly emission suggests that the high-speed outflow is confined to the central region. Lastly, the derived spatially resolved He 2 /Ly and C 4 /Ly maps show a positive gradient with the distance to the QSO hinting at a non-homogeneous ionization parameter distribution.

gmw_00002945.name: Beret
gmw_00002945.overview: The Beret is a single-image lensed galaxy located at a redshift of 1.16 [arXiv:2208.10450]. It hosts a population of 28 detected clumps spanning star-forming regions to star clusters, with ages ranging from a few to several hundreds of millions of years [arXiv:2208.10450].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Beret as a z=1.16 single-image lensed galaxy with high magnification and 28 detected clumps spanning star-forming-region to star-cluster scales.

quotes.quote: The Beret galaxy Galaxy I7, at redshift 1.16, is a single image system, that was named the Beret, because of its morphological resemblance to a cap (see green inset in Figure fig:im_cluster ). The lens model presents for this galaxy a grazing critical curve providing high magnification, however a careful look a the lensing symmetry does not show obvious sign of multiple images. The nearby cluster member galaxy acts as a secondary lens, offering an additional boost of amplification (reaching =24 for the most magnified clump).

It hosts a populous cluster/clump population of 28 detected systems with sizes from 100 down to upper limits of a few parsec, i.e. between star-forming region and star cluster ranges (see top panel of Figure fig:bound_ysc ). The pseudo B-V versus V-I color-color diagram (top panel of Figure fig:CCDs ) show clumps distributed along the evolutionary tracks. Differential extinction can account for the colors distributions, with ages covering a large range from a few to several hundreds of Myr. Masses in the system range between 10^5 and 10^6 , with the brightest clump (probably a proto-bulge structure) being the most massive, a few times 10^8 (see top panels of Figure fig:fig_pos_clumps ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 9483 as one of three low-redshift SMACS galaxies with strong 1.257 micron emission, shock-dominated line ratios, and notable H2 lines.

quotes.quote: Turning now to the more interesting lines, the only regularly detected line is 1.257 which we find in 3042, 9239, and 9483.

The immediate observation we can make is that the three SMACS galaxies with clearly detected 1.257 all fall clearly in the shock dominated region. They lie towards the high B field, low velocity region of the shown grid.

In the present sample only 9483 shows strong \ lines and even then only three: \ 1-0 S(3), \ 1-0 S(2) and \ 1-0 S(1). \ 1-0 S(3) is contaminated by 1.955 , leaving only the \ 1-0 S(2)/ \ 1-0 S(1) ratio as a diagnostic. This ratio has a value of 0.68 0.47

gmw_00002946.name: Sparkler
gmw_00002946.overview: The Sparkler is a strongly lensed galaxy at redshift z=1.378 [arXiv:2212.05078, arXiv:2402.08725] that is a possible progenitor of a Milky Way-mass galaxy [arXiv:2212.05078]. It hosts a population of compact globular cluster candidates [arXiv:2212.07366, arXiv:2306.11814] and likely underwent a merger 100 to 500 million years ago [arXiv:2208.10450].

sources.summary: The paper interprets the Sparkler as a gravitationally lensed z=1.378 galaxy with rapid early chemical enrichment, metal-rich globular-cluster candidates, and possible MW-like mass growth.

quotes.quote: The Sparkler galaxy, which has a current estimated stellar mass of 10 ^9 M _ , is compared to the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC), the disrupted dwarf galaxy Gaia--Enceladus and the Milky Way (MW). The Sparkler galaxy appears to have undergone very rapid chemical enrichment in the first few hundred Myr after formation, with its GC candidates similar to those of the MW's metal-rich subpopulation. We also compare the Sparkler to theoretical AMRs and formation ages from the E-MOSAICS simulation, finding the early formation age of its GCs to be in some tension with these predictions for MW-like galaxies. The metallicity of the Sparkler's star forming regions are more akin to a galaxy of stellar mass 10 ^ 10.5 M _ , i.e. at the top end of the expected mass growth over 9.1 Gyr of cosmic time. We conclude that the Sparkler galaxy may represent a progenitor of a MW-like galaxy, even including the ongoing accretion of a satellite galaxy. galaxies: dwarf -- galaxies: star clusters -- cosmology: galaxy evolution Globular clusters (GCs) are perhaps the oldest stellar systems in the Universe, formed only a few hundred Myr after the Big Bang (Gnedin 2004; Ricotti et al. \ 2016).

The Sparkler thus provides a remarkable opportunity to study the properties of an emerging GC system in an adolescent universe ( 1/3 its current age). The host galaxy was estimated by M22 to have a de-magnified stellar mass of M_ 10^9 M_ . Considering standard growth tracks for galaxies (Behroozi et al. \ 2019), the expected mass for the Sparkler at z=0 is M_ 10^ 10 M _ (with a variation of 0.5 dex), and therefore the Sparkler is a possible progenitor for a MW-like mass ( M_ 10^ 10.5 M _ ) galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Sparkler with system S2, measuring 28 clumps or clusters, high magnification, compact cluster sizes, and color evidence for a recent merger followed by continued star formation.

quotes.quote: In our analysis the Sparkler correspond to system S2. Our lens model presents high magnification (around 7) and shear for the highest magnified image. The 4 Gyr old clusters labeled 1, 2, 4, 8, 10 by mowla2022 , correspond in our sample to C4, C12, C5, C9, C2, respectively. We will refer to this group, hereafter, as old clusters. In total we analysed 28 clumps/clusters in this system. The recovered R_ eff range from several upper-limits of a few parsec to 100 pc. In particular the old cluster sample has consistent upper-limits of 20 pc, and values all consistent with being star clusters.

The color-color diagram of the star cluster population shows exactly the same features as in the Sparkler adamo2020b . A cloud of M34 star clusters gather in the parameter-space color, coinciding with the period of the final merger phase. Numerical simulations of galaxy collisions predict that massive star clusters forming in the regions of highest gas density are then ejected in the outskirts by dynamical interactions e.g., kruijssen2012, renaud2015, pfeffer2018 . It is very likely that the Sparkler has recently undergone a merger around 100 to 500 Myr ago that has formed such large population of star clusters. After this event star formation in the galaxy has continued but at a lower rate, forming a younger population of star clusters.

sources.summary: The paper re-analyzes globular-cluster candidates in the strongly lensed galaxy The Sparkler, finding old metal-poor clusters and a population tracing its self-enrichment history.

quotes.quote: JWST observations of the strongly lensed galaxy The Sparkler have revealed a population of gravitationally bound globular cluster (GC) candidates. Different analyses have resulted in broadly similar ages but significantly different metallicities, questioning the assembly history that has led to the formation of such a population. In this letter, we re-analyse the two sets of photometry available in the literature with the code mcmame especially tailored to fit physical properties of GCs.

The ages and metallicities distribution of GCs in the Sparkler are consistent with those observed in Local Group's galaxies at similar lookback times. Comparing with predictions from E-MOSAICS simulations we confirm that the Sparkler GC population traces the self-enrichment history of a galaxy which might become a few times 10^9 M _ massive system at redshift z = 0 .

sources.summary: The paper treats the Sparkler as a multiply imaged galaxy in the z=1.37 overdensity, notes compact sources in its main body, and includes its three lensed images in the catalogue.

quotes.quote: In Mowla2022 , we further report on the discovery and analysis of twelve compact sources associated with the main body of the galaxy (refered to as the ``Sparkler''). In that work, we analyse the color of the compact sources as well as their spectral energy distributions and find that five of the twelve sources are consistent with being evolved globular clusters comprised of single stellar populations formed at z>9 , i.e., within in the first 500 Myr of cosmic history.
The three images of the Sparkler are included in our final catalogue (IDs 615, 679, and 772, corresponding to images 3, 2, and 1 of the Sparkler in the lensing models, respectively).
On this Figure, the three images of the Sparkler galaxy are indicated by a black rectangle.

sources.summary: The paper uses the strongly lensed z=1.378 Sparkler galaxy to demonstrate JWST deblending of compact sources from extended galaxy light and photometry of candidate globular clusters.

quotes.quote: The ``Sparkler" is a galaxy at redshift z=1.378 strongly lensed by the z=0.39 galaxy cluster SMACS J0723.3-7327. Most of these compact sources (``the sparkles") are unresolved, even with JWST, and 5 of them were identified as globular clusters by , owing to their red Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) compatible with the color of old stellar systems. However, used aperture photometry to measure the SED of these sources and most of them appear to be contaminated by the extended galaxy emission. To demonstrate the deblending capability of on real JWST observations, we apply our two-channel deconvolution method, modeling the compact sources in the point source channel and the contaminating light from the galaxy in the extended source channel.

gmw_00002947.name: Arc S5
gmw_00002947.overview: Arc S5 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of 1.42 [arXiv:2208.10450]. It features a bright central proto-bulge candidate estimated to be 100 million years old, alongside younger star-forming clumps that may represent proto-globular clusters [arXiv:2208.10450].

sources.summary: The paper describes Arc S5 as a z=1.42 lensed galaxy with a bright proto-bulge candidate, younger clumps, high magnification, and compact clumps that may be proto-globular clusters.

quotes.quote: Arcs S5 and S1 Arc S5 at redshift 1.42 shows a bright central clump, a proto-bulge candidate with age of 100 Myr and mass of a few times 10^7 . Younger star-forming clumps are visible in the galaxy which show a small extinction range. For zero extinction the age of the proto-bulge could be as old as 500 Myr.

Magnification values in the image S5.1 varies across the arc from 9 to 200 for the clumps closest to the critical line where the shear is stronger. Magnification values in S1.2 varies across the arc from 6 to 14. In both galaxies, several clumps, especially the most compacts, are potentially gravitationally bound ( 1 ) systems and could indeed be proto-globular clusters.

gmw_00002948.name: arc S1
gmw_00002948.overview: The lensed galaxy arc S1 is located at a redshift of 1.5 [arXiv:2208.10450]. Its clumps occupy a narrow color-color region, indicating a specific star formation episode that occurred 30 to 100 million years ago [arXiv:2208.10450].

sources.summary: The paper describes arc S1 as a z=1.5 lensed galaxy whose clumps occupy a narrow color-color region, indicating a 30-100 Myr star-formation episode with some compact bound candidates.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, clumps in the arc S1 at redshift 1.5 show colors that occupy a very small region in the parameter space, suggesting that a specific star formation episode has led to the formation of these stellar structures. The age range spans 30 to 100 Myr. The brightest central clump is among the youngest structures in the galaxy, opposite to what is observed in S5.1 and the Beret.

Magnification values in S1.2 varies across the arc from 6 to 14. In both galaxies, several clumps, especially the most compacts, are potentially gravitationally bound ( 1 ) systems and could indeed be proto-globular clusters.

gmw_00002949.name: CANDELS 23845
gmw_00002949.overview: CANDELS 23845 is a massive, dust-rich galaxy dominated by a central bulge [arXiv:2210.09888]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1204.1152] that exhibits heavy, potentially Compton-thick obscuration [arXiv:1204.1152].

sources.summary: U4958 has significant AGN emission; mid-IR and optical spectra already implied an active nucleus, and Chandra data indicate heavy, possibly Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: The SED-fitting procedure found that for all but one source (U428) the presence of an AGN is consistent with the photometry, although for only nine sources ( 35 of the sample: U4639, U4950, U4958, U5150, U5152, U5153, U5652, U5805, and U5877) its presence is significant at the 3 confidence level (i.e., solutions with no torus emission have ^ 2 ^ 2 _ min +28.5).

At a visual inspection, the presence of a nuclear component can be inferred for sources with a power-law SED (i.e., U4950 and U5877), for sources where the stellar component alone is not able to reproduce the datapoints around 3 m (i.e., U4958, U5153) or for sources where the starburst component, normalized to the far-IR datapoints, has already declined around 5--6 m (i.e. U5152, U5153, and U5805).

In comparison with the 2 Ms \ data ( 2008ApJS..179...19L ) used by F10, U4958 represents a new AGN detection, although the presence of an active nucleus was already inferred by the mid-IR featureless spectra and the optical spectrum, showing a broad C \ iii ] line and strong N \ v and C \ iv emission lines. All of the \ matched sources have a full-band (0.5--8 keV) detection. One source (U4958) has an upper limit in the soft band (0.5--2 keV); this result is suggestive of heavy absorption, since \ has its highest effective area, and hence best sensitivity, in this energy range. Basic \ spectral analysis (using an absorbed powerlaw with photon index fixed to 1.8, as typically observed in AGN; 2005A&A...432...15P ), actually confirms the presence of strong, possibly Compton-thick obscuration A source is called Compton-thick if its column density N_ H >1/ _ T 1.5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , where _ T is the Thomson cross section. towards this source (N _ H =1.9^ +1.4 _ 0.7 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes CANDELS 23845 as a massive dust-rich bulge-dominated galaxy, using a broad CO integration window and adding a modified blackbody component to model its far-IR excess.

quotes.quote: For the other source (CANDELS 23845), we perform the integral over ( 300 km s ^ 1 , +300 km s ^ 1 ).

However, for CANDELS 23845, 24210, and 25998, the observed far-IR fluxes are systematically higher than the model values, leading to large reduced ^2 4 -5. This ``far-IR excess'' might be caused by a dust-enshrouded stellar population that is strongly attenuated at shorter wavelengths e.g., hodge16, buat19 . We note that CANDELS 23845, 24210, and 25998 are actually similar to the sample of , i.e., they are all massive ( ( /M_ 10.5 ) dust-rich ( (L_ IR /L_ ) 12 ) galaxies.
(a) The mbb module is only used for CANDELS 23845, 24210, and 25998 to improve their fit quality (see sec:sed ).

gmw_00002950.name: CANDELS 24210
gmw_00002950.overview: CANDELS 24210 is a massive, dust-rich early-type galaxy [arXiv:1207.4478, arXiv:2210.09888] with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.566 [arXiv:1207.4478]. It hosts a radio-loud, X-ray-detected active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1207.4478] and exhibits a far-infrared excess [arXiv:2210.09888].

sources.summary: The paper describes J033217.14-274303.3 as a radio-loud, X-ray-detected, clearly extended early-type source below the quasar luminosity limit, with stellar-bump and 8 m SED features.

quotes.quote: J033217.14-274303.3 has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.566 and a photometric redshift of 0.49. It has a radio-loudness log(R) 1.7 and is also detected in the X-ray ( =1.87 ). The radio spectrum is rather flat ( =0.5 ).

An absolute magnitude in z of -22 puts it below the limit for quasars. The object is clearly extended with a early-type morphology. The object shows a clear contribution from a stellar bump as well as a steep rise at 8 m , typical of many of the variability selected objects discussed in the following sections.

sources.summary: The paper finds CANDELS 24210 has far-IR excess as a massive dust-rich galaxy and shows marginally separated blue and red CO contours in spatial analysis.

quotes.quote: However, for CANDELS 23845, 24210, and 25998, the observed far-IR fluxes are systematically higher than the model values, leading to large reduced ^2 4 -5. This ``far-IR excess'' might be caused by a dust-enshrouded stellar population that is strongly attenuated at shorter wavelengths e.g., hodge16, buat19 . We note that CANDELS 23845, 24210, and 25998 are actually similar to the sample of , i.e., they are all massive ( ( /M_ 10.5 ) dust-rich ( (L_ IR /L_ ) 12 ) galaxies.

From this figure, only CANDELS 24210 appears to have (slightly) separated blue versus red contours. The angular distance between the blue and red peaks is 0.5'' . On the other hand, the positional uncertainty for the blue/red line emission is 0.6 (S/N)^ 1 0.26 '' , where 1.5'' is the synthesized beam FWHM e.g., ivison07 . Therefore, the 0.5'' separation is marginally significant at a 2 level.

gmw_00002951.name: CANDELS 25998
gmw_00002951.overview: CANDELS 25998 is a high-redshift, star-forming galaxy characterized by a bulge-dominated, compact structure [arXiv:2210.09888]. Its carbon monoxide-based gas mass is estimated at approximately $10^{10.9}$ solar masses, which slightly exceeds its continuum-based upper limit [arXiv:2210.09888].

sources.summary: The paper singles out CANDELS 25998 because its CO-based gas mass slightly exceeds the continuum limit and its depletion/growth-timescale behavior differs from most of the sample.

quotes.quote: For 3 out of the 4 CO-detected sources (except CANDELS 25998), the CO-based masses are consistent with the continuum-based constraints. For CANDELS 25998, the CO-based mass is slightly higher than the continuum-based upper limit by 0.1 dex (see sec:coev for more discussion of this object).

Six (out of seven) SF sources have < , with the only exception being CANDELS 25998. For these 6 sources, their is more than 2 times shorter than . Our estimates of assume a typical CO-to-gas conversion factor of =4.3 . Some studies suggest a lower value of =0.8 for compact galaxies e.g., bolatto13, carilli13 . Considering that our bulge-dominated galaxies are generally compact e.g., ni19, ni21 , we also plot the result for the assumption of =0.8 in Fig. fig:tvt . Under this alternative assumption, all seven sources, including CANDELS 25998, have at least 4 times shorter than .

For CANDELS 25998, the ( 10^ 10.9 \ M_ ) is consistent with our continuum-based constraint ( <10^ 11.1 \ M_ ), although the two measurements are based on different frequencies and assumptions. The two values from are both below unity, consistent with our sample. For CANDELS 25998, the estimation is similar to ours ( 10^ 11.07 vs.

gmw_00002952.name: GRB 060206
gmw_00002952.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060206 is a faint, high-redshift dwarf starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.05 to 4.08 [arXiv:0809.2608, arXiv:0802.0957]. It features multiple velocity systems spanning 417 km/s, which likely originate from a starburst wind rather than galactic rotation [arXiv:0802.0957].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint HST source as the z=4.048 GRB 060206 host, gives its UV luminosity and ISM properties, and separates it from a likely foreground Mg II absorber galaxy.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy at z=4.048 is found to have N( )=20.85 0.10 and [ S / H ] = -0.85 0.10 based on moderate resolution ( v 40 ) afterglow spectra (Fynbo \ 2006a; Th one \ 2008). No H _2 is detected to a 4- limit of f_ H_2 < 10^ 3.6 (.f. \ Fynbo \ 2006; Tumlinson \ 2007). In addition to the GRB host, two strong Mg II absorbers are found along the line of sight at z=1.48 and z=2.26 with W(2796)=0.95 0.1 \ and W(2796)=1.5 0.1 , respectively (Aoki \ 2006; Hao \ 2007; Th one \ 2008). We obtained late-time images of the field around GRB 060206 on November 25, 2006, using ACS and the F814W filter on board HST (PID = 10817).

At the position of the OT (marked by a circle of 0.5 \ radius), we clearly detect a faint source of AB( F814W )=27.6 0.1 . We identify the source as the host galaxy of GRB 060206. At z=4.048 , the observed F814W magnitude corresponds to a rest-frame absolute magnitude of M_ AB (1600) - 5 h=-17.7 0.1 for the GRB host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060206 host at z=4.084 as having multiple velocity systems over 417 km/s; distances and velocity spread make galaxy rotation unlikely and favor a starburst wind.

quotes.quote: GRB 060206 With z = 4.084 at the opposite end of the redshift distribution, this burst was observed with ISIS/WHT 1.6 h after the burst Fynbo06a, Thoene08b . Four main velocity systems span a range of 417 km/s and three of them are also detected in the finestructure transitions (Fig. 1), unprecedented in GRB afterglow spectra. The ratio between finestructure and ground-state transitions decreases from the red to the bluer components which is consistent with UV pumping of the transitions by the radiation field of the GRB, but collisional excitation is not excluded. In the case of UV pumping, we can infer a distance of the absorbing material to the burst of about 1 to 8 kpc for the different components. This, together with the large velocity spread, makes an origin from rotation of the galaxy unlikely; a starburst wind is also the preferred scenario.

gmw_00002953.name: XID 337
gmw_00002953.overview: XID 337 is a heavily obscured Type 2 quasar located at a redshift of z = 3.660 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410632, arXiv:2003.08631]. The host galaxy lies below the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:2003.08631] and is surrounded by an elongated Lyman-alpha nebula spanning approximately 80 physical kiloparsecs [arXiv:2005.01732].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CDFS-263 as a z=3.660 SCUBA-detected Type 2 QSO with optical counterparts, narrow emission lines, high X-ray luminosity, and large intrinsic X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: We have a firm detection (S/N 4 ) for CDFS-263 XID from (RA =3:32:18.86 DEC =-27:51:35.5 , z =3.660 ) : S _ 850 =4.8 1.1 mJy. Since we already know the position of the X-ray source to sub-arcsec accuracy, the 14 arcsec FWHM SCUBA beam would imply that the sub-mm emission is either coming from this source or something else within a radius of 5 arcsec. Inside this area we find two optical counterparts in a deep ACS z band image centered on CDFS-263 (see left panel of Fig. figure:fig_acs263 ).

The line fluxes are a factor of 20--100 lower compared to those of the prototype Type 2 QSO CDFS-202 ( ). The emission line widths are narrow with a FWHM of 1500 km s ^ 1 , whereas the mean line width of the permitted lines for X-ray selected Seyfert 1 and QSO1 ranges from 3000 to 5000 km s ^ 1 (see ). The narrow line widths and the observed 0.5-10 keV luminosity of 1.6 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 indicate a Type 2 QSO ( ).
This points to a large intrinsic absorption of CDFS-263. Therefore we have frozen the photon index to 1.9 (model 2).

sources.summary: The paper describes XID 337 as a heavily obscured type II QSO, below the main sequence, whose far-infrared photometry is contaminated by a blended bright QSO and whose ALMA line/continuum emission was undetected.

quotes.quote: They derived SFRs of 200 - 900 M_ /yr (except for XID 42 which has 1680 M_ /yr ), which places all sources within 1 from the best fit main sequence relation found by at the corresponding redshift (2 for XID 337 which is below the main sequence). Three of them are well known heavily obscured AGN: XID 337 was firstly detected in the early 1-Ms CDF-S exposure and classified as a type II QSO object in the spectroscopic follow--up szokoly_2004 . This result was later confirmed by who also reported a submillimeter detection at 850 m using SCUBA. Exploiting high resolution ( 0.2") ALMA observations, found that the single object associated with XID 337 in the Spitzer/MIPS, Herschel and SCUBA maps is actually a blend of two sources, whose emission is dominated by a bright QSO at 3.5" separation; thus, the Mid-IR/Far-IR photometric datapoints of XID 337 were converted into upper limits.

For XID 337, XID 551, and XID 746 it was not possible to determine unambiguously a channel in which the brightness at the source position was significantly above the noise level. For these sources no detection was obtained even applying natural weighting, tapering (0.45") and a large channel width (120 km/s) to improve the chance of achieving a significant detection.
As for the line imaging, for XID 337, XID 551, and XID 746 we apply natural weighting and a 0.45" tapering, without achieving a significant detection.

sources.summary: The paper includes Cdfs 04 as a MUSE-WIDE type II AGN whose Ly nebula is the sample's smallest, about 80 pkpc across, with east-west elongation and type-I-like radial asymmetry.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 4 type II AGNs, of which the Cdfs 04 and Cdfs 15 have been selected using the MUSE--WIDE survey Urrutia2019 , UDF 09 is found in the MUSE--Deep survey Bacon2017 and the Bulb AGN was taken from .

The Cdfs 04 nebula is the smallest in our sample, showing a maximal diameter of approximately 80 pkpc. While the innermost part is slightly asymmetric towards the east, the parts further out show a clear elongated structure from east to west.

Instead, the Cdfs 04 source radial asymmetry appears more similar to the type I AGN cases in this method. This is because the central region of this source is very symmetric ( <10 pkpc) and at larger distances, the emission is very faint.

sources.summary: The paper includes PID 131 among possible Compton-thick, reflection-dominated AGN candidates, but reserves the selection because its Fe K evidence lies near an instrumental line and the spectrum is noisy.

quotes.quote: Compton-thick AGN candidates were selected by two characteristic spectral features: 1) large absorbing column density 10^ 24 ; and 2) strong 6.4 keV Fe K line emission arising from reflection fom thick, cold matter. On taking face values of \ measurements, the only source with \ exceeding 10^ 24 \ is PID 316. However, given the uncertainty in the \ measurements and the likely complexity of spectra at large \ discussed in Sect. 4.1, we select three more candidates (PID 66, PID 131, PID 245) with log 23.9 [ ] , for which real values of their \ could reach the Compton-thick \ threshold.

This selection results in five sources (PID 102, PID 114, PID 131, PID 215, and PID 398). They are thus considered as reflection-dominated sources and their true \ would be in the Compton-thick regime.

Among the above selected sources, we tentatively reserve the selection of PID 131, mainly because of the poor data quality: although the strong Fe K line seems to be real albeit lying close to the instrumental Al line at 1.5 keV of the MOS cameras (see Fig. fig:atlas ), the rest of the spectrum is very noisy.

sources.summary: The paper synthesizes CDFS-263 as a low-equivalent-width Type II QSO contrast case and estimates that such a source would need to be much brighter for Chandra detection at z about 6.

quotes.quote: As a contrast to CXO 52, a spectrum for the Type II QSO CDFS-263 at z=3.660 with low equivalent width emission lines discovered by Mainieri is also synthesized, using exactly the same procedure as for CXO 52. This spectrum will be referred to as the ``low equivalent width'' Type II QSO; otherwise colours based on CXO 52 are generally meant when referring to Type II QSOs.

At z 6 , this implies a source like CXO 52 would need to have an AB magnitude at 1.3 m (observed) of about 26.5-28 to be detectable in the x-ray, while a source like CDFS-263 would need to be 2.5 or more magnitudes brighter.

gmw_00002954.name: XID 551
gmw_00002954.overview: XID 551 is a high-redshift, heavily obscured type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1504.06948, arXiv:2003.08631] or quasar [arXiv:2111.10507] at a redshift of approximately 3.7 [arXiv:1209.0916, arXiv:1504.06948]. It is a candidate Compton-thick source [arXiv:1303.5556, arXiv:2004.07604] surrounded by an asymmetric Lyman-alpha nebula and at least two neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2005.01732].

sources.summary: The paper treats PID-144 as one of four secure Compton-thick AGN candidates, with high FeK equivalent width, joint spectral-fit support, and a likely transmission-dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Four out of nine sources (PID-66, 144, 147, 324) present high rest-frame FeK EW (see table 1). These are suggestive of high absorbing column densities likely corresponding to Compton-thick AGN.

In agreement with the spectral analysis, presented in the previous section, four sources (PID-66, 144, 147 and 324) present FeK lines with high (rest-frame) EW ( > 0.4 keV). Therefore, the joint and analysis corroborates that these four sources have a good probability for being Compton-thick.

Two of the sources are most likely transmission dominated (PID-66 and PID-144) while the other two are reflection dominated (PID-147 and PID-324) . Although our sample is by no means statistically complete, it represents the most well studied examples of Compton-thick AGN at moderate to high redshifts. In particular, the redshifts of the four most secure candidate Compton-thick sources are z=1.185, 1.222, 1.53 and 3.7.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Cdfs 15 as a high-redshift X-ray type II AGN whose Ly nebula extends northeast, contains same-redshift neighboring galaxies, and is statistically asymmetric beyond 25 kpc.

quotes.quote: We highlight the source Cdfs 15 (formally Cdf 202), which constitutes one of the first X-ray discovered Type II AGN at high redshifts Norman2002 .

Similarly, the Cdfs 15 nebula is extended more in one direction -- towards the north--east. We point out that the source is surrounded by at least two galaxies within the Lya halo, particularly in the north-east. This second galaxy is situated at the same redshift as the AGN. The large SB and extension of the part of the nebula surrounding this companion galaxy is however incompatible with ionisation from the companion galaxy itself (Ly haloes commonly observed around galaxies are indeed at least one order of magnitude fainter and smaller than this nebula), leaving the AGN as the most plausible source of ionisation.
For a radial distance beyond 25 kpc, we see that the Bulb and the Cdfs 15 nebulae are statistically more asymmetric, with p-values reaching values as low as 0.05.

sources.summary: The paper describes CDF-S202 as a z=3.7 type 2 AGN and compares shock-dominated and AGN-plus-shock models for its UV line ratios and continuum SED.

quotes.quote: Norman et al observed the AGN detected at z=3.7 by the Chandra Deep Field-South 1 Ms exposure. It is a distant type 2 AGN with UV showing FWHM of 1500 . The optical spectrum was obtained with the multislit mode on the FORS1 on the ESO/VLT-ANTU. Detailed modelling (Table 6, cols. 9, 10) indicates that a shock dominated model (MNS) best fits the lines within a maximum discrepancy factor of 2.3 for HeII 1640 (Table 6 last column). This model calculated by =1600 would provide OVI 1035/ 200.

sources.summary: The paper treats PID 144 as a previously suggested and well-known Compton-thick AGN candidate, though its XMM-Newton-only absorption falls just below the adopted threshold.

quotes.quote: Previously suggested Compton-thick AGN candidates, PID 144 norman02,comastri11 , PID 147 comastri11,georgantopoulos13 , and PID 252 i12cdfs , for which Chandra data were also used, fall just below the \ threshold on the use of the XMM-Newton data alone and may add up the number of candidates to 10 (or 11 if PID 131 is included, 5-6 ).

The well-known Compton-thick AGN candidate PID 144 norman02,comastri11 and other previously suggested candidates, PID 147 comastri11,georgantopoulos13 and PID 324 georgantopoulos13 , are present in the selected sources. By design the above two criteria select all the spectral fitting-based, Compton-thick AGN candidates (supposing they are 'true positives' here). The number of sources selected by these criteria is 19, indicating that they do not pick up too many false positives (12). The false positives include Compton-thick AGNs (PID 144, 147, 252, and 324), previously suggested by a combined analysis of Chandra or XMM-Newton and Chandra.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CDFS-202 as a z=3.71 type-2 quasar close enough to CDFS-6664's sightline to plausibly ionize foreground IGM and boost LyC transmission.

quotes.quote: We check the quasar catalog and find that the famous type-2 quasar, CDFS-202 norman2002 , is close to the sightline of CDFS-6664, with a project separation of 0.5 pMpc ( 1.2 , Figure fig:distribution ). CDFS-202 is located at redshift 3.71 . The line-of-sight distance to CDFS-6664 is about 64.9 cMpc. Previous works show that luminous quasars can photoevaporate optically thick absorbers within a 1 pMpc radius or so e.g., hennawi_prochaska2007 . Therefore, it is quite possible that the ionization bubble of CDFS-202 affect the IGM on the sightline of the CDFS-6664 and enhances the transmission of LyC photons.

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID 412 as the only source with a clearly visible statistically significant iron line, deriving rest-frame line energy, equivalent width, column density, and X-ray fluxes.

quotes.quote: The only source for which the iron line was easily visible in the spectrum and statistically significant is XID 412 (i.e. CDF-S 202 in Norman02 ). In this case, we allowed the rest-frame energy of the line to vary during the spectral fitting in a relatively large range. The best-fitting rest-frame energy, EW and N_H that we derived for this source ( E =6.53 keV, EW =738^ +465 _ 451 eV, N_H=91_ 13 ^ +14 10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) are in good agreement with those found by previous Chandra Norman02 and XMM-Newton Comastri11 observations. The soft ( F_ 0.5-2 keV =1.76 10^ 16 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 ) and hard ( F_ 2-10 keV =2.41 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 ) fluxes are consistent with those reported by using the 1 Ms CDF-S data, although they assumed a different spectral model ( =1.8 and N_H=1.5 10^ 24 cm^ 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID 551 as the first z>3 type II AGN detected in the CDF-S 1-Ms exposure, with high X-ray column density and no significant ALMA line/continuum detection.

quotes.quote: XID 551 was the first type II AGN ever detected in the CDF-S 1-Ms exposure at z>3 norman_2002 , for which found a large column density ( N_H > 23 ) performing an X-ray spectrum analysis using 3.3--Ms XMM-Newton survey data.

For XID 337, XID 551, and XID 746 it was not possible to determine unambiguously a channel in which the brightness at the source position was significantly above the noise level. For these sources no detection was obtained even applying natural weighting, tapering (0.45") and a large channel width (120 km/s) to improve the chance of achieving a significant detection.
As for the line imaging, for XID 337, XID 551, and XID 746 we apply natural weighting and a 0.45" tapering, without achieving a significant detection.

sources.summary: The paper treats PID 144 as a known z=3.70 heavily obscured AGN, using its hard X-ray color as the reference threshold for 9-20 keV excess selection.

quotes.quote: PID 144 ( z=3.70 ) is a previously known, heavily obscured AGN with an X-ray absorbing column of (0.6-0.9) 10^ 24 (Norman et al 2002; Comastri et al 2011), located in this interval. We take this object with h/m=1.46 as the reference and sources that have h/m larger than this object were classified as 9-20 keV excess sources.

Seven heavily obscured active galaxies with 0.6 10^ 24 , including one previously known source (PID 144, Norman et al 2002; Comastri et al 2011), were selected by the rest-frame X-ray colour selection, primarily utilizing the excess emission in the 9-20 keV band relative to emission at lower energies.

sources.summary: CDFS 202 is cited as the prototype high-redshift QSO2, with blue observed color from a strong R-band emission line and redder color after line subtraction.

quotes.quote: As an example, the prototype of high--redshift QSO2, CDFS 202 in Norman et al. (2002), has R K 2.5. In this case the rather blue observed colour is mainly due to the presence of a strong emission line in the R filter. When the line flux is subtracted, the R K colour of CDFS 202 is R K 4, which makes this object appreciably red.

gmw_00002955.name: XID 746
gmw_00002955.overview: XID 746 is a massive, quiescent active galactic nucleus host galaxy at high redshift [arXiv:2405.19401] that has also been classified as a type II quasar [arXiv:2003.08631]. It is essentially devoid of a cold molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2405.19401] and exhibits compact helium emission with asymmetric Lyman-alpha structures [arXiv:2005.01732].

sources.summary: The paper presents GS-10578 as a z=3.064 massive quiescent AGN host with stringent CO non-detection, tiny molecular-gas reservoir, neutral outflows, satellites, and net-zero gas inflow history.

quotes.quote: This value makes GS-10578 the earliest unambiguous evidence of a massive galaxy that is essentially devoid of a cold-gas reservoir, having a stellar-to-gas mass ratio of <0.8 . This is the most stringent upper limit on the gas mass for a quiescent galaxy at high redshift. In Fig. fig:Mgas we investigate the cold molecular gas mass (left panel) as a function of the stellar mass and compare GS-10578 to other AGN hosts (black symbols; Perna18 , Bertola et al. submitted), quiescent galaxies (orange symbols; Belli+21,Williams21, Woodrum22 ) and star-forming galaxies (various grey symbols; Daddi10, Decarli16, Spilker16, Tacconi18, Sanders23 ) at z=1-4.

The SFH implies that GS-10578 started on the main sequence gas-fraction relation at z 4.7 (within uncertainties), regardless of whether we use a value of 3 or 0.8 for the gas fraction. We stress that this result is not by construction. The gas-consumption history could in principle end below the star-forming main sequence, for example, if ejective feedback removed a substantial fraction of the gas, or even above the star-forming main sequence if this galaxy had higher-than-average gas inflows. The match between the gas consumption history and star-forming main sequence implies that GS10578 evolved with `net-zero' gas inflow.
