sources.summary: The paper highlights UDF1361 as a classification exception: SED, color, and MIPS methods classify it as dusty/star-forming, but morphology appears old/elliptical, possibly because the galaxy is bulge-dominated or interacting.

quotes.quote: As for UDF1361, it was classified as a DG by the former three classification methods, however, it was classified as OG by the morphology classification method. After checking its image in Figure fig:ziv , we found that one of the possible reason is that the nonparametric classification method can not separate ellipticals and early spiral galaxies with a big bulge, it may also be because of recent or on-going merges and interaction of this galaxy.

The SED fitting method and the nonparametric measures of galaxy morphology method almost offer the same result for EROs classifiction, except for UDF1361. UDF1361 was classified as DGs by the SED fitting method, the (i-K) versus (J-K) diagram, and the Spitzer MIPS 24 m image method, but the nonparametric measures of galaxy morphology method classified it as OGs. This EROs has late type SEDs, but elliptical type morphologies. We classify it as DGs.

sources.summary: The paper describes XID 746 as a little-studied type II QSO in the CDF-S; despite full-depth ALMA coverage, no significant line emission was detected.

quotes.quote: XID 746 is the less studied objects, despite being known as Type II QSO since the first 1--Ms exposure in the CDF-S szokoly_2004 .

Concerning the undetected sources, two of them (XID 337 and XID 551) were observed for half of the time needed to achieve the requested sensitivity, while XID 746 is included in the execution block that was observed twice and reached the requested sensitivity.

For XID 337, XID 551, and XID 746 it was not possible to determine unambiguously a channel in which the brightness at the source position was significantly above the noise level. For these sources no detection was obtained even applying natural weighting, tapering (0.45") and a large channel width (120 km/s) to improve the chance of achieving a significant detection.

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF 09 as a MUSE-Deep type II AGN with compact He II emission, peculiar off-center Ly emission, and a northwest asymmetry.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 4 type II AGNs, of which the Cdfs 04 and Cdfs 15 have been selected using the MUSE--WIDE survey Urrutia2019 , UDF 09 is found in the MUSE--Deep survey Bacon2017 and the Bulb AGN was taken from .

The Bulb shows extended \ emission out to a maximum radial distance of 50 pkpc. The UDF 09 source shows compact emission with only faint extended substructures out to 25--30 pkpc from the center of the AGN.

The UDF 09 is more peculiar: the strongest Ly emission is not coming from the central region of the AGN, but rather from a region towards the south--east. In addition, there is an asymmetry towards the north--west.

gmw_00002956.name: XID 539
gmw_00002956.overview: XID 539 is a massive submillimeter starburst galaxy at redshift 4.755 [arXiv:2002.04816, arXiv:2309.05713] that hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2002.04816, arXiv:2003.08631]. Its cold gas forms a regularly rotating disk [arXiv:2102.05957] containing a central bulge [arXiv:2102.05957] and a fast ionized gas outflow [arXiv:2309.05713].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALESS 073.1 as a z~5 starburst AGN host whose cold gas forms a regular rotating disk and whose rotation curve requires a central bulge.

quotes.quote: Here we present submillimeter observations (with spatial resolution of 700 parsecs) of ALESS 073.1, a starburst galaxy at z 5 when the Universe was 1.2 billion years old. This galaxy's cold gas forms a regularly rotating disk with negligible noncircular motions. The galaxy rotation curve requires the presence of a central bulge in addition to a star-forming disk.

The target, ALESS 073.1 (LESS J033229.3-275619), is a strong sub-millimeter source . Its large far-infrared luminosity suggests an ongoing starburst with an estimated star-formation rate of 1000 solar masses (M _ ) per year . ALESS 073.1 contains a dust-obscured AGN . These extreme properties indicate that the galaxy may drive a massive gas outflow .
The \ disk of ALESS 073.1 is supported by rotation, not turbulence.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA imaging of LESS J033229.4 275619, a z=4.755 submillimetre galaxy hosting a Compton-thick AGN, and derives line, dust, infrared, gas, and depletion properties.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present our ALMA imaging of the \ line in \ (also known as LESS J033229.4 275619), which lies at z=4.755 in the Extended Chandra Deep Field South. It hosts a Compton-thick AGN as revealed by the Chandra data (Xue et al. 2011), and is identified as a submillimetre galaxy (SMG) using ALMA high-resolution continuum observations (Hodge et al. 2013). Its properties, including line emission (e.g. CO(2 1), Coppin et al. 2010; , De Breuck et al. 2011, 2014; Gullberg et al. 2018; and , Nagao et al. 2012) and continuum emission (Coppin et al. 2009; Biggs et al. 2011; Wardlow et al. 2011; Gilli et al. 2014), have been studied extensively throughout X-ray to mm/submm bands. Our new ALMA observations of the \ and \ lines and the corresponding continuum emission at 370 \ (rest-frame) provide new insights into its properties of the (warm and dense) molecular gas and star formation.

By fitting the observed far-infrared (FIR) spectral energy distribution of \ with a single-temperature modified blackbody function, we obtained a dust temperature T_ dust =57.6 3.5 K, 60-to-100 \ flux density ratio f_ 60 /f_ 100 =0.86 0.08 , and total infrared luminosity L_ IR =(5.8 0.9) 10^ 12 L_ . The SED-fit-based \ is consistent with those estimated from various line ratios as advocated by our earlier work, indicating that those proposed line-ratio-based method can be used to practically derive \ for high- z sources. The total molecular gas mass of \ is (3.3 1.7) 10^ 10 M_ , and the inferred gas depletion time is about 43 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper describes XID 539 as the most distant known Compton-thick AGN, detected in ALMA line emission, with previous SED constraints and compact dust and [C ii] sizes.

quotes.quote: XID 539 is the most distant Compton-thick AGN known gilli_2011 , and one of the widely studied sources in the CDF-S coppin_2010,nagao_2012,debreuck_2014 . In particular, have performed an in--depth analysis through an observation of the continuum at 1.3 mm, combining it with continuum observations from ALESS and from . They built a full SED including Herschel data and obtained dust temperature and size and the SFR.
We detect XID 42, XID 539 and XID 666 with a signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) equal to 6.2, 4.7 and 4.0/4.9, respectively.

As for XID 539, the dust size is in agreement with , who constrained the diameter of the 1 mm continuum emission to a region < 2 kpc. They also resolved the [ C ii ] line emission, which has been spatially fit with a Gaussian, obtaining a size of 2.4 0.2 kpc half-light radius, corresponding to about twice the CO(7--6) radius detected in this work (defined as the half of the mean between the major and minor axes). However, we note that -- due to the large error on the CO(7--6) size -- the two measurements are still consistent within 2 .

sources.summary: The paper studies LESS J033229.3-275619 as a massive dusty z=4.755 star-forming SMG hosting a Compton-thick AGN, weak outflow, and rotating warm ionized gas disk.

quotes.quote: In this work, we study the properties of \ (from the LABOCA ECDFS submillimetre survey LESS J033229.3-275619), a massive ( M_ 10^ 11 M _ Circosta:2019 ) SMG at z = 4.755 showing spectral features consistent with a Compton thick AGN ( N_ H = 17.0^ +11.7 _ 6.8 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) Vanzella:2009, Gilli:2011, Giallongo:2019 . has a plethora of observations targeting both rest-frame far-infrared Coppin:2009, Debreuck:2011, Nagao:2012, Debreuck:2014, Damato:2020, Zhao:2020, Lelli:2021 , UV Vanzella:2006, Vanzella:2009, Coppin:2009 , and X-ray emission Tozzi:2006, Gilli:2011, Vito:2013, Gilli:2014, Circosta:2019, Giallongo:2019 . The bright rest-frame far-infrared emission indicates a massive burst of star formation with SFR of 1000 M _ yr ^ 1 Coppin:2009, Gilli:2014, Swinbank:2014, Circosta:2019 and a large dust content ( M_ dust 10^9 M _ ; Swinbank:2014, daCunha:2015 ).

The rest-frame optical emission lines also reveal a fast ( 1700 ) ionized gas outflow marginally resolved in the galaxy center. The high sensitivity of NIRSpec allows us to perform the kinematic analysis of the narrow \ component which indicates that the warm ionized gas velocity field is consistent with disk rotation. Interestingly, we find that in the innermost nuclear regions ( < 1.5 kpc), the intrinsic velocity dispersion of the disk reaches 150 , 2-3 times higher than the velocity dispersion inferred from the 158 m line tracing mostly cold gas. Since, at large radii, the velocity dispersion of the warm and cold gas are comparable, we conclude that the outflows are injecting turbulence in the warm ionized gas in the central region, but they are not sufficiently powerful to disrupt the dense gas and quench star formation.

sources.summary: The paper detects bright [CII] in the z=4.76 submillimetre galaxy, finding starburst-dominated emission, atomic gas comparable to molecular gas, and dense kpc-scale starburst conditions.

quotes.quote: We present the detection of bright [ CII ] emission in the z =4.76 submillimetre galaxy LESS J033229.4-275619 using the Atacama Pathfinder EXperiment. This represents the highest redshift [ CII ] detection in a submm selected, star-formation dominated system. The AGN contributions to the [ CII ] and far-infrared (FIR) luminosities are small. We find an atomic mass derived from [ CII ] comparable to the molecular mass derived from CO. The ratio of the [ CII ] , CO and FIR luminosities imply a radiation field strength G_0 10 ^3 and a density 10 ^4 cm ^ 3 in a kpc-scale starburst, as seen in local and high redshift starbursts.

sources.summary: The paper compares its kinematic method to ALESS 073.1, a massive dusty galaxy whose velocity dispersion and cold disk agree with local-galaxy-like rotational support in the authors' refitting.

quotes.quote: Lelli:2021 studied the kinematic properties of the massive dusty galaxy ALESS 073.1 using the software ^ 3D Barolo DiTeodoro:2016 , which directly takes the beam-smearing effect into account on the datacube and uses a 3D fitting algorithm with tilted-ring models to infer the kinematical properties of galaxies. Our best-fitting results based on the moment maps agrees with the results found by Lelli:2021 , who reported low values of _ gas ranging from 60 km s ^ 1 in the internal regions to 10 km s ^ 1 in the outer region. Our results in Figure Fig:aless show a bimodal distribution of the velocity dispersion that converges at 30 km s ^ 1 when most of the selected pixels were in the outer regions, and to 56 km s ^ 1 when the central pixels were selected. Despite the two different methods of fitting, our results agree with the kinematical cold disk with values of V_ max / _ gas comparable to those obtained for local galaxies. DeBreuck:2014 analyzed the same galaxy using a 2D fitting algorithm of data with a poorer resolution and found values of V_ max / _ gas 3.1, but this is mostly due to the underestimated rotational velocity ( 120 km s ^ 1 ) and not to the overestimated velocity dispersion ( _ gas = 40 10 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID403 as a z=4.76 AGN, describes it as a bright submillimeter galaxy with intense star formation, and links its SED to active assembly.

quotes.quote: XID403 ( _ J2000 =03:32:29.29, _ J2000 =-27:56:19.5) was recognized as an AGN at z=4.76 based on FORS-2 spectroscopy vanzella06 .
Based on a LABOCA detection at 870 m, they showed that this source is a bright submillimeter galaxy with SFR 1000 M_ yr ^ 1 .

In summary, the full SED analysis shows that XID403 is not a classic, type-2 QSO (i.e., a narrow-line, X-ray obscured AGN whose physical properties can be explained within the standard, geometry-based Unified Model; norman02 ), but points to a complex physical picture likely related to its active assembly phase.

sources.summary: AzTEC/GS12 is identified with GS12a, a faint radio red-IRAC galaxy at z=4.762, supported by LABOCA proximity and reported CO emission at z=4.755.

quotes.quote: AzTEC/GS12. The most likely counterpart candidate is a faint radio, red IRAC source GS12a located 4.0 \ away from the AzTEC position with [ 3.6 m ] [ 4.5 m ] = +0.10. This z=4.762 galaxy was also identified as the counterpart to the LABOCA 870 \ survey source LESS J033229.3 275619 ( S_ 870 =5.1 1.4 mJy) by coppin09 based on its proximity to the LABOCA position and the presence of a 3 radio source. Redshifted CO emission at z_ CO =4.755 has been reported by coppin10b , lending further support for the counterpart identification.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LESS J033229.4-275619 at z=4.75 from optical spectroscopy and CO follow-up, and emphasizes it is the only z=4.76 sample SMG not detected at 250 micron/SPIRE.

quotes.quote: Finally, another SMG, LESS J033229.4-275619, in the GOODS-S field was identified at z = 4.75 with both optical spectroscopy and millimeter CO [ 2-1 ] line followup observations coppin2009 .
Six SMGs appear to be detected by SPIRE, while no detection is found for LESS J033229.4-275619 in the ECDFS.
Only one SMG at z = 4.76 , LESS J033229, is not detected at 250 m.

gmw_00002957.name: CXOHDFN J123719.0+621026
gmw_00002957.overview: CXOHDFN J123719.0+621026 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=4.137 [arXiv:astro-ph/0210552, arXiv:astro-ph/0212082]. It exhibits a Seyfert-like luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0212082], possible X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0212082], and a flat X-ray spectrum that suggests intrinsic absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0210552, arXiv:astro-ph/0212082].

sources.summary: The paper describes the z=4.137 AGN as optically faint with a flat effective X-ray photon index, likely intrinsic absorption, and an inferred column density typical of Compton-thin Seyfert 2 galaxies.

quotes.quote: The other two AGNs have flatter effective X-ray photon indices ( 1.1--1.5) , suggesting the presence of intrinsic absorption (provided their underlying X-ray continua are similar to those of lower-redshift AGNs). It is possible that the flat X-ray continuum of the z=4.424 AGN is partially related to its radio loudness. If the z=4.137 AGN suffers from X-ray absorption, the implied column density is N_ H 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

is characterized by a flat effective X-ray photon index ( =1.12 0.25 ), which provides an acceptable fit to our data. There are two possible explanations for this result, both consistent with our data: \ has either an intrinsically flat X-ray spectrum or a steeper ( 2.0) X-ray spectrum plus absorption. Based on previous results for low- and intermediate-redshift quasars (e.g., George et al. 2000; Reeves Turner 2000), optically luminous z>4 quasars (V03), and , we suggest that the underlying continuum is likely a 2.0 power law. When the photon index is fixed to 2, a deficit of X-ray counts is visible below 3 keV (see Fig. 2).
This suggests the presence of a column density of 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (see Table 2), typical of local Compton-thin Seyfert 2 galaxies (e.g., Bassani et al. 1999).

sources.summary: The review describes CXOHDFN J123719.0+621026 as a z=4.137 moderate-luminosity AGN with Seyfert-like luminosity, a flat X-ray spectrum, possible intrinsic absorption, and possible X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: The other two AGN, CXOHDFN J123642.0+621331 ( z=4.424 ; M_ B =-21.6 ; 45 counts) and CXOHDFN J123719.0+621026 ( z=4.137 ; M_ B =-21.4 ; 117 counts), have Seyfert-like luminosities. They have flatter X-ray spectra with effective photon indices of 1.6 and 1.1, respectively, when only Galactic absorption is assumed in the fitting (these fits are statistically acceptable). The X-ray spectrum of CXOHDFN J123719.0+621026 can also be acceptably modeled with a 2.0 power law and an intrinsic column density of 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .
CXOHDFN J123719.0+621026 in the \ Deep Field-North may also be X-ray variable (Vignali et al. 2002).

gmw_00002958.name: Mrk 382
gmw_00002958.overview: Mrk 382 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2212.06183] that exhibits strong host galaxy contamination [arXiv:1408.2337, arXiv:1503.03611] and displays continuum variability [arXiv:1503.03611]. Its active nucleus features two warm absorbing components [arXiv:2212.06183] and has one of the smallest [OIII] line widths in its class [arXiv:astro-ph/0107528].

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 382 has warm absorbers, soft excess, Iron K emission, a measured high-energy cutoff, high reflection fraction, and a lower limit on spin.

quotes.quote: Mrk 382 In the soft energy band the spectra of this source shows two warm absorbing components, one with turbulent velocity of 100 km s^ 1 and one with turbulent velocity of 1000 km s^ 1 plus a soft excess component with a temperature of kT _ bb =25 5 eV. The statistical goodness of Model A was very good ^2 /dof=304/309. Fitting with this model we obtained for the photon index of the primary power-law a value =2.19 0.09 . The high energy cut-off is unconstrained with this model: E_c> 43 keV as well as the reflection fraction: R_ refl <0.77 . We found a flux of 2.09 0.57 10^ 6 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 for the Iron K line.

The photon index of the primary power-law was =2.02 0.03 and we were able to measure the high energy cut-off: E_c=45^ +7 _ 8 keV. We found a higher value of the reflection fraction compared to Model B1, R_ refl =3.52^ +0.54 _ 0.76 but compatible within the errors. The spin parameter was unconstrained but we obtained a lower limit of a >0.89 .

sources.summary: The paper says Mrk 382 has strong host contamination, uses the V-band light curve for cross-correlation, and adopts a 6.6-day rest-frame lag from the cleaner campaign half.

quotes.quote: Mrk 382 : This object shows the strongest host contamination in our sample. Because of this, the 5100 \ flux calibration is uncertain and we chose to use the V band light curve, with their improved precision, in the CCF analysis (the only source in our sample). Using the entire light curve we obtain a peak correlation of r_ max =0.49 and a time lag of = 5.8^ +1.6 _ 1.5 days.

Since the first part of the observations of this object suffers from bad-weather and problems with continuum subtraction from the H line, this resulted in a noisier light curve in the first part of the campaign. Thus, we also analyzed only the second half of the light curve, with its improved sampling (blue points in Figure 2). This gives a stronger correlation with r_ max =0.65 corresponding to a time lag of = 6.6^ +1.1 _ 0.7 days in the rest frame of the source. In the following analysis we use this time lag.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 382 to demonstrate spectral fitting under strong host contamination, recovering continuum variability and finding a longer Fe II lag than H beta.

quotes.quote: The left panel of Figure fig-spec382 shows the fitting to the spectrum of Mrk 382 taken at Julian date (JD) 2456298, after Galactic extinction and redshift correction. The right panel shows the spectrum of Mrk 382 taken at JD 2456299, just one day after the date of the spectrum shown in the left panel.

For the variability amplitude ratio of \ to , the range is from about 0.6 (Mrk 486) to 1.2 (Mrk 42), except for Mrk 382 which has the largest host galaxy contamination. Comparison between \ and As shown in Table tab-lag , \ is roughly equal to \ (counting the uncertainties in both lags) in six of our objects, longer in two (Mrk 335 and Mrk 382) and somewhat shorter in one (Mrk 486). For Mrk 382, the measurement of the AGN continuum was badly affected by this uncertainty, which forced us to use the V band photometry instead. In particular, for Mrk 382, we can now recover the 5100 \ AGN continuum variations to a much better accuracy, which is evident by the good agreement with the V band photometry.

sources.summary: The paper flags Mrk 382 as a narrow-line outlier with one of the smallest [OIII] widths and a comparatively large black-hole mass; excluding it strengthens the fitted MBH relation.

quotes.quote: As seen in Figures  fig:msigma_mf and fig:msigma_geb , three objects, Mrk 382, Mrk 734 and PG 1448+273, which have the smallest [ OIII ] widths, deviate from the other NL objects. The reason that they have somewhat larger MBHs than others is not known. However, if excluding them, the correlation becomes even stronger with R_ s =0.762 ( P_s=2.0 10^ 15 ), which can be fitted (using OLS bisector) by M_ BH = 10^ (7.81 0.006) M_ FWHM _ OIII /2.35 200 km s ^ 1 ^ 3.70 0.51 , as shown by the dotted-long dashed line in the right panel of Figures  fig:msigma_mf and fig:msigma_geb .

gmw_00002959.name: Mrk 486
gmw_00002959.overview: Mrk 486 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:0904.1383] that exhibits flux and spectral energy distribution variations in the X-ray band [arXiv:1408.2337]. Its spectra show hard excess emission requiring an absorbed power-law component [arXiv:astro-ph/0703350], while its velocity-delay maps indicate the presence of infalling gas [arXiv:1808.00705, arXiv:1602.01922].

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1535+547 as a radio-loud periodic quasar whose luminosity and 10-year cycle are broadly consistent with a GW-driven near-equal-mass SMBH binary.

quotes.quote: They identified one object, the radio--loud quasar PG 1535+547, whose structure function shows a periodic component with a period of 10 yr. This source has a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol =10^ 13.44 L_ , implying a BH mass of 3 10^9 (f_ Edd /0.3)^ 1 M_ . Figure fig:tres_q1 shows that equal--mass SMBH binaries with this mass are in the GW--driven regime, and f_ var 20 may exhibit a 10--year period. In comparison, Figure fig:tres_q0.01 shows that unequal--mass binaries with this total mass may be in the gas--driven regime, and periodic variability may be exceedingly rare. Thus, we conclude that the identification of one periodic object is roughly consistent with it being an example of a GW--driven, near--equal mass binary.

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 1535+547 in a highly absorbed quasar subset whose spectra show hard excess emission and require an absorbed power-law component.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the remaining four objects (PG 1004+130, 1411+442, 1535+547, and 2214+139) show broad excess emission at energies above 2--3 keV. We added an absorbed power-law component to the model used above to represent this component. This model provides a good description of the spectra. The hydrogen column densities of the absorbed power law are 10^ 23 cm^ 2 for all the cases.
Miller et al. (2006) and Schartel et al. (2005) found absorbed emission in PG 1004+130 and PG 1535+547, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 486 as X-ray variable, with a weather gap in the campaign and an H-light-curve decrease whose cross-correlation signal relies on late high-quality observations.

quotes.quote: Mrk 486 : The source shows significant flux and SED variations in the X-ray band (Ballo 2008). The gap in the data between JD260-290 (see Figure 2) is due to bad weather. The H light curve shows a monotonic decreases with time and much of the signal in the CCF is due to the flux increase at the end of the campaign, on July 14 and 15, 2013. The observations on those night were taken under very good conditions and we have no reason to suspect these measurements.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 486 as having a low Fe II-to-H beta variability/intensity ratio and an Fe II time lag shorter than its H beta lag.

quotes.quote: For the variability amplitude ratio of \ to , the range is from about 0.6 (Mrk 486) to 1.2 (Mrk 42), except for Mrk 382 which has the largest host galaxy contamination. This result is expected since most of the objects in our sample show roughly equal \ and \ (see Table tab-lag ), except for three objects (Mrk 335, Mrk382, and Mrk486), while the previous studies have \ greater than . Comparison between \ and As shown in Table tab-lag , \ is roughly equal to \ (counting the uncertainties in both lags) in six of our objects, longer in two (Mrk 335 and Mrk 382) and somewhat shorter in one (Mrk 486). The \ time lag is longer in Mrk 335 and Mrk 382, those objects with \ < 1, and shorter in Mrk 486 with \ < 1.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 486 has an inflow-like velocity-delay map, while noting uncertainty in its 35–55 day response because of a large light-curve gap.

quotes.quote: The \ maps of Mrk 486 and MCG +06-26-012 suggest the presence of an inflow and outflow, respectively.

Mrk 486 and MCG +06-26-012. The velocity-delay maps of Mrk 486 and MCG +06-26-012 show signatures of inflow (Figure VDM486 ) and outflow (Figure vdm06 ), respectively, consistent with the velocity-resolved lags in du2016b . The response from 35 to 55 days in the map of Mrk 486 is uncertain to some extent, because of the large gap from Julian day (hereafter JD) 2,456,460 to 2,456,488 in the light curves (see Figure spec486 ).
Mrk 486 also shows inflow signatures in its velocity-delay map.

sources.summary: The paper reports clear velocity-resolved time delays for Mrk 486, interpreting its H beta BLR response pattern as infalling gas similar to Mrk 335.

quotes.quote: Mrk 486. It also demonstrates clear signature of infalling gas as the case of Mrk 335. Except for the first bin, the response to the continuum variation in the blueshifted gas lags the response of the gas corresponding to the redshifted side.
The velocity-resolved lags of Mrk 335 and Mrk 486 show the signature of gas infall, while the BLR clouds in Mrk 142 and MCG +06-26-012 tend to be radial outflowing.

gmw_00002960.name: Mrk 493
gmw_00002960.overview: Mrk 493 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:0812.4817, arXiv:2111.15118] featuring a disk-dominated host with a tiny pseudo-bulge, a bar, and spiral arms [arXiv:1710.02194, arXiv:1909.01355]. Its central active nucleus is surrounded by a composite star-forming ring [arXiv:0812.4817] and exhibits a steep, reflection-dominated X-ray continuum [arXiv:2212.06183].

sources.summary: The paper reports 3D spectroscopy of Mrk 493, finding extended continuum and [SII] emission, a composite AGN/star-forming ring, intermediate-width Fe II, and HII regions in the NLR.

quotes.quote: We report the results of 3D spectroscopic observations of Mrk 493 (NLS1 galaxy) with the integral-field spectrograph MPFS of the SAO RAS 6-m telescope. The difference in the slope of the optical continuum emission intensity across the nucleus part and an extensive continuum emission region is detected. The emission in lines (H , H , [ OIII ] , etc.) coincides with a composite nuclear region: an AGN plus a circum-nuclear star-forming ring observed in the HST UV/optical images. The [ SII ] emission region tends to be up to 1kpc around the center. The H and H could be decomposed into three components (broad 2000 km/s. intermediate 700 km/s and narrow 250 km/s).

We found that width ( 750 km/s) of the Fe II lines correspond to the intermediate component, that may indicate a non-BLR origin of the Fe II lines, or that a large fraction of the Fe II emission arise in the outher parts of the BLR. The weak broad component detected in the H , H and He 4686 may come from the unresolved central BLR, but also partly produced by violent starburst in the circum-nuclear ring. Moreover, diagnostic diagrams clearly show presence of the HII regions (not a Sy 1 nucleus) in the NLR of Mrk 493.

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk 493 among NLS1 applications, using redward Fe II and H shifts as gravitational redshifts and inferring very large virial factors tied to high accretion state.

quotes.quote: Mrk 493, IRAS 04416+1215 and Mrk 42 have the redward shifted optical Fe ii and H lines with respect to [ O iii ] 5007 . The virialized motions of BLR clouds are assumed to estimate M_ RM in the RM of H for these NLS1s Du14,Wa14 . The redward shifts of H and Fe ii may be the gravitational redshifts. The Fe ii lag is consistent with the H lag for the same source (see Table 3). Mrk 493 and Mrk 42 have the Fe ii v_ FWHM indistinguishable from the H v_ FWHM .

Mrk 493 and IRAS 04416 have M _ f=1 =75.9 and M _ f=1 =426.6 , respectively Du15 . Mrk 493 and IRAS 04416 have a mean f=73.3 and f=58.7 , respectively. Thus, Mrk 493 and IRAS 04416 have M _ f =1.04 and M _ f =7.27 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper selects Mrk 493 as a superior narrow-line Seyfert 1 iron-template object because of its narrow broad lines, low reddening, bright nucleus, and moderate Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: The new template is based on a spectrum of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 493 obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope. In comparison with the canonical iron template object I Zw 1, Mrk 493 has narrower broad-line widths, lower reddening, and a less extreme Eddington ratio, making it a superior choice for template construction. We carried out a multicomponent spectral decomposition to produce a template incorporating all permitted and forbidden lines of Fe 2 identified in the Mrk 493 spectrum over this wavelength range, as well as lines from Ti 2 , Ni 2 , and Cr 2 .

From an extensive search of catalogs and literature, we identified Mrk 493 at z = 0.03102 as an ideal target. Mrk 493 has long been known as a very narrow-lined NLS1 galaxy Osterbrock1985 , and its broad emission lines are substantially narrower than those of I Zw 1 (FWHM = 860 versus 1240 ; Greene&Ho2007 ), enabling a more accurate decomposition between blended spectral features. Mrk 493 also has very low Galactic foreground reddening of E(B-V)=0.022 mag Schlafly+2011 and insignificant intrinsic reddening Crenshaw+2002 , while it shows \ emission strength similar to that of I Zw 1 and its nucleus is very bright, with SDSS fiber magnitude 15.5 . While I Zw 1 has an Eddington ratio of 2.5 calculated from the BH mass of 10^ 6.97 \ and bolometric luminosity of 10^ 45.47 \ Huang+2019 , Mrk 493 has a less extreme value of 0.5 , calculated from BH mass of 10^ 6.18 \ and bolometric luminosity of 10^ 44.01 \ Wang+2014 using a bolometric correction factor of 9.26 Shen+2008 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 493 has a strong narrow Gaussian H I profile requiring fixed BusyFit parameters, while prior Arecibo velocities mostly agree.

quotes.quote: Mrk 493: The HI profile of Mrk 493 exhibits a strong, narrow Gaussian shape (see Fig. \ batmen2 ). As standard for fitting a Gaussian-shaped profile, the BusyFit parameters controlling the central trough amplitude, offset, and polynomial degree ( , x_ p , and n , respectively) were set to 0, and we found that we also needed to hold the half-width parameter ( w ) fixed at 10 to allow the profile fit to converge. All previous observations utilized the 305 m Arecibo telescope. The V _ R reported by is consistent with our measurement, and their W _ 50 value agrees with our measurement at the 2 level. also reports a consistent V _ R value.

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 493 with a soft excess, ionized absorption, steep reflection-dominated continuum, Iron K emission, and a constrained high spin in one reflection model.

quotes.quote: Mrk 493 The soft excess component of Mrk 493 shows a temperature of kT _ bb =75 4 eV. In the soft energy band it shows also the presence of one ionized absorption component, one with turbulent velocity 100 km s^ 1 . The first fit with the phenomenological Model A ( ^2 /dof=1002/970) resulted in a steep continuum, =2.42 0.03 with a high energy cut-off E_c= 104 32 keV which appeared to be reflection dominated: R_ refl =1.32 0.08 . The Iron K emission line flux was 9.96 0.58 10^ 7 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

When fitting the spectra of Mrk 493 with Model C4 ( ^2 /dof=1030/971), which accounts for the soft X-ray excess via a broken power-law emissivity function, we found a break radius value of R_ br =4.85_ 0.86 ^ +0.18 and the inner emissivity index q _1=7.44_ 0.83 ^ +0.86 and we are also able to constrain the spin value, which resulted to be a =0.94 0.01 .

sources.summary: The paper models the saturated host as a tiny pseudo-bulge, bar, and spiral disk, with the bar and disk fit using the hybrid method.

quotes.quote: Mrk 493 (Fig. 11.16) --- \ \ The WFPC2 mosaic image is saturated at the center. The host galaxy appears to have tiny pseudo-bulge ( n=0.25 ), a bar, and a disk with spiral arms. The bar and the disk are fit by the hybrid model. Orban de Xivry et al. (2011) also find the host galaxy to contain a pseudo-bulge ( n 0.75 ).

sources.summary: Hermione is a disk-dominated AGN host whose PSF-subtracted image retained star-formation-ionized extended emission; the authors used an empirical threshold, making its calculated outflow rate a lower limit.

quotes.quote: An example of the 5007 centered, continuum centered, continuum subtracted and PSF subtracted images for two sources, Hermione and Padma are shown in Figure fig:examplereduce . At this point we would ideally sum the flux within 1r_ petro (as calculated by the SDSS pipeline) in order to derive the total flux in the outflow. However, whilst Padma does not have any extended emission due to star formation ionisation left in the final PSF subtracted image, it is clear that Hermione does. We therefore also show the one dimensional traces for each source across the final PSF subtracted image in Figure fig:examplesigmadet to show how we determined an empirical limit for Hermione, above which the total flux in the 5007 outflow was summed. This method of empirically determining the limit above which to sum the flux was employed for Hermione ( 4.8 ), Neville ( 2.3 ) and Cho ( 5 ), so that the calculated outflow rates are lower limits.

sources.summary: The paper reports only 27 observations for Mrk 493, yielding a poorer cross-correlation function and a time lag consistent with zero, so it excludes the source from distance analysis.

quotes.quote: Mrk 493 : We obtained only 27 observations from April to June 2013. This makes the quality of the CCF poorer than in other objects and results in a larger uncertainty on the time lag which is consistent with zero (although the peak of the CCF is very clear with r_ max 0.54 ). We report on the various results obtained for this source but do not include it in the distance analysis.

gmw_00002961.name: MCG +06-26-012
gmw_00002961.overview: MCG +06-26-012 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galaxy hosted within an Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1408.2337]. The system contains a sub-Eddington supermassive black hole [arXiv:1602.01922, arXiv:1808.00705] and exhibits velocity-resolved signatures of radial gas outflow [arXiv:1602.01922, arXiv:1808.00705].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG +06-26-012 as an Sb-hosted NLS1 with a significant H lag, measured black-hole mass and host-subtracted luminosity, but not a SEAMBH.

quotes.quote: MCG +06-26-012: This NLS1 is situated in a Sb galaxy (see HST image in Figure 1). We find FWHM(H )=1685 (see also Grupe 1999; Veron-Cetty 2001). The CCF shows a statistically significant time lag of = 23.3_ 5.8 ^ +7.5 days with r_ max 0.9 . From Eqn. ( eq:virial ), we get =(8.3_ 2.3 ^ +2.9 ) 10^6 . The host-galaxy subtracted luminosity is L_ 5100 = (0.47 0.10) 10^ 43 .
Using our method to determine accretion rate [ Eqn. ( eq:dotm_min ) below ] we get m _ min =0.02 , indicating that this object is not a SEAMBH.

sources.summary: The paper finds an ambiguous radial-outflow signature in MCG +06-26-012, noting shorter blue-side H beta delays, large errors, and its sub-Eddington status.

quotes.quote: MCG +06-26-012. A signature of outflow is shown in this object that the time delays ( 20 days) in the blue part of are slightly shorter than those ( 30 days) in the red part, however, the outflow signs are ambiguous a little bit because of the large errors. The large error bars are caused by the inhomogeneous sampling and the short monitoring period comparing with the variation in the light curves. MCG +06-26-012 is the only sub-Eddington object in the present sample (see details in Paper II).
The velocity-resolved lags of Mrk 335 and Mrk 486 show the signature of gas infall, while the BLR clouds in Mrk 142 and MCG +06-26-012 tend to be radial outflowing.

sources.summary: The paper says MCG +06-26-012 is sub-Eddington and finds outflow signatures in its velocity-delay map, though the shortest-delay feature is less reliable.

quotes.quote: MCG +06-26-012 was selected as a SEAMBH candidate in our sample, but subsequent work revealed that it is a sub-Eddington object ( wang2014a,du2015 ).
The \ maps of Mrk 486 and MCG +06-26-012 suggest the presence of an inflow and outflow, respectively.
The weak feature from 0 to 7 days in the map of MCG +06-26-012 is less reliable, due to the gaps from JD 2,456,315 to 2,456,386 in the light curves (see Figure spec06 ).

gmw_00002962.name: GOODSN-17940
gmw_00002962.overview: GOODSN-17940 is an actively star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z = 4.4121 [arXiv:1707.05331]. It exhibits strong H-beta emission lines, a high star formation rate, and a nebular oxygen abundance of approximately 0.2 solar [arXiv:1707.05331].

sources.summary: The paper presents GOODSN-17940 as a z=4.4121 actively star-forming galaxy with detected rest-frame optical lines, strong inferred H emission, high SFR, and roughly 0.2 solar oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: During the MOSFIRE Deep Evolution Field (MOSDEF) survey, we observed the galaxy GOODSN-17940 with the Keck I/MOSFIRE spectrograph. The K band spectrum of GOODSN-17940 includes significant detections of the [ O ii ] 3726,3729 , [ Ne iii ] 3869 , and H emission lines and a tentative detection of H , indicating z_ spec =4.4121 . GOODSN-17940 is an actively star-forming z>4 galaxy based on its K band spectrum and broadband spectral energy distribution.

A significant excess relative to the surrounding continuum is present in the Spitzer /IRAC channel 1 photometry of GOODSN-17940, due primarily to strong H emission with a rest-frame equivalent width of EW(H )=1200 . Based on the assumption of 0.5 Z_ models and the Calzetti attenuation curve, GOODSN-17940 is characterized by M_*=5.0^ +4.3 _ 0.2 10^9 M_ . The Balmer decrement inferred from H /H is used to dust correct the H emission, yielding SFR(H )=320^ +190 _ 140 M_ yr ^ 1 . These M_* and SFR values place GOODSN-17940 an order of magnitude in SFR above the z 4 star-forming ``main sequence." Finally, we use the observed ratio of [ Ne iii ] / [ O ii ] to estimate the nebular oxygen abundance in GOODSN-17940, finding O/H 0.2 (O/H) _ .

gmw_00002963.name: host galaxy of GRB 060218/SN 2006aj
gmw_00002963.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060218/SN 2006aj is a low-luminosity, metal-poor star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610417] with a compact structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603686]. It exhibits high-excitation emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0603686] and has a modest star formation rate of approximately 0.05 to 0.15 solar masses per year [arXiv:astro-ph/0603686, arXiv:astro-ph/0610417].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J032139.68+165201.7 as GRB 060218's compact dwarf starburst host, with coincident transient position, high-excitation line ratios, modest star formation, and low luminosity.

quotes.quote: The position of the OT in an MDM 2.4m image was measured with respect to the apparent host galaxy SDSS J032139.68+165201.7 using a set of unsaturated stars common to both images. We find that the OT is centered on the compact galaxy to less than 0^ .2 (130 pc) in each coordinate. This is to be compared with the galaxy's half-light radius, r_ 1/2 1^ .5 (1.0 kpc).

The observed line ratios are typical of a high-excitation starburst galaxy. In particular, the measured H line flux, uncorrected for extinction at the host galaxy, implies a star-formation rate (SFR) equivalent to 0.05--0.15 M_ yr ^ 1 kennicutt . A similar computation of the SFR using [ O 2 ] yields 0.09 0.05 M_ yr ^ 1 . We also find a good agreement with the SFR derived using an extrapolation of the UV continuum luminosity of the host galaxy as tabulated in cool .
We also estimate M_B 16.01 for SDSS J032139.68+165201.7, which ranks this galaxy in the bright end of Local Group dwarf galaxies mateo .

sources.summary: The paper treats the host of GRB 060218 as an emission-line GRB host with an uncertain [O III] 4363 detection; its temperature-sensitive abundance estimate is handled cautiously.

quotes.quote: The temperature sensitive [ OIII ] 4363/ [ OIII ] 5007 line ratio has been detected in four hosts (Table tte ). These are the hosts of GRB 980425, GRB 020903, GRB 031203, and GRB 060218 (Hammer et al. \ 2006; Prochaska et al. \ 2004; Wiersema et al. \ 2007). Though according to Wiersema et al. \ (2007) the [ OIII ] 4363 line in GRB 060218 is 5 significant, the line in the spectrum is somehow doubtful, because located in a noisy part of the spectrum. We will consider this detection with caution.

Metallicities derived with this method are low (Table tte ). Temperatures in the HII regions are slightly larger than 10^4 K (Table tte ), with the exception of the host of GRB 060218 (Wiersema et al. \ 2007), for which the temperature is twice larger. However, as already said, the detection of O 4363 in this host is weak.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060218/SN 2006aj host as a compact, low-luminosity, metal-poor star-forming dwarf with SDSS magnitudes, small host extinction, measured SFR, and subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: High-resolution optical spectroscopy together with SDSS pre-burst observations revealed that the host galaxy of SN2006aj is a low-luminosity, metal-poor star-forming dwarf galaxy.

The host galaxy of GRB 060218/SN 2006aj was imaged pre-burst by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey . These images were used to obtain its magnitudes , as the SN brightness was hiding the host component, and to measure its diameter ( 2 kpc, for z =0.033). The VLT/UVES spectrum allowed a measurement of the total extinction toward SN 2006aj and it turned out that the main contribution is from our Galaxy ( E(B-V)= 0.127 0.005 ), while E(B-V)_ host is only 0.044 0.001 . The latter is supported by the SED of the galaxy (see Fig. figure4 ). In addition, spectroscopic studies led to an estimate of the SFR 0.07 \ M_ /year and of the metallicity Z 0.07 \ Z_ .

sources.summary: The paper derives BVRI magnitudes for the host galaxy of GRB 060218/SN 2006aj from pre-burst SDSS imaging, correcting calibration offsets so unresolved host light can be subtracted.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB 060218/SN 2006aj was imaged pre-burst by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (Cool et al. , Adelman-McCarthy et al. ). It was pointed out (Modjaz et al. , Hicken et al. ) that there are offsets between the SDSS calibration and later efforts, rendering the transformed host-galaxy magnitudes of Cool et al. ( ) too bright. The values used for the host galaxy in this paper are B=20.57 0.07 , V=20.18 0.04 , R=20.03 0.03 and I=19.58 0.06 , not corrected for Galactic extinction.

We note that our host-galaxy magnitudes are consistent to those of Sollerman et al. ( ) within the errors. As the host galaxy and SN 2006aj were not separated at the resolution of the images, the host magnitudes have to be subtracted.

sources.summary: The paper uses the GRB 060218 host as a well-studied target, connecting supernova context, host properties, metal abundances, ISM velocity structure, and new VLT datasets to extinction analysis.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB 060218 is a particularly attractive target to do this. The supernova accompanying this GRB is well studied, and therefore a connection with host galaxy properties (spectroscopic, spectral energy distribution; see e.g. Savaglio, Glazebrook Le Borgne 2009) may provide tight constraints on the progenitor evolution. In a previous paper (Wiersema et al. 2007; hereafter W07) we performed a study of the metal abundances in this host and the ISM velocity structure, both obtained through emission and absorption lines detected in a VLT UVES spectrum of the supernova (2006aj). We obtained three more datasets of this source, which we will use to probe the extinction properties of this host in this paper.

gmw_00002964.name: brightest galaxy (BCG)
gmw_00002964.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of the cluster 1007 is located at a redshift of z=1.082 [arXiv:1004.0093]. This galaxy is offset from both the apparent center of the galaxy distribution and the cluster's X-ray emission, suggesting an unrelaxed cluster structure [arXiv:1004.0093].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1007's BCG by position, color, and magnitude, noting it is offset from both the galaxy distribution center and X-ray emission, consistent with an unrelaxed cluster.

quotes.quote: The projected spatial distribution of cluster galaxies appears to be elongated, and the brightest galaxy lies off center with respect to the X-ray emission indicating a not yet relaxed structure.

The cluster has its brightest galaxy (BCG) at position = 10^ h 07^ m 49.9^ s and = +12^ 58^ ' 40^ '' between structures B1 and B2 (see Fig. f:opt_composit ), i.e. away from the apparent center of the galaxy distribution by about 20'' and away from the X-ray position. The BCG has an apparent magnitude z_ BCG = 20.51 0.06 , an osberved color (R-z)_ BCG = 1.92 0.08 and an absolute magnitude M_ BCG, z = -23.8 .

We have presented results of an initial study of the X-ray selected cluster 1007. We determine a redshift of z=1.082 based on 19 spectroscopic confirmed members. As most prominent property, the cluster shows several strong lensing features. It is an optically rich cluster; the absence of a dominant BCG and the elongated distribution of member galaxies suggest a not yet relaxed structure.

gmw_00002965.name: J0037+2058
gmw_00002965.overview: J0037+2058 is a double quasar system located at a redshift of z=2.047 [arXiv:2109.06881]. While the system is likely a dual quasar, the possibility of gravitational lensing cannot be excluded [arXiv:2109.06881].

sources.summary: The paper confirms both components of J0037+2058 as z=2.047 quasars and classifies the system as likely dual, while noting lensing remains possible because the spectra are nearly identical.

quotes.quote: Double quasar (dual/lensed quasar) J0037+2058 : The broad \ and \ emission lines in both spectra confirm that both sources are quasars at z=2.047 . Though two spectra are similar, the slopes of the continuum and the intensity of the emission lines such as \ are slightly different. It is likely that J0037+2058 is a dual quasar. However, the lensing hypothesis can not be excluded; the different flux ratio might be due to microlesing or dust extinction in the lens galaxy e.g. Falco1999 .

gmw_00002966.name: J0118-0104
gmw_00002966.overview: J0118-0104 is a dual quasar system located at a redshift of z=0.739 [arXiv:2109.06881]. The two sources exhibit distinct emission line shapes and a noticeable velocity offset, confirming they are a pair of active galactic nuclei rather than a gravitationally lensed image [arXiv:2109.06881].

sources.summary: The paper confirms J0118-0104 as a z=0.739 dual quasar, citing broad emission in both sources, different line shapes, a velocity offset, and prior rejection of a lens interpretation.

quotes.quote: J0118 0104 : The broad \ emission lines in both spectra confirm both sources are quasars at z=0.739 . The shapes of emission lines between two sources are totally different and there is a noticeable velocity offset, both of which affirm that it is a dual quasar. A recent lens survey Lemon20 also reported it as a nearly identical quasar pair, not a lensed quasar.

gmw_00002967.name: J0932+0722
gmw_00002967.overview: J0932+0722 is a dual quasar system located at a redshift of approximately z = 1.99 [arXiv:2109.06881]. The two component sources are confirmed as quasars by their broad emission lines and exhibit a velocity offset of 850 km/s alongside differing optical-to-infrared flux ratios [arXiv:2109.06881].

sources.summary: The paper confirms both J0932+0722 components as quasars at z about 1.99 and classifies the system as dual based on an 850 km/s velocity offset and differing optical/infrared flux ratios.

quotes.quote: J0932+0722 : The broad \ and \ lines in both spectra confirm that both source are quasars at z=1.996 and z=1.989 . The significant velocity offset of 850 km/s between two sources measured from \ lines affirm that it is a dual quasar, not a lens system. The flux ratio in optical is also significantly different than that in Infrared Inada08 .

gmw_00002968.name: PSO J347.2702 + 48.7066
gmw_00002968.overview: PSO J347.2702 + 48.7066 is a massive host galaxy of the repeating fast radio burst FRB 20220912A at a redshift of z = 0.0771 [arXiv:2211.09049, arXiv:2405.04802]. It exhibits a composite nuclear classification [arXiv:2211.09049] and contains unresolved radio emission likely originating from star-forming activity [arXiv:2405.04802].

sources.summary: The DSA-110 localization identifies PSO J347.2702 + 48.7066 as the likely FRB 20220912A host, with redshift, stellar mass, size, dust, star formation, and low host DM measured.

quotes.quote: The DSA-110 localization overlaps with the galaxy PSO J347.2702 + 48.7066 at a redshift z=0.0771 , which we identify as the likely host. PSO J347.2702 + 48.7066 has a stellar mass of approximately 10^ 10 M_ , modest internal dust extinction, and a star-formation rate likely in excess of 0.1 M_ yr ^ 1 . The host-galaxy contribution to the dispersion measure is likely 50 pc cm ^ 3 . The FRB 20220912A source is therefore likely viewed along a tenuous plasma column through the host galaxy. compact objects --- radio interferometry --- radio telescopes --- radio transient sources Eight repeating sources of fast radio bursts (FRBs) have been localized to host galaxies clw+17,mnh+20,mpm+20,bkm+21,kmn+22,rll+22,bha+22,nal+22 .

The localization is consistent with a single host galaxy, PSO J347.2702 + 48.7066, at z=0.0771 0.0001 . The galaxy has a stellar mass of M_ * =10.0 0.1 , and an effective radius of 2.2 0.1 kpc. Nebular emission lines from the nucleus of this galaxy indicates a ``composite'' BPT classification. The DM contributed by the FRB host galaxy along its sightline is low.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSO J347.2702+48.7066 as FRB 20220912A's massive, moderately star-forming host at z=0.0771; uGMRT detects unresolved 1.26 GHz emission likely from star formation rather than an AGN.

quotes.quote: In September 2022, CHIME CHIMEoverview discovered FRB 20220912A, a repeating FRB source having dispersion measure (DM) of 219.46 pc cm ^ 3 McKinven22 , subsequently localized with arcsecond precision in the outskirts of a moderately star forming, massive galaxy at redshift z = 0.0771 ravi23 .

In our uGMRT image at 1.26 GHz, we detect a continuum source spatially coincident with the coordinates of PSO J347.2702+48.7066, the host galaxy of FRB 20220912A. The source integrated and peak flux densities are consistent at 1 level, so we consider it as unresolved in our observations. Its centroid have coordinates R.A. (J2000) =23^ h 09^ m 04.88^ s 0.017 s, Dec. (J2000) =+48^ 42'24.04'' 0.25'' .

Additionally, we reported 3 hr long continuum radio observations of FRB 20220912A field using the band-5 ( 1050-1450 MHz) of the uGMRT. We detected a continuum radio source of 240 40 \ Jy flux density, spatially coincident with the FRB 20220912A VLBI localisation. As being not detected in recent, deep, EVN observations Hewitt23b , we suggest that this continuum radio source is possibly originating from a region of star formation, potentially located in the vicinity of the FRB source. This is corroborated by the 0.6'' offset (0.9 kpc in physical size) between the source centroid and the geometric centre of the optical host galaxy, which excludes the hypothesis that the radio source is being powered by an AGN.

gmw_00002969.name: DFX1
gmw_00002969.overview: DFX1 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located within the Coma cluster [arXiv:2309.15148, arXiv:2405.09104]. It has an effective radius of 3.5 kpc [arXiv:1705.08513], hosts a population of globular clusters [arXiv:1705.08513, arXiv:2401.07388], and exhibits a stellar quenching timescale of 6 to 8 Gyr [arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: DFX1 is identified as a Coma UDG observed with KCWI, with published SED-fitting populations, mixed-time quenching behavior, and spectral-range fitting checks.

quotes.quote: These are the Coma cluster UDGs DFX1 and DF07, along with the Perseus cluster UDG PUDG-R27. DFX1 and DF07 were observed on the nights 2021, April 16th and 17th (Program U105, PI: Brodie) using KCWI with the medium slicer, BL grating and a 4550 central wavelength. Final exposure times were 25200 seconds for DFX1, 19200 seconds for DF07 and 26400 seconds for PUDG-R27.

VCC 1287, PUDG-R24, Yagi358, DFX1 and DF07 have published SED fitting stellar populations (e.g., Pandya2017 ; Buzzo2022 ). 4 UDGs are located in the `mixed times' regions (PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84, DFX1, DF44) all presenting quenching timescales of 6--8 Gyr.

sources.summary: DFX1 is a Coma UDG for which the paper notes disputed globular-cluster totals, adopts a specific count, and derives radial velocity from the reported redshift.

quotes.quote: DFX1 DFX1 is in the Coma Cluster. There is currently some disagreement on the total GC numbers of DF X1 in the literature Saifollahi2021, Saifollahi2022 . See further Forbes2024 for a discussion of these numbers. Following this work, we choose the vanDokkum2017 GC number. When quoting the N_ GC from vanDokkum2017 we use the number listed in their abstract which is slightly different from the number in Table 1.

sources.summary: DFX1 is presented as a Coma UDG similar to Dragonfly 44, with a 3.5 kpc effective radius, measured velocity dispersion, rich globular cluster population, and confirmed cluster membership.

quotes.quote: The galaxies, Dragonfly 44 and DFX1, have effective radii of 4.7 kpc and 3.5 kpc and velocity dispersions of 47^ +8 _ 6 \ and 30^ +7 _ 7 , respectively.
Both galaxies are associated with a striking number of compact objects, tentatively identified as globular clusters: N_ gc =74 18 for Dragonfly 44 and N_ gc =62 17 for DFX1.

DFX1 and Dragonfly 42 contribute to the steadily growing sample of UDGs with confirmed distances (see Kadowaki , Zaritsky , Donnerstein 2017), and our redshifts confirm that Dragonfly 44, DFX1, and Dragonfly 42 are all members of the Coma cluster.

sources.summary: DFX1 is a Coma cluster UDG for which the paper adopts the larger vD17 globular-cluster count after comparing discrepant literature estimates.

quotes.quote: DFX1 DFX1 is located in the Coma cluster. As noted in Table 1, vD17 estimated DFX1 to host 63 17 GCs, while S22 suggested 17 ^ +5 _ 6 GCs. Again, given the general agreement between vD17 and L18, we adopt the vD17 GC count.

gmw_00002970.name: VLSB-B
gmw_00002970.overview: VLSB-B is an extremely diffuse low surface brightness galaxy located within the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1507.02270, arXiv:2401.07388]. It is characterized by an exponential profile [arXiv:1507.02270] and hosts a modest population of candidate globular clusters [arXiv:1507.02270, arXiv:2401.07388].

sources.summary: The paper describes VLSB-B as an extremely diffuse Virgo LSB galaxy with exponential structure, a modest globular-cluster excess, and no obvious tidal distortion.

quotes.quote: Ellipticity estimates for VLSB-B and -C come from GALFIT modeling; VLSB-A is too low in surface brightness to yield a meaningful estimate. We report average colors for VLSB-A and -B, but cannot measure a color for VLSB-C as it falls outside the B imaging footprint of the Schmidt survey. The derived properties given in Table galprops show that, like the extreme Coma LSBs (vD15a, Koda 2015; K15), these objects are reasonably well characterized by exponential profiles ( n 1.0 ). They are large ( =40 -120 , or 3-10 kpc at the Virgo distance), extremely low in central surface brightness ( _ V,0 26.5 ), and fairly red ( =0.6--0.7).

VLSB-B presents an interesting contrast, however, as we find an excess of both resolved sources and point sources within the galaxy. Several of these sources appear to be background galaxies, and the point source excess appears not to be resolved RGB stars, but rather a small population of globular clusters.

We see a modest excess of these candidate globulars in VLSB-B, with N=6 3 objects in the central 0.5 , after removal of elongated objects (presumably background galaxies) and subtraction of a local background. This tentative detection of globular clusters supports the conclusion that VLSB-B is an extremely diffuse LSB galaxy located within Virgo.

sources.summary: VLSB-B is treated as a Virgo cluster galaxy with a rich GC candidate system, Keck/DEIMOS GC velocities, possible foreground contamination, and no stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: VLSB-B Previous selection of GC candidates by from imaging indicated a total GC system of 26.1 9.9. Recently, T23 obtained Keck/DEIMOS spectra for 14 GCs and derived a GC system velocity dispersion of 45 ^ +14 _ 10 km s ^ 1 . This Virgo cluster galaxy has a recession velocity of +40 km s ^ 1 so foreground star interlopers remain a possibility. A stellar velocity dispersion is not available. Following T23 we use their updated distance of 12.7 Mpc to derive an effective radius of R _e = 1.7 kpc after circularisation using b/a=0.83 2018ApJ...856L..31T .

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-11 is a high-dispersion, dark-matter-dominated UDG; HST distance estimates place it in front of Virgo and likely no longer cluster-bound.

quotes.quote: These outliers include: NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, NGVSUDG-20 and NGVSUDG-A04, with NGVSUDG-09 being borderline compatible.

We estimate the dark matter fraction within the radius that contains half of the dynamical tracers in each galaxy using the relation: f_ DM, 1/2 = M_ 1/2 M^*_ 1/2 M_ 1/2 Table table:spec shows the dark matter fraction for each of our UDGs, which ranges from consistent with 0 dark matter in NGVSUDG-10 to 100 for NGVSUDG-05, NGVSUDG-09, NGVSUDG-11, NGVSUDG-19, and NGVSUDG-20, with a median dark matter fraction for all the UDGs in our sample f_ DM,1/2 98 .

These distances suggest that, while NGVSUDG-A04 is on the far end of the cluster, NGVSUDG-11 is in front of the cluster most likely no longer gravitationally associated with it and, thus, now in isolation as there are no other foreground galaxies at that distance projected into the core of the Virgo cluster.

gmw_00002971.name: VCC615
gmw_00002971.overview: VCC615 is an ultradiffuse dwarf galaxy located on the far side of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2111.01553, arXiv:2401.07388]. It exhibits an undisturbed morphology [arXiv:2111.01553] and hosts an estimated system of approximately 30 globular clusters [arXiv:2401.07388].

sources.summary: The paper measures VCC 615's TRGB distance, places it on Virgo's far side, and argues its orbit and mass make it a long-lived UDG.

quotes.quote: We use deep Hubble Space Telescope imaging to derive a distance to the Virgo Cluster ultradiffuse galaxy (UDG) VCC 615 using the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) distance estimator. We detect 5,023 stars within the galaxy, down to a 50 completeness limit of F814W 28.0 , using counts in the surrounding field to correct for contamination due to background sources and Virgo intracluster stars. We derive an extinction-corrected F814W tip magnitude of m_ tip,0 =27.19^ +0.07 _ 0.05 , yielding a distance of d=17.7^ +0.6 _ 0.4 Mpc. This places VCC 615 on the far side of the Virgo Cluster ( d_ Virgo =16.5 Mpc), at a Virgocentric distance of 1.3 Mpc and near the virial radius of the main body of Virgo. Coupling this distance with the galaxy's observed radial velocity, we find that VCC 615 is on an outbound trajectory, having survived a recent passage through the inner parts of the cluster.

Indeed, our orbit modeling gives a 50 chance the galaxy passed inside the Virgo core ( <620 kpc) within the past Gyr, although very close passages directly through the cluster center ( <200 kpc) are unlikely. Given VCC 615's undisturbed morphology, we argue that the galaxy has experienced no recent and sudden transformation into a UDG due to the cluster potential, but rather is a long-lived UDG whose relatively wide orbit and large dynamical mass protect it from stripping and destruction by Virgo cluster tides.

sources.summary: VCC615 is included with an estimated rich GC system, GC velocity dispersion from eight clusters, uncertain dynamical mass, no stellar dispersion, and a Virgo-cluster distance placement.

quotes.quote: VCC615 Of the total GC system of 30.3 9.6 estimated by , T23 measured the radial velocities for 8 GCs. From these they calculated a GC system velocity dispersion = 36 ^ +22 _ 18 km s ^ 1 . With only 8 GCs, and large associated uncertainties, a derived dynamical mass should be considered quite uncertain. A stellar velocity dispersion for VCC615 is not available. We follow T23 and use a distance of 17.7 Mpc which places VCC615 at the far side of the Virgo cluster and take their effective radius of R _e = 2.0 kpc.

gmw_00002972.name: VCC1448
gmw_00002972.overview: VCC1448 is a low-mass galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2401.07388]. It features an unusually rich globular cluster system, a measured stellar velocity dispersion, and evidence of bulk rotation [arXiv:2401.07388].

sources.summary: VCC1448 is described as a Virgo NUDGE galaxy with low-mass stellar content, an unusually rich GC system, measured GC velocity dispersion, stellar dispersion, and evidence for rotation.

quotes.quote: VCC1448 At 1 mag brighter than the standard UDG definition and with R _e = 3.5 kpc (T23), VCC1448 is an example of a NUDGE galaxy, located in the Virgo cluster. Its stellar mass of M _ = 2.6 10 ^9 M _ places it in the low mass galaxy regime yet it has a remarkably rich GC system with estimating 99.3 17.6 GCs. Thus it appears to host around half the number of GCs associated with the Milky Way. T23 measured a GC system velocity dispersion from 9 GCs to be 48 ^ +16 _ 11 km s ^ 1 . However, Gannon, J.

(2023, priv. comm.) measure a stellar velocity dispersion. They also find evidence of bulk rotation in the galaxy at the 20 km s ^ 1 level. In Table 2 we calculate the dynamical mass using the combined rotation and dispersion velocities.

gmw_00002973.name: IRAS 00256-0208
gmw_00002973.overview: IRAS 00256-0208 is an OH megamaser system with a radio redshift of 0.27654 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals a tidal tail, which provides strong evidence that the system is undergoing a galaxy merger [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 00256-0208 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 00256-0208 IRAS 00256-0208 was first found and identified as an OHM by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS00256-0208 ). The radio redshift ( z_ OH =0.27654 ) is in good agreement with the optical observation ( z_ OC =0.27630 ) Saunders2000 , and their spatial offset is less than 1.5 ' . This OHM has the third highest redshift among all known OHMs in the main 1667 MHz OH emission lines, the highest being J095903.22+025356.1 with a redshift of z_ OH 0.7092 , recently discovered by Jarvis2023 . In addition, the OH spectrum of this OHM has a wider linewidth of W_ 50 =279.6 due to its intrinsic multiple velocity components, and its 1667 MHz line is mixed with the 1665 MHz line. This can be a strong indication of merging activity, as could also be seen in the optical RGB image, as evidenced by the tidal tail.

gmw_00002974.name: IRAS 01562+2527
gmw_00002974.overview: IRAS 01562+2527 is an OH megamaser system [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals two compact cores, one of which displays a spiral tidal tail structure that suggests a merging system [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 01562+2527 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 01562+2527 IRAS 01562+2527 has been confirmed as an OHM by the Arecibo OHM Survey Darling2002a . The FAST observed spectral profile is consistent with that of Darling2002a . The only difference is that Darling2002a identified it as the name of IRAS 01562+2528, which is not listed in the PSCz catalog compiled by Saunders2000 . The optical RGB image shows two compact cores, one of which has a spiral tidal tail structure (see Figures Fig:IRAS01562+2527 ). This may indicate a merging system.

gmw_00002975.name: IRAS 02524+2046
gmw_00002975.overview: IRAS 02524+2046 is a very luminous hydroxyl megamaser system [arXiv:1211.2023, arXiv:2401.15397] that exhibits time-variable emission [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging of the galaxy reveals a compact nucleus surrounded by an elongated tidal tail [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F02524+2046 as a very luminous OHM with complex Stokes I and V spectra, requiring many Gaussian components and yielding multiple confident field detections.

quotes.quote: IRAS F02524+2046 : DG02 discovered this very luminous OHM, which features three regions of narrow 1667 MHz emission, and two distinct areas of 1665 MHz emission that correspond to two of the 1667 MHz emission regions. Figure fig:02524 shows a Stokes V spectrum that is similarly rich, relative to the other Stokes V spectra of sources in this survey. There are three large amplitude features right next to one another, at frequencies corresponding to the brightest emission in the Stokes I spectrum. Fitting all of this structure was challenging.

Ultimately, we used thirteen components for fitting 1667 MHz features, which are all listed in Table tab:02524 , and produced a fit to Stokes I that still has difficulty with the brightest feature in the spectrum, and only partially captures the complex Stokes V structure. In particular, a strong dip near 1412.1 MHz is completely missed by the fits, and no combination of Gaussians we attempted was able to simultaneously fit that feature and the other strong features in the Stokes V spectrum of this source. Nevertheless, the fields and errors associated with these fits yield multiple confident detections, as well as two fits that exceed 3 but in which we have no confidence.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 02524+2046 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 02524+2046 This OHM was first confirmed by the Arecibo OHM Survey Darling2002a , which detected an extremely strong peak reaching 40 mJy. However, the peak detected by FAST is 21.78 1.83 mJy. This is probably because the OH flux density is time variable. The emission lines are strong and narrow and show an extremely good correspondence between the 1667 and 1665 MHz lines. This allows us to measure the hyperﬁne line ratio of the both.

The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =2.93 0.23 (see Tables tab:ratio ), which is slightly lower than the measured result Darling2002a , probably due to the flux variation. The optical RGB image shows a compact nucleus with an elongated tidal tail surrounding the central nuclei (see Figure Fig:IRAS02524+2046 ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses IRAS F02524+2046 as an OH megamaser whose matched 1665/1667 MHz components include an unusual high-velocity feature with equal peak fluxes and a low integrated hyperfine ratio.

quotes.quote: IRAS F02524+2046. Darling2002a detected this OHM, noting multiple matched components in the 1665 MHz and 1667 MHz lines. They provided hyperfine ratios for individual narrow features in the spectrum, with values = 1.4, 5.63, 1.88 from high velocity to low. In our observations, shown in Figure fig:02524 , it is only possible to distinguish two peaks in the 1665 MHz emission that align with peaks in the 1667 MHz emission. The highest velocity feature is quite unusual, in that the 1665 MHz feature is so strong. The peak fluxes of the two lines are equal, and the ratio we find for the integrated flux at velocities 54,260--54,360 is = 1.3 , consistent with what Darling2002a found.

gmw_00002976.name: IRAS 03111-0244
gmw_00002976.overview: IRAS 03111-0244 is an OH megamaser system with a measured OH redshift of 0.18956 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals a merging system characterized by elongated tidal tails surrounding its central nucleus [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 03111-0244 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 03111-0244 The OHM IRAS 03111-0244 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS03111-0244 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.18956, while the optically observed redshift is 0.18833 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). The velocity difference is 400 , and the spatial peak offset is 1.5 ' between the two. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. Therefore this source is probably an OHM corresponding to IRAS 03111-0244.
The optical RGB image shows a typical merging system with elongated tidal tails surrounding the central nucleus.

gmw_00002977.name: IRAS 06487+2208
gmw_00002977.overview: IRAS 06487+2208 is an OH megamaser host galaxy that exhibits LINER-like nuclear activity [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging of the galaxy shows an unresolved core, but suggests the possible presence of a second nucleus or a northern tidal tail [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 06487+2208 as an OH megamaser host with LINER-like nuclear activity plus FAST line measurements and interaction or tidal morphology.

quotes.quote: IRAS 06487+2208 This OHM was first found and reported by the Arecibo OHM Surveys Darling2000 . The 1667 MHz peak flux density observed by FAST is consistent with that of Darling2000 . The strong corresponding 1665 MHz line is also visible. The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =3.27 0.63 (see Tables tab:ratio ). This source has been proposed as a composite of and LINER features based on the Osterbrock spectral line ratio classification method Osterbrock1989,Veilleux1995 .
The optical RGB image is unresolved, but there may be another nucleus or tidal tail to the north of the main nucleus.

gmw_00002978.name: IRAS 08201+2801
gmw_00002978.overview: IRAS 08201+2801 is a luminous infrared galaxy hosting an OH megamaser [arXiv:2401.15397]. The system is an advanced merger featuring two nuclei connected by arcs, a starburst classification, and a single kinked tidal tail [arXiv:2401.15397, arXiv:astro-ph/0508557].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08201+2801 as an OH megamaser in a LIRG-like system, reporting line or redshift properties and merger-related optical structure.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08201+2801 IRAS 08201+2801 has been confirmed as an OHM by the Arecibo OHM Surveys Darling2001 . This object was also detected by the FASHI survey. The coordinates and redshift are very close between the two observations, but the OH line profile observed by FAST is consistent with that of Darling2001 . The measured OH and infrared parameters are listed in Tables tab:oh and tab:oc . The FIR luminosity of this object is 10 ^ 11.98 0.03 L_ and it belongs to a LIRG galaxy.

Kim1998 classified the nucleus of this LIRG as a starburst. The OH spectrum of 08201+2801 shows multiple velocity components with W_ 50 =242.3 . In Figure Fig:IRAS08201+2801 , the optical RGB image shows an obvious morphology with two nuclei connected by arcs and a single kinked tidal tail, clearly indicating an advanced merger see also Darling2001 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 08201 + 2801 as a double-nucleus OH megamaser galaxy with starburst classification and a tidal tail, likely an advanced merger, with radio flux constraints from NVSS and Nagar et al.

quotes.quote: 08201 + 2801 : the \ image shows two nuclei (with a separation of 1.1 , corresponding to 3.2 kpc), having a starburst classification, and a tidal tail, suggesting an advanced merger (DG01). The NVSS flux density reported in DG01 is associated with a source at a distance of  23 . Nagar et al. (2003) reported a flux-density upper limit of 0.8 mJy at 15 GHz, roughly corresponding to a 1.4--15 GHz spectral slope of  0.75.

gmw_00002979.name: IRAS 08328+5148
gmw_00002979.overview: IRAS 08328+5148 is a galaxy identified as an OH megamaser source [arXiv:2401.15397]. Its likely optical counterpart is a central object with a photometric redshift of 0.234, which aligns closely with the detected OH emission redshift of 0.23749 [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08328+5148 as a FASHI OH megamaser and discusses its likely optical counterpart using coordinate, color, and redshift evidence.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08328+5148 The OHM IRAS 08328+5148 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS08328+5148 ). At the eastern position (named OC-1 with RA, DEC = 129.1717 ^ , 51.6246 ^ ) of the currently identified OC, there is an optical spectroscopic object with a redshift of 0.237 from the SDSS catalog. However, the OC coordinates are 48 '' away from the OH emission center measured by FASHI. Fortunately, we find another optical object (named OC-2 with RA, DEC = 129.1505 ^ , 51.6267 ^ ) at the center of the OH emission distribution. Although this object has no spectroscopic redshift, its SDSS photometric redshift is 0.234 0.070 , which agrees well with the OH redshift of 0.23749.

In addition, OC-1 and OC-2 have a similar optical color feature. Furthermore, the coordinates of IRAS 08328+5148 are in good agreement with the observed OH line coordinates. Therefore, it is likely that the OC-2 is the optical counterpart of this OHM, but its spectroscopic redshift is needed for further confirmation.

gmw_00002980.name: IRAS 08566-0252
gmw_00002980.overview: IRAS 08566-0252 is an OH megamaser system with a measured OH redshift of 0.20262 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals a possible merging system with two close nuclei, while the OH spectrum shows multiple velocity components that indicate an active merging process [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08566-0252 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08566-0252 The OHM IRAS 08566-0252 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS08566-0252 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.20262, while the optically observed redshift is 0.20201 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). Their velocity difference is 400 , but spatially the two are in good agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. There is a weak emission line ( <3 ) at the position of the corresponding 1665 MHz line.

More integration time is needed to verify its authenticity. The optical RGB image shows a possible merging system with two close nuclei. The OH spectrum shows multiple velocity components, indicating an active merging process.

gmw_00002981.name: IRAS 10106+4708
gmw_00002981.overview: IRAS 10106+4708 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy and OH megamaser located at a redshift of approximately 0.205 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical observations of the system reveal a compact nucleus with extended tidal tails and high optical extinction that leads to the absence of H-alpha and [O III] lines [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 10106+4708 as an OH megamaser in a ULIRG-like system, reporting line or redshift properties and merger-related optical structure.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10106+4708 The OHM IRAS 10106+4708 was first discovered by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS10106+4708 ). The optical coordinates and the spectroscopic redshift z_ =0.20481 ; Saunders2000 are in good agreement with the observed OH line coordinates and redshift ( z_ =0.20529 ) for this object. The strong sharp peak of the OH line is 24.73 1.32 mJy. The OH luminosity is = 10^ 3.67 0.01 (see Table tab:oh ). IRAS 10106+4708 has a FIR luminosity of = 10^ 12.04 0.05 (see Table tab:oc ), which belongs to a ULIRG.

In Figure Fig:IRAS10106+4708 , the optical RGB image shows a compact nucleus with extended tidal tails. The object is bright in all four WISE bands, and the optical data display an evident tidal tail. Despite that, the optical extinction is significant, leading to the absence of the H and [ O III ] lines.

gmw_00002982.name: IRAS 10558+3845
gmw_00002982.overview: IRAS 10558+3845 is an OH megamaser system with a measured OH redshift of 0.20788 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals spiral tidal tails surrounding the nucleus, and its multi-component OH spectrum indicates an active merging process [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 10558+3845 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10558+3845 The OHM IRAS 10558+3845 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS10558+3845 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.20788, while the optically observed redshift is 0.20660 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). The velocity difference is 400 , but spatially the two are in good agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image shows an apparent spiral tidal tails surrounding the nucleus.

The OH spectrum shows multiple velocity components, indicating an active merging process. The velocity position of the 1665 MHz line shows a weak emission profile. The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =1.62 0.52 .

gmw_00002983.name: IRAS 10597+5926
gmw_00002983.overview: IRAS 10597+5926 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2101.05818] and a late-stage merging system [arXiv:2401.15397, arXiv:2101.05818] undergoing a strong starburst [arXiv:2101.05818]. It hosts one of the brightest known hydroxyl megamasers [arXiv:2101.05818], which exhibits multiple velocity components that indicate active merging [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 10597+5926 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10597+5926 This OHM is first found and reported by Willett2012 , where the detected peak flux density is 17.56 mJy, while it is 32.27 2.23 mJy measured by FAST. The both flux densities have large difference. However, the line profiles are consistent between the two. We also detected the 1665 MHz emission. The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =15.22 1.46 between the 1667 and 1665 MHz lines.
The optical RGB image shows a possible merging system with interacting activity. The OH spectrum shows strong and multiple velocity components, indicating an active merging process.
