sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 10597+5926 as a luminous OH megamaser ULIRG, likely a late-stage merger with strong star formation and distinctive OH line properties.

quotes.quote: We present the serendipitous detection of the two main OH maser lines at 1667 and 1665 MHz associated with IRAS 10597+5926 at z_ =0.19612 in the untargeted Apertif Wide-area Extragalactic Survey (AWES), and the subsequent measurement of the OH 1612 MHz satellite line in the same source. With a total OH luminosity of (L/L_ ) = 3.90 0.03 , IRAS 10597+5926 is the fourth brightest OH megamaser (OHM) known. We measure a lower limit for the 1667/1612 ratio of R_ 1612 >45.9 which is the highest limiting ratio measured for the 1612 MHz OH satellite line to date.

Optical imaging shows the galaxy is likely a late-stage merger. Based on published infrared and far ultraviolet fluxes, we find that the galaxy is an ultra luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) with (L_ TIR /L_ ) = 12.24 , undergoing a star burst with an estimated star formation rate of 179 40 \ yr ^ 1 . These host galaxy properties are consistent with the physical conditions responsible for very bright OHM emission.

gmw_00002984.name: IRAS 11029+3130
gmw_00002984.overview: IRAS 11029+3130 is an active galaxy hosting an OH megamaser [arXiv:2401.15397]. Its central core is classified as a LINER-type active galactic nucleus, and the system exhibits an extended western structure that likely indicates an active merger [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 11029+3130 as an OH megamaser host with LINER-like nuclear activity plus FAST line measurements and interaction or tidal morphology.

quotes.quote: IRAS 11029+3130 This OHM was first found and reported by the Arecibo OHM surveys Darling2001 . The line profile and flux density observed by FAST are in good agreement with that of Darling2001 . The only difference is that Darling2001 identified it as the name of IRAS 11028+3130, which is not listed in the PSCz catalog compiled by Saunders2000 . The central core is a LINER-type active galaxy nucleus Alatalo2016 . The optical RGB image is unresolved (see Figure Fig:IRAS11029+3130 ), but there is an extended structure to the west of the main nucleus.
This may be probably indicative of an active merging system.

gmw_00002985.name: IRAS 11087+5351
gmw_00002985.overview: IRAS 11087+5351 is an active galaxy hosting a LINER-type nucleus [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals an extended morphology with a bright central nucleus, which may indicate a galaxy interaction [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 11087+5351 as an OH megamaser host with LINER-like nuclear activity plus FAST line measurements and interaction or tidal morphology.

quotes.quote: IRAS 11087+5351 The OHM IRAS 11087+5351 was first discovered by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS11087+5351 ). The optical coordinates and the spectroscopic redshift z_ =0.14270 ; Saunders2000 are in good agreement with the observed OH line velocities ( z_ =0.14314 ) and spatial coordinates. The 1665 MHz emission line is clearly visible. The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =1.32 0.21 , which is the measured lowest ratio among all sources in this work. In Figure Fig:IRAS11087+5351 , the optical RGB image shows an extended morphology with a bright nucleus in the center that may be indicative of interaction.
The central nucleus is identified as a LINER-type active galaxy nucleus by Toba2014 . The OH and infrared parameters are listed in Tables tab:oh and tab:oc .

gmw_00002986.name: IRAS 11161+6020
gmw_00002986.overview: IRAS 11161+6020 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2401.15397] classified spectroscopically as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2401.15397]. It hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:2401.15397] and displays a compact nucleus with complex tidal tails likely caused by a merger process [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 11161+6020 as an OH megamaser in a ULIRG-like system, reporting line or redshift properties and merger-related optical structure.

quotes.quote: IRAS 11161+6020 The OHM IRAS 11161+6020 was first discovered by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS11161+6020 ). The optical coordinates and spectroscopic redshift z_ =0.26428 ; Abazajian2009 are in good agreement with the observed OH line coordinates and redshift ( z_ =0.26434 ). The OH luminosity is = 10^ 3.68 0.02 (see Table tab:oh ). It has a FIR luminosity of = 10^ 12.21 0.04 (see Table tab:oc ), which belongs to a ULIRG. Toba2014 classified it spectroscopically as a Seyfert 2 galaxy.

In Figure Fig:IRAS11161+6020 the optical RGB image shows a compact nucleus with complex tidal tails. The OH spectrum has multiple velocity components, probably due to the interaction in the merger process.

gmw_00002987.name: IRAS 11301-0523
gmw_00002987.overview: IRAS 11301-0523 is an OH megamaser system with a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 0.23 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals an arc-like structure with several close, connected nuclei, suggesting that it is an interacting merging system [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 11301-0523 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 11301-0523 The OHM IRAS 11301-0523 was first discovered by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS11301-0523 ). The optical spectroscopic redshift z_ =0.23019 ; Saunders2000 is in good agreement with the observed OH redshift ( z_ =0.22984 ). The spatial coordinate offset between them is 1 ' . In Figure Fig:IRAS11161+6020 the optical RGB image shows an arc-like structure with several close and connected nuclei, which may be an interacting merging system. The OH spectrum has multiple velocity components, and the 1665 MHz line is not visible.

gmw_00002988.name: IRAS 12018+1941
gmw_00002988.overview: IRAS 12018+1941 is an OH megamaser system first detected by the Arecibo surveys [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals that it is an active merging system characterized by two extended tidal tails [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 12018+1941 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12018+1941 This OHM was first found and reported by the Arecibo OHM surveys Darling2002a . The line profile and flux density observed by FAST are in good agreement with those of Darling2002a . The 1665 MHz line is clearly visible in the FAST observations, but not in Darling2002a . The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =4.59 1.25 (see Table tab:ratio ). In Figure Fig:IRAS12018+1941 the optical RGB image shows an apparent merging system with two extended tidal tails.

gmw_00002989.name: IRAS 12232+5532
gmw_00002989.overview: IRAS 12232+5532 is an OH megamaser system with a measured OH redshift of 0.23250 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical observations reveal an apparent merging system featuring tidal tails to the east and west of the nucleus [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 12232+5532 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12232+5532 The OHM IRAS 12232+5532 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS12232+5532 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.23250, while the optically observed redshift is 0.23143 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). The velocity difference is only 150 , and spatially the two are in good agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image shows an apparent merging system with tidal tails to the west and east of the nucleus.

The OH spectrum shows several velocity components, probably indicating an active merging process. The velocity position of the 1665 MHz line shows a bright emission profile. The measured line ratio is R_ 1667:1665 =1.54 0.18 .

gmw_00002990.name: IRAS 12447+3721
gmw_00002990.overview: IRAS 12447+3721 is an OH megamaser system with a high star formation rate [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals that it is an apparent merging system featuring tidal tails to the east and west of its nucleus [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 12447+3721 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12447+3721 The OHM IRAS 12447+3721 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS12447+3721 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.15847, which is in good agreement with the optically observed redshift of 0.15870 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). Moreover, the two are in good spatial agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image shows an apparent merging system with tidal tails to the west and east of the nucleus, or a spiral structure surrounding the nucleus.
Duarte2017 classified it as a galaxy with a high star formation rate.

gmw_00002991.name: IRAS 13070+6412
gmw_00002991.overview: IRAS 13070+6412 is an OH megamaser system with a strong OH spectrum that exhibits multiple velocity components [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals a possible merging system characterized by tidal tails extending to the north and south of the nucleus [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 13070+6412 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13070+6412 The OHM IRAS 13070+6412 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS13070+6412 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.20523, which is in good agreement with the optically observed redshift of 0.20492 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). Moreover, the two are in good spatial agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image shows a possible merging system with tidal tails to north and south of the nucleus.

The OH spectrum is strong and has multiple velocity components, probably due to the merging interaction. There is a weak emission at 1665 MHz position, but it does not have enough SNR to be identified as a real signal.

gmw_00002992.name: IRAS 13469+5833
gmw_00002992.overview: IRAS 13469+5833 is an OH megamaser system with a double-velocity component spectrum that likely indicates merging activity [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals a possible merging system characterized by several tidal tails surrounding the galactic nuclei [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 13469+5833 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13469+5833 The OHM IRAS 13469+5833 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS13469+5833 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.15716, while the optically observed redshift is 0.15778 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). The difference between the two is 350 , but the two are in good spatial agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image shows a possible merging system with several tidal tails surrounding the nuclei see also the HST F814W-band images in Murata2017 .
The OH spectrum has double velocity components, probably indicating merging activity. The IRAS 13469+5833 is identified as an galaxy Lin2018 .

gmw_00002993.name: IRAS 14170+4545
gmw_00002993.overview: IRAS 14170+4545 is an OH megamaser system located at a redshift of approximately 0.150 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging of the galaxy reveals a dense nucleus surrounded by extended materials that suggest a possible tidal tail [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 14170+4545 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14170+4545 The OHM IRAS 14170+4545 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS14170+4545 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.15009, which is in good agreement with the optically observed redshift of 0.15026 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). Moreover, the two are in good spatial agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image could not resolve the dense nucleus, but the extended materials show a possible tidal tail surrounding the nucleus.
The OH spectrum has a very weak peak below 3.0 mJy, but the integrated flux shows a compact structure at the position of the OC.

gmw_00002994.name: IRAS 14202+2615
gmw_00002994.overview: IRAS 14202+2615 is an OH megamaser system that is likely a merging galaxy system [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging of the system reveals two close nuclei, one of which displays a visible tidal tail [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 14202+2615 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14202+2615 This source has been observed by the Arecibo OHM surveys Darling2000,Darling2006 , but they do not have sufficient sensitivity to see the OH emission line. In the FASHI survey we detected the OHM emission line with a peak flux density of 4.38 1.15 mJy, and the SNR is only about 3. The optical redshift from Saunders2000 is in good agreement with the OH line measured by FAST, while the spatially projected coordinate offset is 2.0 ' . The optical RGB image shows two close nuclei, one of which has a visible tidal tail. This probably indicates a merging system.

gmw_00002995.name: IRAS 15043+5754
gmw_00002995.overview: IRAS 15043+5754 is an OH megamaser system with a measured OH redshift of 0.15029 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals a possible merging system featuring two interacting nuclei [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 15043+5754 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15043+5754 The OHM IRAS 15043+5754 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS15043+5754 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.15029, which is in good agreement with the optically observed redshift of 0.15059 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). Moreover, the two are in good spatial agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image show a possible merging system with two nuclei interacting.
The OH spectrum has a very weak peak below 4.0 mJy, but the integrated flux shows a compact structure at the position of the optical object.

gmw_00002996.name: IRAS 15160+5307
gmw_00002996.overview: IRAS 15160+5307 is an OH megamaser system with a measured OH redshift of 0.15227 [arXiv:2401.15397]. Optical imaging reveals it is a merging system containing two interacting nuclei and at least two tidal tails [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 15160+5307 as an OH megamaser system, reporting FAST line or redshift measurements and optical evidence for interaction, tidal structure, or merger activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15160+5307 The OHM IRAS 15160+5307 was first found and identified by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS15160+5307 ). Its measured OH redshift is 0.15227, which is in good agreement with the optically observed redshift of 0.15199 Saunders2000 (see Tables tab:oh and tab:oc ). Moreover, the two are in good spatial agreement. A careful search for other optical objects shows that there is no possible match to the radio observation. The optical RGB image shows a possible merging system with two interacting nuclei and at least two tidal tails.
The OH spectrum has a weak peak of 2.5 mJy, but the blue wing is broad and the red wing has an absorption feature.

gmw_00002997.name: IRAS 15261+5502
gmw_00002997.overview: IRAS 15261+5502 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2401.15397] classified as a starburst galaxy [arXiv:2401.15397]. It hosts an OH megamaser and displays two interacting nuclei with a visible tidal tail from an ongoing merger process [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 15261+5502 as an OH megamaser in a LIRG-like system, reporting line or redshift properties and merger-related optical structure.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15261+5502 The new OHM IRAS 15261+5502 was first discovered by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS15261+5502 ). The optical coordinates and spectroscopic redshift z_ =0.22921 ; Abazajian2009 are in good agreement with the observed OH line coordinates and redshift ( z_ =0.22902 ) for this object. The OH luminosity is = 10^ 3.43 0.01 (see Table tab:oh ). It has a FIR luminosity of = 10^ 11.99 0.03 (see Table tab:oc ), which belongs to a LIRG. Hou2009 classified it as a starburst galaxy.

In Figure Fig:IRAS15261+5502 the optical RGB image shows two interacting nuclei with a visible tidal tail. The OH spectrum has a broad linewidth and multiple velocity components, probably resulting from a merger process.

gmw_00002998.name: IRAS 16468+5200
gmw_00002998.overview: IRAS 16468+5200 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2401.15397] classified as a LINER-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2401.15397]. It is a merging system featuring two interacting nuclei, one of which displays an elongated tidal tail, and hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 16468+5200 as an OH megamaser in a LIRG-like system, reporting line or redshift properties and merger-related optical structure.

quotes.quote: IRAS 16468+5200 The new OHM IRAS 16468+5200 was first discovered by the FASHI survey (see Figures Fig:IRAS16468+5200 ). The OH and the corresponding infrared parameters are listed in Tables tab:oh and tab:oc . The optical coordinates and the spectroscopic redshift z_ =0.15054 ; Saunders2000 are in good agreement with the observed OH line coordinates and redshift ( z_ =0.15032 ) for this object. The OH luminosity is = 10^ 2.60 0.04 (see Table tab:oh ). It has a FIR luminosity of = 10^ 11.84 0.02 (see Table tab:oc ), which belongs to a LIRG.

Weedman2008 classified it as a LINER-type active galaxy nucleus. In Figure Fig:IRAS15261+5502 the optical RGB image shows a typical merging system with two interacting nuclei, one of which has an elongated tidal tail. The OH spectrum has multiple velocity components.

gmw_00002999.name: IRAS 23129+2548
gmw_00002999.overview: IRAS 23129+2548 is an interacting merging galaxy system that displays visible tidal tails [arXiv:2401.15397]. The nucleus of this galaxy is classified as a LINER, and it hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:2401.15397].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 23129+2548 as an OH megamaser host with LINER-like nuclear activity plus FAST line measurements and interaction or tidal morphology.

quotes.quote: IRAS 23129+2548 This source has been confirmed as an OHM by the Arecibo OHM surveys Darling2001 . In the FAST observation the 1665 MHz line is not blind to the 1667 MHz line, but the 1665 MHz line shows no emission feature. The optical RGB image shows an interacting merging system with visible tidal tails. The nucleus of this OHM host is classified as a LINER by Veilleux1999 . The OH spectrum has a broad linewidth and multiple velocity components, probably due to a merging process.

gmw_00003000.name: SSTSL2 J124007.83-113647.1
gmw_00003000.overview: SSTSL2 J124007.83-113647.1 is an SBc-type Seyfert galaxy with an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2308.06187]. It features a central bar with reddish hues, a surrounding blue ring, and possibly two spiral arms containing dust and hot young stars [arXiv:2308.06187].

sources.summary: The paper argues SSTSL2 J124007.83-113647.1 is an SBc AGN/Seyfert galaxy rather than a globular-cluster candidate, citing spiral-like SEDs, central structure, dust, infrared features, and X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: After a new search in NED, the object in region 710, SSTSL2 J124007.83-113647.1, seems to be the one. The author has compared the object's SED plot with those of spiral galaxy models in Optical-NIR-MIR (Nicole P. V. 2012), and the object's NED plot points fit quite well. HST Legacy has some images taken with 435 and 555 filters, which are equivalent to B, V and R filters. So the bandwidth is from B to NIR. The results of an RGB of the images in Aladin Sky Atlas, (Figure 1b) show (limited by the resolution) a galactic centre and a central bar with reddish hues (555 filter, R of the Wide Field and Planetary Camera 2 [ WFPC2 ] ) and a blue ring around it (435 filter, B+V of the Advanced Camera for Surveys [ ACS ] ).

CONCLUSIONS The author has recategorised an object erroneously catalogued by Simbad as a Globular Cluster Candidate as an SBc AGN Seyfert galaxy, z = 0.004545 +/- 0.000027, using VO tools. The Aladin Sky Atlas RGB suggests an SBc galaxy with a dominant central arm, nucleus and possibly two spiral arms with hot young stars and dust. VizieR and NED SED plots are consistent with spiral galaxies.

gmw_00003001.name: 3C 189
gmw_00003001.overview: The galaxy 3C 189 is a Fanaroff-Riley Class I radio galaxy [arXiv:1107.2511, arXiv:2110.07373] classified as a host-dominated low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:2205.13652]. It features asymmetric X-ray emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0106246], twin radio jets [arXiv:1107.2511], and an optical jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0106246] surrounded by circular projected lobes [arXiv:1107.2511].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2484/SDSS0758 as an X-ray AGN fossil-group BCG with a core-like metallicity profile, no age gradient, and an inside-out formation signature.

quotes.quote: NGC 2484, a well known X-ray AGN, exhibits a flat core-like structure in its metallicity gradient, but no detectable age gradient. The element abundance ratio ( [ E/Fe ] ) profiles of the two galaxies are also significantly different. SDSS J073422.21+265133.9 exhibits a slightly positive gradient ( [ E/H ] / R 0.1), perhaps again suggesting a more recent central burst of star formation, while NGC 2484 shows a negative gradient ( [ E/H ] / R 0.1), indicating that star formation may have happened "inside out". Our analysis of these two galaxies of similar mass, morphology and kinematics therefore suggests two different mechanisms to have been in action during their formation. Consequently, we conclude that the central galaxies of fossil groups can not be considered a homogeneous group with regard to their formation processes or star formation histories.

In SDSS0758, on the other hand, the galaxy exhibits no detectable age gradient but a negative [ E/Fe ] gradient. Although the galaxy also exhibits negative [ Fe/H ] and [ Z/H ] gradients, there is a strong suggestion of a flattening in this gradient in the central regions. Indeed the [ Fe/H ] gradient appears to be flat all the way out to (/r_ eff )=-1 , and from our full-spectrum fitting results, we measure a flat gradient even out to (/r_ eff )=-0.5 . The [ E/Fe ] gradient in this galaxy suggests that this galaxy also formed over a relatively extended period of time, except in this case the later star formation took place in the outer regions, rather than in the central regions as was the case in SDSS0734.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0755+37 as a lobed FR I source with circular projected lobes, ridges and shell-like structures, contrasting lobe substructure, and distinct spectral-index regions.

quotes.quote: We present deep, high-resolution imaging of the nearby Fanaroff-Riley Class I (FR I) radio galaxies NGC 193, B2 0206+35, B2 0755+37 and M 84 at frequencies of 4.9 and 1.4 GHz using new and archival multi-configuration observations from the Very Large Array. In addition, we describe lower-resolution observations of B2 0326+39 and a reanalysis of our published images of 3C 296. All of these radio galaxies show twin jets and well-defined lobes or bridges of emission, and we examine the common properties of this class of source. We show detailed images of total intensity, brightness gradient, spectral index, degree of polarization and projected magnetic-field direction.

0755+37 Fig. fig:0755all shows the total-intensity, | I| and distributions over all of 0755+37 at two resolutions and the polarization at low resolution. Fig. fig:0755all (a) at 1.4 GHz, 4.0-arcsec FWHM resolution, shows that the large-scale structure consists of two lobes, again roughly circular in projection, with well-defined but not particularly sharp outer edges to the W and E, plus fainter diffuse emission to the N and S. The E lobe has a series of narrow ridges and brightness steps, all roughly arcs of circles in projection, in the region where the brighter jet appears to terminate. They are recessed from the E boundary of this lobe and some may be the edges of thin shells.

sources.summary: The paper studies the isolated FR I radio galaxy B 2 0755+37, mapping Faraday rotation, depolarization, radio lobes, jets, and X-ray gas in its sparse group environment.

quotes.quote: We explore the magneto-ionic environment of the isolated radio galaxy B 2 0755+37 using detailed imaging of the distributions of Faraday rotation and depolarization over the radio source from Very Large Array observations at 1385, 1465 and 4860 MHz and new X-ray data from XMM-Newton . The Rotation Measure (RM) distribution is complex, with evidence for anisotropic fluctuations in two regions. The approaching lobe shows low and uniform RM in an unusual `stripe' along an extension of the jet axis and a linear gradient transverse to this axis over its Northern half. The leading edge of the receding lobe shows arc-like RM structures with sign reversals.

0755+37 is an extended FR I radio source Fana74 whose optical counterpart, NGC 2484, is a D-galaxy with a redshift of 0.04284 Falco99 . We assume a cosmology with H_0 = 71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _M = 0.3, and _ = 0.7, implying that 1 arcsec corresponds to 0.833 kpc. The source is one of the most isolated radio galaxies in the B2 sample and is classified as the central member of a very poor group Mul03 .

sources.summary: The paper says 1-71872 was the only type-1 AGN missed after host stellar subtraction, gives its flux-ratio behavior, and classifies it as a host-dominated LERG with radio jets.

quotes.quote: Looking for type-1 AGN, the same objects were recovered as in the case of no stellar contribution subtraction except one, 1-71872. This object was found above the upper limit only in / \ ratio. Its / \ ratio lies just below the limit, 1.015.

The three AGN with the highest NVSS radio flux density shown in Figures fig:LoiiivsLRadio fig:butiglionini as blue stars in circles are 1-594493, 1-604860, and 1-71872, were classified as LERGs. Figure fig:Radiojets is a mosaic showing their corresponding FIRST images. The images show clear evidence radio jet structures powered by the active nucleus, in agreement with the definition of LERGs. The three galaxies with radio jets are of the host-dominated type according to our empirical classification.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 189 as a Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxy with a one-sided jet, broader collimated counter-jet emission, and unresolved ROSAT X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: 3C 189 (NGC 2484, B2 0755+37) is a radio galaxy with jet, class Fanaroff-Riley type I radio source. The radio structure was shown in de Ruiter et al. to be that of a weak, one-sided, jet within a diffuse, low surface-brightness, envelope. But sensitive, well-resolved Very Large Array imaging shows that jet in the source has a two-component structure transverse to it axis. Close to the jet axis, a centrally-darkened counter-jet lies opposite to a centrally-brightened jet, but both are surrounded by broader collimated emission that is brighter on the counter-jet side . The ROSAT High Resolution Imager (HRI, 0.2 - 2 keV) data are consistent with an unresolved source of luminosity L (3.7 0.4) 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0755+37 has reliable spectral indices only in the main jet, with problematic lobe subtraction and outflow-dominated spectra despite backflow modeling.

quotes.quote: 0755+37 Spectral-index images of the whole source and for the jets have been presented elsewhere, as for 0206+35 LGBPB,LB12 . Only the main jet has enough unblanked emission for us to derive a reliable spectral index. We considered using lower-resolution images to improve the signal-to-noise ration, but lobe subtraction was problematic. Although we model the jets as a mixture of outflow and backflow LB12 , the spectral indices in the present paper are dominated by the outflow component.

sources.summary: The catalog treats 3C 189 as a newly associated nearby FRI, noting a nearby quasar whose radio emission blends with 3C 189 and complicates the association probability.

quotes.quote: Many of the newly-associated non-blazar objects are nearby FRIs -- J0758.7+3747 (3C 189, a.k.a., B2 0755+37) and 3C 264.

We remark that 0.8 away from 3C 189 lies the quasar SDSS J075825.87+374628.7 with redshift 1.50. With the resolution of the NVSS, this source cannot be disentangled from the radio emission of 3C 189. This may be the reason why this source is not present in the NVSS catalog, precluding the estimation of the association probability with the gamma-ray source.

sources.summary: B2 0755+37 resembles B2 0206+35, with asymmetric X-ray emission along the dominant kpc-scale radio jet, extended galaxy-scale gas, and a detected optical jet for future spectral-energy-distribution modelling.

quotes.quote: As seen in Figs 0206image and 0755image , B2 0206+35 and 0755+37 are very similar, with asymmetric X-ray emission coincident with the dominant kpc-scale radio jet.

Best-fit values of and _ cx are 0.7 and 1.15 arcsec, and 0.8 and 2.1 arcsec, for 0206+35 and 0755+37, respectively. The extended emission can be attributed to galaxy-scale gas with a luminosity of a few times 10^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 .
Interestingly, B2 0755+37 is one of the three sources with detected optical jets, and so more detailed modelling of the spectral energy distribution of this jet will be possible.

gmw_00003002.name: 3C 207
gmw_00003002.overview: 3C 207 is a lobe-dominant FR II radio galaxy [arXiv:1803.09856] and steep-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1501.06054] located at a redshift of z=0.684 [arXiv:1501.00635, arXiv:1803.09856]. It features a curved parsec-scale jet [arXiv:1803.09856] that exhibits superluminal motion [arXiv:1501.06054] and a 40-degree twist between its parsec- and kiloparsec-scale structures [arXiv:1703.10227].

sources.summary: The paper confirms 3C 207 as a gamma-ray-emitting, marginally lobe-dominant radio quasar/AGN, noting strong radio-core behavior, localization within the LAT error radius, and month-scale gamma-ray variability.

quotes.quote: 3C 207 at a redshift z=0.684 has been reported as a ray emitter in the first and second LAT AGN catalogs (Abdo et al. 2010d; Ackermann et al. 2011). It shows a marginally lobe-dominant ( R=0.45 ) radio morphology (Hough Readhead 1989). Its radio core flux density is the second strongest in the sample (Hough et al. 2002). Besides of the significant variability of the radio core, structure variation with _ app 10 is reported, which is the typical behavior of blazars (Hough et al. 2002; Lister et al. 2013) and an inclination angle 11^ is inferred (Aars et al. 2005).

We confirm that the radio position of 3C 207 is still within the 95 error radius. However it is at the edge of error radius (see Figure 6). Nevertheless, 3C 207 remains as the only known RL-AGN within the ray localization error radius. The 7-month time bin light curve indicates that 3C 207 has significant variability at timescale of months.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 207 as a lobe-dominant FR II radio galaxy, using Fermi/LAT and multiwavelength knot-A data to argue that steady gamma-ray emission may arise from its jet knots.

quotes.quote: 3C 207 is an FR II radio galaxy at redshift of z=0.684 with lobe-dominant radio morphology (Hough Readhead 1989). The ratio of core to extended lobe emission at 8.4 GHz is 0.82 (Hough et al. 2002). Its pc-scale jet structure was resolved by the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) at 8.4 GHz and is distinctly curved with a bright inner knot misaligned from the jet axis defined by the outer weaker jet features (Hough et al. 2002). It has a powerful one sided radio and X-ray large-scale jet and the extended X-ray emission coincident with the western radio lobe is also detected in the opposite direction (Brunetti et al. 2002). Three radio knots at 1. 4 ( 9.94 kpc, knot-A), 4. 6 (32.66 kpc, knot-B), and 6. 5 (46.15 kpc, knot-C, maybe a hotspot) are resolved and detected in the Chandra image.

We have presented an analysis of the Fermi /LAT data in the past 9 years for 3C 207. It is found that the ray emission is steady without detection of flux variation over 2 confidence level. We suspect that the ray emission is from its knots in the kpc-scale.

sources.summary: The core spectrum is fit by a flat single power law, with no required extra absorption or iron line, and has a 2-10 keV flux modestly below earlier work.

quotes.quote: 3C 207 : the core spectrum of 3C 207 is best-fitted with a single power-law of rather flat slope =1.27 0.05 . This is in agreement with what was found previously by brunetti02 ( =1.22 0.06 ), and gambill03 ( =1.36 0.06 ). We do not find evidence for absorption above the Galactic value as found by brunetti02 , and, although we do not exclude emission of ionized gas in the nucleus, the addition of a Gaussian iron line does not improve the fit statistically. Hotspot, jet, lobe and extended emission were also previously discussed (e.g., brunetti02 , mjh04 , gambill03 ). The 2.0--10.0-keV flux we found is somewhat lower than found by gambill03 , but the two values agree within 20 per cent.

sources.summary: The catalog notes 3C 207 was first gamma-ray detected in 1LAC, is a steep-spectrum radio quasar in 3CRR, and has a flat-spectrum core with superluminal jet motion.

quotes.quote: The gamma-ray detections of 3C 207 and 3C 380 were first reported in 1LAC. They appear in the 3CRR catalog Lai83 by virtue of their bright low-frequency emission due to the presence of kpc-scale extended steep-spectrum radio lobes, thus are formally classified as steep-spectrum radio quasars (SSRQs). However, they contain pronounced flat-spectrum radio cores with superluminal motions measured in their parsec-scale jets, indicating that they are the most well-aligned sources to our line of sight amongst the SSRQs in the 3CRR e.g., Wil91,Hou13,Lis13 .

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 207 as the close analogue for PSO J334+01, noting its rare 40-degree radio-structure twist, similar point-like-to-extended flux ratio, and VLBI-supported conical jet-ejection model.

quotes.quote: A twist of 39 between elongation PAs on parsec- and kiloparsec-scales is atypically large for a lobe-dominated quasar, being achieved by only one of the 16 quasars with such measurements available in the hough2002 sample: 3C 207 which twists by 40 . Both 3C 207 and PSO J334+01 have similar ratios of point-like to extended flux densities, R 0.49 hough2002 and R = 0.33 , respectively, so both could be near the extreme of _ los 10 postulated for this quasar class. Extensive VLBI monitoring of 3C 207 underpins a model wherein jet components are ejected at different angles around a cone, with the flow then recollimating downstream to align with the kiloparsec-scale emission hough2013 .

gmw_00003003.name: PKS 0943 76
gmw_00003003.overview: PKS 0943 76 is a radio galaxy and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1501.06054]. Although previously classified as having a Fanaroff-Riley II morphology, this claim was based on low-resolution radio data [arXiv:1501.06054].

sources.summary: The paper says PKS 0943 76 is not confirmed in 3LAC, its earlier FR II claim used low-resolution radio data, and it was mis-associated in 2LAC.

quotes.quote: The same conclusion applies to PKS 0943 76 studied in magn . The previous claim that it has a FRII morphology was based on a low-resolution radio map from bur06 . The offset between the 4 year source and PKS 0943 76 is 0 22 while the radius of the source location region at the 95 confidence level is 0 12 . ESO 323 G77 (type 2 Seyfert galaxy), and PKS0943 76 (radio galaxy), both reported in 2LAC, were actually both mis-associated because of an error in the LR association method 2LACErr .

gmw_00003004.name: MG4 J000800 + 4712
gmw_00003004.overview: MG4 J000800 + 4712 is a high-redshift blazar associated with the gamma-ray source 3FGL J0008.0 + 4713 [arXiv:1501.06054]. It has a revised redshift of 2.1 and is classified as a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1501.06054].

sources.summary: The catalog associates MG4 J000800 + 4712 with a 3FGL source, revises its redshift from 0.28 to 2.1, and reclassifies its SED from LSP to HSP.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0008.0 + 4713 is associated with MG4 J000800 + 4712. The redshift reported in 2LAC was 0.28 and its SED classification was LSP. derived a redshift of 2.1 from the clear onset of the Lyman- forest and their new procedure for estimating SED class together with WISE data classified this source as a HSP.

gmw_00003005.name: B2 2114 + 33
gmw_00003005.overview: B2 2114 + 33 is a blazar associated with the gamma-ray source 3FGL J2116.1+3339 [arXiv:1501.06054]. It has a revised redshift of 1.596 based on a broad C IV emission feature, though a lower redshift remains possible [arXiv:1501.06054].

sources.summary: The catalog associates B2 2114 + 33 with a 3FGL source, revises its 2LAC redshift to 1.596 from C IV, and notes a lower redshift remains possible.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J2116.1+3339 is associated with B2 2114 + 33. The redshift quoted in 2LAC was 0.35, but a recent measurement by gives z=1.596 identifying a significant broad emission feature with C IV, consistent with a weak bump in the far blue at Ly . A lower redshift is possible if the purported Ly line is not real.

gmw_00003006.name: BZB J0202+0849
gmw_00003006.overview: BZB J0202+0849 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar [arXiv:1501.06054]. It is considered a transitional object that was previously classified as a BL Lac object [arXiv:1501.06054].

sources.summary: The catalog identifies BZB J0202+0849 as an HSP-FSRQ, reports its LAT photon index, says it was reclassified from BL Lac, and treats it as transitional.

quotes.quote: Two HSP-FSRQs have been detected: BZB J0202+0849 (3FGL J0202.3+0851) and NVSS J025037+171209 (3FGL J0250.6+1713) with LAT spectral photon indices of 2.05 0.16 and 1.98 0.19 respectively. 3FGL J0202.3+0851 was classified as a BL Lac in 1LAC but new observations from led to a reclassification as an FSRQ. These objects are probably transitional objects that show broad lines in the optical band when the continuum is low see, e.g., Rua14 .

gmw_00003007.name: 2MASX J22445464+5328318
gmw_00003007.overview: 2MASX J22445464+5328318 is a head-tail radio galaxy located near the galaxy 2MASX J22445565+5327578 [arXiv:1708.07125]. Based on K-band magnitude-redshift relations, it has an estimated redshift of approximately 0.1 to 0.2 [arXiv:1708.07125].

sources.summary: The paper associates 2MASX J22445464+5328318 with a head-tail radio galaxy near S1 and estimates a redshift close to CJ2242 from K-band magnitude-redshift relations.

quotes.quote: Moreover at the centre of the X-ray source we found a head-tail radio galaxy that further supports the presence of a dense medium, typical of a galaxy cluster. The head-tail is associated with the galaxy 2MASX J22445464+5328318 and at less than 0.7 ^ we also found the galaxy 2MASX J22445565+5327578. No spectroscopic redshifts are available for these galaxies, however, by using the K-band magnitude-redshift relations of inskip we estimated a redshift z 0.1 0.2 close to that of CJ2242. We obtained a similar result (z 0.15 0.2) when considering the K-band magnitude-redshift relation for the brightest cluster galaxies stott .

gmw_00003008.name: 2MASX J22445565+5327578
gmw_00003008.overview: 2MASX J22445565+5327578 is an optical galaxy with an extinction-corrected K-band magnitude of 12.67 [arXiv:1708.07125]. It has an estimated redshift of approximately 0.1 to 0.2, which supports the presence of a candidate galaxy cluster in its vicinity [arXiv:1708.07125].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J22445565+5327578 near S1, gives its K-band magnitude, estimates a redshift similar to CJ2242, and uses it as cluster-candidate support.

quotes.quote: The radio contours are overlaid on an optical Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope (CFHT) image http://www.cadc-ccda.hia-iha.nrc-cnrc.gc.ca/en/search/ , where two blue crosses indicate the position of two optical galaxies: 2MASX J22445464+5328318, at less than 0.2 ^ from S1, and 2MASX J22445565+5327578, at less than 0.7 ^ . The galaxies have a K-band magnitude of M=(13.5 0.1) mag and M=(12.67 0.06) mag, respectively, already corrected for extinction see Appendix in schlafly . By using the K-band magnitude-redshift relations of inskip we found that these galaxies have a redshift z 0.1-0.2, similar to CJ2242. We obtained a similar result ( z 0.15-0.2 ) when considering the K-band magnitude-redshift relation for brightest cluster galaxies see Fig.5 in stott . Even if at the moment there are only two optical galaxies identified in the vicinity of S1, this fact, together with its X-ray properties and the fact that S1 is an head-tail radio source, corroborates the hypothesis of S1 as a cluster candidate.

gmw_00003009.name: GMP 3565
gmw_00003009.overview: GMP 3565 is an early-type galaxy classified as an E/S0 galaxy [arXiv:0803.0464]. It has a low measured velocity dispersion and represents the lowest-metallicity member of the LRIS sample [arXiv:0803.0464].

sources.summary: GMP 3565 is described as an E/S0 galaxy with low measured velocity dispersion, matching higher-resolution data, and as the lowest-metallicity LRIS sample member.

quotes.quote: We note that for three galaxies, GMP 3291, GMP 3534, and GMP 3565, the measured velocity dispersions are significantly below the resolution limit of 125 and thus may be significantly in error, even given the high signal-to-noise of the present spectra.

Two of these galaxies, GMP 3534 and GMP 3565, were recently observed at 35 resolution by MG05 , and our measured velocity dispersions match theirs within the 1 joint errors for each of these two objects.
Such a low metallicity is not seen in the ETGs in LRIS sample (the lowest metallicity is that of GMP 3565, which has =-0.25 dex).

gmw_00003010.name: GMP 3534
gmw_00003010.overview: GMP 3534 is an early-type galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:0803.0464]. It is characterized by a low velocity dispersion that is below the nominal resolution limit of some instruments [arXiv:0803.0464].

sources.summary: GMP 3534 needed a different velocity-dispersion template; its low dispersion was below the nominal resolution yet agreed with higher-resolution measurements.

quotes.quote: For the galaxies GMP 3534 and GMP 3565, the G0 dwarf HR 6458 provided a somewhat better fit.

We note that for three galaxies, GMP 3291, GMP 3534, and GMP 3565, the measured velocity dispersions are significantly below the resolution limit of 125 and thus may be significantly in error, even given the high signal-to-noise of the present spectra.

Two of these galaxies, GMP 3534 and GMP 3565, were recently observed at 35 resolution by MG05 , and our measured velocity dispersions match theirs within the 1 joint errors for each of these two objects.

gmw_00003011.name: GMP 3291
gmw_00003011.overview: GMP 3291 is an early-type galaxy located within the Coma Cluster [arXiv:0803.0464]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy indicate a measured velocity dispersion that is significantly below the nominal resolution limit of 125 km/s [arXiv:0803.0464].

sources.summary: GMP 3291 has a velocity dispersion below the nominal resolution; the paper says it is probably close to true but discrepant with other measurements.

quotes.quote: We note that for three galaxies, GMP 3291, GMP 3534, and GMP 3565, the measured velocity dispersions are significantly below the resolution limit of 125 and thus may be significantly in error, even given the high signal-to-noise of the present spectra.

While this does not guarantee that our velocity dispersion measurement of GMP 3291 is correct, it does suggest that our measurement is not far from the true value (see Appendix sec:others for more detailed comparisons).
Detailed examination of Figure fig:comparisons shows that the M02 measurements of GMP 3291 and GMP 3534 are significantly discrepant in \ (and ).

gmw_00003012.name: GMP 3269
gmw_00003012.overview: GMP 3269 is an early-type galaxy located within the Coma Cluster [arXiv:0803.0464]. It has a large peculiar motion of approximately 1000 km/s toward the rear of the cluster [arXiv:0803.0464].

sources.summary: GMP 3269 is one of two divergent-age ETGs and has one of the sample largest peculiar motions, about 1000 km/s to the cluster rear.

quotes.quote: Discarding the two most divergent galaxies -- GMP 3269 and GMP 3639 -- the mean age of the ten remaining ETGs is _ =0.72 0.02 dex ( 5.2 0.2 Gyr).
The two deviant ETGs, GMP 3269 and GMP 3639 are notable for having the largest peculiar motions of the sample.
GMP 3639 has a peculiar motion of 2200 , more than 2 _ cl [ _ cl ( Coma )=1021 , Smith04 ] in front of the cluster, while GMP 3269 has a peculiar motion of 1000 to the rear of the cluster.

gmw_00003013.name: GMP 3639
gmw_00003013.overview: GMP 3639, also known as NGC 4867, is an S0 early-type galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886, arXiv:0803.0464]. It exhibits asymmetric far-ultraviolet extensions that suggest a possible gravitational interaction with the neighboring galaxy NGC 4864 [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4867 as an S0 Coma galaxy with asymmetric FUV extensions, a possible connection to NGC 4864, and likely gravitational influence between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: NGC 4867 This is a typical S0 galaxy in the Coma cluster ( z=0.016 ), around 1.6 kpc ( 5 ) from the most dominant galaxy at the centre. NGC 4867 (seen as component C1 in Fig. images2 ) shows FUV emission extending towards west and southeast, but invisible in the optical image. A weak feature also appears to extend towards another FUV source C2 towards the left, which has no optical counterpart. The western tail from C1 seem to be extending towards NGC 4864 ( z=0.022 ; component C3), which is also a Coma cluster member, separated in redshift space by 1800 km s ^ 1 from NGC 4867. Hence, it is possible that the two galaxies are gravitationally influencing each other.

sources.summary: GMP 3639 is one of two divergent-age ETGs and is notable for a peculiar motion more than twice the Coma cluster dispersion in front of the cluster.

quotes.quote: Discarding the two most divergent galaxies -- GMP 3269 and GMP 3639 -- the mean age of the ten remaining ETGs is _ =0.72 0.02 dex ( 5.2 0.2 Gyr).
The two deviant ETGs, GMP 3269 and GMP 3639 are notable for having the largest peculiar motions of the sample.
GMP 3639 has a peculiar motion of 2200 , more than 2 _ cl [ _ cl ( Coma )=1021 , Smith04 ] in front of the cluster, while GMP 3269 has a peculiar motion of 1000 to the rear of the cluster.

gmw_00003014.name: IRAS 16288+3929
gmw_00003014.overview: IRAS 16288+3929 is an infrared-luminous Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:0912.2515]. Its X-ray spectrum features a soft component modeled as a black body, alongside a hard component modeled as an absorbed power law with a narrow Gaussian emission line [arXiv:0912.2515].

sources.summary: The paper presents the first X-ray detection of the infrared-luminous Seyfert 2 IRAS 16288+3929 and models its soft and hard spectral components.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:spec we show a sample case of our XRT spectra for the source IRAS 16288+3929. This source is an infrared luminous Seyfert 2 galaxy and this constitutes the first detection of X-ray emission from this object. The spectrum indicates the presence of a soft component together with hard emission above 3 keV. A line complex appears to be present at E 6--7 keV.

We have found that the soft emission can be well described by a black body model with temperature kT=0.09^ +0.07 _ 0.03 keV and no absorption in excess of the Galactic value, while the hard component can be modeled with an absorbed power law plus a narrow Gaussian emission line. The best fit model was obtained for a typical photon index of 1.6 (fixed) and an absorbing column density N_ H =34_ 10 ^ +9 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00003015.name: IRAS F22491-1808
gmw_00003015.overview: IRAS F22491-1808 is a pre-merger ultraluminous infrared galaxy containing two nuclei separated by approximately 2.2 to 2.5 kpc [arXiv:1805.03667, arXiv:1604.00185]. The system features tidal tails [arXiv:1805.03667, arXiv:1604.00185], starburst-dominated infrared emission [arXiv:1609.01291], and a deeply buried active galactic nucleus in its eastern nucleus [arXiv:1805.03667, arXiv:1803.02408].

sources.summary: The paper studies 22491 1808 as a low-redshift close-binary, pre-merger ULIRG with H II nuclei, placing its weak AGN at the E continuum peak with hot-dust HCN emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study three low- z ( d 350 Mpc) ULIRGs (six individual nuclei) with L_ IR L_ =12.0-12.3 (see Table tbl_sample ) based on their mid- and far-IR spectral energy distribution modeling (Section ss:ir_lum ) . These three ULIRG systems seem to be in a similar dynamical state. They were classified as type III by Veilleux2002 , which corresponds to a pre-merger stage characterized by two identifiable nuclei with well defined tidal tails and bridges. They also belong to the subclass of ``close binary'' (i.e., ``b'') as the projected separation of their nuclei is smaller than 10 kpc. Their nuclei are classified as low ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINER; 12112+0305 and 14348 1447; e.g., Colina2000, Evans2002 ) or H ii ( 22491 1808; e.g., Veilleux1999 ) and in all systems a weak AGN contribution ( 10-15 ) is detected in their mid-IR \ spectra Veilleux2009 .

For 12112+0305 and 22491 1808, we assume that the AGN is at the brightest nucleus in the radio/sub-mm continuum, i.e., 12112+0305 NE and 22491 1808 E (see below). In addition, vibrationally excited HCN J=4-3 emission is detected in 12112+0305 NE and 22491 1808 E which can be a signature of hot dust heated by an AGN Imanishi2016, Imanishi2018 .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes IRAS 22491 1808's Cycle 0 J=4-3 lines after finding them too narrow, adopts revised profiles, and says ALMA dense-gas temperatures exceed earlier CO-based estimates.

quotes.quote: However, we noticed that the HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC J=4--3 emission lines of IRAS 22491 1808 obtained in ALMA Cycle 0 were substantially narrower than J=3--2 and J=2--1 data obtained in later ALMA Cycles. This is because (1) the molecular-line-derived redshift of z = 0.0776 ima14 was found to be significantly larger than the optically derived redshift of z = 0.076 kim98 , which had been assumed at the time of the Cycle 0 observation setup, and (2) low-frequency (high-velocity) side of the emission tail was located at the edge of the ALMA spectral windows, which resulted in continuum oversubtraction owing to the improper inclusion of data points with significant emission components. Subsequently, we reanalyzed the HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC J=4--3 Cycle 0 data of IRAS 22491 1808 by redefining a constant continuum flux level. We found that the revised profiles of the J=4--3 emission lines were more consistent with those of the J=2--1 and J=3--2 data, and thus adopt the revised results.

For the three ULIRGs, IRAS 08572 + 3915, IRAS 12112 + 0305, and IRAS 22491 1808, molecular gas temperature was estimated to be 60--150 K, based on large beam-sized CO multiple J-transition line observations and RADEX modeling pap12a . Our ALMA HCN and HCO ^ + line study provides significantly higher temperature for nuclear molecular gas in all the three ULIRGs (see Figure fig:ULIRGfit and Table tab:bestfit ). Results for IRAS 15250 + 3609, the Superantennae, and IRAS 22491 1808 are not shown because we are unable to provide model-calculated results which reproduce the observed results very well with small systematic discrepancy.

sources.summary: The paper reports broad-component emission in the E galaxy of IRAS 22491-1808, interpreting it as a likely low-velocity nuclear outflow needing future confirmation.

quotes.quote: IRAS 22491-1808 The broad-component emission is seen in the E galaxy of this galaxy pair. The broad component is most prominent and blueshifted west of the nucleus. It spans a cone that is aligned roughly 10 65 ^ off the minor axis of the narrow-component disk, which we estimate has PA 50 ^ , based on the 0 \ iso-velocity contour.

A faint redshifted counterpart could be present towards the south, but this needs to be verified. The broad-component emission stretches 0.7 kpc from the nucleus, where it also shows the highest bulk velocities of roughly -100 \ with respect to the systemic velocity. The maximum velocity of the outflow reaches only -500 \ at zero-intensity.

sources.summary: IRAS 22491 1808 is described as a ULIRG with PAH-rich starburst-like infrared spectra, a possibly deeply embedded AGN, and elevated HCN/HCO+ ratios.

quotes.quote: IRAS 22491 1808 ( z = 0.0776) is a ULIRG with L _ IR 10 ^ 12.2 L _ (Table 1). We reported the results of our ALMA Cycle 0 observations of IRAS 22491 1808 at HCN/HCO ^ + /HNC J=4--3 lines in ima14b , and detailed information relating to this ULIRG is presented in that paper. In summary, similar to IRAS 12112 + 0305, the infrared spectra of IRAS 22491 1808 at 3--35 m are characterized by large equivalent width PAH emission features ima07b,vei09,ima10 . The observed infrared emission at 3--35 m is interpreted as originating from starburst activity, and any putative AGN in this ULIRG must be particularly deeply embedded to elude infrared spectroscopic observations. Similar to IRAS 12112 + 0305, the PAH-to-infrared luminosity ratio is smaller than that expected from modestly obscured starburst-dominated galaxies by a factor of 3--8, depending on the 3.3 m, 6.2 m, and 11.3 m PAH features ima07b,ima10 .

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 22491 1808 as a starburst-like ULIRG whose vibrational HCN, CS emission, and HCN ratios indicate a submillimeter-detectable buried AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12112 + 0305 and IRAS 22491 1808 similarly show starburst-like spectra without any discernible AGN signature in the infrared, and yet show signatures of vibrationally excited HCN v _ 2 =1f emission lines at J=3--2, most likely originating in the infrared radiative pumping by absorbing infrared photons coming from AGN-heated hot dust emission ima16c .

For IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE and IRAS 22491 1808, the HCN v _ 2 =1f to v=0 flux ratios at J=3--2, estimated in the same way as J=4--3, are both 0.06, which is also higher than that of IRAS 20551 4250 ( 0.04) ima16b,ima16c . Although some emission component of the nearby bright HCO ^ + v=0 line may contaminate the moment 0 maps of the HCN v _ 2 =1f line, the HCN v _ 2 =1f line is estimated to be emitted fairly strongly in IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE and IRAS 22491 1808.
We thus regard IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE and IRAS 22491 1808 as ULIRGs containing infrared-elusive, but (sub)millimeter detectable, extremely deeply buried AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 22491 1808 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 22491 1808. This z=0.078 ULIRG is a close (2.2 kpc) pair with two tidal tails vks02 . cui01 have proposed a multiple merger origin for this system, which is also supported by its mid- IR spectrum lutz99 . However, this ULIRG is one of the few objects with the largest AGN contribution ( L_ AGN /L_ IR 0.7 ) in the sample of farrah03 's SED study along with I15250.

sources.summary: IRAS F22491-1808 has ambiguous [C II] broad-component fitting, is identified as the OH-inflow object, and has HST tails plus two nuclei.

quotes.quote: The difference can be very large in some cases: F05189-2524 and Mrk 273 are best fitted either with a second narrow Gaussian or with a broad component, while 07251-0248, 13120-5453, F14378-3651 and F22491-1808 can be fitted either with or without a broad component.
The object with an OH inflow is IRAS F22491-1808.
Hubble Space Telescope observations of IRAS F22491-1808 reveal bright tails, which extend to 16 kpc from the center, and two nuclei separated by 2.5 kpc Surace-2000 .

sources.summary: IRAS F22491 1808 is described as a ULIRG with two nuclei, differing gas kinematics, conjectured streaming or outflowing shocked gas, and an eastern-galaxy outflow coincident with maximum line-ratio spaxels.

quotes.quote: IRAS F22491 1808 IRAS F22491 1808 is a ULIRG system with two kinematically distinct nuclei in the K band continuum and in emission approximately 2.2 kpc apart (see Paper I for detailed flux and kinematics map of and ). Since the and do not share the same kinematics, the presence of a strongly streaming or outflowing shocked gas has been conjectured. Indeed, outflows have been detected by from the eastern galaxy, which is the dimmer fainter component in the system and co-spatial with our maximum / spaxels .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 22491--1808 as the South America galaxy and finds no polarized signal, confirming a low polarization upper limit for scattered H flux.

quotes.quote: In the two other sources (IRAS 20100--4156 and IRAS 22491--1808) no polarized signals are detected.

IRAS 22491--1808 . The South America galaxy (Carico, 1990) was observed in polarized light by Young et al. (1996) in the R band, but no polarization was detected. We confirm the lack of polarization ( <1 ) and set an upper limit of 5 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 to the H scattered flux.

sources.summary: The paper says IRAS22491 FUV data are explained by leakage, but its inferred gas color excess behaves anomalously relative to the extrapolated ULIRG extinction relation.

quotes.quote: For Mrk273, IRAS08572 and IRAS12112, the FUV data taken by the HST are consistent with the best-fitting model without considering the leakage (dashed line), while for IRAS15250 and IRAS22491 the FUV data can be explained by the best-fitting model with the leakage (solid line).

Except for IRAS22491, _V^ eff of the ULIRG sample are consistent with the simply extrapolated relation from the less attenuated UVSBGs. For IRAS22491 (the only HII galaxy in our sample), E(B-V)_ gas derived from Pa /H and H /H differs in the opposite sense.

gmw_00003016.name: B-HiZELS 1
gmw_00003016.overview: B-HiZELS 1 is the most luminous H-alpha emitting galaxy known from the HiZELS survey at a redshift of z=2.23 [arXiv:1702.04721]. Although its high luminosity suggests the presence of an active galactic nucleus, the galaxy is undetected in X-rays [arXiv:1702.04721].

sources.summary: B-HiZELS 1 is described as the most luminous known z=2.23 HiZELS H emitter, also detected in Ly and [O III], with AGN-like luminosity but no X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: The most luminous H emitters at z=2.23 B-HiZELS 1 is the most luminous H emitter known from HiZELS at z=2.23 i.e. Brizels and also detected as Ly and [ O iii ] emitter. Although its high luminosity (L _ H = 7.9 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) suggests that it is an AGN, it is X-ray undetected (L _X <3 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , or <L^ _ X , LaFranca2005 ).

Although B-HiZELS 15 also has a neighbouring galaxy (at z=2.242 and a projected separation of 50 kpc) and the H luminosity is only a factor two lower than that of B-HiZELS 1, several other properties are different.

gmw_00003017.name: B-HiZELS 27
gmw_00003017.overview: B-HiZELS 27 is a dual-emitter galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.2 [arXiv:1702.04721]. It is likely a low-mass extreme emission-line galaxy or a shocked gas cloud undergoing high interaction-induced star formation with little dust extinction [arXiv:1702.04721].

sources.summary: B-HiZELS 27 is a nearby dual emitter with high H and [O III] equivalent widths, likely an extreme emission-line galaxy or shocked gas cloud.

quotes.quote: There is also a dual-emitter (B-HiZELS 27), detected in Oiii and H at the same redshift only 6 '' away (a projected distance of 50 kpc). This source has an estimated H EW _0 of 400 and Oiii EW _0 375 . This places it at the very high end of the H EW distribution at z=2.2 Fumagalli2012,Sobral2014 , and the galaxy is thus likely a low mass extreme emission line galaxy e.g. vanderWel2011 . These strong emission-lines could indicate that this galaxy may be undergoing high interaction-induced SFR combined with little extinction due to dust, or is a shocked gas cloud.

gmw_00003018.name: B-HiZELS 15
gmw_00003018.overview: B-HiZELS 15 is an active galactic nucleus at redshift z=2.244 [arXiv:1702.04721]. It is a luminous H-alpha emitter that is detected in X-rays and exhibits [O III], H-beta, and C IV emission lines [arXiv:1702.04721].

sources.summary: B-HiZELS 15 is the second-most-luminous z=2.2 H emitter, detected in [O III], H and C IV, with X-ray emission and faint continuum.

quotes.quote: The second most luminous H emitter at z=2.2 is B-HiZELS 15 at z=2.244 (L _ H = 3.6 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ). We also detect [ O iii ] , H and C iv emission-lines (there are no Ly observations at its position). Although B-HiZELS 15 also has a neighbouring galaxy (at z=2.242 and a projected separation of 50 kpc) and the H luminosity is only a factor two lower than that of B-HiZELS 1, several other properties are different. B-HiZELS 15 is X-ray detected, has a higher H EW _0 (360 versus 120 ) and is more than three magnitudes fainter in the optical and NIR continuum.

gmw_00003019.name: SDSS J110648.32+480712.3
gmw_00003019.overview: SDSS J110648.32+480712.3 is a Type 1 red quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.4350 [arXiv:2306.12541]. The system hosts a kiloparsec-scale outflowing nebula that extends over approximately 20 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2306.12541].

sources.summary: The paper maps a powerful kiloparsec-scale outflow in the z=0.435 red quasar SDSS J110648.32+480712.3 and compares optical and mid-IR tracers.

quotes.quote: Here we present a comparison of ground-based, seeing-limited \ and space-based, diffraction-limited \ maps of the powerful, kiloparsec-scale outflow in the Type 1 red quasar SDSS J110648.32+480712.3. The \ data are from the Mid-InfraRed Instrument. There is a close match in resolution between the datasets ( 0 6), in ionization potential of the O ^ +2 and S ^ +3 ions (35 eV), and in line sensitivity ( 1-2 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 ). The \ and \ line shapes match in velocity and line width over much of the 20 kpc outflowing nebula, and \ is the brightest line in the rest-frame 3.5--19.5 m range, demonstrating its usefulness as a mid-IR probe of quasar outflows.

The nearest quasar in the Q3D sample is a Type 1 quasar with z = 0.4350 and bolometric luminosity L_ bol =10^ 46 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 shen23 . SDSS J110648.32 + 480712.3 was selected as a red quasar from a cross-correlation of the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty Centimeters (FIRST; 1995ApJ...450..559B ) and Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS; skrutskie06 ) surveys 2012ApJ...757...51G . 2012ApJ...757...51G give it the catalog label F2M110648.35 + 480712.3--- \ for short---and report J - K = 2.31 mag, R - K = 4.01 mag, and = 0.44 mag, which are at the blue edge (in R - K ) of the red quasar color-color selection region.

gmw_00003020.name: J0354 1340
gmw_00003020.overview: J0354 1340 is a radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2312.13351, arXiv:2204.00020] hosted within a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2312.13351]. It features a compact, flat-spectrum core [arXiv:2312.13351, arXiv:2006.01700] that launches a parsec-scale jet [arXiv:2312.13351] and exceptionally large, double-sided radio jets [arXiv:2204.00020].

sources.summary: J0354 1340 is presented as a radio-quiet/intermediate NLS1 with a 100-kpc two-sided jet, pc-scale VLBA jet, compact core, and barred-spiral host.

quotes.quote: We present new 1.5--8.5 GHz Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations and 0.32--1.26 GHz Giant Meterwave Radio Telescope (GMRT) observations of J0354 1340, which is the only known `radio-quiet' (RQ) or `radio-intermediate' (RI) narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy with a 100-kpc two-sided radio jet. A pc-scale one-sided jet in the southeast direction from the core emission is found in the VLBA observations, while the kpc-scale jet observed with Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and GMRT is in the south-north direction. The core spectra on pc and kpc scales are presented in combination with the archival VLASS observations at 3.0 GHz and the VLA C configuration observations at 5.5 GHz. The pc-scale emission dominates the kpc-scale emission above 5 GHz, and the spectrum is inverted due to synchrotron self-absorption. This indicates a compact synchrotron source with a size of 0.04 pc, which is associated with either the jet base or the corona.

J0354 1340 ( z = 0.076, corresponding to 1.488 pc mas ^ 1 or kpc arcsec ^ 1 ) was classified as a NLS1 based on the optical spectrum from the Six-degree Field Galaxy Survey (6dFGS) Jones2009 with a FWHM of the broad H line of 2100 km s ^ 1 Chen2018 .

J0354 1340 is hosted in a barred spiral galaxy as found in a photometric decomposition study of its host galaxy Vietri2022 , which is consistent with NLS1s preferably hosted in disk-like galaxies, often with pseudo-bulges and bars Crenshaw2003b,Deo2006,Orbandexivry2011,Mathur2012,Jarvela2018,Olguiniglesias2020 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 6dFGS gJ035432.8-134008 as a radio-quiet NLS1 in a spiral/disk host with flat-spectrum core emission and unusually large double-sided relativistic jets.

quotes.quote: Here we present a remarkable example of a radio-quiet NLS1, 6dFGS gJ035432.8-134008 (J0354-1340). In our Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array observations at 5.5 GHz the source shows a bright core with a flat spectral index, and extended emission corresponding to very elongated jets. These are the largest double-sided radio jets found to date in an NLS1, with a deprojected linear size of almost 250 kpc. We also analysed near-infrared and optical images obtained by the Magellan Baade and the European Southern Observatory New Technology Telescope.

By means of photometric decomposition and colour maps, we determined that J0354-1340 is hosted by a spiral/disk galaxy. Fully evolved relativistic jets have traditionally been associated with high-mass elliptical galaxies hosting the most massive black holes, but our results confirm that also less massive black holes in spiral galaxies can launch and sustain powerful jets, implying that the launching of the jets is governed by factors other than previously believed.

sources.summary: The paper presents J0354 1340 as a compact-core NLS1 with high luminosity, flat core spectrum, extended north/south radio components, and NVSS evidence for an elongated structure.

quotes.quote: J0354 1340 This target has a very compact central core at kpc-scale with a flux density of S_ int 5.09 mJy. The VLA map is shown in Fig. J0354 . The luminosity and black hole mass are L_ int 39.58 and (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.99 respectively, which are higher than the mean values of this sample. The in-band spectral slope is flat with _ in-band 0.12. A southern component (centered at R.A. 03:54:32.09 and Dec. 13:41:08.46) has a separation of 62.2 arcsec from the central target.

gmw_00003021.name: B2 0206+35
gmw_00003021.overview: B2 0206+35 is a Fanaroff-Riley Class I radio galaxy [arXiv:1101.1807, arXiv:1107.2511] situated within a dumb-bell system at the center of a galaxy group [arXiv:1101.1807]. The galaxy hosts two-sided radio jets [arXiv:1101.1807, arXiv:1107.2511] and asymmetric X-ray emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0106246] surrounded by a galaxy-scale gas atmosphere [arXiv:1101.1807, arXiv:astro-ph/0106246].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0206+35 as an FR I radio galaxy in a dumb-bell system with two-sided jets, X-ray atmosphere, banded RM structure, and additional lower-amplitude isotropic RM fluctuations.

quotes.quote: 0206+35 is an extended Fanaroff-Riley Class I (FR I; Fana74 ) radio source whose optical counterpart, UGC1 1651, is a D-galaxy, a member of a dumb-bell system at the centre of a group of galaxies. At a resolution of 1.2 arcsec the radio emission shows a core, with smooth two-sided jets aligned in the NW-SE direction and surrounded by a diffuse and symmetric halo. LB11b have estimated that the jets are inclined by 40 with respect to the line of sight, with the main (approaching) jet in the NW direction. 0206+35 has been observed with both the ROSAT PSPC and HRI instruments Worr94,Worr00,Trus97 and with Chandra Worr01 . The X-ray emission consists of a compact source surrounded by a galactic atmosphere which merges into the much more extended intra-group gas.

We present detailed imaging of Faraday rotation and depolarization for the radio galaxies 0206+35, 3C 270, 3C 353 and M 84, based on Very Large Array observations at multiple frequencies in the range 1365 to 8440 MHz. All of the sources show highly anisotropic banded rotation measure (RM) structures with contours of constant RM perpendicular to the major axes of their radio lobes. All except M 84 also have regions in which the RM fluctuations have lower amplitude and appear isotropic.

sources.summary: The paper presents B2 0206+35 as a lobed FR I source whose overlapping lobes have sharp boundaries, internal jet structure, meandering jets, and spectral-index features tracing outflow into caps.

quotes.quote: We present deep, high-resolution imaging of the nearby Fanaroff-Riley Class I (FR I) radio galaxies NGC 193, B2 0206+35, B2 0755+37 and M 84 at frequencies of 4.9 and 1.4 GHz using new and archival multi-configuration observations from the Very Large Array. In addition, we describe lower-resolution observations of B2 0326+39 and a reanalysis of our published images of 3C 296. All of these radio galaxies show twin jets and well-defined lobes or bridges of emission, and we examine the common properties of this class of source. We show detailed images of total intensity, brightness gradient, spectral index, degree of polarization and projected magnetic-field direction.

0206+35 Fig. fig:0206all shows the total-intensity, brightness gradient, and spectral-index distributions over the whole of 0206+35 at two resolutions. Fig. fig:0206all (a) at 1.4 GHz, 4.5-arcsec FWHM resolution, shows that the large scale structure consists of two lobes, overlapping and circular in cross-section, with well-defined outer edges to the NW and SE of the source, superimposed on fainter diffuse emission to the N and S. Fig. fig:0206all (b), at 4.9 GHz and 1.2-arcsec resolution, shows the source in more detail but with reduced sensitivity to the largest-scale emission. At this resolution, both lobes show sharp outer boundaries.

sources.summary: The paper notes 0206+35 has lobe-subtracted jet spectra affected by a small high-frequency ripple, unresolved high-emissivity regions, and profiles including modeled backflow.

quotes.quote: 0206+35 Spectral-index images of the whole source were shown by LGBPB and for the jets alone (with a less stringent blanking criterion than used here) by LB12 . A residual calibration error caused a high-frequency ripple in spectral index transverse to the jet axis, visible in the brighter parts of the jets (Fig. fig:profiles d). The amplitude of this ripple is roughly 0.01 , and we do not believe that it has any systematic effect on our results. The high-emissivity region is not well enough resolved from the core for us to determine accurate spectral indices. We have included the part of the jet and counter-jet emission that we model as backflow LB12 in the binned spectral-index profiles and averages, since these are very hard to separate from the outflow component at 1.2-arcsec resolution.

sources.summary: Chandra observations of B2 0206+35 show asymmetric X-ray emission coincident with its dominant kpc-scale radio jet, with profile fits indicating extended, galaxy-scale gas of a few 10^41 erg/s.

quotes.quote: We observed B2 0206+35, 0331+39 and 0755+37 with the Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer (ACIS) on board the Chandra X-ray Observatory on the dates given in Table xrayobs .
As seen in Figs 0206image and 0755image , B2 0206+35 and 0755+37 are very similar, with asymmetric X-ray emission coincident with the dominant kpc-scale radio jet.

Best-fit values of and _ cx are 0.7 and 1.15 arcsec, and 0.8 and 2.1 arcsec, for 0206+35 and 0755+37, respectively. The extended emission can be attributed to galaxy-scale gas with a luminosity of a few times 10^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 .

gmw_00003022.name: B2 0331+39
gmw_00003022.overview: B2 0331+39 is a radio galaxy that lacks an obvious radio jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0106246]. This lack of asymmetry may be due to a small viewing angle or the absence of well-collimated kiloparsec-scale plasma [arXiv:astro-ph/0106246].

sources.summary: B2 0331+39 lacks the X-ray/radio jet asymmetry seen in the other B2 sources; its radial profile fits the PRF and only upper limits are found for galaxy-scale gas.

quotes.quote: B2 0331+39 (Fig. 0331image ) shows no such asymmetry, but in this less powerful radio source there is no obvious radio jet, either because of a small viewing angle, which could superimpose the jet X-ray emission on the core, or because well-collimated kpc-scale plasma is simply absent in this source. The relatively high ratio of core (Table radiotab ) to extended (Table xrayobs ) radio emission in this source might argue in favour of the former explanation.
The radial profile of B2 0331+39 gives a good fit to the PRF (Fig. 0331profile ).
Upper limits for B2 0331+39 allow for the presence of galaxy gas at a luminosity only a factor of a few below that measured in the other two sources.

gmw_00003023.name: Cen A-MM-Dw1
gmw_00003023.overview: Cen A-MM-Dw1 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy that is a satellite of Cen A [arXiv:1901.02679, arXiv:1907.02012]. It has a precise distance measurement from the Hubble Space Telescope and is located 249 kiloparsecs away from its host galaxy [arXiv:1901.02679].

sources.summary: Cen A-MM-Dw1 has a precise HST distance, lies 249 kpc from Cen A where the EFE matters, and is predicted to have a MOND dispersion of 11.8 km/s.

quotes.quote: We note that two of the UDGs within our sample -- Cen A-MM-Dw1 and M 96-DF6 -- already have precise distance measurements available 2018arXiv180905103C,2018ApJ...868...96C . The three dimensional separation between Cen A and Cen A-MM-dw1 is 249 kpc with a distance uncertainty for Cen A-MM-dw1 of 120 kpc. This galaxy resides at a separation where the EFE plays a role. In Figure pred1 we indicate the measured as well as the allowed 1 separations derived from the errors. Assuming that Cen-A-MM-Dw1 follows our typical estimates, it should have a velocity dispersion of =11.8_ 1.5 ^ +2.2 km s ^ 1 .

Two of our UDGs -- Cen A-MM-Dw1 and M 96-DF6 -- have high-precision distance measurements with the HST. In these cases, we have calculated the three dimensional distances to their host and made a more narrow prediction of the expected velocity dispersion in MOND, assuming that they follow a typical M/L ratio of 2. For Cen A-MM-Dw1 we expect a velocity dispersion of =11.8_ 1.5 ^ +2.2 km s ^ 1 , and for M 96-DF6 a velocity dispersion of =9.5_ 1.0 ^ +1.0 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Cen A-MM-dw1 as a Cen A dwarf satellite in proposed close pairs with Cen A-MM-dw2 and Cen A-MM-dw3, then excludes the latter pair.

quotes.quote: 2014ApJ...795L..35C announced the first discovery of a pair of satellites outside of the Local Group, the dwarf galaxies Cen A-MM-dw1 and Cen A-MM-dw2, which are satellites of Cen A. Their projected separation is only 3 arcmin (= 3.9 kpc at Cen A's distance), and they shared the same distance estimates. But with their updated distances 2019ApJ...872...80C , Cen A-MM-dw2 seems to be located 200 kpc further away than what was previously assumed. 2019ApJ...872...80C still argue that the probability for a chance alignment is negligible and, therefore, that they are indeed a pair of satellites given the distance uncertainties.

There are two other galaxies, Cen A-MM-dw1 and Cen A-MM-dw3, with a projection separation of 18 arcmin (= 20 kpc at a distance of 3.89 Mpc) and a 3D separation of 36 kpc. However, Cen A-MM-dw3 is a tidally disrupted dwarf extending over several hundred kpc, which complicates the analysis. We therefore excluded these two galaxies from the list of pairs.

gmw_00003024.name: NGC 4594-DGSAT-1
gmw_00003024.overview: NGC 4594-DGSAT-1 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1901.02679]. Under Modified Newtonian Dynamics, the galaxy is expected to have a typical velocity dispersion of 5.8 km/s [arXiv:1901.02679].

sources.summary: NGC 4594-DGSAT-1 is identified as the sample galaxy most affected by the EFE; the paper uses it to illustrate a MOND velocity dispersion of 5.8 km/s.

quotes.quote: The most affected galaxy in terms of the EFE in our sample -- max( _ iso / _ typical ) -- is NGC 4594-DGSAT-1. Let us make a gedankenexperiment, where we live in a MONDian universe, but fail to realize that, and thus describe it in terms of Newtonian dynamics. Assume that the calculated typical velocity dispersion correctly describes the UDGs, and thus that NGC 4594-DGSAT-1 has a typical velocity dispersion of =5.8 km s ^ 1 . Now our Newtonian observers have exactly measured this value to infinite precision, and using it with 2010MNRAS.406.1220W they will derive a mass within r_ 1/2 of 5.0 10^7 M _ . The mass of the baryonic content of the galaxy inferred from the light is 6 10^7 M _ , giving a dynamical to visible mass ratio within r_ 1/2 of only 1.7, and hence a dynamical mass-to-light ratio M_ dyn /L of 3.4.

gmw_00003025.name: NGC 1533
gmw_00003025.overview: NGC 1533 is a barred lenticular galaxy in the Dorado group that features an outer, UV-bright incomplete ring structure [arXiv:1803.03967, arXiv:1808.04491]. The galaxy is currently interacting with its dwarf neighbors, IC 2038 and IC 2039, via an extended hydrogen gas arm [arXiv:2009.04090, arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: NGC 1533 is presented as an SB0 galaxy in Dorado with a UV-bright ring and H I cloud; simulations reproduce it through a 2:1 merger and recent outer-ring star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1533, classified (RL)SB0 ^0 Comeron14 , has a prominent UV-bright incomplete ring structure (Figure fig:1b ). It is located in a clump of the Dorado group ( =0.88 galaxies Mpc ^ 3 , Tully88 ), in a large cloud of HI (7.4 10^ 9 M _ see Table tbl-1 ).
