Mazzei14b provided details on the evolution of NGC 1533, from the simulation accounting for its global properties. The simulation succeeded in reproducing its absolute magnitude, SED, morphology including the ring (their Fig. 6), with a merger between halos with mass ratio 2:1 and perpendicular spins.

The predicted galaxy age is 13.7 Gyr, that estimated from weighting the age of its stellar populations by B-band luminosity is 6 Gyr, younger than that of NGC 1426. Using line-strength indices, Annibali07 calculated a luminosity weighted age of 11.9 6.9 Gyr with the large uncertainty due to the presence of emission lines which perturb the measure of H . However, the far and near ultraviolet emissions revealed by GALEX Marino11c and by our Swift UVOT images suggest quite recent SF events in the outer ring of this galaxy. Summarizing, the local ( z=0 ) global properties of both NGC 1426 and NGC 1533, the first without, the second with peculiar features in the ultraviolet respectively, are explained in terms of a major merger event.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1533 as a barred Dorado Group S0 dominated by old, near-solar stellar populations, with little disk-dominated signal and only a small recent component.

quotes.quote: NGC 1533 This S0 galaxy is a part of the Dorado Group with a clearly discernable bar structure. It exhibits a smooth appearance and one look at the spectrum of the galaxy reveals that it is dominated by a very old population. An age gradient plot reveals that indeed, 95 of the galaxy light is dominated by old stars.

The metallicity is also near solar which is not too surprising given other features of this galaxy. This galaxy is clearly quite different from the first two examples in our sample - devoid of star formation and dominated by older stars. It is also different in another major way - it is a barred galaxy. Its formation history is consistent with an old coeaval population but with a small component of recently formed stars. These stars could have formed through gas infall induced by bars.

sources.summary: NGC 1533 is a lenticular Dorado galaxy with blue and red supergiants, an H I ring and arm toward IC 2038, and young ring stars whose high metallicity points to NGC 1533 gas.

quotes.quote: The diagram of the galaxy NGC 1533 (Fig. fig4 ) is interesting, because in the lenticular galaxy, where large regions of star formation are not visible, there are blue and red supergiants. Radio observations at H I Ryan-Weber_etal_2004 revealed a ring structure around NGC 1533 and an extended arm connecting it to the IC 2038 dwarf galaxy, with which it interacts Cattapan_etal_2019 . The structure of the gas arm between galaxies is clearly visible in the study of Werk_etal_2010 (diagram 7). The obtained CM diagram of NGC 1533 (Fig. fig4 a) shows a branch inhabited by globular clusters at a color index (V-I) = 1 .

An important question in the study of young stars in NGC 1533 is the source of gas from which the young stars, visible in the CM diagram, were born. Since NGC 1533 interacts with the dwarf irregular galaxy IC 2038, which has hydrogen, it can be assumed that the periphery of the dwarf galaxy could have been stripped off and became a source of hydrogen for NGC 1533.

The high metallicity of the stars of the ring indicates that the hydrogen, from which they were born, can only belong to NGC 1533, despite the fact that this galaxy is poor in hydrogen. The lower metallicity of the stars in the arm between NGC 1533 and IC 2038 probably indicates the participation of the dwarf galaxy IC 2038, which has low metallicity hydrogen in sufficient quantity, in the processes of star formation in the arm between these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1533 as an S0 with ring or arm-like star formation, matching its SED, surface brightness, kinematics, and late-to-early-type transition through a merger simulation.

quotes.quote: As a case study we focus on simulations of two S0s with ring/arm like structures, detected in the far UV and/or narrow band optical images, immersed in extended HI emission, namely NGC 1533 (Fig. HI4 ) and NGC 3626 (Fig. HI4_II ).

NGC 1533 is a SB 0- in the RC3 catalogue DV91 , a SB 02(2)/ SBa in the RSA catalogue Sandage87 , and a (L)SB(rs)0^0 in the NED database. This latter classification implies a large lens outside the bar and an inner ring Laurietal06 . Ant11_II found a prominent outer ring in the UV images extending from 39 ^" to 80 " , and consisting of young ( 200 Myr old) stellar populations. More details on GALEX observations are given in Ant11 . Werketal10 found a large-scale distribution of HI around the galaxy, as shown in Fig. HI4 . RW03 reported HI arcs around the galaxy, spanning from 2 ' to 11 ' .7 outside the FUV ring, with a mass of 9.3 10 ^8 M _ (their Table A1). They concluded that the HI is most likely the remnant of a tidally disrupted galaxy. This HI mass estimate increases by at least 6 times adopting the distance in dG07 . From optical spectroscopy in the central region of this galaxy, Ann07 derived an age of 11.9 6.9 Gyr. dG07 describes NGC 1533 as a galaxy which is completing a transition from late to early type: it is red, but not completely dead.
NGC 1533 The same simulation which best fits the global properties of NGC 3626, also matches those of NGC 1533 at a snapshot 2.15 Gyr older. This corresponds to a galaxy age of 13.7 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1533 as a Dorado-group barred lenticular with decoupled gas-star kinematics, outer star-forming rings, satellites, and extended gas reservoirs.

quotes.quote: We have fulfilled a detailed long-slit spectroscopic analysis for two SB0 galaxies -- NGC 1533 and NGC 1543, -- belonging to the Dorado group. Our spectral data reveal asymmetric decoupled kinematics of the stars and ionised gas in these barred lenticular galaxies that give evidences for external origin of the gas in the rings. We have calculated the star formation rates in the rings by using the ultraviolet fluxes of the rings corrected for the foreground and intrinsic absorption; and we have estimated parameters of the stellar populations in the inner parts of the galaxies confirming that they are old -- except the nucleus of NGC 1543, which demonstrates signatures of re-juvenation less than 5 Gyr ago.

NGC 1543 looks quite isolated, while NGC 1533 has two close dwarf satellites, IC 2038 and IC 2039, in some 60 kpc from it. Ultraviolet (UV) features of recent star formation were found in NGC 1533 and NGC 1543 long ago. Over this field great efforts were applied by Roberto Rampazzo with co-authors. Firstly, they have used UV data of the spacecraft Swift Trinchieri_Swift,Rampazzo_Swift to discover outer UV-bright rings in these SB0 galaxies -- with the radius of up to R_ ring 50^ in NGC 1533 and up to R_ ring 3^ in NGC 1543.

Finally, NGC 1533 has been deeply mapped with the Indian UV space telescope Astrosat/UVIT, and an outer low surface brightness UV ring was found which is coincident with the HI distribution, rather peculiar around NGC 1533.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1533 as having UV-bright arm-like structures visible in Swift images, dominating NUV luminosity profiles and appearing as light excesses in Sersic residuals.

quotes.quote: This feature is also visible in our Swift UVOT images. In particular, the top right panel of Figure NGC1533-sb shows that the UV bright structures are reminiscent of arms. Approximating the edge of the UV bright North-West ``arc'' with an ellipse having PA=120 ^ 2 and semi-major and semi-minor axes a=58 and b=45 , respectively, we note that this ellipse does not include the UV bright arm-like structure in the South-West. These arm-like structures are predominant features in the NUV luminosity profiles (Figure NGC1533-sb , mid panel). The single S ersic fit, shown in Figure NGC1533-sb (bottom panel), includes the NUV bright structures.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1533 as an E-S0 Dorado member with an outer ring, FUV ring/residual structures, extended FUV emission, and an H I ring consistent with a tidally destroyed galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 1533 The galaxy, classified as E-S0 ( HyperLeda ) is known to have an outer ring and a inner bar, so Comer on et al. (2014) classified it as (RL)SB0 ^0 . However, in FUV this galaxy shows no signature of the bar.

Cattapan et al. (2019; see their Figure 7) superposed the emission of NGC 1533 in the W2 filter derived by Rampazzo et al. (2017) from Swift-UVOT observations to their wide field, deep g-band image obtained at VST (see Figure Dorado-group ). They found that the FUV ring of NGC 1533 is superposed to spiral-like residuals obtained after a model of the optical luminosity profile has been subtracted.

The presence of this FUV emission would be consistent with the N-body SPH numerical simulations by Ryan-Weber et al. (2003) describing the \ ring around NGC 1533 as the remnant of a tidally destroyed galaxy (see Section 6.2).

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 1533 as an interacting ring system whose optical and HI morphologies differ and whose HI extends toward IC 2038 and IC 2039.

quotes.quote: NGC 1533: This is an interesting galaxy as the optical morphology and the 1 morphology are very different as seen in the bottom right panels in Fig. fig:mosaic4 . The optical image of this galaxy shows a bright nucleus, a bar and a very uniform early-type morphology. There appears to be a hazy smooth outer ring. The optical image also shows two smaller neighbouring galaxies (IC 2038 and IC 2039) to the northwest of NGC 1533. show that the 1 gas from NGC 1533 extends all the way to both IC 2038 and IC 2039, thus hinting that these galaxies are currently interacting.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1533 as a barred S0 with an incomplete UV ring whose blue, star-forming structure lies at the end of the bar.

quotes.quote: The sample includes five S0 galaxies: NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 (interacting with NGC 5638, an elliptical galaxy). Notice that NGC 2974 is erroneously classified as elliptical both in RC3 and RSA, although an exponential disk is evident from the optical and UV luminosity profiles see also Jeong07, Jeong09, Marino10a . In addition, in NGC 2974 Krajnovic05 found, with SAURON observations deZeeuw02 , the existence of non-axisymmetric perturbations consistent with the presence of inner bars.

NGC 1533 UV image shows an incomplete, clumpy ring-like structure, while NGC 2962, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 which have well defined and complete rings, although not uniform, in the FUV and NUV bands. Spiral arm-like structures are visible in both NGC 2962 and in NGC 2974. Both the ring and the arm-like structure appear bluer than the nucleus and the galaxy body.

S0s with rings like NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 are located in the so called green valley, where evolving objects are found, while the ring structures are located in the blue sequence. The blue, star forming rings in NGC 4245, NGC 5636 and NGC 1533 are located at the end of the bar structure. The optical and UV images, shown in Figure  fig1 , suggests that NGC 2962 has two rings, one at the edge of the bar, the other is an outer ring. Only the outer ring in NGC 2962 is visible in UV GALEX images.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1533 as a barred E-S0 with rings, elevated central equivalent width, and detected H-alpha regions in the galaxy outskirts.

quotes.quote: NGC 1533 The galaxy is classified E-S0 with bar (Type=-2.5 0.6) by HyperLeda and (RL)SB0 ^0 by Comeron2014 , i.e. the galaxy has an outer ring, a lens and an inner bar.
NGC 1553, IC 2039 and NGC 1533 show higher EW values at the centre, 1, 1, and 1.5, respectively. Moreover, the quasi-constant trend of NGC 1533 sets on an higher value than on average.
We detect \ regions in the galaxy outskirts of NGC 1533 and reference therein Werk2010 and in NGC 1553, detected also by GALEX as FUV-emitting regions.

gmw_00003026.name: NGC 1672
gmw_00003026.overview: NGC 1672 is a barred spiral Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1811.00984, arXiv:2205.05679] located within the Dorado group [arXiv:2011.03324]. It features a strong stellar bar [arXiv:0801.2356, arXiv:1103.3796], a bright circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:1104.1649, arXiv:2301.03811], and a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1104.1649, arXiv:1105.4610].

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra and XMM-Newton observations of NGC 1672, a nearby barred spiral with vigorous star formation, ULXs near bar ends, and a faint hard central X-ray source within a circumnuclear starburst ring.

quotes.quote: We present some preliminary results from new Chandra and XMM-Newton X-ray observations of the nearby barred spiral galaxy NGC 1672. It shows dramatic nuclear and extra-nuclear star formation activity, including starburst regions located near each end of its strong bar, both of which host ultraluminous X-ray sources (ULXs). With the new high-spatial-resolution Chandra imaging, we show for the first time that NGC 1672 possesses a faint ( L_X 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 ), hard central X-ray source surrounded by an X-ray bright circumnuclear starburst ring that dominates the X-ray emission in the region. The central source may represent low-level AGN activity, or alternatively the emission from X-ray binaries associated with star-formation in the nucleus.

For this pilot study, we have selected the nearby (16.3 Mpc) late-type barred spiral galaxy NGC 1672 (SB()bc). It is known to have a strong bar (2.4 arcmin in length), and vigorous star formation is seen at its ends. It has a high infrared (IR) luminosity (log L_ FIR /L_ = 10.4 ), and many H II regions in its four spiral arms ( brandt96 , and references therein). Optical studies have given conflicting evidence as to the nature of the nuclear activity in NGC 1672.

sources.summary: For NGC 1672, the paper models ALMA torus velocity-dispersion maps, adopting a 10 pc torus radius, a 1e8 solar-mass SMBH, and central maxima interpreted as a torus throat.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 and NGC 1672 velocity dispersion maps Here we apply our simulations to other AGNs for which the observational data of a torus are available. As examples, we choose NGC 1326 and NGC 1672, since the central regions are identified as the obscuring tori. Their velocity dispersion maps are presented in fig. fig:alma bottom from Combes2019 .

The major radii of the tori were taken from the ALMA observational maps: R=10 pc for NGC 1672 and R=8 pc for NGC 1326. The best combinations of the parameters are the following; for NGC 1672, the SMBH mass M_ smbh = 1 10^8 M_ ; the inclination angle of the torus = 60^ , with the relative cloud radius _ cl =0.01 or = 45^ with _ cl =0.025 ; for NGC 1326, M_ smbh = 1.5 10^7 M_ ; = 60^ with _ cl =0.01 or = 45^ with _ cl =0.025 . For all cases, the initial half-opening angle of the wind is = 45^ . Fig. fig:alma shows that our model velocity dispersion maps and the ALMA observational maps of the central regions of NGC 1672 and NGC 1326 are in qualitative agreement (as well as the NGC 1068 maps in Imanishi2018 discussed above). Velocity dispersion maps of NGC 1672 and NGC 1326 show central maxima that we identify as a torus throat, while the maximum in the left part of NGC 1326 torus can also be associated with external accretion.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1672 as a barred Seyfert 2 with central star formation, obscured low-luminosity AGN activity, a contrasted CO ring, and a decoupled molecular torus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1672 is a strongly barred Seyfert 2 galaxy, with a high star-forming activity in its center. The AGN activity is therefore hard to find through line diagnostics Storchi1996, Kewley2000 . with ROSAT find a diffuse X-ray nuclear source, rather soft, compatible with a superbubble interpretation (i.e., thermal emission from star formation).

The ring is located at the inner ILR, and is also quite contrasted in the CO(3-2) emission (figure fig:1566-1672 ). Inside the ring, some thin filaments join towards a central concentration, which looks like a torus of radius 0.5 =27 pc seen more inclined than the large-scale disk. A kinematic decoupling helps to define the torus, with a kinematic major axis of the torus of PA=90 ^ , and an outside axis of PA=135 ^ . The continuum emission peaks just at the center.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1672 as a nearby late-type barred spiral hosting a ULX population, with nine ULXs inside its D25 area and most studied sources lying along a spiral arm.

quotes.quote: Therefore, this work is focused on NIR counterparts of ULXs in the case of NGC 1672, a late-type barred spiral galaxy at a distance of 16.3 Mpc 2000AJ....119..612D . This galaxy has been studied by 2011ApJ...734...33J , who identified 9 ULXs, and 2022MNRAS.515.3632A who investigated possible optical counterparts of these ULXs. The primary goal of this work is to search for and identify possible NIR counterparts of these nine ULXs by deploying all available JWST NIRCam images. X-ray study of the nearby barred spiral galaxy NGC 1672 was performed by . Nine out of 28 X-ray sources were identified as ULXs within the D _ 25 area of this galaxy.

It is noteworthy that while source ULX-4 is located close to the center of the galaxy, remaining ULXs are located in a spiral arm (see Figure F:rgb ). This distribution is not a surprise, as ULXs are often located in dense or star-forming regions of host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1672 has leading-side bar velocity gradients, no preferred ionization class in high-shock regions, and high nuclear-ring CO, H, and velocity gradients.

quotes.quote: However, in NGC 1672 and NGC 3627, velocity gradients are slightly offset towards to the peak of the CO emission. Velocity gradients are enhanced on the leading side of the bar in NGC 1672 and in the southern part of the NGC 3627.
For NGC 1672, high shocks and shear regions are not preferentially occupied by any types of ionizing sources.

NGC 1672 In Figure fig:sfrsd_vg , there are two branches that stick out prominently, one with high _ mol , and one with lower _ mol . The branch with high _ mol , where the _ mol ranges between 10^ 1 and 10^0 and the velocity gradients between 0.2 and 0.7 , belong to the nuclear region (R < 2.5'') inside the nuclear ring. In this region, CO (2 1) and H intensity is high, and also the velocity gradient is extraordinarily high. The elevated velocity gradients may be caused by intense winds from young stars, as it appears to be the case in other galaxies with star-bursting nuclear rings, such as NGC 3351 (see leaman_19 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength X-ray observations of barred spiral NGC 1672, identifying many X-ray sources, a hard low-luminosity nuclear source, and a bright circumnuclear star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: We have performed an X-ray study of the nearby barred spiral galaxy NGC 1672, primarily to ascertain the effect of the bar on its nuclear activity. We use both Chandra and XMM-Newton observations to investigate its X-ray properties, together with supporting high-resolution optical imaging data from the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ), infrared imaging from the Spitzer Space Telescope , and ATCA ground-based radio data. We detect 28 X-ray sources within the D_ 25 area of the galaxy, many of which correlate spatially with star-formation in the bar and spiral arms, while two are identified as background galaxies in the HST images.

With the spatial resolution of Chandra , we are able to show for the first time that NGC 1672 possesses a hard ( 1.5 ) nuclear X-ray source with a 2--10 keV luminosity of 4 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . This is surrounded by an X-ray bright circumnuclear star-forming ring, comprised of point sources and hot gas, which dominates the 2--10 keV emission in the central region of the galaxy. The spatially resolved multiwavelength photometry indicates that the nuclear source is a low-luminosity AGN (LLAGN), but with star formation activity close to the central black hole.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1672 as a nearby, strongly barred, actively star-forming spiral, using HST and MUSE data to resolve compact H II regions across its disk.

quotes.quote: We constrain the relative importance of the various feedback mechanisms from young massive star populations by resolving \ regions across the disk of the nearby star-forming galaxy NGC 1672. We combine measurements of ionised gas nebular lines obtained by PHANGS-MUSE, with high-resolution (PSF FWHM 0.1 ; 10 pc) imaging from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) in both the narrow-band H and broad-band (NUV, U , B , V , I ) filters. We identify a sample of 40 isolated, compact \ regions in the HST H image.

We make use of HST and VLT/MUSE observations towards a single galaxy: NGC 1672 (see Emsellem2022, Lee2021 for survey overviews). NGC 1672 is a nearby (19.4 2.9 Mpc; Anand2021 ), strongly-barred and actively star-forming spiral galaxy. It is a good candidate for this initial study thanks to its high star formation rate (7.6 M_ yr^ 1 ; Leroy2021a ), which yields a large sample of \ regions to study, and moderate inclination ( i 40 ^ ; Lang2020 ), which limits the effects of extinction and line-of-sight confusion.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1672 as a barred spiral with a star-forming circumnuclear ring, low-level nuclear activity, and stellar-population gradients tied to its bulge and inner disc contrast.

quotes.quote: NGC 1672: An SB(s)b, barred spiral galaxy, with a circumnuclear ring of star formation at 0.350 kpc (S ersic Pastoriza 1965 SP65 ; Comeron et al. 2007) surrounding a nucleus with low-level activity (Veron-Cetty Veron 1986 VV86 ), also containing a star forming region. The bar size is 5.1 kpc (Buta 1987) and the inner Lindblad resonance (ILR) is calculated to be 0.49 kpc from the galaxy center, coincident with the edge of the circumnuclear ring.

In particular, the decreasing [ E/Fe ] profile in NGC 1672 is due to the contrast between the bulge region and the inner disc region, where the stellar populations show a minimum in metallicity and [ E/Fe ] . The contrast between these two regions is also responsible for the positive age gradient and for the very steep metallicity gradient in the bulge region, where the star formation is proceeding from low-metallicity gas. The higher values of [ E/Fe ] and metallicity in the central region may be indicating that the star formation was more intense in the central region than in the circumnuclear ring () .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1672 as a Seyfert galaxy in the Dorado group, derives a TRGB distance of 15.8 Mpc, and uses that distance to study its nucleus and brightest stars.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert galaxy NGC 1672 (Figure  Fig8 ) is probably part of the Dorado group S1966 .

Using archival images of the Hubble Space Telescope (ID10354 and ID15654), we performed stellar photometry of this galaxy with the DAOPHOT II Stetson_1987, Stetson_1994 and DOLPHOT 2.0 software packages.

The obtaned distance value ( D = 15.8 0.8  Mpc) confirms that NGC 1672 belongs to the Dorado group. The obtained distance allows us to measure the energy of the galactic nucleus, and to determine the luminosities of the brightest stars, which are visible on the obtained CM diagram (Figure  Fig9 ). Figure  Fig8 , a circle indicates a young star cluster surrounded by a gas shell (Figure  Fig10 ) and containing stars up to M_V = -10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1672 as an Sb galaxy with specified inclination, position angle, optical radius, distance, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 1672 is a Sb galaxy (morphological type code T = 3.3 0.6). The inclination angle of NGC 1672 is i = 43 , and the position angle of the major axis PA = 134 Lang2020 . The optical radius is 3.30 arcmin or 198.2 arcsec RC3 . At the distance of d = 19.40 Mpc Anand2021 , the physical optical radius is R_ 25 = 18.64 kpc. The stellar mass is M _ = 5.37 10 ^ 10 M _ Leroy2021 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1672 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing There is a bright spiral arm that almost completely wraps around the center of the bulge; Near 6 this spiral breaks into more spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 1672 .SBS3../Sb(rs) Pseudobulge. There is a bright spiral arm that almost completely wraps around the center of the bulge. Near 6 this spiral breaks into more spiral arms. The decomposition yields n_b=2.1 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =2.0 mag.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 1672 is a primary-sample galaxy with low-luminosity AGN evidence, but its central X-ray source was not detected under the authors' wavdetect settings.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies from the primary sample are known to have a low luminosity AGN: NGC 1672, NGC 3079 and M51A 2006A&A...455..773V . Whereas the central sources of M51A, NGC 3079 were detected and excluded from the further analysis, no central X-ray source was found in NGC 1672. However, 2011ApJ...734...33J found in this galaxy an evidence for a low-luminosity nucleus, with the 2-10 keV luminosity of 4 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . Its non-detection in our analysis is likely a result of different wavdetect settings, in particular of a higher detection threshold used (in fact, visual inspection of the image did reveal a hump on top of the diffuse emission in the center of the galaxy).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1672 as an edge-on Seyfert 2 torus example where ALMA resolves a compact velocity gradient and gas-dynamical modeling gives a black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: The example of the Sy2 NGC 1672, where the torus is seen almost edge-on, is displayed in Fig. fig3 . The ALMA resolution is 3pc. The position-velocity diagrm reveals a strong velocity gradient of about 180 km/s in 30pc. A 3D modelisation of the dynamics of the nuclear disk has led to a black hole mass of 5 10 ^7 M _ Combes2019 .

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1672 as the sample galaxy with auroral-line abundance checks confirming strong-line azimuthal trends and clear chemical enrichment along a spiral arm.

quotes.quote: These yield qualitatively similar results to our preferred Scal method. used auroral line detections to directly measure abundance variations for one galaxy in our sample, NGC 1672, across \ regions in one spiral arm. Those authors find very good qualitative agreement with the trends identified using the Scal strong line method, increasing our confidence in the use of this calibration, particularly in relation to relative metallicity trends.

In addition, the detailed azimuthal trends we reported in Paper I based on strong-line abundances have been confirmed with auroral line electron temperature determinations in one of our galaxies (NGC 1672; Ho+2019 ), increasing our confidence in the statistical trends we report in this paper.

As the expected trends depend on a determination of the pattern speed and corotation radius Spitoni2019 , a full dynamical model of individual galaxies would be needed to reveal trends see, e.g. the case study of NGC 1672, where clear chemical enrichment is observed along the spiral arm ridge Ho+2019 , but this is beyond the scope of this paper.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 1672 has homogeneous sub-0.2 kpc ISM variance, good KT18 semivariogram-shape agreement, and extra O3N2 diagnostic uncertainty.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1672, NGC 4254, and NGC 4535, the amount of variance seen between H ii regions separated by less than 0.2 kpc is consistent with the amount of variance expected from measurement error, indicating that the ISM is reasonably homogeneous for these galaxies on scales below 0.2 kpc.

While the total amount of variance predicted by the KT18 model is far less than what is observed, the scale over which metallicities are correlated and the overall shape of the predicted semivariograms show very good agreement to the data, especially for NGC 1087, NGC 1672, and NGC 3627.

For NGC 1672, NGC 2835, and to some extent NGC 3627, the shape of the empirical semivariograms computed using the O3N2 diagnostic matches the shape of the semivariogram computed using the Scal diagnostic, with the overall values of the semivariogram translated upwards by a constant factor (typically 4 10^ 4 8 10^ 4 ). This reflects the additional measurement uncertainty associated with the O3N2 diagnostic: for NGC 1672, this corresponds to an additional uncertainty of _Z = 0.03 dex, whereas for NGC 2835, this corresponds to an additional uncertainty of _Z = 0.04 dex.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1672 as having a sharp age drop at the bar radius and a stellar disk whose radial trend turns negative in the outer regions.

quotes.quote: Some galaxies show a sharp drop in LumW age at the outer radius of the bar (NGC 1300, NGC 1365, NGC 1672, NGC 7496), consistent with a scenario where the oldest populations dominate in the inner and rounder part of the bar, while young populations are located on more elongated orbits, populating the bar ends, where they spend a larger fraction of their orbiting period Beuther2017, Wozniak2007, Neumann2020 .

Other galaxies, such as NGC 1672 or NGC 1300 show a positive trend across parts of their stellar disk, and then a negative trend in the outer radii. Such a negative trend suggests that in these galaxies, the outer regions have remained relatively quiescent for long periods of time, compared to the inner radii. This does not imply that they lack current star formation, but it means they have not formed a substantial amount of stellar mass in the last 5 Gyr.

sources.summary: NGC 1672 is presented as a large-barred Seyfert with very strong nucleus, bar, and inner-arm radio emission, a starburst-surrounded nucleus, and upstream polarized emission turning inward.

quotes.quote: NGC 1672 (Fig. 20) has the second largest bar in our sample ( 20 kpc). Its radio emission is very strong in the nucleus, the bar and the inner part of the spiral arm region. The nucleus is known to be of Seyfert 2 type and is surrounded by a starburst region (Evans et al. \ ). Polarized emission is strongest in the northeastern region upstream of the dust lanes, with magnetic field lines at large angles to the bar, smoothly turning towards the centre as in NGC 1097 and 1365 (Fig. 26).

sources.summary: NGC 1672 is identified as a Sy2 Dorado galaxy; the paper cites a prior Tully-Fisher distance and uses its extended stellar halo periphery for TRGB red-giant distance work.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433 and NGC 1672 belong to Sy2 galaxies, NGC 1566 to Sy1, and NGC 1533 to LINER galaxies (NED).
Using this method, Tully_etal_2009 determined that the distances to NGC 1433 and NGC 1672 were 8.32 Mpc and 11.9 Mpc, respectively.

In NGC 1672, the stellar halo extends far beyond the galaxy's body visible in Fig. fig1 , so an image of the galaxy's periphery, where bright stars occupy only a part of the image, was used to search for red giants.

sources.summary: NGC 1672 is one of the paper's outflow candidates, but cited high-resolution CO, hot molecular, and ionized-gas studies do not find outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: Our study may have missed one potential outflow candidate among the PHANGS galaxies, namely NGC 1068, and for two of our candidates (NGC 1365, NGC 1672) high resolution CO (3--2) observations of the galaxy centers provided no clear evidence for molecular outflows.

NGC 1672 : Combes2019ALMATori study the cold molecular gas torus in the central region and do not find evidence for an outflow. Fazeli2020 rules out the presence of an outflow in hot molecular and ionized gas.

sources.summary: NGC 1672 is noted for enriched regions tracing spiral structure, a supernova-contaminated observation, and a particularly bright nuclear star-forming ring needing careful deblending.

quotes.quote: While in some galaxies the enriched regions (in red) appear to strongly trace the spiral pattern (e.g. NGC 1365, NGC 1566, NGC 1672), they can also generally be found throughout the entire disk.
Finally, our observation of NGC 1672 happened to occur only shortly after a supernova event in the galaxy, AT2017gax, and as a result is poorly fit in our catalogue and has ID 429.

Three galaxies (Figure fig:starburstrings ) host particularly bright nuclear star-forming rings (NGC 1672, NGC 3351, NGC 4321), and we caution against over-interpretation of these integrated nebular fluxes without more careful spatial decomposition or deblending of objects.

gmw_00003027.name: IC 2038
gmw_00003027.overview: IC 2038 is an Scd spiral galaxy [arXiv:2009.14731] that is also classified as a hydrogen-rich dwarf irregular [arXiv:2009.04090]. It exhibits clumpy star formation [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2012.10306] and shows signatures of interaction with NGC 1533 [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2009.04090], which is likely stripping its gas [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2009.04090].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 2038 as an interacting Scd spiral with clumpy H-alpha emission, depressed star formation, and evidence for stripping by NGC 1533.

quotes.quote: IC 2038/IC 2039 The two galaxies are classified Scd (Type=7.0 0.5) and E-S0 (Type=-3.1 0.7), respectively. According to Cattapan2019 the galaxies show clear signatures of interaction. The \ distribution of IC 2038, shown in Figure IC2038 , is clumpy and marks the presence of \ regions, without any obvious arm structure. \ regions are distributed along the galaxy body which appears slightly elongated towards the companion galaxy, in the SE direction see their Figure 2 Cattapan2019 .

Our observations confirm that SF is detected not only in the spiral galaxy IC 2038 and in the centre of NGC 1533 but also along the \ tails. IC 2038 SF is depressed if compared with objects of the same morphological type (Figure SFR_comparison ) likely due to the on-going stripping see e.g.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2038 as an Sbc galaxy with clumpy central FUV emission, disk-dominated profile, FUV emission out to optical size, and star-formation behavior compared with H-alpha results.

quotes.quote: The FUV emission appears clumpy in the centre of the galaxy, where \ regions are detected by Rampazzo et al. (2020), while an arm-like structure appears out the both sides of the galaxy body. The FUV surface brightness profile is flat in the central 20 The best fit to the entire profile has a S ersic index of n=0.81 0.09 suggesting the galaxy is dominated by the disk. In the central 25 \ there is an excess of luminosity with respect to the S ersic law best fit, in correspondence to FUV clumps. The FUV emission extends out to the optical size of the galaxy as shown by Figure Tricromia_IC2038 and Figure Dorado-group : there is no evidence of a XUV disk (Thilker 2008).

The SFR of IC 2038, the only LTG in the sub-structure, do not deviate from Lee et al. relatio However, according to Rampazzo et al. (2020) the SFR of IC 2038 estimated from \ is below the average for its morphological class estimated by James et al. (2004).

sources.summary: IC 2038 is described as a hydrogen-rich dwarf irregular interacting with NGC 1533; its lower-metallicity gas likely participates in star formation in the arm between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: Radio observations at H I Ryan-Weber_etal_2004 revealed a ring structure around NGC 1533 and an extended arm connecting it to the IC 2038 dwarf galaxy, with which it interacts Cattapan_etal_2019 .

Since NGC 1533 interacts with the dwarf irregular galaxy IC 2038, which has hydrogen, it can be assumed that the periphery of the dwarf galaxy could have been stripped off and became a source of hydrogen for NGC 1533.

The lower metallicity of the stars in the arm between NGC 1533 and IC 2038 probably indicates the participation of the dwarf galaxy IC 2038, which has low metallicity hydrogen in sufficient quantity, in the processes of star formation in the arm between these galaxies.

gmw_00003028.name: BDF521
gmw_00003028.overview: BDF521 is a bright Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=7.012 [arXiv:2203.04976]. It resides within a reionized bubble alongside other ionizing sources [arXiv:2203.04976, arXiv:1601.03442], including the nearby galaxy BDF2195 to which it is likely physically connected [arXiv:2203.04976].

sources.summary: BDF521 is a bright z=7.0121 Ly-emitting galaxy in the BDF bubble; XSHOOTER measured Ly flux, EW 60, and FWHM 263 km/s while constraining its ionizing budget.

quotes.quote: We investigate the ionizing properties of the pair of bright Ly emitting galaxies BDF521 and BDF2195 at z=7.012 in order to constrain their contribution to the formation of the BDF "reionized bubble" Castellano2016,Castellano2018 in which they have been shown to reside. We obtain constraints on UV emission lines (CIV 1548 doublet, HeII 1640 , OIII ] 1660 doublet, and CIII ] 1909 doublet) from deep VLT-XSHOOTER observations and compare them to those available for other high-redshift objects, and to models with mixed stellar and AGN emission. We use this spectroscopic information together with the photometry available in the field to constrain the physical properties of the two objects using the spectro-photometric fitting code BEAGLE .

Ly emission We detect Ly line emission for both objects: the flux-weighted mean wavelength of the line corresponds to z=7.0121 and z=7.0124 for BDF521 and BDF2195, respectively. These redshifts are slightly higher than previously estimated from the peak of the Ly emission in FORS2 spectra (z=7.008) but do confirm that the objects are approximately at the same redshift and, given the close separation on the sky (91.3kpc, C18), likely physically connected. We will use these new estimates in the present analysis. The total Ly fluxes are 12.8 1.0 and 21.8 1.1 10^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 for BDF521 and BDF2195, respectively.
We find EW(Ly )=60 5 , FWHM(Ly )=263 5 km/s for BDF521, and EW(Ly )=62 3 , FWHM(Ly )=250 3 km/s for BDF2195.

sources.summary: The paper describes this close z~7 Ly emitter as part of a bright pair with strong Ly equivalent width, 4.4 pMpc separation, and Ly visibility requiring ionized surroundings plus nearby sources.

quotes.quote: The BDF is the only field where two close-by bright Ly emitting galaxies, BDF-3299 (z=7.109) and BDF-521 (z=7.008) have been found Vanzella2011 . These two L L ^* objects, originally selected as z-dropout candidates C10b hereafter Castellano2010b , show Ly equivalent width > 50 and are separated by a distance of only 4.4 proper Mpc (pMpc). The detection of bright Ly emission from them can be explained by these sources being embedded in an HII region that allows Ly photons to redshift far away from the line center before they reach the almost neutral IGM. Vanzella2011 has compared the size of the HII region these galaxies can build Loeb2005 to the minimum ionized radius allowing their Ly photons to escape Wyithe2005 , finding that they both cannot generate HII regions large enough to explain the visibility of their lines even if a maximum Lyman continuum escape fraction f _ esc =1 is assumed. Instead, the visibility of their Ly can be explained by the presence of additional ionizing sources in their vicinity Dayal2009 .

gmw_00003029.name: BDF2195
gmw_00003029.overview: BDF2195 is a bright Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=7.0124 [arXiv:2203.04976]. It resides within the BDF reionized bubble and is likely physically connected to the nearby galaxy BDF521 [arXiv:2203.04976].

sources.summary: BDF2195 is a bright z=7.0124 Ly-emitting galaxy in the BDF bubble; XSHOOTER measured stronger Ly flux, EW 62, and FWHM 250 km/s while constraining its ionizing budget.

quotes.quote: We investigate the ionizing properties of the pair of bright Ly emitting galaxies BDF521 and BDF2195 at z=7.012 in order to constrain their contribution to the formation of the BDF "reionized bubble" Castellano2016,Castellano2018 in which they have been shown to reside. We obtain constraints on UV emission lines (CIV 1548 doublet, HeII 1640 , OIII ] 1660 doublet, and CIII ] 1909 doublet) from deep VLT-XSHOOTER observations and compare them to those available for other high-redshift objects, and to models with mixed stellar and AGN emission. We use this spectroscopic information together with the photometry available in the field to constrain the physical properties of the two objects using the spectro-photometric fitting code BEAGLE .

Ly emission We detect Ly line emission for both objects: the flux-weighted mean wavelength of the line corresponds to z=7.0121 and z=7.0124 for BDF521 and BDF2195, respectively. These redshifts are slightly higher than previously estimated from the peak of the Ly emission in FORS2 spectra (z=7.008) but do confirm that the objects are approximately at the same redshift and, given the close separation on the sky (91.3kpc, C18), likely physically connected. We will use these new estimates in the present analysis. The total Ly fluxes are 12.8 1.0 and 21.8 1.1 10^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 for BDF521 and BDF2195, respectively.
We find EW(Ly )=60 5 , FWHM(Ly )=263 5 km/s for BDF521, and EW(Ly )=62 3 , FWHM(Ly )=250 3 km/s for BDF2195.

gmw_00003030.name: GDS9945
gmw_00003030.overview: GDS9945 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.50 [arXiv:1909.00702, arXiv:2007.08172]. The galaxy exhibits a complex morphology consisting of three components and shows a possible active galactic nucleus signature through weak NV emission [arXiv:1909.00702].

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE 1185 to compare separate and simultaneous fits of its Lyman-alpha spectrum and surface-brightness profile, finding tighter combined parameter constraints.

quotes.quote: To Fit Spectra and Surface brightness profiles Separately Figures fig-lnliksp-1185 and fig-lnliksbp-1185 show the posterior distributions of the parameters for the spectrum ( L _ sp ) and surface brightness profile ( L _ SBP ), respectively, of MUSE 1185. Note that posterior values are normalized by their maximum (denoted by L ). Overall, spectrum provides tighter constraints on the parameters than surface brightness profile does.

Figure fig-lnliktot-1185 shows those for MUSE 1185 (refer to similar figures in Appendix sec-othertargets for other target galaxies). The total posterior distributions become much sharper than those of spectrum or surface brightness profile. The model spectrum (left) and surface brightness profile (middle) with the best-fit parameter set obtained from L _ tot are shown in Figure fig-bestfit-tot-1185 .

sources.summary: The paper describes GDS9945 as a z=4.50 Lyman emitter with possible weak NV AGN emission, uncertain X-ray association, contamination concerns, and a three-component morphology.

quotes.quote: GDS9945 has an uncertain X-ray association.

This analysis has provided the detection of GDS9945 as a clear Lyman emitter at z=4.50 with a possible AGN signature of weak NV emission. This source was already known as Lyman emitter by means of previous unpublished spectroscopic information (see G15), however since its X-ray position could be contaminated by a close brighter galaxy (see Figure 9 in the Appendix) the source was not used for the computation of the LF.
A complex morphology appears also for GDS9945, a source with three components (the central point-like) just north of the brightest galaxy.

gmw_00003031.name: dw1908m6343
gmw_00003031.overview: The galaxy dw1908m6343 is a blue, highly irregular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2203.00014]. Located in close projected proximity to NGC 6744, it is considered a highly likely satellite companion of that system [arXiv:2203.00014].

sources.summary: The paper treats dw1908m6343 as a likely NGC 6744 satellite despite a problematic high-S/N SBF distance, citing its blue irregular morphology, similar fluctuation strength, and projected proximity.

quotes.quote: dw1908m6343 from the NGC 6744 system had a high S/N SBF measurement but yielded far too small of a distance. This is a blue, very irregular dwarf which can bias the fluctuation too high since a S e rsc model is assumed in the SBF measurement. Considering the fact that the SBF amount is visually about the same as other, more regular blue satellites in this system and dw1908m6343 is quite near NGC 6744 in projection, this is very likely a satellite.

gmw_00003032.name: dw1300p1843
gmw_00003032.overview: The dwarf galaxy dw1300p1843 is a confirmed satellite of the NGC 4826 system [arXiv:2203.00014]. It exhibits a visually noticeable surface brightness fluctuation distance consistent with its host and possesses a large effective radius [arXiv:2203.00014].

sources.summary: The paper confirms dw1300p1843 as an NGC 4826 satellite, arguing that its SBF is host-consistent, visually noticeable, and paired with a large size unlikely for a background object.

quotes.quote: dw1300p1843 from the NGC 4826 system has a SBF distance consistent with that of the host but only with S/N 3 . However, it has visually noticeable SBF that appears to be the same level has other satellites in this system. Additionally, its large r_e 30  size indicates it is unlikely to be background (it is already an outlier to the mass-size relation). Thus, we consider it confirmed.

gmw_00003033.name: dw1046p1259
gmw_00003033.overview: The dwarf galaxy dw1046p1259 is a confirmed satellite of the NGC 3379 system [arXiv:2203.00014]. It is situated within a halo of scattered light from a nearby star, which biases its surface brightness fluctuation measurements [arXiv:2203.00014].

sources.summary: The paper confirms dw1046p1259 as an NGC 3379 satellite, explaining that stellar scattered light likely biases its strong SBF signal toward a slightly low distance.

quotes.quote: dw1046p1259 from the NGC 3379 system has a very strong SBF signal but yields a slightly lower distance than its host. This dwarf is in a halo of scattered light from a nearby star which often biases the fluctuation measurement high (and thus distance low) carlsten2020b . We consider this a confirmed satellite.

gmw_00003034.name: UGCA337
gmw_00003034.overview: UGCA337 is a slightly irregular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2203.00014]. It has a redshift that places it within the NGC 5055 system, making it almost certainly a satellite companion of NGC 5055 [arXiv:2203.00014].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGCA337 as almost certainly an NGC 5055 satellite, despite a slightly biased SBF upper bound, because it is irregular and passes the redshift cut.

quotes.quote: UGCA337 (dw1312p4147) from the NGC 5055 system has an extremely strong SBF signal but the 2 distance upper bound is slightly closer than NGC 5055. It is slightly irregular which is biasing the SBF a little high. It has a redshift that makes the cz <275 km/s cut, and is almost certainly a satellite.

gmw_00003035.name: dw1240m1140
gmw_00003035.overview: The dwarf galaxy dw1240m1140 is a confirmed companion of the M104 system [arXiv:2203.00014]. It is located very close to M104 in projection and exhibits a dwarf spheroidal morphology [arXiv:2203.00014].

sources.summary: The paper includes dw1240m1140 as a confirmed M104 satellite, interpreting its foreground SBF signal as biased by M104's halo and citing its dSph morphology and projected proximity.

quotes.quote: dw1240m1140 from the M104 system shows a strong SBF signal that puts it in the foreground. However, this dwarf is located very close to M104 in projection, and the halo of M104 appears to be adding fluctuation power to the SBF measurement. Considering its dSph morphology, proximity to M104, and SBF, we include it in the `confirmed' category.

gmw_00003036.name: dw1241p3251
gmw_00003036.overview: The galaxy dw1241p3251 is a confirmed satellite companion in the NGC 4631 system [arXiv:2203.00014]. Although surface brightness fluctuations initially suggested it was slightly in the foreground, its redshift is consistent with NGC 4631 [arXiv:2203.00014].

sources.summary: The paper confirms dw1241p3251 as an NGC 4631 satellite, arguing that the SBF foreground estimate is underestimated and that its redshift is consistent with the host.

quotes.quote: dw1241p3251 from the NGC 4631 system appears slightly foreground from the SBF measurement. However, this galaxy is not well fit by a S e rsic profile, and thus the distance is likely underestimated. This galaxy has a redshift consistent with NGC 4631 ( cz 60 km/s) and is considered `confirmed'.

gmw_00003037.name: UCD13
gmw_00003037.overview: UCD13 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:1402.0687]. It is likely the remnant nucleus of a tidally stripped dwarf elliptical galaxy that has retained a large portion of its stellar envelope [arXiv:1402.0687].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UCD13 as the largest Perseus UCD, measures its size, dispersion, mass, and metallicity, and argues it is a tidally stripped dwarf-elliptical nucleus.

quotes.quote: We also measure a central velocity dispersion of a third, UCD13 ( _ 0 = 38 8 km s ^ 1 ), the most extended UCD in our sample. We determine it to have radius R_ e = 85 1.1 pc, a dynamical mass of (2.3 0.8) 10^ 8 M _ , and a metallicity [ Z/H ] = -0.52^ +0.33 _ 0.29 dex. UCD13 and the cluster's central galaxy, NGC 1275, have a projected separation of 30 kpc and a radial velocity difference of 20 km s ^ 1 . Based on its size, red colour, internal velocity dispersion, dynamical mass, metallicity and proximity to NGC 1275, we argue that UCD13 is likely the remnant nucleus of a tidally stripped dE, with this progenitor dE having M_ B 16 mag and mass 10^ 9 M _ .

The largest UCD (UCD13) is likely the nuclear remnant of a tidally stripped dwarf elliptical that has retained a large fraction of its stellar envelope, consistent with the simulations of 2013MNRAS.433.1997P . Its dynamical mass is (2.3 0.8) 10^ 8 M _ , making it one of the most massive UCDs identified to date. Its internal velocity dispersion, _ 0 = 38 9 km s ^ 1 , along with its metallicity [ Z/H ] = -0.52^ +0.33 _ 0.29 dex, and colour (B-R)_ 0 = 1.37 , are consistent with its origin as a dE with an M_ B of 16 and a mass 10^ 9 M _ . Its size ( R_ e = 85 1.1 pc) and location at the centre of Perseus, the UCD is inconsistent with being a massive star cluster.

gmw_00003038.name: UGC 6093
gmw_00003038.overview: UGC 6093 is a barred spiral galaxy featuring a central bulge, a bar, a large-scale ring, and two counterclockwise spiral arms [arXiv:1705.09762]. The galaxy hosts a confirmed edge-on disk megamaser system [arXiv:2302.02572, arXiv:1801.06332] that orbits a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1801.06332].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 6093 as a barred-Sbc galaxy with a central bulge, bar, large-scale ring, spiral arms, and an uncertain small nuclear bulge classification.

quotes.quote: UGC 6093 UGC 6093 is a barred-Sbc 1991Vaucouleurs with a luminosity distance of 158 Mpc 2000Mould . The large-scale structure consists of a central bulge and a bar connected to a large-scale ring, from which two large spiral arms extend counterclockwise. As the central structure we identify a region with a minimum in the F814W ellipticity profile and a change in position angle opposite for F110W and F814W at 0.2 arcsec ( 150 pc). No additional structure is visible in the brightness profile.

While there appears to be a near-circular feature visible in the structure map, it is nearing on the PSF scale, so we classify this region as an uncertain bulge. Additionally, a dim two-arm grand-design spiral may be distinguished outside this region in the structure map. It is, however, too uncertain to be included in the region's classification.

sources.summary: UGC 6093 is described as a confirmed disk maser whose 183 GHz spectrum shows systemic features and only sub-significant hints of high-velocity counterparts.

quotes.quote: The 22 GHz maser system in UGC 6093 was discovered by the MCP, and VLBI observations presented in Zhao_2018 confirm that it is a disk maser system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of UGC 6093 is shown in the upper lefthand panel of fig:hex2 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Pesce_2015 (see Fig. 1 in that paper). The 22 GHz spectrum is an average of multiple epochs of spectral monitoring observations taken with the GBT.

The only significant component seen in the 183 GHz spectrum of UGC 6093 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 30 around a central velocity of 10848 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 16.0 mJy, which is a factor of 12 fainter than the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. Similar to ESO 558-G009, the overlaid 22 GHz spectrum in fig:hex2 draws the eye to some features in the 183 GHz spectrum of UGC 6093 near 10100 and 11600 that are suggestive because they coincide with high-velocity features at 22 GHz. However, we again find that none of these features rise above the 3 significance level, so we cannot confirm the presence of high-velocity masers in the 183 GHz spectrum of UGC 6093.

sources.summary: The paper maps UGC 6093's nuclear megamasers as an edge-on disk with little warping, fits a 3-D model, and measures a 2.58e7 solar-mass SMBH.

quotes.quote: UGC 6093 The VLBI map of UGC 6093 (Figure mapa ) reveals an edge-on disk. The well-aligned systemic masers and high-velocity masers define a disk plane with little or no warping. The redshifted side of the disk plane has a position angle PA 20^ east of north (this definition of position angle PA is kept in the following subsections). The diameter of the disk is about 0.9 mas or 0.6 pc , which is within the typical range of sizes for megamaser disks.

The 3-D geometric model (an edge-on disk at inclination angle i = 93.8^ and position angle PA = -20.1^ ) constrained by our observations is a good fit. The SMBH lies within 0.01 mas of the reference center of the VLBI map, and the fitted mass is M_ SMBH = 2.58^ +0.11 _ 0.09 10^7 M_ .

gmw_00003039.name: NGC 6926
gmw_00003039.overview: NGC 6926 is a spiral luminous infrared galaxy containing a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:2206.08072, arXiv:1501.02022] and a complex water megamaser system [arXiv:1801.06332, arXiv:1501.02022]. The galaxy is in an early stage of interaction with the dwarf galaxy NGC 6929 [arXiv:2206.08072], resulting in a tidally distorted disk with bright H II regions [arXiv:1501.02022].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6926 as a complex megamaser system with bent geometry and uncertain disk origin, using maser extent and velocity offset to set an SMBH mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: NGC 6926 The VLBI map for NGC 6926 in Figure mapa reveals a complex, bent geometry. The redshifted maser complex appears on the map as a cluster with no organized velocity structure. We do not detect any masers with radial velocities >6192 km s ^ 1 . Only one blueshifted maser at 5775 km s ^ 1 was detected in the VLBI map, while the other at 5815 km s ^ 1 seen in the GBT spectrum 10 days earlier than the VLBI observation was not detected.

Whether the "systemic" maser is a part of a potential disk maser system is not clear. The extent of the maser spots is about 11 mas or 4.4 pc, which is larger than the typical diameters of other megamaser disks. Although the observations do not clearly identify a disk origin in this case, we still make an estimation of the NGC 6926 SMBH mass based on a flat, edge-on Keplerian-disk assumption.

We estimate a disk radius =2.2 pc as half the extent of the megamaser emission, and use the largest velocity offset of the high-velocity masers from the systemic velocity ( 309 km s ^ 1 for NGC 6926) as the rotation velocity ( V_ rot ) to estimate an upper limit to the enclosed mass: M_ SMBH 4.8 10^7 ( 2.2 pc ) ( v_ rot 309 km s ^ 1 )^2 ( D_A 82.2 Mpc ) M_

sources.summary: The metallicity-statistics paper highlights NGC 6926 as an unusually large-correlation-length LIRG/Seyfert in an early interaction with NGC 6929, possibly showing gas exchange or diagnostic failure in a starburst environment.

quotes.quote: They are NGC 6926 and JO206, and we show their (undeprojected) H maps and metallicity fluctuation maps in fig:large_lcorr . Both galaxies show large non-axisymmetric metallicity fluctuations, demonstrating that the pipeline two-point correlation function computation is detecting a real feature of the galaxy metallicity distributions. NGC 6926 is a spiral luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) with a Seyfert nucleus, and is at a very early stage of interaction with the dwarf galaxy NGC 6929 that lies 4 arcmin to its east outside the MUSE FoV; Dopita15, Herrero-Illana17 . JO206 is a jellyfish galaxy experiencing gas stripping caused by ram-pressure during its infall into a cluster Ramatsoku19 .

Given that NGC 6926 is experiencing an interaction, there are two plausible explanations for its unusual metallicity fluctuation map. First, the regions where metallicities are enhanced [ ( O/H) > 0 ] roughly match the spiral arms. This could indicate that a burst of the extreme star formation in the arms, triggered by shocks caused by the tidal effect, has led to a real jump in the metallicity, which would then be diffused away as the galaxy settles. Second, it is also possible that the map of NGC 6926 simply reflects the failure of our metallicity diagnostic. The D16 diagnostic is calibrated based on H ii regions in the Milky Way and similar nearby galaxies, and the H ii regions found in a starburst like NGC 6926 may explore ranges of dust extinction or ionisation parameter beyond those where the model is reliable.

sources.summary: NGC 6926 has a Seyfert 2 nucleus, dynamically disturbed ENLR, water megamaser, bright H II regions in a tidally distorted LIRG disk, and possible minor merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 6926: A Seyfert 2 nucleus with a dynamically-disturbed ENLR. This source contains an H _2 O megamaser Greenhill03 . Many bright HII regions are seen in what appears to be a tidally-distorted LIRG disk galaxy. A narrow-band H image has been published by Dopita02b . The region to the north of the nucleus may contain a low-metallicity \ region complex, based on its weak \ and strong \ relative to H .
We may speculate that this galaxy is undergoing a minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6926 among flat-continuum sources, reporting optically thin plasma emission, an APEC plus reflection continuum, and a strong 6.4 keV Fe-K line.

quotes.quote: The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show excess emission around 0.6--1.0 keV suggesting the presence of emission from optically thin plasma. We used an APEC plasma model to represent this feature. An APEC + reflection model describes the continua of IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, and NGC 6926.

The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2XMMi J184540.6 630522, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show an emission line at around 6.4 keV in the source rest frame with an EW in the range of 1.1--4.6 keV.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6926 as an extra CON candidate whose spectra meet PAH criteria but suffer reduction issues and show aperture-size trends.

quotes.quote: We chose targets without staring mode spectra with the exception of NGC 6926, which was selected as a CON candidate by Garcia-Bernete2022 to provide an additional candidate to investigate.

Additionally, NGC 6926 meets both PAH EW ratios with the staring mode but this spectrum suffers from some reduction issues. For the two known CONs (ESO 302-G030, ZW049.057) we see a clear trend with how the EW ratios change with aperture size. As more of the galaxy disk is included within the aperture, the EW ratio moves away from the CON selection region and towards the mean values of the star-forming calibration sample. This shows that dilution from the host galaxy can cause objects to be missed by the PAH EW method however higher spatial resolution will overcome this issue. The latter point is important considering the factor 10 increase in the spatial resolution of the Mid Infrared Instrument (MIRI) on the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) compared to the Spitzer spectral maps. A few other objects show trends with aperture size such as the other potential CON candidates Mrk 938 and NGC 6926 but also NGC 7552 and UGC 03410.

gmw_00003040.name: NGC 2824
gmw_00003040.overview: NGC 2824 is a galaxy that hosts a water megamaser [arXiv:1801.06332]. The megamaser emission lacks high-velocity disk features, instead displaying a broad spectral profile and spatial distribution characteristic of parsec-scale nuclear jet masers [arXiv:1801.06332].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2824 lacks high-velocity disk-maser features; its broad megamaser profile and VLBI distribution resemble pc-scale nuclear jet masers.

quotes.quote: The single-dish and VLBI spectra of both NGC 2824 and J0350 0127 show no high velocity features characteristic of a disk maser. Most of the megamaser emission from NGC 2824 comes from a broad line complex in the velocity range from 2500 to 3000 km s ^ 1 (Figure spectrab ). The broad profile of the spectrum of NGC 2824 is similar to those of NGC 1052 and Mrk 348, in which the masers originate from the pc-scale nuclear jet clau98, fal2000, pec03 .

NGC 2824 and J0350 0127 On the VLBI map of NGC 2824 (Figure mapb ), the masers are distributed over an area spanning 20 mas 3.8 pc . Although we do not detect continuum emission in this galaxy, the "broad line" spectral profile is reminiscent of pc-scale jet masers in NGC 1052 and Mrk 348 clau98, fal2000, pec03 .

gmw_00003041.name: J0350 0127
gmw_00003041.overview: J0350 0127 is a spiral or irregular galaxy [arXiv:1702.06545] that hosts a broad, luminous water megamaser [arXiv:1702.06545] likely associated with an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1702.06545]. The galaxy lacks high-velocity disk-maser features [arXiv:1801.06332], with its clustered masers potentially tracing a disk fragment or diffuse outflows [arXiv:1801.06332].

sources.summary: The paper reports that J0350 0127 lacks high-velocity disk-maser features, has masers clustered near the map center, and may trace a disk fragment or diffuse outflows.

quotes.quote: The single-dish and VLBI spectra of both NGC 2824 and J0350 0127 show no high velocity features characteristic of a disk maser.
In J0350 0127, the masers are confined to a narrow velocity range between 12320 and 12380 km s ^ 1 and peak at 12352 km s ^ 1 , only 45 km s ^ 1 from the systemic velocity.

For J0350 0127, most of the masers are clustered near the map center, while a few extend to the north by about 0.2 mas 0.2 pc , and others to the west by about 0.5 mas ( 0.5 pc ). The origin of these masers is unclear. They may reveal part of a maser disk, or they may be associated with diffuse outflows.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0350 0127 as a poorly known spiral or irregular galaxy with a broad luminous water maser, no detected 20 GHz continuum, and an ambiguous but likely AGN-associated maser.

quotes.quote: J0350 0127 is an almost otherwise unknown spiral or possibly irregular galaxy included in the 2MASS Redshift Survey huchra2012 and detected in water maser emission by the GBT programs GBT09-051 footnote:maser_url in 2010 and GBT10C-019 footnote:maser_url in 2011. The peak flux density was 350 mJy in 2011, in good agreement with our VLA measurement of 347 4 mJy (Table tab:masers and Figure fig:2MASX0350 ). This is a broad and luminous maser ( L_ iso = 5181 108 \ L_ ) with no associated 20 GHz continuum, down to an rms noise of 15 Jy beam ^ 1 (Table tab:obs ). Without a continuum detection, the nature of this maser remains ambiguous, although it is almost certainly associated with an AGN.

gmw_00003042.overview: The galaxy 41908, also designated A2744-10, exhibits a star-forming disk and tail [arXiv:2402.13315]. It displays a positive disk metallicity trend [arXiv:2402.13315] and contains heavily obscured star formation indicated by polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon and Brackett emission lines [arXiv:2303.01115].

sources.summary: The paper flags A2744-10 as exceptional, with positive disk metallicity trends, higher or outward-growing tail metallicity, star-forming disk and tail emission, and unavailable stellar-mass surface density.

quotes.quote: This is evident in cases like A370-01, A370-03, A2744-06, A2744-09, and A2744-10. A370-06, the only one with an AGN in the sample, and A370-08 stand out instead for having the whole disk dominated by composite spaxels. Metallicity trends in the disks are typically negative or flat with two positive exceptions, A370-06, and A2744-10. Note that A370-06, the one with the AGN, has a large number of AGN-powered spaxels along an extended ionization cone and Fig. fig:BPT_MAPS 2022ApJ...925....4M , which are likely to affect our capability to assess the profile. In most cases, the tail metallicity declines with distance along the tail and is generally lower than in the corresponding disk, with three notable exceptions: A2744-04, A2744-09, and A2744-10.

Overall, the global metallicity gradient (disk + tail) is negative or almost flat (except for A2744-10 and P04 estimate of A2744-09 which have sharp positive slopes as already discussed above). figure 9 h figure 9 h figure 9 h h In addition, ionization characteristics (composite vs. SF) of tails and disks are not necessarily coupled (see fig. fig:BPT_MAPS ). In 5 galaxies, these properties agree, being both tail and disk dominated ( >95 ) either by SF (A370-07, A2744-03, AA2744-04, A2744-10) or by composite emission (A370-06). However, in 3 other galaxies, the emission in the tail has probably a different origin from that in the disk see low-redshift studies 2019MNRAS.482.4466P,2021ApJ...922..131T . For instance, in A370-01 the tail is mostly composite while the disk is mostly SF. Also, there are cases such as A370-08 where the disk is dominated by composite emission, while in the tail numerous spaxels are found both in the composite and in the SF regime. It is interesting to assess whether the observed trends depend on galaxy stellar mass. The two lowest mass galaxies, A2744-03 and A2744-04, (of the order of (M_ /M_ ) 8 ) have trends consistent with a flat metallicity gradient across disk and tail, although with a large uncertainty and a few points. Also, considering the proximity of the disk angular size of these galaxies to the observation's resolution limit, this might be simply a resolution effect. The only galaxy in which the tail metallicity appears to be consistently higher than in the disk ( pyqz ), or at least to have a growing outward profile (P04), is A2744-10, with a mass (M_ /M_ )=9.3 .
Moreover, the surface density of stellar mass for A2744-10 is not yet available.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 41908 as a red-excess galaxy with NIRSpec PAH and Brackett emission that can explain elevated F356W/F444W fluxes and suggests dust-obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: As part of the GLASS-JWST survey (ERS 1324, PI Treu; Treu2022 ), we observed one red excess galaxy (41908) with JWST/NIRSpec in multi-object spectroscopy (MOS).

Figure fig:spectrum shows this spectrum shifted to the rest frame. Two lines are clearly visible: the most prominent feature is the 3.3 m Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbon (PAH) line, which has a rest-frame Equivalent Width (EW) of 590 30 . Also the Brackett line is observed, with an emission EW of 30 1 . The prominence of these lines can be responsible of the elevated fluxes measured in F356W and especially in F444W.
Thus, we hypothesize that the PAH observed in 41908 is due to heavily obscured star formation, as also suggested by the analysis of the SFR-mass relation and E(B-V) distribution.

gmw_00003043.name: IC 2531
gmw_00003043.overview: IC 2531 is a southern, edge-on spiral galaxy classified as an Sb or Sc type [arXiv:1507.03095, arXiv:1605.06239] that features a regular dust lane [arXiv:1305.3130] and a peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:1003.3110, arXiv:1507.03095]. Its structure includes a warped stellar and H I disk [arXiv:1507.03095], outer-disk dust corrugations [arXiv:2005.10290], and an X-like morphology indicating a bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0512244].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO435-G025 as the only large spiral in the sample, an Sb barred system with HI line splitting, extended column density, filaments, and low-S/N flaring evidence.

quotes.quote: ESO435-G025 (IC2531) ESO435-G25 is the only large spiral galaxy in our sample. It is an Sb galaxy with a bright peanut-shaped bulge shown to be a bar from the ``figure-8'' signature in the optical emission line kinematics bf1999 . The line splitting faintly seen as a figure-8 signature in the \ XV diagram in Fig. fig:ch2-eso435-g025-combi-plot was first detected by bf1997 . We use the same ATCA \ observations in our study.

The \ column density map shows the galaxy extends to 25 beamwidths on either side of the galactic centre, and three beamwidths on either side of the \ midplane. Above the galactic centre, the \ column density map in Fig. fig:ch2-eso435-g025-m0 shows \ filaments extending to 3 kpc above and below the midplane. Like ESO109-G021, the other distant galaxy, the peak signal-to-noise is quite low at 10.9 . Although the \ disk appears to be flared in the \ column density map (Fig. fig:ch2-eso435-g025-m0 ), the flaring is not visibly obvious in the \ channel maps due to the low signal-to-noise.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2531 as the sample's only Sc galaxy, with high gas mass, high rotation speed, a flat H I layer, asymmetric kinematics, and a possible weak bar pattern.

quotes.quote: IC 2531 is the only Sc galaxy in our sample. The classification is different from that of Bureau1999A and OBrien2010A , where the galaxy has been classified as an Sb galaxy. As an Sc galaxy, it has the largest gas mass of the sample, and has a maximum rotation velocity v _ max which is twice as high as the rest of the sample (table tbl:HISpectrumProperties ). The H I layer is remarkably flat, with the disk seemingly having a smaller scale-height than the stellar disk (figure fig:IC2531-DSS ).

The PV diagram itself is, however, fairly asymmetric. Interestingly, HIPASS also reports a more asymmetric profile. The asymmetry is also present in the analysis of the galaxy in figure 15 OBrien2010C , where the two sides of the derived surface density can be seen to deviate strongly. The PV-diagram shows a so-called ``figure 8''-pattern, for which Bureau1997A suggest that it is due to a very extended weak bar.

sources.summary: IC 2531 is a southern Sb/Sc galaxy with a peanut-shaped bulge, a warped stellar and H I disk, asymmetric sides, and irregular extraplanar H I features.

quotes.quote: IC 2531 IC 2531 is the only Southern galaxy in our sample and is located at a distance of 36.8 Mpc. It is classified as an Sb or Sc type galaxy and has a prominent peanut-shaped bulge. ascribe this to a barred potential, although optical emission line observations do not show the characteristic line splitting in the bulge region bureau97,bureau99,kregel04b . A false colour V-band image taken with the Faulkes Telescope South is shown in the top panel of Fig. fig:I2531_V_mom0 .

Like the stellar disk, the H i disk is also slightly warped and more extended towards the NE side. This was already observed by . The individual channel maps show that the behaviour of the H i disk somewhat deviates from that of a perfect regularly rotating disk (see Fig. fig:IC2531_final_channels in the appendix). The approaching and receding side behave quite differently, for example regarding the shape of the channel maps and the (projected) peak intensities. In addition, (projected) extraplanar features seem to appear and disappear irregularly throughout the cube.

sources.summary: IC 2531 is presented as an almost perfectly edge-on southern spiral with a prominent regular dust lane, extended dust features, and a previously modeled extended dust distribution.

quotes.quote: IC 2531 IC 2531 is the only galaxy on the southern hemisphere in our sample. It is located at a distance of 36.8 Mpc and it has a D_ 25 diameter of 7 5. It is classified either as an Sb or as an Sc type galaxy showing H ii regions, and it is characterised by a conspicuous peanut-shaped or boxy bulge 1986AJ.....91...65J, 1987A&AS...70..465D . With a fitted inclination of 89 6 0 2 1999A&A...344..868X , this galaxy is oriented almost perfectly edge-on. The dust lane is very prominent and regular, and extends from the galactic centre to the edge of the disc, as well as into the higher stellar layers, which is evidenced by dust features traceable up to a few scaleheights 2000MNRAS.313..800D .

Many attempts have been made to determine the dust distribution from fitting extinction models to the dust lane by several works including 1989ApJ...337..163W , 1996A&A...309..715J , 1998AJ....115.1438K and 1999A&A...344..868X , but only the latter performed a global fit. According to the model by 1999A&A...344..868X , which includes both absorption and scattering, the dust in IC 2531 has a horizontally extended distribution with a scalelength of 75 or 13.7 kpc, about 50 larger than the fitted stellar scalelength.

sources.summary: The paper models IC 2531 as a nearby edge-on late-type spiral, finding that radiative-transfer fits still underpredict far-infrared dust emission by about a factor of two.

quotes.quote: We investigate the dust energy balance for the edge-on galaxy IC 2531, one of the seven galaxies in the HER OES sample. We perform a state-of-the-art radiative transfer modelling based, for the first time, on a set of optical and near-infrared galaxy images. We show that taking into account near-infrared imaging in the modelling significantly improves the constraints on the retrieved parameters of the dust content. We confirm the result from previous studies that including a young stellar population in the modelling is important for explaining the observed stellar energy distribution. However, the discrepancy between the observed and modelled thermal emission at far-infrared wavelengths, the so-called dust energy balance problem, is still present: the model underestimates the observed fluxes by a factor of about two.

IC 2531 is a nearby late-type spiral galaxy ( D 36.8 Mpc; see Table table1 ) located in the southern hemisphere. It is viewed almost directly edge-on: in Fig. IC2531_rgb we can see that the sharp dust lane divides the galaxy body into two equal parts.

sources.summary: The paper models IC 2531 as the sample's only Sb galaxy, finding the highest circular velocity, imperfect bulge reproduction, measured stellar scale dimensions, and a prominent dust lane.

quotes.quote: IC 2531 Galaxy IC 2531 is the only Sb galaxy in our sample, and has the highest maximum circular velocity at v_ max =260.5 km/s (Table tbl:stellar-main1 ). This is nearly twice the maximum circular velocity of the other galaxies in our sample. The central brightness component has not been reproduced correctly. This bulge is most likely peanut-shaped or boxy, which requires a different bulge model than adopted here Jarvis1986A,deSouza1987A . The scale length is h_ R ,* =5077 262 pc and the scale height h_ z ,* =613 46 pc. Kregel2002 report a much longer h_ R ,* =12511 2643 pc and h_ z ,* =658.5 65.8 pc.
The galaxy features a very prominent dust lane, which Kregels I-band model does not treat for and so their scale length might be overestimated.

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO NTT data for IC 2531, finding clear outer-disc dust corrugations with sub-arcsecond deviations and a dust lane that becomes wavy outward.

quotes.quote: The outer discs of all the five galaxies display clear corrugations. UGC 7522 and IC 2531 show sub-arcsecond mid-plane deviations but NGC 4607, NGC 4302 and NGC 5470 show greater waviness. We use Fourier transform to extract the wavelengths of these patterns (see the right panel of Figs 2-6). Sometimes, more than one peak (the observed corrugation could be a superposition of two or more wavelengths) is seen. We then take the wavelength corresponding to the strongest peak as our best measurement.
On the other hand, in IC 2531, the dust lane is pretty much straight and aligned with the stellar mid-plane in the central parts, becoming progressively wavy outwards.

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 2531 as a real Sc-galaxy prototype for dust-emission simulations, adopting prior stellar and dust models while noting its dust energy-balance problem.

quotes.quote: In our simulations we essentially used a well-defined model of the galaxy IC 2531, varying its inclination angle and the distance to it (i.e. its spatial resolution). Two models for the dust component, an exponential disc and a \ disc, are adopted to show if we can see a difference between the fitted dust emission profiles in these mock galaxies. IC 2531 is a typical Sc galaxy.

2016A&A...592A..71M performed a consistent fitting of the stellar and dust structural components and obtained a plausible complex model of one of the galaxies from the HER OES ( HER schel Observations of Edge-on Spirals, 2013A&A...556A..54V ) sample, IC 2531. Despite the fact that for this galaxy they found the dust energy balance problem, we adopted their results in the current study to create realistic galaxy simulations and then estimate the dust distribution parameters in the FIR/submm for these galaxy images using the technique described in Sect. sec:decomp .

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 2531 as one of three edge-on disk galaxies whose residual profiles reveal X-like morphology attributed to a bar after subtracting axisymmetric components.

quotes.quote: In a pilot survey we compare the orbital structures which lead to the appearance of ``peanuts'' and ``X''-like features with the edge-on profiles of three disc galaxies (IC 2531, NGC 4013 and UGC 2048). The subtraction from the images of a model representing the axisymmetric component of the galaxies reveals the contribution of the non-axisymmetric terms. We find a direct correspondence between the orbital profiles of 3D bars in models and the observed main morphological features of the residuals.

The main conclusion of the present study is that the careful subtraction of axisymmetric components from the profiles of the three edge-on disk galaxies (NGC 4013, IC 2531 and UGC 2048) reveals an ``X''-like morphology, which indicates the presence of a bar.

gmw_00003044.name: QSO 36
gmw_00003044.overview: QSO 36 is a radio-quiet quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.2356 [arXiv:1612.04661]. It has a close companion galaxy at the same redshift situated 53 kpc away, as well as another compact object intercepted by the slit [arXiv:1612.04661].

sources.summary: The paper describes QSO 36 as a radio-quiet quasar at z=0.2356 with a same-redshift close companion galaxy and another compact slit-intercepted object.

quotes.quote: QSO 36 Radio quiet quasar at z = 0.2356 as derived from [ OIII ] emission lines. The optical spectrum of closest companion galaxy (labeled B, = 19.1; dist. 53 kpc from QSO) shows clear absorption features of Ca II, G band, H _ , and MgI 5175 at z = 0.2356. The chosen slit position intercepts also another compact object (label A) at 33.5 arcsecs from the QSO.

gmw_00003045.name: QSO 40
gmw_00003045.overview: QSO 40 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.2335 [arXiv:1612.04661]. It has a physically associated companion galaxy at a projected distance of 27 kpc that exhibits strong emission lines and star formation [arXiv:1612.04661].

sources.summary: The paper identifies QSO 40 as a quasar with a close companion at matching redshift, strong emission lines, and significant companion star formation.
